EDCS-00381062.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis XVIII consul IIII pater patriae a Rapidu milia passuum III\n
EDCS-00381062.translation: Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, [ordered the construction of a road] for 3 miles.
EDCS-00381062.summary: This inscription records a milestone or road marker, commissioned by Emperor Antoninus Pius, dating to his reign.

EDCS-00381063.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pertinaci Augusto Parthico Arabico et Parthico Azabenico pontifici maximo patri patriae tribuniciae potestatis II imperatori III consuli II proconsuli a Rapidu milia passuum XV\n
EDCS-00381063.translation: For Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, Arabia, and Albania, chief priest, father of the fatherland, holder of tribunician power for the 2nd time, Imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, [this marker was placed] for 15 miles.
EDCS-00381063.summary: This inscription records a milestone or road marker, commissioned by Emperor Septimius Severus, dating to his reign.

EDCS-00381064.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Volusiae Tertullinae maritae castae et incomparabili grammaticae quae vixit annos XLIII menses III dies V quae cum marito fecit annos XX mensem I dies XIIII Domitius Flavianus incompa(rabil) \n
EDCS-00381064.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Volusia Tertullina, chaste and incomparable wife, a teacher, who lived 43 years, 3 months, 5 days, and who was with her husband for 20 years, 1 month, 14 days. Domitius Flavianus, her incomparable...
EDCS-00381064.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Volusia Tertullina, a respected teacher, and her marriage, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03301004.text_edition: \n Geminia Thevestina et Valerius Saturninus votum libentes animo solverunt\n
EDCS-03301004.translation: Geminia Thevestina and Valerius Saturninus have gladly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-03301004.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Geminia Thevestina and Valerius Saturninus, likely dedicated to a deity.

EDCS-03301013.text_edition: \n Hanc struem perennis arae posuit his in sedibus Iulius Festae Secundus coniugi karissimae vixit annos sextriginta bisque viginti dies pondus uteri enisa decimum luce rapta est tertia nata claro Rubriorum genere de primoribus sancta mores pulchra visu praecluens prudentia exornata summo honore magno iudicio patrum aurea vitta et corona Mauricae provinciae haec et divum consecuta est summa pro meritis bona quinque natos lacte mater ipsa quos aluit suo sospites superstitesque liquit votorum potens\n
EDCS-03301013.translation: This mound for an everlasting altar was placed in these seats by Julius Festae Secundus for his most beloved wife, who lived sixty-six years and twenty days, who bore her tenth child and was taken from the light on the third day, born of the noble Rubriorum family, from the foremost people, with holy character, beautiful to behold, endowed with outstanding wisdom, honored with the highest esteem and great judgment of the elders, she achieved this and divine honors for her great merits. Having the power of vows, she left behind five sons whom she nursed herself, safe and surviving.
EDCS-03301013.summary: This funerary inscription describes a monument erected by Julius Festae Secundus for his wife, detailing her noble lineage, virtues, and motherhood, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03301015.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fortunatus ex decurione annos vixit \n
EDCS-03301015.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunatus, formerly a decurion, lived [duration missing] years.
EDCS-03301015.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Fortunatus, a former decurion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03301019.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulitana ann pater piissimus\n
EDCS-03301019.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulitana, father most dutiful.
EDCS-03301019.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Iulitana, whose father is described as most dutiful, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700827.text_edition: \n ------] pro salute Auggggustorum dominorum nnnostrorum Impperatorum Severi et Antonini et Getae nobilissimi Caesaris et Publi Aeli Peregrinini praesidis nostri Messius Priscus beneficiarius ex voto aram restituit\n
EDCS-03700827.translation: [...] for the safety of our lords Augusti, Emperors Severus and Antoninus and the most noble Caesar Geta and our governor Publius Aelius Peregrinus, Messius Priscus, a beneficiarius, restored this altar from a vow.
EDCS-03700827.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an altar by Messius Priscus, a military official, for the safety of Emperors Severus, Antoninus, Geta, and the governor, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03700828.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Clodio Pupienio Maximo Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli et Imperatori Caesari Decimo Caelio Calvino Balbino Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli et Marco Antonio Gordiano nobilissimo Caesari Augusto principi iuventutis nepoti Gordianorum divorum res publica coloniae Nervianae Au<g=O>(ustae) Martialis veteranorum Sitifiensis decreto decurionum pecunia publica mille I \n
EDCS-03700828.translation: For Emperor Caesar Marcus Clodius Pupienus Maximus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and Emperor Caesar Decimus Caelius Calvinus Balbinus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, and Marcus Antonius Gordianus, most noble Caesar Augustus, prince of youth, grandson of the Gordians, the republic of the colony of Nerviana Augusta Martialis of veterans of Sitifis, by decree of the decurions, at public expense, 1000 [units of currency or measure].
EDCS-03700828.summary: This inscription records a decree by the colonial republic of Sitifis honoring the emperors Pupienus and Balbinus and Caesar Gordianus, funded by public money, dating to 238 CE.

EDCS-03700829.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Firmo Tubusuctu ex veteran--- legionis II adiutricis vixit nnos LXXX defuncto Martiis Caesarea Sossio Prisco et Apollinare consulibus provincia CXXX et Firmae filiae virgini vixit annos X defunctae isdem consulibus eadem provincia Persca coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-03700829.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Firmo, formerly a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, lived 80 years, died in the month of March during the consulship of Caesareus Sossius Priscus and Apollinaris, in the province [unknown province abbreviation] province 130. And to Firma, his virgin daughter, lived 10 years, died in the same consulship, in the same province. To Persca, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-03700829.summary: This funerary inscription honors Firmo, a veteran, his daughter Firma, and his wife Persca, with precise dating to the consulship of Sossius Priscus and Apollinaris, likely in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-04901112.text_edition: \n D(is)? M(anibus)? s(acrum) Alezeiuei Rogati praefecti gentis Nabuxorum vixit annos XXXVIII anno provinciae CLVII\n
EDCS-04901112.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sacred. For Alezeiues Rogatus, prefect of the Nabuxori tribe, who lived 38 years in the 157th year of the province.
EDCS-04901112.summary: A funerary inscription for Alezeiues Rogatus, a tribal prefect, dated by the provincial year.

EDCS-04901113.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia [potestate ------\n
EDCS-04901113.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power...
EDCS-04901113.summary: A fragment of an inscription likely dedicating a monument to Emperor Hadrian, mentioning his titles and powers.

EDCS-04901115.translation: For Severus Alexander, the council of Rapidensis.
EDCS-04901115.summary: A dedication by the ordo of Rapidensis, likely to Emperor Severus Alexander.

EDCS-04901125.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio [] [] Claudio Invicto Pio Felici Augusto milia passuum IIII\n
EDCS-04901125.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius [---] [---] Claudius the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, [for a distance of] 4 miles.
EDCS-04901125.summary: A milestone inscription recording a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Claudius, indicating a distance of four Roman miles.

EDCS-04901128.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Aziabenicus sic Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis XII imperator XII consul III pater patriae et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus tribuniciae potestatis VII consul II et Publius Septimius Geta Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Aziabenici sic Parthici maximi filii Marci Aureli Antonini Pii frater populis novis ex Africa inlatis oppidum Usinazensem sic per Publium Aelium Peregrinum procuratorem CC suum constituerunt\n
EDCS-04901128.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, chief priest, holding tribunician power 12 times, acclaimed emperor 12 times, consul 3 times, father of the country, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, holding tribunician power 7 times, consul 2 times, and Publius Septimius Geta, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, brother of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, have established the town of Usinaza for new peoples brought from Africa, through Publius Aelius Peregrinus, their procurator, at a cost of 200 [denarii/sestertii?].
EDCS-04901128.summary: An official decree by Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta establishing the town of Usinazensis for newly arrived populations, overseen by a procurator.

EDCS-05000862.text_edition: \n Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIII imperatori II consuli IIII patri patriae decreto decurionum \n
EDCS-05000862.translation: By decree of the decurions, to Augustus Pius, chief priest, holding tribunician power 18 times, emperor twice, consul 4 times, father of the country.
EDCS-05000862.summary: A dedication, made by local councilors (decurions), to an emperor (likely Hadrian or Antoninus Pius) who is honored with multiple titles and extensive tribunician power.

EDCS-05000868.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudia.
EDCS-05000868.summary: A short funerary inscription naming Claudia.

EDCS-05000869.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Saturnina vixit annos XCV\n
EDCS-05000869.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Saturnina lived 95 years.
EDCS-05000869.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and age of Iulia Saturnina.

EDCS-08300584.text_edition: \n carissimis parentibus Iulio Victorii patri et Adelfiae Fortunatae matri filius eorum Pascentius naturae pietate cogente et eorum caritate singulari mensam testimonium amoris sui et perpetuae posteritatis eorum gratia posuit qu\n
EDCS-08300584.translation: To dearest parents, Iulius Victorius the father and Adelfia Fortunata the mother, their son Pascentius, compelled by nature and piety, and by singular love for them, placed this table as a testimony of his love and for their perpetual remembrance.
EDCS-08300584.summary: A funerary dedication by a son, Pascentius, to his parents Iulius Victorius and Adelfia Fortunata, expressing love and remembrance.

EDCS-08300585.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Clodia Prima vixit annos XXX Helvius Hospes vixit annos XX Lucius Helvius Extricatus filius parentibus piissimis fecit Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Helvius Extricatus vixit annos LV\n
EDCS-08300585.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Clodia Prima lived 30 years. Helvius Hospes lived 20 years. Lucius Helvius Extricatus, son, made [this] for his most dutiful parents. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Helvius Extricatus lived 55 years.
EDCS-08300585.summary: A funerary inscription for multiple family members, mentioning the ages of Clodia Prima, Helvius Hospes, and Lucius Helvius Extricatus, with the latter dedicating it to his parents.

EDCS-08300586.text_edition: \n lia Luciosa vixit annis Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Aemilius Felix vixit annos LXXXVI hic situs\n
EDCS-08300586.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Aemilius Felix lived for 86 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08300586.summary: A tombstone for Publius Aemilius Felix, who lived to be 86 years old.

EDCS-08300587.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Domitius Pariator vixit annos XII menses VII dies VIIII horas XI Emineus filio dulcissimo fecit\n
EDCS-08300587.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Domitius Pariator lived for 12 years, 7 months, 9 days, and 11 hours. Emineus made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-08300587.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Domitius Pariator, who died at the age of 12 years, 7 months, 9 days, and 11 hours, erected by his father Emineus.

EDCS-08300588.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Catelius Porcellus vixit annos XXVIII Catelia Urbana vixit annos XVII Crepereia Victoria filiis fecit\n
EDCS-08300588.translation: To the departed spirits. Catelius Porcellus lived for 28 years. Catelia Urbana lived for 17 years. Crepereia Victoria made this for her children.
EDCS-08300588.summary: A tombstone for Catelius Porcellus (28) and Catelia Urbana (17), erected by their mother Crepereia Victoria.

EDCS-08300589.text_edition: \n Monnata vixit annis XXII mensibus VIIII diebus VII Satrius Optatus veteranus filiae posuit anno provinciae CCVII\n
EDCS-08300589.translation: Monnata lived for 22 years, 9 months, and 7 days. Satrius Optatus, a veteran, placed this for his daughter. In the provincial year 207.
EDCS-08300589.summary: A tombstone for Monnata, who lived to 22 years, 9 months, and 7 days, erected by her father Satrius Optatus, a veteran, in the provincial year 207.

EDCS-08300590.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Pompeius Venator vixit annos LXV\n
EDCS-08300590.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Pompeius Venator lived for 65 years.
EDCS-08300590.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Pompeius Venator, who lived to be 65 years old.

EDCS-08300592.text_edition: \n Ter senos annos natus in flore iuventae hunc tumulum raptus Felicianus habet spes ut magna patris sit maxima causa doloris Euphrosynes matris protegit ossa suae quos pius et coniux NIIOPCA OSCVT OSTE\n
EDCS-08300592.translation: Having lived for eighteen years, snatched away in the bloom of youth, Felicianus has this tomb; may the greatest cause of grief for his mother Euphrosyne be his great hopes for his father. He protects the bones of his mother, whom, a devoted husband...
EDCS-08300592.summary: A tomb inscription lamenting the death of Felicianus at 18, expressing the grief of his mother Euphrosyne and his father, and mentioning his mother's bones being protected.

EDCS-08300593.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cerellia Quarta vixit annis XXXI Helvius Probus uxori \n
EDCS-08300593.translation: To the departed spirits. Cerellia Quarta lived for 31 years. Helvius Probus made this for his wife.
EDCS-08300593.summary: A tombstone for Cerellia Quarta, who lived to be 31 years old, erected by her husband Helvius Probus.

EDCS-08300594.text_edition: \n Nautiae Saturninae matri piissimae Caius Iulius Felix\n
EDCS-08300594.translation: Caius Iulius Felix for his most devoted mother, Nautia Saturnina.
EDCS-08300594.summary: A dedication by Caius Iulius Felix for his mother, Nautia Saturnina.

EDCS-08300595.summary: An inscription likely recording the act of writing or commissioning by Pontius and Nundina.

EDCS-08300596.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum tumulum posuit Caius Iulius Celsinus Iulio Caeciliano filio suo innocentissimo vixit annos VII\n
EDCS-08300596.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Celsinus placed this tomb for his most innocent son, Iulius Caecilianus, who lived for 7 years.
EDCS-08300596.summary: A tombstone for Iulius Caecilianus, who died at age 7, erected by his father Caius Iulius Celsinus.

EDCS-08300597.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum OSVNIA Honorata vixit annos XXX\n
EDCS-08300597.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Osvnia Honorata lived for 30 years.
EDCS-08300597.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Osvnia Honorata at the age of 30, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300599.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aurellius sic Imten ex praefecto gentis Milidiorum vixit annos XLV us huc sic musuleum sic d anno provinciae CCII\n
EDCS-08300599.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurellius, formerly prefect of the Milidians, lived for 45 years; this is his mausoleum, dating from the 202nd year of the province.
EDCS-08300599.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Aurellius, former prefect of the Milidians, dating to 202 AD or later.

EDCS-08300600.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Numerius Gellius Onesimus Gellia Gaetula patrono bene merenti faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08300600.translation: Numerius Gellius Onesimus had this constructed for his well-deserving patron Gellia Gaetula, sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-08300600.summary: A tombstone erected by Numerius Gellius Onesimus for his patron Gellia Gaetula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300601.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Callippus vixit annis L Iulius Castus patronus liberto fecit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300601.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Callippus lived for 50 years. Iulius Castus erected this for his freedman. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08300601.summary: A tombstone for Iulius Callippus, erected by his patron Iulius Castus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300602.text_edition: \n Saturninus Cai Iuli Cracci servus vixsit sic menses V dies XX hic situs est\n
EDCS-08300602.translation: Saturninus, slave of Caius Iulius Craccus, lived for 5 months and 20 days; he is buried here.
EDCS-08300602.summary: A tombstone for Saturninus, a slave of Caius Iulius Craccus, indicating a short lifespan, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300603.text_edition: \n Primogenia sic Fausta Valeriaes sic Cai Iuli Zeti pistoris uxsoris sic liberta ob meritis suis vixit annos XX\n
EDCS-08300603.translation: Primogenia Fausta, freedwoman of Caius Iulius Zetus the baker, lived for 20 years.
EDCS-08300603.summary: A tombstone for Primogenia Fausta, a freedwoman of a baker, who died at the age of 20, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300604.text_edition: \n Titus Valerius Felix Ecleni ancillae suae monumentum fecit vixit nnos XXIII sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300604.translation: Titus Valerius Felix erected this monument for his slave Eclen, who lived for 23 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08300604.summary: A tombstone erected by Titus Valerius Felix for his slave Eclen, who died at the age of 23, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300605.text_edition: \n Reductus dec Apollinaris scribae Peregrinus Iulianus \n
EDCS-08300605.translation: Reductus, scribe of Apollinaris; Peregrinus, scribe of Iulianus.
EDCS-08300605.summary: A fragment that appears to record two scribes, possibly related to funerary contexts, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300608.summary: A dedication to a wife, likely a funerary monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300610.text_edition: \n Claudia Pheme annorum XX hic sita est Claudius Bassio et Vibia Lethe parentes fecerunt te rogo praeteriens dicas sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300610.translation: Claudia Pheme, aged 20, is buried here. Claudius Bassius and Vibia Lethe, her parents, erected this. We ask you, as you pass by, to say: May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08300610.summary: A tombstone for Claudia Pheme, erected by her parents, who died at the age of 20, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300612.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Claudio Saturioni filio qui vixit annos IIII menses VIII dies XX Tiberius Claudius pater hic situs est\n
EDCS-08300612.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudius Saturio, who lived 4 years, 8 months, and 20 days. Tiberius Claudius, his father, is buried here.
EDCS-08300612.summary: A tombstone records the death of a child, Claudius Saturio, and identifies his father, Tiberius Claudius, as the person who placed the inscription.

EDCS-08300616.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Fabricius [died...]
EDCS-08300616.summary: This fragmentary tombstone commemorates Quintus Fabricius, with the rest of the inscription lost.

EDCS-08300617.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavia Apama vixit annis L Caius Annius Optatus socrae suae bene merenti fecit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300617.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Apama lived 50 years. Caius Annius Optatus made this for his deserving mother-in-law. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08300617.summary: A son-in-law, Caius Annius Optatus, dedicated this tombstone to his mother-in-law, Flavia Apama, who lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-08300618.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavio Iuliano vixit annis XVI mensibus VII diebus XVII Flavius Caturo fratri pientissimo\n
EDCS-08300618.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavius Iulianus lived 16 years, 7 months, and 17 days. Flavius Caturo for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-08300618.summary: A brother, Flavius Caturo, erected this tombstone for his younger brother, Flavius Iulianus, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-08300619.text_edition: \n ------ vi]xit annis GILO viro stipendiorum terra sit tibi levis us Fronto s fili \n
EDCS-08300619.translation: [...lived years... GILOn. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Us Fronto, his son.]
EDCS-08300619.summary: This is a very fragmentary tombstone, likely for a soldier named Gilo, erected by his son Fronto.

EDCS-08300621.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Ianuarius vixit nnos XVII Iulius Saturninus faciendum curavit filio piissimo hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300621.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulius Ianuarius lived 17 years. Iulius Saturninus arranged for this to be made for his most devoted son. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08300621.summary: A father, Iulius Saturninus, dedicated this tombstone to his son, Iulius Ianuarius, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-08300622.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Licinianus uxor eius votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-08300622.translation: Caius Iulius Licinianus and his wife willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-08300622.summary: Caius Iulius Licinianus and his wife satisfied a votive offering, likely for a deity, indicating a fulfilled promise.

EDCS-08300623.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Neptunali vixit annis XXV Caius Iulius Atticus fratri piissimo sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300623.translation: To the departed spirits. To Caius Iulius Neptunalis, who lived 25 years. Caius Iulius Atticus for his most devoted brother. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08300623.summary: A brother, Caius Iulius Atticus, erected this tombstone for his deceased brother, Caius Iulius Neptunalis, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-08300624.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai Metelli filius Seneca annorum mensium III \n
EDCS-08300624.translation: Caius Iulius, son of Cai Metellus, Seneca, 3 months old.
EDCS-08300624.summary: This incomplete inscription records the death of a child, Caius Iulius Seneca, son of Cai Metellus, at the age of three months.

EDCS-08300625.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Victor Strobili filius hic situs est\n
EDCS-08300625.translation: Caius Iulius Victor, son of Strobili, is buried here.
EDCS-08300625.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Caius Iulius Victor, son of Strobili.

EDCS-08300626.summary: This inscription likely records the names of two individuals, possibly related or a couple.

EDCS-08300627.summary: This inscription records the name of Iulia Secunda, possibly as a dedication or part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-08300629.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Numeriliae Iuliosae vixit nnis XIII mensibus XIIII diebus XXII \n
EDCS-08300629.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Numerilia Iuliosa lived 13 years, 14 months, and 22 days.
EDCS-08300629.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the life span of Numerilia Iuliosa.

EDCS-08300633.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Porciae Saturninae filiae piissimae vixit annos XII Lucius Porcius pater fecit\n
EDCS-08300633.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Porcia Saturnina, most devoted daughter. She lived 12 years. Lucius Porcius, her father, made this.
EDCS-08300633.summary: This is a tomb inscription set up by Lucius Porcius for his devoted daughter, Porcia Saturnina, who died at age 12.

EDCS-08300634.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Satrius Maurus vicsit sic annis plus minus XXVI Baebius Saturni[nus ------\n
EDCS-08300634.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Satrius Maurus lived approximately 26 years. Baebius Saturninus...
EDCS-08300634.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the death of Lucius Satrius Maurus at approximately 26 years old, with an incomplete dedication by Baebius Saturninus.

EDCS-08300635.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Semproniae Iunianae vixit annis XXII mensibus IIII Heraclides fecit coniugi bene merenti hic sita sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300635.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sempronia Iuniana lived 22 years and 4 months. Heraclides made this for his deserving wife, buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08300635.summary: This is a tomb inscription set up by Heraclides for his deserving wife, Sempronia Iuniana, who died at 22 years and 4 months.

EDCS-08300636.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that appears to be a dedication or memorial, possibly to a person named Simpl.

EDCS-08300637.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Sitius Geminus vixsit sic menses VI hic situs Lucius Geminus pater fecit\n
EDCS-08300637.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Sitius Geminus lived 6 months. Lucius Geminus, his father, made this, buried here.
EDCS-08300637.summary: This is a tomb inscription set up by Lucius Geminus for his son, Lucius Sitius Geminus, who died at 6 months old.

EDCS-08300639.text_edition: \n ------ vixit] nnos XXXV Strobilus coniugi dignae fecit\n
EDCS-08300639.translation: ...lived 35 years. Strobilus made this for his worthy wife.
EDCS-08300639.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the death of a wife at 35 years old, with the monument made by her husband, Strobilus.

EDCS-08300640.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Proculus Catintaninus vixit annis XXIII Catelma uxor viro piissimo monumentum fecit sit tibi terra s\n
EDCS-08300640.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Valerius Proculus Catintaninus lived 23 years. Catelma, his wife, made this monument for her most devoted husband. May the earth be...
EDCS-08300640.summary: This is a tomb inscription set up by Catelma for her husband, Lucius Valerius Proculus Catintaninus, who died at 23 years old.

EDCS-08300641.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Valeria Pupa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300643.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, mentioning Marcus Ulpius, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300644.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpia Marcia vixit annis XIII cum viro suo fecit Festus coniugi carissimae\n
EDCS-08300644.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Ulpia Marcia lived for 13 years. Festus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-08300644.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Festus for his wife Ulpia Marcia, who died at age 13, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300647.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Iuliae Aelianae vixit annis XXXV mensibus V dies sic XII XIIIIIVIDECORPO\n
EDCS-08300647.translation: To the good memory of Julia Aeliana, who lived 35 years, 5 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-08300647.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Julia Aeliana, who lived 35 years, 5 months, and 12 days, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300648.text_edition: \n Domini Dei gratia Anianus Portesis et Suttun cum suis fecerunt\n
EDCS-08300648.translation: By the grace of God the Lord, Anianus Portesis and Suttun with their families made this.
EDCS-08300648.summary: An inscription recording a dedication made by Anianus Portesis and Suttun with their families, possibly with Christian overtones, from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-08300650.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Quotvuldeunis sic vixit annos XX in pace mater fecit\n
EDCS-08300650.translation: To good memory. Quotvuldeunis lived 20 years in peace. Mother made this.
EDCS-08300650.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quotvuldeunis, who lived 20 years in peace, set up by her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300651.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Domitia Urbana vixit nnos LX Iulius Extricatus maritai sic suae meritai sic cupulam fecit\n
EDCS-08300651.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Domitia Urbana lived 60 years. Iulius Extricatus made this sepulchral basin for his deserving wife.
EDCS-08300651.summary: A funerary epitaph for Domitia Urbana, who lived 60 years, set up by her husband Iulius Extricatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300652.text_edition: \n Pacate vixit annos XXVI menses VII dies X Caius Iulius Vitalio maritae merenti fecit\n
EDCS-08300652.translation: Pacate lived 26 years, 7 months, and 10 days. Caius Iulius Vitalio made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-08300652.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pacate, who lived 26 years, 7 months, and 10 days, set up by her husband Caius Iulius Vitalio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300653.inscription_type: carmina, reges, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-08300653.text_edition: \n Iuliae regis Iubae libertae Calloni Caius Iulius regis Iubae libertus hospes qui tumulum hunc vides si non forte grave est disce hic Callo iaceo tellure aspera finibus Italiae lucis adolesco coniunxs sic ad superos rem et frater quorum est luctus ignotis cara et nulli non grata quis obitus noster est sinister nunc hospes hoc titulo lecto dic discedens Callo sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300653.translation: To Julia Callo, freedwoman of King Juba. Caius Julius, a guest-friend and freedman of King Juba, who sees this tomb, learn, if it is not too much trouble, that Callo lies here in rough earth, in the borders of Italy. I grew up under the light of day; she was my wife and sister, whose sorrow is dear to strangers and to no one unwelcome. Our passing is ill-omened. Now, guest, as you read this epitaph, depart and say, 'May the earth lie lightly on Callo.'
EDCS-08300653.summary: A detailed funerary epitaph for Julia Callo, a freedwoman of King Juba, set up by her guest-friend and fellow freedman Caius Julius, expressing grief and a wish for her peace, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300654.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Fa qui decessit et eius coniu dolore acer ABNACCAN POLLI hic Potestas conliberta animo amoo \n
EDCS-08300654.translation: Caius Julius Fa, who died, and his wife, in bitter grief. ABNACCAN POLLI. Potestas, his fellow freedwoman, with love...
EDCS-08300654.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Caius Julius Fa and his wife, with a dedication by his fellow freedwoman Potestas, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300655.text_edition: \n Ego quae iaceo fatis sum rapta sinistris secta meis semper dulcis ubique fui sine funestos mater iam scindere crines atque leva luctus frater utique minor\n
EDCS-08300655.translation: I, who lie here, was taken away by ill fortune; I was always sweet and beloved. Mother, now tear your hair in mourning, and you, younger brother, also grieve.
EDCS-08300655.summary: An epitaph for a deceased woman, lamenting her premature death and urging her mother and brother to mourn her.

EDCS-08300656.text_edition: \n nnum X dolere noli mater hoc faciendum fuit properavit aetas \n
EDCS-08300656.translation: Mother, do not grieve for X years; this had to be done, age hastened it.
EDCS-08300656.summary: A short epitaph, likely for a child, offering consolation to the mother for the deceased's short life.

EDCS-08300658.text_edition: \n Iulia Iucunda vixsit sic annis XXVII filium reliquit annis XI filiam reliquit bimam et mensium sexs sic\n
EDCS-08300658.translation: Iulia Iucunda lived 27 years; she left a son 11 years old, and a daughter 2 years and 6 months old.
EDCS-08300658.summary: An epitaph for Iulia Iucunda, recording her age at death and the ages of her surviving children.

EDCS-08300662.text_edition: \n sui et Rubria me eius ani tissimo fecit\n
EDCS-08300662.translation: She and Rubria made me for her most beloved.
EDCS-08300662.summary: A dedication inscription, likely for a funerary monument, made by Rubria for her beloved.

EDCS-08300663.summary: A simple epitaph for a person named Restutus, wishing them peace in the afterlife.

EDCS-08300666.text_edition: \n Faustio filius Fausti Quadrati vixit menses IIII sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08300666.translation: Faustius, son of Faustus Quadratus, lived 4 months. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-08300666.summary: An epitaph for Faustius, an infant son, with a common funerary wish.

EDCS-08300667.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum L Marthae et Claudiae Marthosae et o et Abbae et Monnae L Daphne liberta ob merita eius\n
EDCS-08300667.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed: for Martha, and Claudia Marthosa, and O, and Abba, and Monna, and Daphne, her freedwoman, because of her merits.
EDCS-08300667.summary: A dedication to the Dis Manes, listing several individuals, likely family members or beneficiaries, and a freedwoman, honoring them for their merits.

EDCS-08300669.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio o dulcissime flore puerorum vixit annos XIII raptum Aelia Audiffil filio dedicavit\n
EDCS-08300669.translation: To Publius Aelius, my sweetest, flower of boys, who lived 13 years. Aelia Audiffil dedicated this to her son.
EDCS-08300669.summary: A funerary dedication by Aelia Audiffil to her son Publius Aelius, commemorating his young life.

EDCS-08300670.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Egnatia Festa vixit annos L Munatius Saturninus maritus fecit\n
EDCS-08300670.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed: Egnatia Festa lived 50 years. Munatius Saturninus, her husband, made this.
EDCS-08300670.summary: A funerary monument erected by Munatius Saturninus for his wife Egnatia Festa, recording her age at death.

EDCS-08300671.text_edition: \n Pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti deo Soli Elagabali possessores Altavenses ex sua collatione templum fecerunt procurante Iulio Cestillo procuratore Augusti provinciae CLXXXII\n
EDCS-08300671.translation: For the health of our lord the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, the possessors of Altava, from their contributions, built a temple to the god Sol Elagabalus, with Julius Cestillus, procurator of the Emperor in the province, overseeing it, in the 182nd year.
EDCS-08300671.summary: A dedication by the landowners of Altava for the health of Emperor Caracalla, recording the construction of a temple to Sol Elagabalus and mentioning the procurator and regnal year.

EDCS-08300672.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pequaria vixit annum et menses sex Fulvius Pequarius filiae bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-08300672.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pequaria lived for one year and six months. Fulvius Pequarius made this for his deserving daughter.
EDCS-08300672.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Fulvius Pequarius to his daughter Pequaria, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08300673.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Curai Guddemis vixit annos XXXX Cadarai coniugi dulcissimo fecit\n
EDCS-08300673.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Curai Guddemis lived for 40 years. Cadarai made this for her sweetest husband.
EDCS-08300673.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Cadarai to her husband Curai Guddemis, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08300678.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Bomona Sissoi vicsit annos L Rufina filia eiius vicsit sic nnos sa gener fecit\n
EDCS-08300678.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Bomona Sissoi lived for 50 years. Rufina, her daughter, who lived for [unclear number] years, made this for her mother. [---] general made this.
EDCS-08300678.summary: A funerary epitaph by Rufina for her mother Bomona Sissoi, mentioning a general, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08300679.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Gellius Felix Cai Gelli Felicis et Laeliae Seranae S vixit vixit annos L reliquos heres meus adicit\n
EDCS-08300679.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Gellius Felix lived for 50 years. My heir will add the remainder. Made by Caius Gellius Felix and Laelia Serana.
EDCS-08300679.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Gellius Felix, who lived for 50 years, with his heir adding further provisions, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08300680.translation: To the spirits of the departed for Aurelia Caeciliana [---]
EDCS-08300680.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph for Aurelia Caeciliana, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08300681.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cocceia Reducta vixit annis XXXI maritus faciendum curavit hic sita est\n
EDCS-08300681.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cocceia Reducta lived for 31 years. Her husband saw to its construction. She lies here.
EDCS-08300681.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cocceia Reducta, who lived for 31 years, erected by her husband, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400561.text_edition: \n Memoria <F=E>lavi Reginiani cornicularii Cimbrianorum vixit annos XLIIII mensem unum dies XXIIII horas VII amenestravet sic annos XVIII actarius annos IIII cornicularius annos XIIII memoriam <f=E>ecit Aemilia Ulpiola dulcissima coniux \n
EDCS-08400561.translation: In memory of Flavia Reginiana, a cornicularius of the Cimbri, who lived 44 years, one month, 24 days, and 7 hours. She served as an actarius for 18 years and as a cornicularius for 14 years. Aemilia Ulpiola, her dearest wife, made this memorial.
EDCS-08400561.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Flavia Reginiana, a military official who served for 18 years as actarius and 14 years as cornicularius, erected by her wife Aemilia Ulpiola, likely dating to the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-08400563.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeriae Saturninae Fabia Maura avia sua\n
EDCS-08400563.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabia Maura for her granddaughter Valeria Saturnina.
EDCS-08400563.summary: A funerary dedication by Fabia Maura for her granddaughter Valeria Saturnina, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400564.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aurelius Taxuma hic situs est vixit annis LXXX decessit (anno) provinciae CCLXX\n
EDCS-08400564.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Taxuma lies here. He lived 80 years. He died in the provincial year 270.
EDCS-08400564.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Taxuma, who lived 80 years and died in provincial year 270, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400565.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Luciosa quae vixit annis XXIIII hic sita est\n
EDCS-08400565.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Lucciola, who lived 24 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08400565.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Lucciola, who lived 24 years, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08400566.text_edition: \n vicxit! annos menses VII dies VII Flavia Quintosa filia eius et Sittius Victor gener sedano sic doloris fecerunt\n
EDCS-08400566.translation: She lived 7 years, 7 months, and 7 days. Flavia Quintosa, his daughter, and Sittius Victor, son-in-law, set this up in sorrow.
EDCS-08400566.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of an individual and erected by her daughter and son-in-law.

EDCS-08500645.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Potinus in pace hic quiescit vixit annos LXXIII decessit anno provinciae CCXCIII\n
EDCS-08500645.translation: Potinus rests here in peace, to good memory. He lived 73 years and died in the 293rd year of the province.
EDCS-08500645.summary: A funerary epitaph in memoriam of Potinus, noting his age and death date relative to a provincial year, likely from Roman North Africa.

EDCS-08500648.text_edition: \n DIE RBISSI Quintus Valerius Eutyches et Valeria Cyrilla parentes\n
EDCS-08500648.translation: On the day of his return, Quintus Valerius Eutyches and Valeria Cyrilla, parents.
EDCS-08500648.summary: A fragmented inscription possibly marking a return or a death on a specific significant day, set up by parents.

EDCS-08500649.text_edition: \n diebus X Quintus Aelius Icarissimae FELIIDVII\n
EDCS-08500649.translation: For 10 days Quintus Aelius [died] for Icarissima, most happy [?].
EDCS-08500649.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly recording a very short lifespan or a period of mourning.

EDCS-08500650.text_edition: \n Vus veteranus CISMObus vixit annos XXXXI Aurelius Noricus et Valerius Omollus sic sesquiplicarius de rebus hic situs est\n
EDCS-08500650.translation: Vus, a veteran, lived 41 years. Aurelius Noricus and Valerius Omollus, a sesquiplicarius (soldier receiving 1.5x pay), buried him here.
EDCS-08500650.summary: A funerary epitaph for a veteran named Vus, recording his age and burial by two fellow soldiers.

EDCS-08500651.text_edition: \n Victoriae Auggustorum Titus Fabius Quintilianus Maati anus eques Romanus ob honorem aedilitatis posuit dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08500651.translation: Titus Fabius Quintilianus Maatianus, a Roman knight, placed and dedicated this for Victory of the Augusti, in honor of his aedileship.
EDCS-08500651.summary: A dedication by Titus Fabius Quintilianus Maatianus, a Roman knight, to Victoria Augustorum related to his office as aedile.

EDCS-08500652.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudia Saturnina vixit annos XXXV\n
EDCS-08500652.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claudia Saturnina lived 35 years.
EDCS-08500652.summary: A standard funerary epitaph invoking the Di Manes for Claudia Saturnina, stating her age at death.

EDCS-08500653.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Tertula Aurelio Iovino posuit marito dulcissimo vixit annos XXXIII\n
EDCS-08500653.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Tertula erected this for Aurelius Iovinus, her most beloved husband, who lived 33 years.
EDCS-08500653.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by Iulia Tertula for her husband Aurelius Iovinus, stating his age and expressing affection.

EDCS-08600667.text_edition: \n Bibulliae sic Saturninae Quinti Bibulli sic Baricis filiae Iulia Saturnina mater et Claudiae Monnae nepoti\n
EDCS-08600667.translation: For Bibullia Saturnina, daughter of Quintus Bibullius Baricus, and for Claudia Monna, granddaughter. Iulia Saturnina, mother.
EDCS-08600667.summary: A funerary dedication possibly intended for multiple individuals, mentioning a mother, daughter, and granddaughter.

EDCS-08600669.text_edition: \n Clodiae Masculinae bene merenti feminae coniugi innocentissimae Caius Iulius Larciosus pontifex\n
EDCS-08600669.translation: To Clodia Masculina, a deserving woman, most innocent wife. Caius Iulius Larciosus, priest.
EDCS-08600669.summary: A funerary dedication to Clodia Masculina by her husband Caius Iulius Larciosus, a priest, praising her virtues.

EDCS-08600670.text_edition: \n Caio Grittio Cai filio Quirina Felici ex testamento suo\n
EDCS-08600670.translation: To Gaius Grittius Felix, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, by his own will.
EDCS-08600670.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Gaius Grittius Felix, possibly a tomb or a public benefaction, as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-08600681.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Viatorius Exsuperi vixit annis III Viatorius Senecio a militis merenti fecit hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600681.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Viatorius Exsuperius lived for 3 years. Viatorius Senecio, a soldier, made this for the deserving deceased. He is buried here.
EDCS-08600681.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Viatorius Exsuperius, erected by Viatorius Senecio, likely his father and a soldier, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600682.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aemilius Festus vixit annos XLV Florus sodali merenti fecit hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600682.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aemilius Festus lived for 45 years. Florus made this for his deserving comrade. He is buried here.
EDCS-08600682.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Marcus Aemilius Festus at age 45, with the monument erected by his comrade Florus.

EDCS-08900717.text_edition: \n aeus inis filius ensis princeps annorum L monumentum fecit impensa sua sibi et Pollae Eumenis filiae\n
EDCS-08900717.translation: [...]eus, son of Inis, a centurion of [XXX] ensis, 50 years of age, made this monument at his own expense for himself and for Polla, daughter of Eumenes.
EDCS-08900717.summary: This is a tomb inscription for a centurion and his wife Polla, built at his own expense, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900725.text_edition: \n L vixit mensibus VIII diebus X Quintus Aelius Ianuarius coniugi rarissimae fecit die VIII Kalendas Ianuarias (anno) provinciae CXXXI sit terra levis\n
EDCS-08900725.translation: She lived 8 months and 10 days. Quintus Aelius Ianuarius made this for his most rare wife on the 8th day before the Kalends of January in the 131st year of the province. May the earth be light upon her.
EDCS-08900725.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a wife at a young age and was erected by her husband, Quintus Aelius Ianuarius, in the 131st year of the province (likely Roman Syria).

EDCS-08901202.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-08901202.text_edition: \n Mensa Maximi pueri et Dativi martures sic\n
EDCS-08901202.translation: The table of the boys Maximi and Dativi, martyrs, thus:
EDCS-08901202.summary: This inscription, possibly relating to a tomb or memorial, mentions two boys named Maximi and Dativi, identified as martyrs.

EDCS-08901203.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-08901203.text_edition: \n Mensa Iulias Victorias quoi sic vixit annos sexaginta IPOETIS\n
EDCS-08901203.translation: The table of Iulia Victoria, who lived sixty years [possibly followed by a name like 'IPOETIS' or similar, but unclear].
EDCS-08901203.summary: This inscription appears to be a tomb marker for Iulia Victoria, who lived to be sixty years old.

EDCS-09001665.text_edition: \n Mara Diodotei filia heic sita est\n
EDCS-09001665.translation: Mara, daughter of Diodotei, is buried here.
EDCS-09001665.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Mara, daughter of Diodotei, is buried at this location.

EDCS-09400759.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Severi Pii nepoti divi Magni Antonini Pii filio Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V consuli II patri patriae proconsuli Cissiari decreto decurionum pecunia publica\n
EDCS-09400759.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, by decree of the decurions of Cissa, at public expense.
EDCS-09400759.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander, likely on a public building or monument in the town of Cissa, funded by the local council.

EDCS-09400766.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Victoriae Noreiae sacrum vexellatio sic alae Augustae Sentius Exoratus Spectatius Viator decuriones exercitus Norici quibus praeest Iulius Primus |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-09400766.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to Victoria, and to Noreia. The detachment of the Ala Augusta, Sentius Exoratus Spectatius Viator, decurions of the Norican army, under the command of Iulius Primus, a centurion of the 14th Legion Gemina, fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09400766.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by the decurions of a cavalry unit (Ala Augusta) stationed in Noricum, to various deities, including local ones.

EDCS-09400768.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate consul III pater patriae cohors II Sardorum fecit\n
EDCS-09400768.translation: Emperor Caesar Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the third time, consul for the third time, father of his country, made by the Second Cohort of Sardinians.
EDCS-09400768.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Second Cohort of Sardinians to Emperor Hadrian, likely commemorating a construction project during his reign in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09401659.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Invicto Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III patri patriae cons(uli) proconsuli nepoti divorum Gordianorum poni sic curante Faltonio Restitutiano procuratore suo\n
EDCS-09401659.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordian, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the third time, father of his country, consul, proconsul, grandson of the deified Gordians; erected by his procurator Faltonius Restitutianus.
EDCS-09401659.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Gordian III, likely erected on a public building or monument, overseen by his procurator Faltonius Restitutianus in the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09700397.text_edition: \n Deo Herculi Augusto Publius Aedinius Urbanus Lucius Lollius Felix Marcus Alfius Ianuarius vvota ssolverunt libentes animo anno provinciae CCII Lucius Cornelius Donatus\n
EDCS-09700397.translation: To the divine Hercules, Publius Aedinius Urbanus, Lucius Lollius Felix, and Marcus Alfius Ianuarius paid their vows willingly in the 202nd year of the province; Lucius Cornelius Donatus (was responsible).
EDCS-09700397.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules, dedicated by three individuals who fulfilled their vows in the 202nd year of the province (likely 202 AD or later), with Lucius Cornelius Donatus overseeing the process.

EDCS-09700403.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Hilarus vixit annos X Aureli T \n
EDCS-09700403.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Hilarus lived 10 years. Aurelius T...
EDCS-09700403.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Hilarus, a child who died at the age of 10, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700404.text_edition: \n miles legionis II Italicae cohortis VIII Aurelius Valens et Aurelius Marcianus T feceerunt militavit nnos VII vixit nnos XXXV\n
EDCS-09700404.translation: Aurelius Valens and Aurelius Marcianus, soldiers of the Eighth Cohort of the Second Italian Legion, made (this); he served for 7 years and lived 35 years.
EDCS-09700404.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives of two soldiers, Aurelius Valens and Aurelius Marcianus of the Second Italian Legion, who served for 7 years and lived to 35, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700411.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a tombstone or marker, dedicated to a woman named Donatilla Saturnina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700419.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Pudens vixit annos CII et Felicia vixit annis LXXI anno provinciae CCLVII\n
EDCS-09700419.translation: Caius Iulius Pudens lived 102 years and Felicia lived 71 years, in the 257th year of the province.
EDCS-09700419.summary: This funerary inscription records the long lives of Caius Iulius Pudens and Felicia, who died in the 257th year of the province (likely 257 AD or later).

EDCS-09700429.text_edition: \n Mensa Sabassanae vicsit sic nnm unum menses tres dies XVII parentes amanti posuerunt provinciae CCXCV\n
EDCS-09700429.translation: The table of Sabassana lived one year, three months, seventeen days. Her parents placed (this) for their beloved, in the 295th year of the province.
EDCS-09700429.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sabassana, who died at a young age, placed by her parents in the 295th year of the province (likely 295 AD or later).

EDCS-09700450.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Caecilius Donatus vicsit sic annos LXXV Caecilio no vixit et vixit LXXXX anno provinciae CCLXIII\n
EDCS-09700450.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Caecilius Donatus lived 75 years. For Caecilius... lived 90 years, in the 263rd year of the province.
EDCS-09700450.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Quintus Caecilius Donatus (aged 75) and another Caecilius (aged 90), set up in the 263rd year of the province (likely 263 AD or later).

EDCS-09700452.text_edition: \n Memoria Iuli Donatiani fratri carissimo Iulius Tertullus posuit anno provinciae CCLXXXVIII\n
EDCS-09700452.translation: For the memory of Iulius Donatianus, dearest brother. Iulius Tertullus placed (this) in the 288th year of the province.
EDCS-09700452.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iulius Tertullus for his brother, Iulius Donatianus, in the 288th year of the province (likely 288 AD or later).

EDCS-09700455.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Sempronius Donatus vixsit sic annis XLV provinciae CCXXVI\n
EDCS-09700455.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Sempronius Donatus lived 45 years, 226 years of the province.
EDCS-09700455.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Sempronius Donatus, who died at age 45 after 226 years of provincial service, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-09700461.text_edition: \n Marcus Gallius Maximus votum solvit libens animo dedicavit XV Kalendas Februarias anno provinciae CCXV\n
EDCS-09700461.translation: Marcus Gallius Maximus willingly paid his vow and dedicated it, on the fifteenth day before the Kalends of February, in the 215th year of the province.
EDCS-09700461.summary: A dedication by Marcus Gallius Maximus, fulfilling a vow on a specific date in the 215th year of the province.

EDCS-09700465.text_edition: \n Mensa Mamili Donati vixit annos LXXXXI decessit Idus Maias anno provinciae CCLXXVI fecerunt filii\n
EDCS-09700465.translation: Mensa Mamili Donati lived 91 years, died on the Ides of May, in the 276th year of the province. Her sons made this.
EDCS-09700465.summary: A tombstone for Mensa Mamili Donati, who died at age 91 in the 276th year of the province, erected by her sons.

EDCS-09700481.text_edition: \n Augusto sacrum Iulius Datus sacerdos S dedicavit provinciae CLXXVII\n
EDCS-09700481.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Iulius Datus, priest of S, dedicated it in the 177th year of the province.
EDCS-09700481.summary: A dedication to Augustus by Iulius Datus, a priest, in the 177th year of the province.

EDCS-09701296.text_edition: \n Iulia Salvia vixit annos LV provinciae CLXXXXVI sepulta est\n
EDCS-09701296.translation: Iulia Salvia lived 55 years, in the 196th year of the province, she was buried.
EDCS-09701296.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Salvia, who died at age 55 in the 196th year of the province, indicating her burial.

EDCS-09701306.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Pio Felice Augusto a Cen milia passuum XIIII\n
EDCS-09701306.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, fourteen miles.
EDCS-09701306.summary: This inscription likely marks a distance of 14 miles under the reign of Emperor Severus Alexander.

EDCS-09701307.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Titius Donatus princ<e=I>ps vir prior ordinis vicxit sic annis LXXX menses sic VI discessit XVI Kalendas Februarias anno provinciae CCXC patri dulcissimo fili fecerunt \n
EDCS-09701307.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Titius Donatus, leader of the first rank of the order, lived 80 years and 6 months, he departed on the sixteenth day before the Kalends of February, in the 290th year of the province. His sons made this for their dearest father.
EDCS-09701307.summary: A funerary inscription for Titius Donatus, a military leader, who died at age 80 and 6 months in the 290th year of the province, erected by his sons.

EDCS-09701310.text_edition: \n Confessione sancti et bassilica dominica et memoria bbeatorum vvirorum Luci Honorati L P Tannoni Victoris zaconi et Tannoni Rufiniani L P fecit Lucius Tannonius Rogatus s IIII ab Honorato anno provinciae CCLXX\n
EDCS-09701310.translation: By the confession of the saint and the basilican lord and the memory of the blessed men Lucius Honoratus, son of Lucius Tannoni Victor, and Lucius Rufinianus, son of Lucius Tannoni, Lucius Tannonius Rogatus made this, four from Honoratus, in the 270th year of the province.
EDCS-09701310.summary: A dedication commemorating blessed men, possibly related to a church or religious site, made by Lucius Tannonius Rogatus in the 270th year of the province.

EDCS-09800422.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Masauca veteranus vixit nnos C hic situs est\n
EDCS-09800422.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Titus Flavius Masauca, a veteran, lived 100 years, he is buried here.
EDCS-09800422.summary: A tombstone for Titus Flavius Masauca, a veteran who lived to 100 years old.

EDCS-09800462.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VI consuli III patri patriae coloni coloniae Iuliae Augustae Tubusuctitanae legionis VII immunis pecunia publica decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09800462.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the sixth time, consul for the third time, father of the country. The colonists of the colony Iulia Augusta Tubusuchtitana, from the Seventh Legion, from public funds by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09800462.summary: A public monument erected by the colonists of Colonia Iulia Augusta Tubusuchtitana and the Seventh Legion, funded by public money and a decree of the decurions, in honor of Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-09800472.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus et Marcus Opellius Antoninus Caesar Augustus miliarium constituerunt per Titum Aelium Decrianum procuratorem suum \n
EDCS-09800472.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Caesar Augustus erected this milestone through Titus Aelius Decrianus, their procurator.
EDCS-09800472.summary: This inscription records the erection of a milestone by Emperors Macrinus and his son Antoninus, dated to their joint reign.

EDCS-09800473.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Anto[ninus] Pius Felix Augustus pater patriae cons(ul) divi Magni Antonini filius divi Severi nepos miliaria N\n
EDCS-09800473.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, consul, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of deified Severus, [erected] this milestone...
EDCS-09800473.summary: This inscription is a fragment of a milestone, mentioning Emperor Caracalla and his titles, with the beginning of a recording of his accomplishments or a dedication.

EDCS-10701969.text_edition: \n Marcus Gallius Maximus votum solvit libens animo dedicavit XV Kalendas Februarias anno provinciae CCXV\n
EDCS-10701969.translation: Marcus Gallius Maximus willingly and with a cheerful heart fulfilled his vow and dedicated [this] on the fifteenth day before the Kalends of February in the 215th year of the province.
EDCS-10701969.summary: This inscription records a personal votive offering dedicated by Marcus Gallius Maximus on a specific date in the provincial year 215.

EDCS-10701970.text_edition: \n Genio decumanae Augustae sacrum cultores et possessores impensis suis fecerunt anno provinciae CXXXI\n
EDCS-10701970.translation: To the Genius of the Augustan decumana [tax collection district], the cultivators and landowners made [this] at their own expense in the 131st year of the province.
EDCS-10701970.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of a tax district by local landowners and cultivators, dated to the provincial year 131.

EDCS-10702014.text_edition: \n Infatigabili indulgentia domini nostri Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti auctis viribus et moenibus suis castellani Perdicenses muros extruxerunt curante Licinio Hieroclete procuratore Augusti praeside provinciae anno provinciae CLXXXVIII\n
EDCS-10702014.translation: By the tireless indulgence of our lord Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, with their strength and walls augmented, the inhabitants of Perdicca built their walls, under the supervision of Licinius Hierocles, procurator of the Emperor, governor of the province, in the 188th year of the province.
EDCS-10702014.summary: This inscription records the construction of defensive walls by the people of Perdicca, likely during the reign of Severus Alexander, under the administration of a provincial governor.

EDCS-10702020.summary: This is a very short, personal inscription, possibly an epitaph or a playful message, addressed to someone named Laurentius.

EDCS-11700287.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely noting the use or presence of Punic and Aramaic languages or inscriptions.

EDCS-12300260.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XI imperator VI consul V pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis tribus et cohortibus decem quae appellantur I Nerviana Augusta fidelis |(miliaria) et II Thracum Augusta pia fidelis et Parthorum et I Augusta Nerviana velox et I Corsorum civium Romanorum et I Pannoniorum et I Nurritanorum et I Flavia Musulamiorum et I Flavia Hispanorum et II Brittonum et II Breucorum et II Gallorum et IIII Sugambrorum et sunt in Mauretania Caesarensi sub Tito Caesernio Macedone quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione item classicis quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VIII Kalendas Decembres Caio Iulio Longino Caio Valerio Paullino consulibus cohortis IIII Sugambrorum cui praeest Tiberius Claudius Tiberi filius Quirina Magnus ex pedite Lovessio Maximi filio Bracaro descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XI imperator VI consul V pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis tribus et cohortibus decem quae appellantur I Nerviana Augusta fidelis |(miliaria) et II Thracum Augusta pia fidelis et Parthorum et I Augusta Nerviana velox et I Corsorum civium Romanorum et I Pannoniorum et I Nurritanorum et I Flavia Musulamiorum et I Flavia Hispanorum et II Brittonum et II Breucorum et II Gallorum et IIII Sugambrorum et sunt in Mauretania Caesarensi sub Tito Caesernio Macedone quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione item classicis quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VIII Kalendas Decembres Caio Iulio Longino Caio Valerio Paullino consulibus cohortis IIII Sugambrorum cui praeest Tiberius Claudius Tiberi filius Quirina Magnus ex pedite Lovessio Maximi filius Bracari descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae Quinti Aemili Soterici Cai Iuli Eut<y=V>chi Titi Villi Heraclidae Auli Larci Phronimi Cai Pomptinii Hylli Publi Anni Trophimi Cai Norbani Primi \n
EDCS-12300260.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 11th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the fatherland, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in three alae and ten cohorts, which are called I Nerviana Augusta fidelis (miliaria) and II Thracum Augusta pia fidelis and Parthorum and I Augusta Nerviana velox and I Corsorum civium Romanorum and I Pannoniorum and I Nurritanorum and I Flavia Musulamiorum and I Flavia Hispanorum and II Brittonum and II Breucorum and II Gallorum and IIII Sugambrorum, and are in Mauretania Caesariensis, under Titus Caesernius Macedo, having completed twenty-five or more years of service and been honorably discharged; likewise to the regular soldiers whose names are listed below, granted citizenship to them, their children, and their descendants, and the right of marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they would later marry, provided they took only one wife each, on the 8th day before the Kalends of December, when Gaius Iulius Longinus and Gaius Valerius Paullinus were consuls. Of the cohort IIII Sugambrorum, commanded by Tiberius Claudius, son of Tiberius, of the Quirina tribe, Magnus, from infantryman Lovessius, son of Maximus Bracarus. Transcribed and verified from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of deified Augustus near Minerva. Emperor Caesar, son of deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 11th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the fatherland, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in three alae and ten cohorts, which are called I Nerviana Augusta fidelis (miliaria) and II Thracum Augusta pia fidelis and Parthorum and I Augusta Nerviana velox and I Corsorum civium Romanorum and I Pannoniorum and I Nurritanorum and I Flavia Musulamiorum and I Flavia Hispanorum and II Brittonum and II Breucorum and II Gallorum and IIII Sugambrorum, and are in Mauretania Caesariensis, under Titus Caesernius Macedo, having completed twenty-five or more years of service and been honorably discharged; likewise to the regular soldiers whose names are listed below, granted citizenship to them, their children, and their descendants, and the right of marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they would later marry, provided they took only one wife each, on the 8th day before the Kalends of December, when Gaius Iulius Longinus and Gaius Valerius Paullinus were consuls. Of the cohort IIII Sugambrorum, commanded by Tiberius Claudius, son of Tiberius, of the Quirina tribe, Magnus, from infantryman Lovessius, son of Maximus Bracari. Transcribed and verified from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome by Quintus Aemilius Soterichus, Gaius Iulius Eutyches, Titus Vilius Heraclides, Gaius Larcius Phronimus, Gaius Pomptinius Hyllus, Publius Annius Trophimus, Gaius Norbanus Primus.
EDCS-12300260.summary: This is a lengthy imperial grant of citizenship and marriage rights issued by Emperor Trajan to veteran soldiers who served in various military units in Mauretania Caesariensis, recorded on a bronze tablet in Rome.

EDCS-12500364.text_edition: \n Sexto Cornelio Eucaeriano municipium Hadrianum Drobetense ex Dacia\n
EDCS-12500364.translation: To Sextus Cornelius Eucarianus, from the municipality of Hadrianum Drobeta in Dacia.
EDCS-12500364.summary: This is a dedication or dedication fragment, possibly an epitaph, honoring Sextus Cornelius Eucarianus from the Roman municipality of Drobeta in Dacia.

EDCS-12700174.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate III consul III praesidium Sufative per cohortem I Flaviam Musulamiorum factum sub cura Luci Sei Aviti procuratoris Augusti\n
EDCS-12700174.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 3rd time, the fortification of Sufative was made by Cohort I Flavia Musulamiorum, under the supervision of Lucius Seius Avitus, procurator of the Emperor.
EDCS-12700174.summary: This inscription records the construction of a fortification at Sufative by the Cohort I Flavia Musulamiorum during the third consulship of Emperor Hadrian, overseen by a procurator.

EDCS-12700189.text_edition: \n Saturno numini sancto et Opi sanctae Rodio Lucius sacerdos aram dedicavit suis sumptibus anno provinciae CCLXIII bonis bene\n
EDCS-12700189.translation: To the holy divine Saturn and the holy Ops, Lucius the priest dedicated an altar at his own expense in the 263rd year of the province, [for the good] of good people.
EDCS-12700189.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Saturn and Ops by a priest named Lucius in the 263rd year of a province.

EDCS-13000405.text_edition: \n Deanae Nemorensi Titus Iulius Germanus decurio alae Thracum praepositus cohortis Sardorum\n
EDCS-13000405.translation: To Deana of the Grove, Titus Iulius Germanus, decurion of the Thracian cavalry and commander of the Sardinian cohort, [dedicated this monument].
EDCS-13000405.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Deana Nemorensi by Titus Iulius Germanus, a military officer.

EDCS-13000406.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus [[Opellius]] Severus [[[Macrin]us]] Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-13000406.summary: This inscription is a fragment of an imperial titulature for Emperor Macrinus.

EDCS-13000408.translation: Victorianus lived for [3] years, died on [3] in June in the 266th year of the province.
EDCS-13000408.summary: This inscription records the lifespan and death date of Victorianus in the 266th year of a province.

EDCS-13000409.translation: A table was made by Lucius Tannonius Rogatus, son of Lucius, in the 267th year of the province, from the fourth part (?) under Honoratus, by order of the holy bishop (?) for the Sunday basilica and the memorial of the blessed men Lucius Honoratus, son of Lucius, deacon, and Tannoni Rufinianus, son of Lucius, deacon.
EDCS-13000409.summary: This inscription details the construction of a table for a basilica and memoria by Lucius Tannonius Rogatus, with contributions from named deacons, in the 267th year of a province.

EDCS-13000410.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Cecilius Saturninus lived 71 years, died on the 5th of February. His sons made this in the 270th (?) year of the province.
EDCS-13000410.summary: This tombstone records the death of Cecilius Saturninus and was erected by his sons in the 270th (?) year of a province.

EDCS-13000413.translation: Memorial for Cecilia Tecla, dearest mother, lived 68 years, died on the 11th day before the Kalends of January in the 284th year of the province.
EDCS-13000413.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Cecilia Tecla, recorded as dearest mother, who died in the 284th year of a province.

EDCS-13000414.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Iulius Victoris, one of the 'secundones', lived 58 years, died on the 16th day before the Kalends of November. Erected for a most beloved father in the 287th year of the province.
EDCS-13000414.summary: This tombstone records the death of Iulius Victoris, a 'secundones', and was erected for him by his son in the 287th year of a province.

EDCS-13000415.translation: Memorial for Flavius Messoris, lived 37 years, died on the Kalends of April in the 290th year of the province. Erected by his beloved wife.
EDCS-13000415.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Flavius Messoris, who died in the 290th year of a province, and was erected by his wife.

EDCS-13000416.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Iunius Titurius lived 2 years and 2 months, died on the 3rd day before the Nones of August in the 290th year of the province. Erected for a most beloved son.
EDCS-13000416.summary: This tombstone records the death of Iunius Titurius at a young age and was erected by his father in the 290th year of a province.

EDCS-13000417.translation: In memory of Flavius Victor's son, the innocent. He lived for 4 years and died on the 5th day before the Kalends of October, in the 290th year of the province.
EDCS-13000417.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of a child, Flavius Victor's son, in the 290th year of the province.

EDCS-13000419.translation: [He] lived for 55 years and died on the fourth day before the Nones of August, in the 291st year of the province.
EDCS-13000419.summary: A funerary inscription recording a person's lifespan and death date in the 291st year of the province.

EDCS-13000420.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Mallerius lived for 50 years and died on the 8th day before the Kalends of January. Aeventianus with his most beloved sons and brother placed this in the 291st year of the province.
EDCS-13000420.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Julius Mallerius by his family, dated to the 291st year of the province.

EDCS-13000421.translation: In memory of Iulius Felix. He lived for 42 years and died the day before the Ides of February, in the 292nd year of the province. May he prosper. May his family live.
EDCS-13000421.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Felix, who died in the 292nd year of the province, with common formulaic phrases.

EDCS-13000422.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Felix lived for 50 years. Victoriana with her most beloved daughter made this. He died on the 5th day before the Kalends of March, in the 292nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000422.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Felix, erected by his daughter Victoriana and granddaughter, dated to the 292nd year of the province.

EDCS-13000423.translation: In memory of Caecilius Donatus. He lived for 40 years and died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of February. His most beloved brothers made this in the 294th year of the province.
EDCS-13000423.summary: A funerary inscription for Caecilius Donatus, erected by his brothers, dated to the 294th year of the province.

EDCS-13000424.translation: In memory of Iulius Donatus. He lived for nine years and died on the Nones of July, in the 296th year of the province.
EDCS-13000424.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of a child, Iulius Donatus, in the 296th year of the province.

EDCS-13000425.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titius Peregrinus lived for 62 years and died on the 14th day before the Kalends of February. His most beloved sons made this in the 299th year of the province.
EDCS-13000425.summary: A funerary inscription for Titius Peregrinus, erected by his sons, dated to the 299th year of the province.

EDCS-13000426.translation: In memory of Iulius Ianvarius, an innocent infant. He lived for 2 years and died the day before the Kalends of March, in the 300th year of the province. His most beloved father made this.
EDCS-13000426.summary: A funerary inscription for the infant Iulius Ianvarius, erected by his father, dated to the 300th year of the province.

EDCS-13000427.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilius Renatus lived for 55 years and died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of November. I placed this for my most beloved husband, in the 301st year of the province.
EDCS-13000427.summary: A funerary inscription for Caecilius Renatus, erected by his wife, dated to the 301st year of the province.

EDCS-13000428.translation: In memory of Letoria Florida, who lived 70 years and died on the 8th day before the Ides of October, in the 210th year of the province.
EDCS-13000428.summary: This epitaph records the death of Letoria Florida, with her age and date of death noted, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13000430.translation: In memory of Fuscia Victorina, his dearest wife, who lived 40 years and died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of March, in the 203rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000430.summary: This tombstone commemorates Fuscia Victorina, recording her relationship to the dedicator, her age, and the date of her death in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13000431.translation: died on the 3rd day of April, father placed [this] for his dearest son, in the 303rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000431.summary: This epitaph records a father's dedication for his son, with the son's death date and the provincial year of dedication noted, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-13000432.translation: In memory of Sulpicia Munassa, who lived 75 years and died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of August, in the 304th year of the province.
EDCS-13000432.summary: This epitaph records the death of Sulpicia Munassa, noting her age and date of death in the 4th century CE.

EDCS-13000433.translation: lived 55 years and died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of January, in the 304th year of the province; her dearest husband made [this].
EDCS-13000433.summary: This tombstone records the death of an unnamed woman, with her age, date of death, and the provincial year, dedicated by her husband in the 4th century CE.

EDCS-13000436.translation: In memory of Matronica, wife of [---], who lived 40 years and died on the 3rd day before the Ides of April, in the 306th year of the province.
EDCS-13000436.summary: This epitaph commemorates Matronica, recording her age, marital status, date of death, and provincial year, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-13000437.translation: In memory of Caecilius Donatus, who lived 45 years and died on the 11th day before the Kalends of January, in the 306th year of the province.
EDCS-13000437.summary: This epitaph records the death of Caecilius Donatus, including his age, date of death, and the provincial year, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-13000438.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sulpicius Peregrinus lived 41 years and died the day before the Ides of September, in the 307th year of the province; his dearest brothers dedicated [this].
EDCS-13000438.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Sulpicius Peregrinus, noting his age and death date, and is dedicated by his brothers in the 4th century CE.

EDCS-13000439.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Florentius Pompeianus lived 70 years and died on the Ides of March, in the 307th year of the province; his brothers made [this] together with his sons.
