EDCS-09300603.translation: To Publius Marius Honoratus, son of Publius, of the Fabia tribe, twice aedile and twice a duovir, and imperial priest, from his own funds.
EDCS-09300603.summary: This inscription records a dedication or payment made by Publius Marius Honoratus, who held significant local magistracies and priesthoods, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09300605.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Asinius Felix, son of Marcus, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 72 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09300605.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Asinius Felix, who lived 72 years.

EDCS-09300606.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Blaesius Naiades lived 85 years.
EDCS-09300606.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Blaesius Naiades, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-09300607.translation: Cameria Secunda, daughter of Quintus, lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09300607.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cameria Secunda, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-09300608.translation: Quintus Camerius Martialis, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, adorned with every honor, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09300608.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Camerius Martialis, a distinguished local official who lived 75 years.

EDCS-09300609.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cerialis, son of Lucius, of the Scaptia tribe, lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09300609.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cerialis, who lived 20 years.

EDCS-09300610.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cominius Victor lived 60 years.
EDCS-09300610.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cominius Victor, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-09300611.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Comitatis, a dutiful slave girl, lived 8 years.
EDCS-09300611.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Comitatis, a young slave girl.

EDCS-09300612.translation: Lucius Cornelius Maximus, son of Lucius, of the Collina tribe, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09300612.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius Maximus, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-09300613.translation: To the memory of [---]nius, son of Quintus, of the Stellatina tribe, decurion, duovir, quinquennial duovir, flamen of Augustus, prefect of a Spanish cohort, lived 80 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09300613.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a man named [---]nius who held several important civic and military offices, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300614.translation: To the Divine Shades. Decia Maximilla, daughter of Marcus, lived 15 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09300614.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young woman named Decia Maximilla, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300615.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Getula lived.
EDCS-09300615.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Iulia Getula, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300616.translation: [---]us, son of Lucius, Iustus lived 24 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09300616.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a man named Iustus, son of Lucius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300617.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Licinius Victor lived for [---] years.
EDCS-09300617.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publius Licinius Victor, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300618.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a woman named Matrona, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300619.translation: To the Divine Shades. Perellia Secunda, daughter of Caius, lived 50 years.
EDCS-09300619.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Perellia Secunda, daughter of Caius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300620.translation: Pomponia Fausta, daughter of Lucius, lived 31 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09300620.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Pomponia Fausta, daughter of Lucius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300621.translation: Pomponia Rogata, daughter of Marcus, lived 81 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09300621.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Pomponia Rogata, daughter of Marcus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300622.translation: To the Divine Shades. Pullaenia Threpte lived 91 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09300622.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Pullaenia Threpte, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09300623.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Titius Modestus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09300623.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Titius Modestus, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-09300624.translation: … Re lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09300624.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of an individual whose name is partially lost.

EDCS-09300625.translation: … of the Quirina tribe… lived 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09300625.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of an individual, with partial information about their tribal affiliation.

EDCS-09300627.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Lusius Nivalis lived 1 year; mosa made this.
EDCS-09300627.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Quintus Lusius Nivalis, noting his age at death and that someone named mosa erected it.

EDCS-09300628.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fabius V...
EDCS-09300628.summary: This is a funerary inscription beginning with a dedication to the spirits of the underworld and the name Fabius, but incomplete.

EDCS-09300631.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus C. Martialis lived 85 years.
EDCS-09300631.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Quintus C. Martialis, dedicated to the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-09300633.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Valerius Primigenius lived...
EDCS-09300633.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Valerius Primigenius, dedicated to the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-09301291.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Lusius Nivalis lived 3 years; mosa made this.
EDCS-09301291.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Quintus Lusius Nivalis, noting his age at death and that someone named mosa erected it.

EDCS-09301292.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fabius...
EDCS-09301292.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription beginning with a dedication to the spirits of the underworld and the name Fabius.

EDCS-09301294.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Bifectolus Saturninus, with partial information about his name.

EDCS-09301295.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus O Martialis lived 85 years.
EDCS-09301295.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus O Martialis.

EDCS-09301296.translation: Pacca ANEV lived X years. Paccus PIBONO lived 20 years.
EDCS-09301296.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of Pacca ANEV and Paccus PIBONO.

EDCS-09301297.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Primi lived X years.
EDCS-09301297.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Valerius Primi.

EDCS-09301298.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Lucius F Senecio willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09301298.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater by Lucius F Senecio.

EDCS-09301299.translation: While alive, Caius Fabricius Cai freedman Titius M VIR.
EDCS-09301299.summary: This is an epitaph for Titius M VIR, a freedman of Caius Fabricius Cai, set up during his lifetime.

EDCS-09301300.translation: Lucius Lucius Africanus made this while alive for his brother Lucius Mauritio and his mother Luciae Primillae, and for Fausta Q Hygia and himself.
EDCS-09301300.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucius Lucius Africanus for his family members and himself.

EDCS-09301301.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rontia Secundilla, daughter, made this for Lucius Sacrinio Crescenti, who lived 40 years and 14 days, as he deserved well.
EDCS-09301301.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Rontia Secundilla to Lucius Sacrinio Crescenti.

EDCS-09301302.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name of Fulvius Philotaerus.

EDCS-09301303.translation: Aurelius ternus FIVS D IF, 8 years of service.
EDCS-09301303.summary: This is likely a military dedication or record of service for Aurelius ternus.

EDCS-09400760.translation: Bocchus, a cavalryman of the II Thracian alae, squadron of Antonius, 2 years of service. His heirs by will, Silvanus Leuri son and Vegetus Reburri son, cavalrymen of the same ala.
EDCS-09400760.summary: This is a military inscription recording the service of Bocchus and naming his heirs.

EDCS-09400761.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Maria Fortunata, Aemilius Humullianus, a veteran, [dedicated this] to his pious wife. She lived [several] years and 10 months. To the spirits of the departed. To Lucius Aemilius Homolanus, a veteran. He lived [several] years.
EDCS-09400761.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aemilius Humullianus for his wife Maria Fortunata and his father Lucius Aemilius Homolanus, both veterans.

EDCS-09400762.translation: Lucius Domitius Agatianus, a veteran, lived for 60 years.
EDCS-09400762.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life span of Lucius Domitius Agatianus, a veteran.

EDCS-09400763.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Maraxa, a veteran, served for 31 years and lived for 70 years.
EDCS-09400763.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating that Iulius Maraxa, a veteran, served in the army for 31 years and lived for 70 years.

EDCS-09400764.translation: Umbri and veterans of this place.
EDCS-09400764.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that seems to commemorate Umbri and local veterans.

EDCS-09401270.translation: In memory of Envmi and Annia Saturnina. She lived for 65 years. They made this for themselves while alive, together with their sons Lucius and Migginus, in the year of the province.
EDCS-09401270.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Annia Saturnina and her husband Envmi, who set it up for themselves and their sons Lucius and Migginus during their lifetime.

EDCS-09401660.translation: To the prefect of the Thracian ala and the Mazici tribe, for [his] outstanding courage and diligence, the public community of Oppidonovensium [dedicates this] as patron.
EDCS-09401660.summary: This is a dedication by the community of Oppidonovensium to their patron, the prefect of the Thracian ala and the Mazici tribe, in recognition of his virtue and industry.

EDCS-09700398.summary: This is a dedication to the god Saturn Augustus.

EDCS-09700399.translation: Sextus Acilius Victor willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09700399.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sextus Acilius Victor, who willingly paid a vow.

EDCS-09700401.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Arria Pilica lived for 8 years. Arrius Augustalis, her father, made this.
EDCS-09700401.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arria Pilica, who died at the age of 8, set up by her father Arrius Augustalis.

EDCS-09700402.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Aufidius Rufinus lived...
EDCS-09700402.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Quintus Aufidius Rufinus.

EDCS-09700406.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aurellia Donata lived 60 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09700406.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurellia Donata, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-09700407.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Aurelia Victorina, dearest daughter, who lived 7 months. Flavia Celsina, a most distinguished woman, her mother [erected this].
EDCS-09700407.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Flavia Celsina for her daughter Aurelia Victorina, noting the daughter's short lifespan.

EDCS-09700408.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Baria, pious. [She] lived [number?] years. Aelius [erected this].
EDCS-09700408.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baria, likely set up by Aelius, with her age incomplete.

EDCS-09700409.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Cocceius Tertius, freedman of the colony, lived 70 years. Victor and his freedmen [erected this] to their most precious father.
EDCS-09700409.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Marcus Cocceius Tertius, a freedman of the colony, honored by his freedmen.

EDCS-09700410.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Donata, the tympanist, who lived 110 years. To one who deserved well.
EDCS-09700410.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Donata, a tympanist, recording her exceptionally long lifespan.

EDCS-09700412.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Eros, steward of Abascantus, freedman of the emperor. To one who deserved well. The care was undertaken by Onesimus, fellow slave. Lived 18 years.
EDCS-09700412.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Eros, an imperial freedman serving as a steward, honored by a fellow slave.

EDCS-09700413.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Eucarpus, slave of the emperor Superstites. Lived [number?] years.
EDCS-09700413.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Eucarpus, an imperial slave, with his age not fully recorded.

EDCS-09700414.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Faosa lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09700414.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Faosa, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-09700415.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gargilia Victorina lived 35 years. Quintus Aemilius Iovinus made [this] for his wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-09700415.summary: This funerary epitaph records Gargilia Victorina's age and was erected by her husband, Quintus Aemilius Iovinus.

EDCS-09700416.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Flavia Peregrina, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-09700416.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Flavia Peregrina.

EDCS-09700417.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Geminianius Martialis, son of Gaius, pious, lived 75 years.
EDCS-09700417.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Gaius Geminianius Martialis.

EDCS-09700418.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Germinius Martialis lived 70 years. Iolia Saturnina lived 105 years.
EDCS-09700418.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the ages at death of Publius Germinius Martialis and Iolia Saturnina.

EDCS-09700420.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Lalius lived...
EDCS-09700420.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Lalius, with his age at death incomplete.

EDCS-09700421.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Mamilia Tertia lived 25 years. Her son, Felix Maximus, lived 4 months. She is buried here.
EDCS-09700421.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the ages at death of Mamilia Tertia and her son Felix Maximus.

EDCS-09700422.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcia Romana lived...
EDCS-09700422.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Marcia Romana, with her age at death incomplete.

EDCS-09700423.summary: An inscription likely recording the name of Marcia Crispina, possibly on a table or altar.

EDCS-09700424.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintius Maurus lived 80 years. Maxima lived 65 years.
EDCS-09700424.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the ages at death of Quintius Maurus and Maxima.

EDCS-09700426.translation: Lontius Urbanus lived 14 years. His father Lontius erected this.
EDCS-09700426.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Lontius Urbanus, erected by his father.

EDCS-09700427.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Plotius Malchio lived 45 years.
EDCS-09700427.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Lucius Plotius Malchio.

EDCS-09700428.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publicia lived 39 years. Her sister Felicitas and daughter Secundula made this.
EDCS-09700428.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Publicia, made by her sister and daughter.

EDCS-09700430.translation: Sabinus lived 9 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09700430.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sabinus, a child who lived 9 years, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-09700432.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Secundula, a public servant, lived 15 years. Cocceia Felicitas made this.
EDCS-09700432.summary: A funerary epitaph for Secundula, a freedwoman, who lived 15 years, erected by Cocceia Felicitas.

EDCS-09700435.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Victor, veteran, lived 80 years.
EDCS-09700435.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Victor, a veteran, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-09700439.translation: Of Marci Quintilli Basilei. May he rest in peace (or may the earth lie lightly on him).
EDCS-09700439.summary: A funerary inscription for Marci Quintilli Basilei, likely a tombstone with an abbreviated formula indicating a wish for the deceased's peace.

EDCS-09700440.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Aemilius For lived 60 years. Pet Vict. Gaius Aemilius For and his son made this for themselves.
EDCS-09700440.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Aemilius For, who lived 60 years, made by himself and his son, likely a joint tomb.

EDCS-09700441.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Tiberius Anianus lived 82 years.
EDCS-09700441.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Tiberius Anianus, who lived 82 years.

EDCS-09700442.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurelius Exuperans, a young man, lived 21 years. This was made by Marcus Aurelius Emeritus, his father, who dedicated it.
EDCS-09700442.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Exuperans, a young man who died at 21, erected by his father, Marcus Aurelius Emeritus.

EDCS-09700443.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Monnica lived 32 years.
EDCS-09700443.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Monnica, who lived 32 years.

EDCS-09700444.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Castula lived 30 years.
EDCS-09700444.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Castula, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-09700446.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Velius Felix lived 30 years.
EDCS-09700446.summary: A funerary epitaph for Velius Felix, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-09700447.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Septimius Septimianus lived 28 years and 6 months.
EDCS-09700447.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimius Septimianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700448.translation: Umbria Tertulla lived 27 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09700448.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Umbria Tertulla, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700449.translation: Burial place of Felix, deposited on the eighth day before the Ides of October.
EDCS-09700449.summary: This inscription records the burial date of Felix.

EDCS-09700451.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Marcus Cordius Contentus the Younger lived 29 years.
EDCS-09700451.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Cordius Contentus the Younger, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700453.translation: In memory of Iulius Iunioris, who lived 51 years and died the day before the Ides of September. His son Gaudiosus with his brothers made this in the year 332 of the province.
EDCS-09700453.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius Iunioris and was erected by his son and brothers in the provincial year 332 (likely 332 AD).

EDCS-09700454.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Restitutus, son of Aelius Primitivus, who managed the estate at Satala, lived 55 years.
EDCS-09700454.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Restitutus, who managed an estate at Satala, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700456.translation: In memory of Sextilius Victorinianus, who lived 30 years and died on the 5th day before the Nones of May. His mother Iulia Donata deposited this in the provincial year 320.
EDCS-09700456.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Sextilius Victorinianus and was erected by his mother in the provincial year 320 (likely 320 AD).

EDCS-09700458.translation: In memory of Urbanus. His son Cresconius made this. He lived 60 years.
EDCS-09700458.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Urbanus, erected by his son Cresconius.

EDCS-09700462.translation: Honoratus, [husband/son] of Ssis, [son/husband] of A. [---]. Caecilius [---]. Caecilia Quinta.
EDCS-09700462.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription potentially related to family members, including Caecilius and Caecilia Quinta.

EDCS-09700463.translation: Caecilia G. Rogatus, Lucius Honorius Ia.
EDCS-09700463.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the names Caecilia, Rogatus, and Lucius Honorius.

EDCS-09700464.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Aelius Primus.
EDCS-09700464.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the gods of the underworld by Lucius Aelius Primus.

EDCS-09700467.summary: This inscription likely records a table dedicated by Alfius Martialis, possibly in a funerary context or for public use.

EDCS-09700468.translation: Iulia Saturnina's table. She lived 100 years. Satule made it.
EDCS-09700468.summary: A funerary inscription recording the table of Iulia Saturnina, who lived to 100 years, and its maker Satule.

EDCS-09700469.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Octavianus, who lived for 75 years.

EDCS-09700470.translation: Fuscus and their mother Venusta made and dedicated this.
EDCS-09700470.summary: This inscription records the dedication of something made by Fuscus and their mother Venusta.

EDCS-09700471.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Arelia Saturnina lived 72 years. Caius Iulius Rogatus lived 92 years.
EDCS-09700471.summary: A funerary inscription for Arelia Saturnina (aged 72) and Caius Iulius Rogatus (aged 92), dedicated to the gods of the underworld.

EDCS-09700472.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Octavius V.
EDCS-09700472.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the gods of the underworld by Caius Octavius V.

EDCS-09700473.translation: BA RDI ni's father Aureli Mu made and dedicated this.
EDCS-09700473.summary: This inscription records the dedication by Aureli Mu of something made for BA RDI ni's father.

EDCS-09700474.summary: This inscription records the maker of an object named Ger viva.

EDCS-09700475.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Cornelius Contentus lived 60 years. Pomponia Sabina lived 50 years.
EDCS-09700475.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Cornelius Contentus (aged 60) and Pomponia Sabina (aged 50), dedicated to the gods of the underworld.

EDCS-09700476.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Ami Bar [dedicated this].
EDCS-09700476.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the departed spirits, records the name of Caius Ami Bar.

EDCS-09700478.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Venusus lived 80 years. Faonius the younger [dedicated this].
EDCS-09700478.summary: This funerary inscription honors Venusus, who lived 80 years, and was likely dedicated by Faonius.

EDCS-09700479.translation: To the departed spirits. I, Vibia Ururia, lived 20 years. Vibius Saturus, her father, made this.
EDCS-09700479.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Vibia Ururia, who lived 20 years, and was made by her father, Vibius Saturus.

EDCS-09700483.summary: This very brief inscription records the death of Mensa R.

EDCS-09700484.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cresconius Crescens lived 45 years.
EDCS-09700484.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Lucius Cresconius Crescens, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-09700485.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Rogatianus made this. Valeria Dativa lived 55 years. Caius Iulius Rogatianus lived 51 years.
EDCS-09700485.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives of Valeria Dativa (55 years) and Caius Iulius Rogatianus (51 years), and was made by Caius Iulius Rogatianus.

EDCS-09700486.translation: Mensa Barsei lived 22 years in the 330th year of the province.
EDCS-09700486.summary: This funerary inscription records that Mensa Barsei lived 22 years, dated to the 330th year of the province.

EDCS-09700487.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Commanded by the master, Crescens PP promised and completed [this]. Live and rejoice with yours and with the donated ones.
EDCS-09700487.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the departed spirits, records a promise fulfilled by Crescens PP, likely a freedman, with well wishes to the deceased and their family.

EDCS-09700488.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Cluvius Secundus is buried here, in accordance with the provisions of his will. Titus Cluvius Rogatus, one of the heirs, made this.
EDCS-09700488.summary: This funerary inscription states that Titus Cluvius Secundus is buried here as per his will, and was made by his heir Titus Cluvius Rogatus.

EDCS-09700492.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Cerelius Sabinus lived 71 years. Marcus Cerilius Ianuarius made this for his father.
EDCS-09700492.summary: This funerary inscription records that Marcus Cerelius Sabinus lived 71 years and was made by his son, Marcus Cerilius Ianuarius.

EDCS-09700790.translation: Antusa made this for Ptolemaeus the cellarer.
EDCS-09700790.summary: This is a dedication by Antusa for Ptolemaeus, who served as a cellarer, likely on a building or object.

EDCS-09700791.translation: Claudia Spes made this mound for you, her son, from the summit, so that no one may be ignorant.
EDCS-09700791.summary: This is a funerary monument or dedication by Claudia Spes for her son, possibly indicating a significant construction or a memorial on a high place.

EDCS-09701267.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gabinius Lo...
EDCS-09701267.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (spirits of the dead) for Gabinius Lo, likely incomplete.

EDCS-09701291.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Antoninus Augustus, conqueror of Britain, Germany, and Parthia, grandson of the divine Severus Arabicus Adiabenicus, great-grandson of the divine Commodus and the divine Marcus Antoninus, great-great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius and the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan and the divine Nerva, eight miles from the colony of Nervia Augusta Martialis of the veterans of Sitifis.
EDCS-09701291.summary: This is a milestone inscription dating to the Severan period, likely commemorating a road built or maintained by the veterans of Sitifis, indicating distance from their colony and listing imperial titles.

EDCS-09701292.translation: Gaius Julius Crispus, son of Silvanus, lived 20 years.
EDCS-09701292.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Julius Crispus.

EDCS-09701293.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Julius Priscus.

EDCS-09701294.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Julius lived 16 years.
EDCS-09701294.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-09701295.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Julius Victor lived... years.
EDCS-09701295.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Victor, recording his name and dedication to the Manes.

EDCS-09701300.translation: For the divine Genius of the rock and the holy numen, for the health of Publius Aelius Classicus, imperial procurator, and his children, at the instance of Aelius Primus, centurion,lius Tyrannus erected and consecrated this with proper workmanship.
EDCS-09701300.summary: This is a dedication of a structure or monument bylius Tyrannus, at the request of Aelius Primus, for the health of the imperial procurator Publius Aelius Classicus and his family, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09701301.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly part of a larger text or a dedication, referring to 'Honorius' and 'perpetual'.

EDCS-09800417.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Pius, son of Publius, lived 40 years.
EDCS-09800417.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Pius, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-09800418.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aelius Saturninus, buried here, lived 12 years.
EDCS-09800418.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelius Saturninus, who was buried here and lived 12 years.

EDCS-09800419.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Timinius Saturus lived piously for 48 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800419.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Timinius Saturus, who lived piously for 48 years and is buried here.

EDCS-09800420.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Considia Prisca lived 64 years.
EDCS-09800420.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the spirits of the underworld for Considia Prisca, who lived 64 years.

EDCS-09800421.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Helvia Ianuaria lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800421.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Helvia Ianuaria, who lived 30 years and is buried here.

EDCS-09800423.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aelia Novella lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800423.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Novella, who lived 25 years and is buried here.

EDCS-09800424.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Tiberius Claudius Forensis lived 18 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800424.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Forensis, who lived 18 years and is buried here.

EDCS-09800425.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Ces, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-09800426.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Tur Veneria lived 28 years; here.
EDCS-09800426.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tur Veneria, who lived 28 years.

EDCS-09800427.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Tiberius Claudius Ventidianus Arnensi lived 35 years.
EDCS-09800427.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Ventidianus Arnensi, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-09800429.translation: Gaius Octavius Saturninus lived for 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800429.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Octavius Saturninus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-09800431.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Vom Felix lived for 75 years.
EDCS-09800431.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes, recording the lifespan of Publius Vom Felix.

EDCS-09800432.translation: De Secunda, pious daughter of Lucius, lived for 10 months.
EDCS-09800432.summary: A funerary epitaph for De Secunda, recording her lifespan in months.

EDCS-09800433.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Coelia Tertulla lived for 17 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800433.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes for Coelia Tertulla, recording her lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-09800434.translation: [...]ia, daughter of Servius, lived for 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09800434.summary: A funerary epitaph for [...]ia, daughter of Servius, recording her lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-09800435.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Pac Rog, pious, lived for 18 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800435.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes for Marcus Pac Rog, recording his lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-09800436.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Timinia Secunda, pious, lived for 70 years.
EDCS-09800436.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes for Timinia Secunda, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-09800437.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Caelius Donatus lived for 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800437.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes for Lucius Caelius Donatus, recording his lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-09800439.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Sempronius Fortunatus lived for 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09800439.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes for Lucius Sempronius Fortunatus, recording his lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-09800440.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marius lived for 20 years; his freedman Flavius Titi filius made this for a deserving slave.
EDCS-09800440.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes by Flavius Titi filius for his slave Marius, recording Marius' lifespan.

EDCS-09800442.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Clodius Docimus lived for 80 years and lay here for 6 days.
EDCS-09800442.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Publius Clodius Docimus.

EDCS-09800443.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Fabius Mus lived for 25 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09800443.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucius Fabius Mus.

EDCS-09800444.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Servandus, the devoted slave of Lucius Julius Fortunatus, lived for 20 years and is placed here.
EDCS-09800444.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Servandus, a slave, recording his lifespan and burial place.

EDCS-09800445.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Antonia Matrona lived for 45 years. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Hirtius Fortunatus lived for 4 years and 2 months.
EDCS-09800445.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespans of two individuals, Antonia Matrona and Quintus Hirtius Fortunatus.

EDCS-09800447.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Spes lived for 2 years.
EDCS-09800447.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the short lifespan of Spes.

EDCS-09800448.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Antonia Maxima is placed here. She lived for 70 years. Her most devoted aunt, Antonia Matrona, made this.
EDCS-09800448.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Maxima, set up by her aunt Antonia Matrona.

EDCS-09800449.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Castor.

EDCS-09800450.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Pisonia Saturnina lived for 25 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09800450.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Pisonia Saturnina.

EDCS-09800451.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Timinius Donatus lived dutifully and is placed here.
EDCS-09800451.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the placement of Caius Timinius Donatus.

EDCS-09800453.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Postumia S_____ , daughter, made this for her parents, Marcus Postumius Optatus the veteran and Aelia Saturnina his wife.
EDCS-09800453.summary: This funerary epitaph records a daughter's dedication to her parents, a veteran and his wife.

EDCS-09800454.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Themo lived 22 years.
EDCS-09800454.summary: A tombstone commemorating Marcus Ulpius Themo, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-09800455.translation: Donata lived 65 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09800455.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Donata at the age of 65.

EDCS-09800458.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Vitalis, standard-bearer of the III Italica legion, of the 1st and 2nd cohorts, made this for Proculus Valalius, deserving man. He lived 30 years, served 10 years.
EDCS-09800458.summary: A tombstone set up by a soldier, Aurelius Vitalis, for a deserving comrade, Proculus Valalius, who died after 10 years of service at the age of 30.

EDCS-09800459.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Restuta lived 36 years and 7 months.
EDCS-09800459.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Valeria Restuta at the age of 36 years and 7 months.

EDCS-09800460.translation: Marcus Safidius Suavis died aged 21 years and 6 months.
EDCS-09800460.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Marcus Safidius Suavis at the age of 21 years and 6 months.

EDCS-09800461.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Maior lived 19 years.
EDCS-09800461.summary: A tombstone commemorating Aelia Maior, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-09800465.translation: Quintus Petronius, son of Caius, Cornelian Novatus, prefect of the 5th cohort of Gauls, tribune of the 14th Gemina legion, cavalry prefect of the 1st Hispana Campagona, procurator of the Emperor for the 20th tax on inheritances through Asia, Phrygia, Lycia, Galatia, patron of the colony by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09800465.summary: An inscription detailing the extensive military and administrative career of Quintus Petronius Cornelian Novatus, culminating in his status as patron of a colony.

EDCS-09800466.summary: A very brief inscription identifying Titianus as the one who laid tiles, possibly on a building or floor.

EDCS-09800469.translation: To the memory of the most distinguished lady Victoria, who lived eighteen years, eight months, and fifteen days. She departed in peace on the fourth day before the Kalends of January in the 386th year of the province.
EDCS-09800469.summary: A tombstone commemorating Victoria, who died at the age of 18 years, 8 months, and 15 days, with her death date calculated according to the provincial era.

EDCS-10701972.summary: A very brief inscription naming two individuals, Iuliana and Iuggagius, likely on a tombstone or a shared monument.

EDCS-10701973.translation: Gallica died as an infant in peace. She lived 4 months. Her mother made this memorial for her.
EDCS-10701973.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an infant named Gallica, indicating her mother erected the monument.

EDCS-10701975.translation: Memorial of Miggina. She lived 1 year, 4 months, 6 days. She departed on the 3rd day before the Nones of July in the 390th year of the province.
EDCS-10701975.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and death date of Miggina, likely erected by her family.

EDCS-10701976.translation: Honorata rested in peace. A faithful one, she died on the tenth day before the Kalends of January in the 339th year of the province.
EDCS-10701976.summary: This epitaph commemorates Honorata, a faithful Christian, and records her date of death.

EDCS-10701977.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for a faithful individual named Vilaticus, recording only his name and a mention of his life.

EDCS-10701978.translation: Cresconia the faithful lived in peace. She died on the day before the Ides of June in the 339th year of the province.
EDCS-10701978.summary: This epitaph records the death of Cresconia, a faithful Christian, along with her lifespan and date of death.

EDCS-10701979.translation: Sweetest life, leaving behind the body itself, they would have consumed the years until the ages, the summit of merits is recalled. Born in R. Alive in spirits. The Kalends of September, from Numidia, in the 376th year of the province.
EDCS-10701979.summary: This is a complex epitaph, possibly for a Christian from Numidia, celebrating their life, merits, and enduring presence in memory, dated to the 376th year of the province.

EDCS-10701980.translation: Memorial of Sacuntina the faithful. She departed in peace. She lived 10 years. She died in September.
EDCS-10701980.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sacuntina, a faithful Christian, noting her age and death month.

EDCS-10701981.translation: Memorial of Astula. She departed on the seventeenth day before the Kalends of October in the 357th year of the province.
EDCS-10701981.summary: This epitaph commemorates Astula and records her date of departure, likely from a Christian context.

EDCS-10701983.translation: Memorial of Victorinus. He lived 70 years more or less. He departed on the 2nd day before the Kalendas of March in the 358th year of the province.
EDCS-10701983.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and death date of Victorinus, likely erected by his family.

EDCS-10701984.translation: Indeed, endowed with the honor of untimely death, she lies here mourned by her own, whose name is Honorata, fitting and virtuous, joining justice with the word. Having followed the promises of heaven, she exchanged the briefness of life for heaven's honor, condemning vices, she rejoices in such commerce and, secure, rejoices to have ended labor. She lived 18 years. She died in peace on the day before the Ides of June in the 371st year of the province.
EDCS-10701984.summary: This extensive epitaph praises Honorata, who died at 18, for her virtues and faithful Christian life, noting her death date in the 371st year of the province.

EDCS-10701985.translation: Fidelis Forag returned what he received on the fourth day before the Ides of January in the provincial year; he lived for...
EDCS-10701985.summary: This inscription, likely from a tombstone, records a person named Fidelis Forag returning something and the date of the provincial year.

EDCS-10701986.translation: In memory of the priest Felix; he lived...
EDCS-10701986.summary: This is a memorial inscription for a priest named Felix, noting his lifespan.

EDCS-10701987.translation: Here rests Valeria, who lived 33 years and 7 months; she departed on the tenth day before the Kalends of November, buried on the same day in the 337th provincial year. Here rests Margarita, who lived 21 years; she departed on the sixth day before the Ides of September, buried in the 337th provincial year.
EDCS-10701987.summary: This tombstone records the burial of two women, Valeria and Margarita, including their ages, lifespans, and dates of death and burial within the provincial year 337.

EDCS-10701989.translation: Burial of Abundus on the seventh day before the Kalends of September in the 363rd provincial year.
EDCS-10701989.summary: This inscription records the burial of Abundus on a specific date in the 363rd provincial year.

EDCS-10701990.translation: An infant named Onorata departed, 5 months old, on the thirteenth day before the Kalends of July in the 365th provincial year.
EDCS-10701990.summary: This inscription records the death of an infant named Onorata at the age of 5 months in the 365th provincial year.

EDCS-10701991.translation: In memory of Fedifactais, faithful, who departed in peace; she lived for...
EDCS-10701991.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Fedifactais, described as faithful, noting her peaceful departure and lifespan.

EDCS-10701993.translation: The infant Victorinus lived 6 years; he died on the second day before the Nones of April in 365.
EDCS-10701993.summary: This inscription records the death of an infant named Victorinus at the age of 6 years, with the date of death given as the second day before the Nones of April in 365.

EDCS-10701994.translation: In memory of Honorius, faithful, who departed in peace; he lived 5 years and months...
EDCS-10701994.summary: This memorial inscription is for Honorius, who is described as faithful and having departed in peace, noting his lifespan of 5 years and some months.

EDCS-10701995.translation: In memory of Flavius Sescentus; he lived 52 years and his burial took place on the seventh day before the Kalends of December in the 355th provincial year.
EDCS-10701995.summary: This tombstone commemorates Flavius Sescentus, stating his age at death (52 years) and the date of his burial in the 355th provincial year.

EDCS-10701996.translation: In memory of Blatta, who lived in peace for 69 years, and her burial is on the Ides of June in the provincial year...
EDCS-10701996.summary: This memorial inscription is for Blatta, who lived 69 years and died peacefully, with her burial date noted as the Ides of June in an unspecified provincial year.

EDCS-10701997.summary: This inscription honors V. Ntin, noting her chastity.

EDCS-10701998.translation: In memory of the faithful Innocentius and...
EDCS-10701998.summary: This inscription is a memorial to Innocentius, noted as faithful.

EDCS-10702000.translation: In memory of Maria Equitiola, also called Siliqua, who lived 29 years and departed in peace on the day before the Ides of March in the 354th year of the province.
EDCS-10702000.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Maria Equitiola (Siliqua), recording her age at death and the date of her passing in the provincial year 354.

EDCS-10702001.translation: Ulpia Honorata, also called Aura, died on the fifth day before the Kalends of January in the 353rd year of the province.
EDCS-10702001.summary: This inscription records the death of Ulpia Honorata (Aura) on a specific date in the provincial year 353.

EDCS-10702002.translation: In memory of Gemellina, also called Blatta, who lived 30 years; her burial took place on the 8th day before the Kalends of November in the 353rd year of the province.
EDCS-10702002.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Gemellina (Blatta), noting her age at death and the date of her burial in the provincial year 353.

EDCS-10702003.translation: In memory of Sur, he rested in peace for 25 years, died immaculate by the baptism of Christ in the 362nd year of the province.
EDCS-10702003.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Sur, who lived 25 years and died in the provincial year 362 after receiving Christian baptism.

EDCS-10702005.translation: The depositio of the venerable, chaste Mellita is on the 7th day before the Ides of May; she lived 32 years, died in the 353rd year of the province.
EDCS-10702005.summary: This inscription records the death and burial date of Mellita, noting her chastity, age, and the provincial year 353.

EDCS-10702006.translation: In memory of Marcellus, the faithful.
EDCS-10702006.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Marcellus, who is described as faithful.

EDCS-10702007.translation: In memory of Victorina, daughter, and Lucina.
EDCS-10702007.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Victorina, identified as a daughter, and Lucina.

EDCS-10702008.translation: Here Pacatus has placed his seat, pure by baptism; for four years a lustrum (a period of five years) of mourning when struck by death.
EDCS-10702008.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Pacatus, likely a bishop, referencing his baptism and a period of mourning, possibly for a deceased individual or as a marker of time.

EDCS-10702009.translation: Ianus is buried here. He lived in peace for 8 years, 1 month, and died on November 9th, in the 350th year of the province.
EDCS-10702009.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Ianus and provides details of his age at death and the provincial year of his passing.

EDCS-10702011.translation: The resting place of Adeodatus. He was buried on the 11th day before the Kalends of June in the 390th year of the province.
EDCS-10702011.summary: This inscription marks the burial of Adeodatus, specifying the date of his interment and the provincial year.

EDCS-10702012.translation: The resting place of Cresconia. She departed on the Nones of May.
EDCS-10702012.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cresconia, noting the date of her passing.

EDCS-11600300.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Prima lived for 36 years and left behind 7 sons. Aemilius Felix erected this monument.
EDCS-11600300.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Iulia Prima, detailing her lifespan, number of sons, and naming Aemilius Felix as the dedicator.

EDCS-11600303.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodiae Ligurinae lived for 81 years.
EDCS-11600303.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the memory of Clodiae Ligurinae, stating her age at death.

EDCS-11600305.translation: Clodia, daughter of Clodius Baricbalis, willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11600305.summary: This inscription records Clodia, daughter of Clodius Baricbalis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11600306.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Ancestius Auxeticus.

EDCS-11600307.translation: Saturnina, slave of Tiberius Claudius Placidus, is buried here. She lived for 30 years. Tertius, her companion, erected this.
EDCS-11600307.summary: This funerary inscription marks the burial of Saturnina, a slave of Tiberius Claudius Placidus, and states that her companion Tertius erected the monument.

EDCS-11600308.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Tettal, son of Baricbalis.

EDCS-11600309.translation: Vitalis, 11 months, and Thundaris, your sister, 2 years and 4 months. Bestia is with you. Quadratus, your father, and Aretusa, your mother, dedicated this gift. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11600309.summary: This funerary inscription laments the death of Vitalis and his sister Thundaris, naming their parents Quadratus and Aretusa as the dedicators and wishing them peace.

EDCS-11700285.translation: To Rusticus, Stasius, and Thalerus for their services.
EDCS-11700285.summary: This is a dedication to three individuals, Rusticus, Stasius, and Thalerus, in recognition of their services, likely on a tombstone or a monument commemorating them.

EDCS-11700286.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Manlia Corinthia, mother, Manlius Tabernarius, son, and Manlia Eutactilla, daughter, are buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-11700286.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating a burial place to Manlia Corinthia, her son Manlius Tabernarius, and her daughter Manlia Eutactilla, with a wish for peace.

EDCS-11800658.translation: Gaius Julius Castus, second in the priestly rank of sacred law, son of Gaius Julius Honoratus, already 59 years of age, decided for himself that while alive and active in human affairs he would establish this eternal memorial for his memory, calling it the cella martyrum of Lucian and Lucilia, deposited on the 6th day before the Kalends of September in the 321st year of the province.
EDCS-11800658.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument established by Gaius Julius Castus, who held a priestly office, for his own memory and dedicated to Lucian and Lucilia, dated to the 321st provincial year.

EDCS-11800725.translation: This memorial was made by C ISEXI E, his wife and daughter, from their own property.
EDCS-11800725.summary: This funerary inscription records that a memorial was established by the wife and daughter of C ISEXI E from their own funds.

EDCS-13000418.translation: I placed this memorial for Flavius Crescentius, my dearest brother, who lived 55 years and departed on the 6th day before the Kalends of February in the provincial year.
EDCS-13000418.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by an unknown individual for their brother, Flavius Crescentius, who died in the provincial year.

EDCS-13000434.translation: This memorial for Julia Quincta, who lived ten years and departed on the 11th day before the Kalends of May in the 305th year of the province. Her father made it for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-13000434.summary: This is a funerary epitaph created by a father for his daughter, Julia Quincta, who died at the age of ten in the 305th provincial year.

EDCS-13000435.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Accius Marcianus the younger departed on the 11th day before the Kalends of November, aged 52, in the 305th year of the province.
EDCS-13000435.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Accius Marcianus the younger, who died at the age of 52 in the 305th provincial year.

EDCS-13000440.translation: To my dearest daughter, who lived 24 years and departed in October in the 408th year of the province.
EDCS-13000440.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed daughter, who died at the age of 24 in October of the 408th provincial year.

EDCS-13000443.translation: Memorial for Melisune, who lived 30 years and departed on the Kalends of July in the 312th year of the province. Her dearest father made it.
EDCS-13000443.summary: This is a funerary epitaph created by a father for his daughter, Melisune, who died at the age of 30 in July of the 312th provincial year.

EDCS-13000444.translation: Memorial for Sedata Maximina, who lived 10 years and departed on the 16th day before the Kalends of January in the 312th year of the province.
EDCS-13000444.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sedata Maximina, who died at the age of 10 in January of the 312th provincial year.

EDCS-13000445.translation: In memory of Crepereus Secundinus, who lived 5 years and died on the 22nd of November in the 313th year of the province; his most loving father made this.
EDCS-13000445.summary: A funerary monument for Crepereus Secundinus, erected by his father, dating to the 313th year of the province.

EDCS-13000446.translation: In memory of Valerius Saturninus, who lived 4 years and died on the 9th of August in the 315th year of the province.
EDCS-13000446.summary: A funerary monument for Valerius Saturninus, dating to the 315th year of the province.

EDCS-13000447.translation: In memory of Valerius Flavianus, who lived 40 years and died on the 29th of October in the 321st year of the province; his son lived.
EDCS-13000447.summary: A funerary monument for Valerius Flavianus, erected by his son, dating to the 321st year of the province.

EDCS-13000449.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits of Iulius Saturus, one of the secundiones, who lived 65 years and died on the 29th of January in the 323rd year of the province; his most loving sons made this for their father.
EDCS-13000449.summary: A funerary monument for Iulius Saturus, dedicated by his sons, dating to the 323rd year of the province.

EDCS-13000450.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits of Titia Iulia, who lived 51 years and died on the 1st of May; her husband and sons made this in the 324th year of the province.
EDCS-13000450.summary: A funerary monument for Titia Iulia, dedicated by her husband and sons, dating to the 324th year of the province.

EDCS-13000451.translation: In memory of Iulius Victor, who lived about 50 years and died on Friday, the 19th of February in the 326th year of the province; his most loving brother made this.
EDCS-13000451.summary: A funerary monument for Iulius Victor, erected by his brother, dating to the 326th year of the province.

EDCS-13000452.translation: In memory of Florentius, son of Pompeianus, his most loving son, who lived 55 years and died on the Nones of November in the 333rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000452.summary: A funerary monument for Florentius, erected by his son, dating to the 333rd year of the province.

EDCS-13000453.translation: In memory of Iulia Africana, who lived 55 years and died on the 13th of July in the 345th year of the province; her most loving son made this.
EDCS-13000453.summary: A funerary monument for Iulia Africana, erected by her son, dating to the 345th year of the province.

EDCS-13000454.translation: In memory of Sulpicia Secura, who lived [---] years and died in August; her most loving sons made this in the 355th year of the province.
EDCS-13000454.summary: A funerary monument for Sulpicia Secura, erected by her sons, dating to the 355th year of the province.

EDCS-13000455.translation: In memory of Mindia Sabina, who lived 55 years and died on the 5th of May in the 346th year of the province.
EDCS-13000455.summary: A funerary monument for Mindia Sabina, dating to the 346th year of the province.

EDCS-13000457.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. In memory of Erenia Ianuaria. She lived 85 years and died on the 6th day before the Ides of October in the 348th year of the province. Her dearest sons made this.
EDCS-13000457.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Erenia Ianuaria by her sons, likely from the Roman province in 348 AD.

EDCS-13000458.translation: In memory of Iulius Maccalis. He lived 5 years and died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of November in the 348th year of the province.
EDCS-13000458.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Iulius Maccalis, likely from the Roman province in 348 AD.

EDCS-13000459.translation: In memory of Iulius Quitus. He lived 25 years and died on the 2nd day before the Kalends of September in the 349th year of the province.
EDCS-13000459.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Iulius Quitus, likely from the Roman province in 349 AD.

EDCS-13000460.translation: In memory of Titia Saturina, dearest daughter. She lived 3 years and died on the 16th day before the Kalends of November in the 350th year of the province.

EDCS-13000461.translation: In memory of Sempronia Luciosa. She lived 42 years and died on the 8th day before the Kalends of September in the 352nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000461.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Sempronia Luciosa, likely from the Roman province in 352 AD.

EDCS-13000462.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. In memory of Claudius Saturninus, the innocent. He lived 3 years and died on the 7th day before the Kalends of October in the 352nd year of the province. His dearest father made this.
EDCS-13000462.summary: A funerary monument commemorating the young Claudius Saturninus, erected by his father, likely from the Roman province in 352 AD.

EDCS-13000463.translation: To the memory of Pitucius Euassus. He lived 80 years and died on the 15th day before the Kalends of June in the 354th year of the province.
EDCS-13000463.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Pitucius Euassus, likely from the Roman province in 354 AD.

EDCS-13000464.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. In memory of Satellius Faustinus. He lived 30 years and died on the 14th day before the Kalends of December in the 354th year of the province.
EDCS-13000464.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Satellius Faustinus, likely from the Roman province in 354 AD.

EDCS-13000466.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Saturninus. He lived 4 years and died on the 13th day before the Kalends of December in the 360th year of the province.

EDCS-13000467.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Macrina lived for 4 years and died on the 5th day before the Kalends of November in the 362nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000467.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Macrina, recording her lifespan and date of death, likely from the Roman province in the year 362.

EDCS-13000468.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Ianuarius lived for 45 years and died on the day before the Nones of January in the 363rd year of the province.

EDCS-13000469.translation: In memory of Sicilia Primosa. She lived for 70 years and died on the 11th day before the Kalends of July in the 364th year of the province.

EDCS-13000470.translation: To the memory of Iulia Florida. She lived for 70 years and died on the 9th day before the Kalends of August. Her dearest sons made this in the 366th year of the province.
EDCS-13000470.summary: A funerary monument erected by sons for Iulia Florida, noting her age at death and the year 366.

EDCS-13000471.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Vicarius lived for 60 years and died on the Nones of July in the 367th year of the province.
EDCS-13000471.summary: This epitaph records the death of Flavius Vicarius, including his age and the date within the 367th year of the province.

EDCS-13000472.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabbia Macaris lived for 40 years and died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of August in the 367th year of the province.
EDCS-13000472.summary: A funerary inscription for Fabbia Macaris, detailing her age and death date in the 367th year of the province.

EDCS-13000474.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Monnata lived for 40 years and died on the 5th day before the Nones of October in the 368th year of the province.
EDCS-13000474.summary: This epitaph records the death of Iulia Monnata, specifying her age and date of death in the 368th year of the province.

EDCS-13000475.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabia Fortunata lived for 7 years and died on the 7th day before the Kalends of September in the 369th year of the province.
EDCS-13000475.summary: A funerary monument for the child Fabia Fortunata, stating her age at death and the year 369.

EDCS-13000476.translation: For the health of the community of the Altavensians and the safety of our lords the most invincible Augusti Honorius and Theodosius. Crepereius Victor, his family, and citizens made this.
EDCS-13000476.summary: A dedication by Crepereius Victor and his community for the well-being of the state and the reigning emperors Honorius and Theodosius.

EDCS-13000477.translation: In memory of Aurelia Prima. She lived for 20 years and died on the fourth day before the Kalends of August in the 370th year of the province.
EDCS-13000477.summary: This memorial inscription for Aurelia Prima records her age at death and the date in the 370th year of the province.

EDCS-13000478.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Accius Granius lived 75 years and died on the 6th day before the Kalends of September in the 370th year of the province.
EDCS-13000478.summary: A funerary epitaph for Accius Granius, recording his lifespan and date of death in the 370th provincial year.

EDCS-13000481.translation: In memory of Caecilius Maccalis. He lived 90 years and died on the 15th day before the Kalends of June in the 373rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000481.summary: A funerary inscription for Caecilius Maccalis, noting his age and date of death in the 373rd provincial year.

EDCS-13000482.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulius Mavadio lived 25 years and died on the 10th day before the Kalends of September in the 373rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000482.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Mavadio, stating his age and date of death in the 373rd provincial year.

EDCS-13000483.translation: In memory of Satellius Faustinus. He lived 18 years and died on the 6th day before the Ides of October in the 373rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000483.summary: A memorial inscription for Satellius Faustinus, recording his age and death date in the 373rd provincial year.

EDCS-13000485.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius lived one hundred years and died on the 12th day before the Kalends of July in the 378th year of the province.
EDCS-13000485.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius, recording his age of 100 years and date of death in the 378th provincial year.

EDCS-13000486.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Celius Gloriosus lived 65 years and died on the 13th day before the Kalends of October in the 380th year of the province.
EDCS-13000486.summary: A funerary epitaph for Celius Gloriosus, detailing his age and date of death in the 380th provincial year.

EDCS-13000487.translation: In memory of Aurelia Donata. She lived 65 years and died on the 3rd day before the Nones of December in the 380th year of the province, and Aurelia Getula lived 40 years.
EDCS-13000487.summary: A memorial inscription for Aurelia Donata, noting her age and death date in the 380th provincial year, and also mentioning Aurelia Getula's age.

EDCS-13000488.translation: Exuperantius, son of Urbanus, a deacon, made this for himself after [---] 150 [---] for 84 years.
EDCS-13000488.summary: An inscription by a deacon named Exuperantius, son of Urbanus, recording a self-made monument and referencing a period of 84 years.

EDCS-13000490.translation: In memory of Caecilia Victorina. She lived 80 years and died on the 4th day before the Ides of January in the 390th year of the province.
EDCS-13000490.summary: A memorial inscription for Caecilia Victorina, stating her age and date of death in the 390th provincial year.

EDCS-13000491.translation: In memory of Caecilia Rogata. She lived 58 years and died on the 3rd day before the Ides of February in the 390th year of the province.
EDCS-13000491.summary: A memorial inscription for Caecilia Rogata, recording her age and date of death in the 390th provincial year.

EDCS-13000493.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Victorina lived 65 years and died on the 16th day before the Kalends of December in the 391st year of the province.
EDCS-13000493.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Iulia Victorina in the 391st year of the province.

EDCS-13000494.translation: In memory of Aufidius Mellancrus. He lived 20 years more or less and died on the 3rd day before the Nones of October in the 396th year of the province.
EDCS-13000494.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Aufidius Mellancrus in the 396th year of the province.

EDCS-13000495.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavius Ianuarius lived 35 years and died on the 3rd day before the Ides of June in the 397th year of the province.
EDCS-13000495.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Flavius Ianuarius in the 397th year of the province.

EDCS-13000497.translation: In memory of Caecilia Saturina. She lived 15 years and died on the 12th day before the Kalends of October in the 402nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000497.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Caecilia Saturina in the 402nd year of the province.

EDCS-13000498.translation: In memory of Geminius Saturinus. He lived 30 years and died on the 13th day before the Kalends of February. His most beloved father erected this in the 404th year of the province.
EDCS-13000498.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by a father for his son, Geminius Saturinus, in the 404th year of the province.

EDCS-13000500.translation: In memory of Aus Maximus. He lived 55 years and died on the Nones of January in the 405th year of the province.
EDCS-13000500.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Aus Maximus in the 405th year of the province.

EDCS-13000501.translation: In memory of Flavia Monnica. She lived 45 years and died on the 16th day before the Kalends of February in the 405th year of the province. Her most beloved son erected this for his mother.
EDCS-13000501.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by a son for his mother, Flavia Monnica, in the 405th year of the province.

EDCS-13000502.translation: In memory of Florentia Onerata. She lived 13 years and died on the 4th day before the Ides of November in the 407th year of the province.
EDCS-13000502.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Florentia Onerata in the 407th year of the province.

EDCS-13000503.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Sittia Germana lived 85 years and died on the 4th day before the Nones of November in the 408th year of the province.
EDCS-13000503.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Sittia Germana in the 408th year of the province.

EDCS-13000505.translation: In memory of Flavia Victorina. She lived 60 years and died on the 3rd day before the Nones of March in the 417th year of the province.
EDCS-13000505.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Flavia Victorina in the 417th year of the province.

EDCS-13000507.translation: Memorial to Caecilius Saturninus, lived 38 years more or less, departed on the Kalends of January in the 428th year of the province.
EDCS-13000507.summary: This epitaph records the death of Caecilius Saturninus, who died on January 1st, in the year 428 of the provincial era.

EDCS-13000508.translation: Memorial to Isflacia Prima, lived 18 years, departed on the 11th day before the Kalends of January in the 430th year of the province.
EDCS-13000508.summary: This epitaph records the death of Isflacia Prima, who died on December 22nd, in the year 430 of the provincial era.

EDCS-13000509.translation: Memorial to Julius Saturinus, lived 55 years, departed on the 8th day before the Ides of February in the 430th year of the province.
EDCS-13000509.summary: This epitaph records the death of Julius Saturinus, who died on February 6th, in the year 430 of the provincial era.

EDCS-13000510.translation: Memorial to Crepereia Lercias, lived 20 years, departed on the Ides of June in the 432nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000510.summary: This epitaph records the death of Crepereia Lercias, who died on June 13th, in the year 432 of the provincial era.

EDCS-13000511.translation: Memorial to Flavia Faustina, lived 63 years, departed on the 14th day before the Kalends of August in the 432nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000511.summary: This epitaph records the death of Flavia Faustina, who died on July 19th, in the year 432 of the provincial era.

EDCS-13000512.translation: Memorial to Julius Libanus, lived 18 years, departed on the 18th day before the Kalends of January in the 432nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000512.summary: This epitaph records the death of Julius Libanus, who died on December 15th, in the year 432 of the provincial era.

EDCS-13000513.translation: Memorial to Valerius Iunianus, lived 15 years, departed on the 3rd day before the Kalends of the year in the 439th year of the province.
EDCS-13000513.summary: This epitaph records the death of Valerius Iunianus, who died on December 30th, in the year 439 of the provincial era.

EDCS-13000514.translation: Memorial to the deceased, lived X years, departed on the 16th day before the Kalends of February in the 441st year of the province.
EDCS-13000514.summary: This epitaph records the death of an unnamed individual who died on January 17th, in the year 441 of the provincial era.

EDCS-13000515.translation: Memorial to Renatus, lived 50 years, departed on the 10th day before the Kalends of October in the 441st year of the province.
EDCS-13000515.summary: This epitaph records the death of Renatus, who died on September 22nd, in the year 441 of the provincial era.

EDCS-13000517.translation: In memory of Sittia Prima, she lived 16 years and departed on the 11th day before the Kalends of March in the 446th year of the province.
EDCS-13000517.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of Sittia Prima, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000518.translation: In memory of Crepereia ExSSemta, she lived 50 years and departed on the 4th day before the Ides of August in the 448th year of the province.
EDCS-13000518.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of Crepereia ExSSemta, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000519.translation: In memory of Manila, she lived 8 years and departed on the 5th day before the Kalends of October in the 453rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000519.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of Manila, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000520.translation: In memory of Tissia Prima, she lived 20 years and departed on the 4th day before the Kalends of March in the 355th year of the province.
EDCS-13000520.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of Tissia Prima, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000521.translation: She lived 60 years and departed on the 9th day before the Kalends of January in the 458th year of the province.
EDCS-13000521.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of an unnamed individual, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000522.translation: In memory of Crepereia Satura, she lived more or less 50 years and departed on the 2nd day before the Nones of December in the 459th year of the province.
EDCS-13000522.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of Crepereia Satura, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000523.translation: She lived more or less 60 years and departed on the Kalends of February in the 468th year of the province.
EDCS-13000523.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of an unnamed individual, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000525.translation: ---iae eria, who is also called Honoria, set up this memorial; she lived 56 years and departed on the 14th day before the Kalends of July in the 482nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000525.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of ---iae eria (Honoria), likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000526.translation: In memory of Ulpius Maximus, bishop, he lived 85 years and departed on the 14th day before the Kalends of September in the 490th year of the province.
EDCS-13000526.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of Bishop Ulpius Maximus, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000528.translation: In memory of Julia Rogata, she lived 25 years and departed on the 2nd day before the Kalends of February in the 493rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000528.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death dates of Julia Rogata, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-13000530.translation: In memory of Julius Victorinus. He lived 40 years. He died on the 7th day before the Ides of September in the 493rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000530.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Julius Victorinus, recording his lifespan and date of death in the 493rd provincial year.

EDCS-13000531.translation: In memory of Aurelia Cru delis. She lived about 20 years. She died on the Kalends of May in the provincial year.
EDCS-13000531.summary: This memorial inscription records the life and death of Aurelia Cru delis, with an incomplete provincial year date.

EDCS-13000532.translation: In memory of Julius Dominicus. He lived 18 years. He died on the 2nd day before the Nones of June in the 493rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000532.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Julius Dominicus, noting his age and death date in the 493rd provincial year.

EDCS-13000533.translation: In memory of...ntus Cru delis. He lived about 1 year. He died on the 5th day before the Kalends of September in the 497th year of the province.
EDCS-13000533.summary: This memorial inscription commemorates a young individual named ...ntus Cru delis, with a date of death in the 497th provincial year.

EDCS-13000535.translation: In memory of Julius Spendeu. He lived about 62 years. He died on the 15th day before the Kalends of January in the 502nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000535.summary: This memorial inscription records the life and death of Julius Spendeu, dated to the 502nd provincial year.

EDCS-13000537.translation: In memory of Julia Iusta, mother of the family. Her sons made her an eternal home. She lived about 85 years. She died on the 6th day before the Kalends of September in the 504th year of the province.
EDCS-13000537.summary: This memorial inscription honors Julia Iusta, a mother of the family, erected by her sons, and dated to the 504th provincial year.

EDCS-13000538.translation: In memory of Iulia Nome. She lived 75 years. She died on the 10th day before the Kalends of May in the 505th year of the province.
EDCS-13000538.summary: This memorial inscription records the life and death of Iulia Nome, dated to the 505th provincial year.

EDCS-13000541.translation: In memory of Aurelius Bonifatius. He lived about 61 years. He died on the 10th day before the Kalends of April in the 513th year of the province.
EDCS-13000541.summary: This memorial inscription commemorates Aurelius Bonifatius, noting his lifespan and death date in the 513th provincial year.

EDCS-13000542.translation: In memory of Iulia Maxima Cru delis. She lived about 30 years. She died on the 16th day before the Kalends of November in the 513th year of the province.
EDCS-13000542.summary: This memorial inscription records the life and death of Iulia Maxima Cru delis, dated to the 513th provincial year.

EDCS-13000543.translation: In memory of Aurelius Bonifatius. He lived 62 years. He died on the 15th day before the Kalends of November in the 519th year of the province.
EDCS-13000543.summary: This memorial inscription is for Aurelius Bonifatius, recording his age and death date in the 519th provincial year.

EDCS-13000544.translation: In memory of Aurelius ERNV DE, lived approximately 45 years, died on the 5th day before the Ides of February in the 522nd year of the province.
EDCS-13000544.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Aurelius ERNV DE, likely from the Roman province of Africa Proconsularis, dating to 522 Anno Pontificatus (AP) or 522 Anno Domini (AD) depending on context.

EDCS-13000547.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Ulpius Marturias, for whom his sons made an eternal home; he lived 32 years, died on the 8th day before the Ides of October in the 553rd year of the province.
EDCS-13000547.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Marturias, commissioned by his sons, likely from Roman North Africa, dating to 553 AP/AD.

EDCS-13000548.translation: In memory of Aurelius Montanus, father of the family, for whom his sons and grandsons made an eternal home; he lived approximately 61 years, departed in peace on the 15th day before the Kalends of July in the 560th year of the province.
EDCS-13000548.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Montanus, commissioned by his descendants, likely from Roman North Africa, dating to 560 AP/AD.

EDCS-13000550.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Caius Julius Victor.

EDCS-13000552.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Marcus Titius Egussus, lived 45 years, departed on the 3rd day before the Ides of February, I made this for my dearest father.
EDCS-13000552.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Titius Egussus, commissioned by his son, likely from Roman North Africa, dating to the 3rd day before the Ides of February (February 11th) of an unspecified year.

EDCS-13000553.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Magdira lived 45 years, returned her life to the one who gave it.
EDCS-13000553.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Magdira, indicating her death at age 45.

EDCS-13000554.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Oppia Saturina lived 17 years, departed.
EDCS-13000554.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Oppia Saturina, recording her death at age 17.

EDCS-13000556.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gargilia Sorora lived 65 years, Germana made this for her mother.
EDCS-13000556.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gargilia Sorora, commissioned by her daughter Germana, recording her death at age 65.

EDCS-13000557.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Aurelius Aretinus lived.
EDCS-13000557.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Iulius Aurelius Aretinus, recording that he lived, but without specifying lifespan or date.

EDCS-13000558.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Tes lived.
EDCS-13000558.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Aurelia Tes, indicating that she lived but without further details.

EDCS-13000559.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. His sons made this for him, well-deserving. Valerius Rogatus, his elder son, lived 50 years, 6 months, and 1 day.
EDCS-13000559.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by sons to a deceased father, including the son's name and lifespan.

EDCS-13000560.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Accius Tertius placed this tombstone for his brother, Caius Iulius Donatus. He lived about 20 years.
EDCS-13000560.summary: A funerary monument with a tombstone placed by a brother for his deceased brother, including his approximate age.

EDCS-13000561.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavia Donatula made this for her most beloved husband, Omidius Marcus. He lived 56 years. He is buried here.
