EDCS-13000561.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, including his age and burial confirmation.

EDCS-13000562.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavia Fortunata [made this] for her most dutiful mother.
EDCS-13000562.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a daughter to her mother.

EDCS-13000563.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. [Dedicated to] Sempronius.
EDCS-13000563.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Sempronius, with the dedicant missing.

EDCS-13000564.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Sulpicius Secundus lived 5 years and died on the 9th day before the Kalends of June.
EDCS-13000564.summary: A funerary monument recording the lifespan and death date of a child.

EDCS-13000565.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Titius Faussanus, a leading man of his community from the decemprimi, lived 90 years and 4 months, and Titius Cocceius lived 21 years and 6 months, and Titius Victor, his grandson, lived 11 years. Donatus and Castorius made this for their well-deserving and dearest father.
EDCS-13000565.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by sons to their father, recording the lifespans of the father and two other individuals, possibly family members.

EDCS-13000566.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulius Kastulus.
EDCS-13000566.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Iulius Kastulus, with the dedicant missing.

EDCS-13000567.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Ulpius Quintianus, my dearest brother.
EDCS-13000567.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a sibling to their deceased brother.

EDCS-13000568.translation: An eternal house for Iulia Altavessa, for whom her grandchildren made this.
EDCS-13000568.summary: A funerary monument built by grandchildren for their grandmother, Iulia Altavessa.

EDCS-13000570.translation: In memory of Flavius Vettenius, cruel. He lived about 27 years, and died on the Nones.
EDCS-13000570.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Flavius Vettenius, stating his name, a characterization, his approximate age at death, and the date of death.

EDCS-13000571.translation: In memory of Iulia Honorata, his mother, for whom her husband and sons made an eternal home. She lived about 65 years, and died on the day before the Ides of August in the 517th year of the province.
EDCS-13000571.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Iulia Honorata, indicating she was a mother for whom her family built a tomb, stating her approximate age at death and the date of death with a provincial year.

EDCS-13000572.translation: In memory of Iulius Fortunatus. He lives.
EDCS-13000572.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Iulius Fortunatus, stating his name and asserting his continued existence, likely in the afterlife.

EDCS-13000576.translation: In memory of Aurelius Maxsimus, for whom his sons made an eternal home. He lived about 55 years, and died in peace on the 3rd day of the Nones of January in the 500th year of the province.
EDCS-13000576.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Aurelius Maxsimus, indicating his sons built his tomb, stating his approximate age at death and the date of death with a provincial year.

EDCS-13000578.translation: In memory of Crepereius Primitivus. He lived for [---] years.
EDCS-13000578.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Crepereius Primitivus, stating his name and indicating his lifespan in years, though the exact number is lost.

EDCS-13000579.translation: In memory of Iulia Honorata. She lived for 4 years and died on the 5th.
EDCS-13000579.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Iulia Honorata, stating her name, her very young age at death (4 years), and the date of death.

EDCS-13000581.translation: In memory of Tissus Urbanus. He lived for 45 years, died piously on the 8th day before the Ides of September in the provincial year and Quitosus.
EDCS-13000581.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Tissus Urbanus, stating his name, his age at death, and the date of death with a provincial year and an unclear additional reference.

EDCS-13000582.translation: In memory of Ulpia Serena Longa. She lived for 65 years, and died on the Nones of August.
EDCS-13000582.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Ulpia Serena Longa, stating her name, her age at death, and the date of death.

EDCS-13000583.translation: In memory of Ulpius Altavesis. He lived for [---] years and died.
EDCS-13000583.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Ulpius Altavesis, stating his name and indicating his death, with the duration of his life lost.

EDCS-13000586.translation: In memory of Iulia Victoria. She lived for 60 years and died.
EDCS-13000586.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Iulia Victoria, stating her name, her age at death, and that she died.

EDCS-13000587.translation: In memory of Iulius Donatus Maximus, he lived 39 years, he departed in January of the provincial year.
EDCS-13000587.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and death of Iulius Donatus Maximus.

EDCS-13000833.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Donatus, priest of Saturn, lived 53 years. His heirs, Iulius Donatus and Constans, made this for him who deserved well.
EDCS-13000833.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by the heirs of Caius Iulius Donatus, a priest of Saturn.

EDCS-13000834.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Maior lived 29 years and 5 months. Flavius Secundus made this for his dearest wife, who lies here.
EDCS-13000834.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Flavius Secundus for his wife Aelia Maior.

EDCS-13000835.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Annaeus Fortunatus made this tomb for his dearest wife Tittoris, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-13000835.summary: A funerary inscription by Annaeus Fortunatus for his wife Tittoris.

EDCS-13000836.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mannilius Fortunatus lived 60 years, he lies here.
EDCS-13000836.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Mannilius Fortunatus.

EDCS-13000837.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Crepereia Rogata, a pious mother, lived 52 years. Her son, her heir, made this, she lies here.
EDCS-13000837.summary: A funerary inscription by the son and heir of Crepereia Rogata.

EDCS-13000838.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Dativa lived 3 years and 10 months. Callippus made this for his sweetest daughter, who lies here.
EDCS-13000838.summary: A funerary inscription by Callippus for his young daughter Dativa.

EDCS-13000839.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Emeritus lived 85 years, he lies here.
EDCS-13000839.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Iulius Emeritus.

EDCS-13000840.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felicianus lived 11 years.
EDCS-13000840.summary: A funerary inscription recording the short lifespan of Felicianus.

EDCS-13000841.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Donatus, a pious father, lived 65 years. His son, his heir, made this, he lies here.
EDCS-13000841.summary: A funerary inscription by the son and heir of Flavius Donatus.

EDCS-13000842.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Gemellina lived for 3 years and 10 months. Flavius Secundus made this for his sweetest daughter. She lies here.
EDCS-13000842.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Flavia Gemellina at age 3 years and 10 months, with the monument erected by her father, Flavius Secundus.

EDCS-13000843.translation: Tiberius Iustinius Fortunatus, pious, lived for 55 years. He lies here. His pious sons made this for their pious father.
EDCS-13000843.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Iustinius Fortunatus, who lived to 55, erected by his sons.

EDCS-13000844.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Felicus lived for 2 years and 11 months. Callippus made this for his sweetest son. He lies here.
EDCS-13000844.summary: A funerary epitaph for Felicus, who died at age 2 years and 11 months, with the monument erected by his father, Callippus.

EDCS-13000845.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Thereba the pious lived for 55 years. Aelius Maamon made this monument for his pious wife. She lies here.
EDCS-13000845.summary: A funerary epitaph for Thereba, who lived to 55, erected by her husband, Aelius Maamon.

EDCS-13002628.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Caecina the pious lived for 75 years, more or less.
EDCS-13002628.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Flavia Caecina, who lived for approximately 75 years.

EDCS-13002629.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flora lived for 20 years.
EDCS-13002629.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Flora, who lived for 20 years.

EDCS-13002630.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Hostilius Faustus lived for 75 years. His sons and heirs made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-13002630.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Hostilius Faustus, who lived to 75, erected by his sons and heirs.

EDCS-13003621.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Anicius Martialis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 23 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003621.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Anicius Martialis, who died at age 23, including a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003622.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Anicia Urbana, daughter of Sextus, lived for 65 years. She lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003622.summary: A funerary epitaph for Anicia Urbana, who died at age 65, including a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003623.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Cornelius Annianus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 45 years. He lies here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003623.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Cornelius Annianus, who died at age 45, including a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-13003624.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Donatus, freedman of Sextus, lived 19 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003624.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Donatus, a freedman, who lived 19 years.

EDCS-13003625.translation: To the Divine Shades. Racilia Silvana, daughter of Lucius, lived 13 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003625.summary: A funerary inscription for Racilia Silvana, daughter of Lucius, who lived 13 years.

EDCS-13003626.translation: Marcus Iulius Biccar, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003626.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Biccar, son of Marcus, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-13003627.translation: Iulius Marcellus, son of Lucius, lived 62 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003627.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Marcellus, son of Lucius, who lived 62 years.

EDCS-13003628.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Iulius Pacatus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 86 years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003628.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Pacatus, son of Caius, who lived 86 years.

EDCS-13003629.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Lullosa, daughter of Marcus, lived 57 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003629.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Lullosa, daughter of Marcus, who lived 57 years.

EDCS-13003630.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Sororor, daughter of Caius, lived 25 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003630.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Sororor, daughter of Caius, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-13003631.translation: Libinia Iuliana lived 80 years; she is buried. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003631.summary: A funerary inscription for Libinia Iuliana, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-13003632.translation: To the Divine Shades. Esquilinius Marcellus lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-13003632.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Esquilinius Marcellus, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-13003633.translation: To the Divine Shades. Silia Octavia, daughter of Silia, lived 61 years; she is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-13003633.summary: A funerary inscription for Silia Octavia, daughter of Silia, who lived 61 years.

EDCS-13003634.translation: To the divine shades. Quintus Popilius Martialis, son of Caius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 81 years. He rests here. May his bones lie peacefully.
EDCS-13003634.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Popilius Martialis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003635.translation: Sittius Castus, son of Publius, of the tribe Quirina, lived 55 years. He rests here. May your bones lie peacefully.
EDCS-13003635.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittius Castus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003636.translation: Sittia, daughter of Publius, lived XX years. She rests here. May your bones lie peacefully.
EDCS-13003636.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13003741.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Felix, son of Curtius, of the tribe Papiria, lived XX years. Placentius and EL, his heirs, made this.
EDCS-13003741.summary: A funerary epitaph for Felix, likely made by his heirs Placentius and EL, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302433.translation: Cleopatra lived 23 years and 7 months. Gaius Antonius [dedicates this].
EDCS-13302433.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Antonius for Cleopatra, who died at the age of 23 years and 7 months.

EDCS-13302473.translation: In memory of Isflacca Prima, who lived 18 years and departed on the 11th day before the Kalends of January in the 430th year of the province.
EDCS-13302473.summary: A memorial inscription for Isflacca Prima, noting her age at death and the date relative to a provincial era.

EDCS-13302475.translation: For the memory of Aurelius Montanus, father, brother, head of the household, for whom his sons and grandsons made this eternal home. He lived more or less 61 years and departed in peace on the 15th day before the Kalends of July in the 560th year of the province.
EDCS-13302475.summary: A funerary monument erected by his sons and grandsons for Aurelius Montanus, indicating his family roles and lifespan.

EDCS-13400121.summary: A short, personal inscription with an ethical or admonitory message.

EDCS-13400225.summary: An inscription recording the burial of a place struck by lightning, likely due to superstition.

EDCS-13500225.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Augustus Pius Felix, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, 3 miles.
EDCS-13500225.summary: An milestone inscription marking a distance of 3 miles in honor of Emperor Philippus.

EDCS-13500229.translation: To our Lords Emperors Flavius Valentinianus and Valens, always Augusti, 22 Roman miles.
EDCS-13500229.summary: This milestone inscription records a distance of 22 Roman miles dedicated to the emperors Valentinian and Valens.

EDCS-13600224.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the creation of a memorial by Donatus.

EDCS-13600229.translation: Marcus Ianuarius, priest, fulfilled his vow to Saturn Augustus.
EDCS-13600229.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Ianuarius, a priest, to the god Saturn Augustus.

EDCS-13600264.translation: Masestam, son of artifices Martialis and Innocentius, built this memorial in honor of the matron, giving thanks to God for its completion.
EDCS-13600264.summary: This funerary inscription records the construction of a memorial for a matron by Masestam, son of artifices Martialis and Innocentius, with thanks to God.

EDCS-13600273.translation: Emperor Caius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the country, proconsul, and Caius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, erected new milestones through Publius Aelius Vitalianus, their procurator, from [here] 7 Roman miles.
EDCS-13600273.summary: This milestone inscription records the erection of new milestones by Emperors Maximinus and his son Maximus, through their procurator Publius Aelius Vitalianus, dating to their reigns.

EDCS-13600419.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus, Unconquered, Pious, Happy Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul, grandson of the divine Gordiani, from [here] 1 Roman mile.
EDCS-13600419.summary: This milestone inscription records a distance of 1 Roman mile dedicated to Emperor Gordian III, dating to his reign.

EDCS-13600420.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, Unconquered, Pious, Happy Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, father of the country, from [here] 1 Roman mile.
EDCS-13600420.summary: This milestone inscription records a distance of 1 Roman mile dedicated to Emperor Philip I, dating to his reign.

EDCS-13600421.translation: Victor, an outstanding man, procurator, governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis.
EDCS-13600421.summary: This is an official inscription identifying Victor as the procurator and governor of Mauretania Caesariensis.

EDCS-13700319.summary: This is a dedication to Saturn, possibly as a 'commemorator' or recorder.

EDCS-13700320.summary: This is a dedication to the god Hercules Augustus.

EDCS-13700321.translation: Sacred to Caelestis Augusta. Caelia Satura willingly paid her vow.
EDCS-13700321.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caelia Satura to the goddess Caelestis Augusta, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-13700363.translation: While our Emperors Caesar Constantius and Constantius lived, Maximianus, the prefect, [rebuilt] their gates and doors with his own substance, after the consulship of Amantius and Albinus, in the 307th year of the province.
EDCS-13700363.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of gates and doors by Maximianus, a prefect, during the reign of Emperors Constantius and Constantius, dated to the provincial year 307 (likely 312 CE).

EDCS-13700364.translation: Aurelius Innocentius, son of Cis, dedicated it from the foundations after the death of his father, together with his sons, in the 302nd year of the province.
EDCS-13700364.summary: This is a dedication by Aurelius Innocentius, likely of a building or structure, made after his father's death and dated to the provincial year 302 (likely 307 CE).

EDCS-13800129.translation: Aemilius Sinsicio the master builder made [this], may he live in Christ.
EDCS-13800129.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aemilius Sinsicio, a master builder, expressing a Christian wish for the deceased.

EDCS-13800220.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Parthicus Arabicus Adiabenicus, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius, brother of the deified Commodus, nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Chief Priest, with tribunician power for the 6th time, Imperator for the 8th time, Consul for the 2nd time.
EDCS-13800220.summary: This is a lengthy imperial titulature of Lucius Septimius Severus, dating to a period when he held tribunician power for the 6th time and was consul for the 2nd time, likely around 197-202 CE.

EDCS-13900527.translation: To Opr [et] Frugi, through Decimus, the procurator.
EDCS-13900527.summary: This is a dedication to deities, possibly Sol Invictus (OPR) and a feminine deity (NI), or perhaps an abbreviation for 'honor' and 'virtue', made by Decimus, a procurator.

EDCS-13900530.translation: To the Divine Shades. Antonius Valens, a standard-bearer of the II Cohort of Sardinians, served for 10 years, lived for 40 years.
EDCS-13900530.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonius Valens, a soldier of the II Cohort of Sardinians, detailing his service and lifespan.

EDCS-14100385.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying 'D[eie]ccons' as a soldier.

EDCS-14200094.translation: To Sextus Julius Severus, son of Brutus, of the Quirina tribe, a Roman knight, primipilus, most dutiful and loving father, Julius Pompilius and Bassinus, Roman knights, [dedicate this] to their incomparable patron.
EDCS-14200094.summary: This is a dedication by two Roman knights to their patron, Sextus Julius Severus, honoring his military rank and familial virtues.

EDCS-14200096.translation: Iria lived sixteen years, deserving well.
EDCS-14200096.summary: This is a brief epitaph for Iria, who died at the age of sixteen, noting her good character.

EDCS-14200099.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Cornelius Felix lived 25 years. Lucius Cornelius Felicio, his father, made this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-14200099.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a father to his deceased son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200100.translation: To the Divine Shades. Octavia Pacata lived 35 years. Octavius Expeditus made this for his freedwoman and wife.
EDCS-14200100.summary: A funerary epitaph by a husband for his wife and freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200101.translation: To the Divine Shades, sacred. Caius Suractus made this for his dearest son, who lived 3 years, 9 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-14200101.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a father to his young son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200102.translation: Fadius, son of Fadius Expeditius, is buried here; he lived 8 days.
EDCS-14200102.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of a very young child, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200103.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marco Domitius Primitivus lived 1 year, 4 months. Viator, his father, made this.
EDCS-14200103.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a father to his young son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200104.translation: Iucartes, son of Goutis, is buried here.
EDCS-14200104.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of an individual named Iucartes, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200105.translation: Marcus Aemilius Seneco and Maria placed this for their daughter.
EDCS-14200105.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by parents for their daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200106.translation: To the Divine Shades. Antonio lived 16 years and 7 days. Aemilius Asper, in memory of his brother.
EDCS-14200106.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a brother to his deceased sibling, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200107.translation: Cornelius Longinus made this monument for Victorina, his wife, who lived 17 years. Modi Felicis, daughter, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-14200107.summary: A funerary epitaph by a husband for his deceased wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200109.translation: Caius Iulius, son of Zora Anina, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-14200109.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of an individual, likely of foreign origin, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14200110.translation: Eleutheris is buried here. Mario, the works manager, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-14200110.summary: This is a funerary monument for Eleutheris, funded by Mario, an officinator, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14200111.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Clodius Rogatus lived for 22 years and 2 months. His father made this for his son; may the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-14200111.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Clodius Rogatus, erected by his father, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14200112.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Valeria Secunda lived for 55 years. Castus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-14200112.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Secunda, dedicated by her husband Castus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14200113.translation: Publius Basilius Syneros, freedman of Atacis, is buried here. Maria Faustilla, wife of Mimariomus, is buried here.
EDCS-14200113.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of two individuals, Publius Basilius Syneros and Maria Faustilla, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14200115.translation: Popillia Tranquilla, daughter of Lucius, is buried here. She lived for 18 years.
EDCS-14200115.summary: A funerary epitaph for Popillia Tranquilla, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14200117.translation: Gaius Domitius Valentinus gladly paid his vow to the god Saturn.
EDCS-14200117.summary: A votive inscription to the god Saturn, dedicated by Gaius Domitius Valentinus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14200118.translation: Iulia Daphnis gladly paid her vow to Saturn.
EDCS-14200118.summary: A votive inscription to the god Saturn, dedicated by Iulia Daphnis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14300050.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Nacius.
EDCS-14300050.summary: A funerary dedication to the Dis Manibus by Quintus Nacius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15000104.translation: To the Emperors, our Lords Diocletian and Maximian, eternal Augusti, and to Constantius and Maximianus, most strong Caesars, princes of youth, for the centenarium which is called Aqua Viva, by order of Valerius Alexander, most perfect man, acting as deputy prefect of the praetorium, and Valerius Florus, most perfect man, governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, acting as governor and prefect of the province of Dacia Inferior, procurator of the XXth part of inheritances in the province of Syria Palaestina, procurator for the census.
EDCS-15000104.summary: This inscription records various official appointments and responsibilities of Titus Flavius Priscus Gallonius Frontonus Marcius Turbonus, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-15100042.translation: For our Emperors, Lords Diocletian and Maximian, eternal Augusti, and Constantius and Maximianus, most strong Caesars, princes of youth. The centenarium, called Aqua Viva, has been constructed from the ground up by order of Valerius Alexander, most perfect man, acting prefect of the praetorian prefecture, and Valerius Florus, most perfect man, governor of the province of Numidia, under the supervision of Valerius Ingenuus, prefect of the frontier, and dedicated to our Lords Diocletian VIII and Maximian VII Augusti, consuls.
EDCS-15100042.summary: This is a dedication inscription for a public work, the centenarium called Aqua Viva, funded by imperial order and local officials, likely dating to the consulship of Diocletian VIII and Maximian VII (293 CE).

EDCS-15100043.translation: Lucius Afranius Victor, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, made this for himself and his wife while he was alive. Farewell.
EDCS-15100043.summary: A funerary inscription by a veteran of Legio III Augusta for himself and his wife, made during his lifetime.

EDCS-15100047.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marcus Aurelius Romanus, a *cornicularius* of a most distinguished man, the prefect of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, who lived 35 years, 3 months, and 8 days. May he be courageous, and enjoy good fortune.
EDCS-15100047.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Romanus, a *cornicularius* in the Misenum fleet, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100048.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time. From the colony Nerviana Augusta Martialis.
EDCS-15100048.summary: A dedication to Emperor Trajan by the colony of Nerviana Augusta Martialis, likely commemorating his second consulship and tribunician power.

EDCS-15200114.translation: In memory of Aurelia Tistcia. I established the burial and the plot for myself, I prepared for my mother the work that was needed. She lived 70 years. May Tranquilla enjoy her life. Her body rests in the lowest regions. Peregrinus, her son, made this. Year of the province 315.
EDCS-15200114.summary: A funerary monument erected by Peregrinus for his mother Aurelia Tistcia, recording her age and the date of its construction in provincial era 315.

EDCS-15300363.translation: To Gaius Pompeius Sallustianus, *duovir* and priest of the sacred rites, [and] to Iulia Bonia, his most worthy husband.
EDCS-15300363.summary: A dedication to Gaius Pompeius Sallustianus, who held the office of *duovir* and priest, and to his wife Iulia Bonia.

EDCS-15300364.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. You who stand passing by and look upon the end, the death of my monument, behold how worthy was the life given to me. I lived thirty-seven years and was the heir of Gaius Vibus Cosmus, the agent.
EDCS-15300364.summary: A self-commemorative epitaph of an individual who lived 37 years and was the heir of Gaius Vibus Cosmus, an agent.

EDCS-15600021.translation: In the name of Felix the bishop, concerning promises.
EDCS-15600021.summary: A brief inscription related to religious matters, possibly a title or a heading for a document, mentioning Bishop Felix.

EDCS-15600022.translation: In memory of Donata Lais. She rests here on the sixth day before the Ides of January in the year 416.
EDCS-15600022.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Donata Lais, with her date of rest on January 8th in the provincial year 416.

EDCS-15600031.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Iahin made this table for himself together with his wife Donata. They made this table for Donata, his mother-in-law, in the 375th and 5th year of the province.
EDCS-15600031.summary: A funerary inscription by Lucius Iahin for his wife Donata and his mother-in-law Donata, dated to provincial year 380.

EDCS-15600033.translation: Table in memory of Modus. He lived 90 years and died on the Ides of December in the 364th and fourth year of the province.
EDCS-15600033.summary: A funerary inscription for Modus, recording his age at death and the date in provincial year 368.

EDCS-15600034.translation: To the memory of Verius Ianurius, who lived more or less 17 years, buried on the 6th day before the Kalends of December in the 346th year of the province. His mother [made this] for her fortunate son.
EDCS-15600034.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Verius Ianurius, recording his age at death and burial date, and stating it was dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-15600035.translation: To the underworld spirits. Paramithius, son of Iulianus, a deputy administrator, lived 40 years. Marcus Cocceius Alexander made this for his most devoted father.
EDCS-15600035.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Paramithius, recording his age at death and stating it was dedicated by his son.

EDCS-15600036.translation: Sacred to the underworld spirits. Victor, an actor who was a former administrator, lived 70 years. He is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-15600036.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Victor, recording his age at death and his former profession, with a wish for his repose.

EDCS-15600037.translation: To the underworld spirits. Clementianus, a steward, lived 38 years. He served as steward in Sitifis and in the opposing port and garrisons. He is buried here.
EDCS-15600037.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clementianus, recording his age at death and his administrative roles in Sitifis and its port and garrisons.

EDCS-15600038.translation: Canicius Agentius, 17 years old, has passed on.
EDCS-15600038.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for Canicius Agentius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-15800338.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, father of the country, with tribunician power, consul, proconsul, ordered this milestone to be erected through Publius Flavius Clemens, his procurator, 7 miles from Aras.
EDCS-15800338.summary: This is an inscription on a milestone commissioned by Emperor Severus Alexander and erected by his procurator, marking a distance of 7 miles from Aras.

EDCS-15900069.translation: For the safety of King Ptolemy, son of King Juba, in his tenth regnal year, Antistia Galla willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Saturn, with the victim accepted, through Iulia Respecta, daughter of Vitalis from Rusgunia.
EDCS-15900069.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Saturn by Antistia Galla for the safety of King Ptolemy, son of King Juba.

EDCS-15900135.translation: Balaterus, son of Melius, of the citizen body of the Melqumeni, a soldier in the 6th cohort of Dalmatians, 28 years old, aged 8 [denarii?]. He is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-15900135.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Balaterus, a Dalmatian soldier, recording his age, cohort, and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-15900345.translation: For the Emperors Caesar Marcus Clodius Pupienus and Decimus Caelius Calvinus Balbinus, Pious Happy Augustuses, chief priests, with tribunician power, fathers of the country, twice consuls, proconsuls, and for Marcus Antonius Gordianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, nephew of the deified Gordians, [this milestone was placed] by the ala of the military column, 7 miles.
EDCS-15900345.summary: This is an inscription on a milestone commissioned by the emperors Pupienus and Balbinus, and for Caesar Gordianus, marking a distance of 7 miles.

EDCS-15900384.translation: A decurion of the 2nd cohort of Britons, he rests here, lived 42 years. I wish you light earth, pure soul.
EDCS-15900384.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a decurion of the 2nd cohort of Britons, recording his age and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-15900387.translation: Memorial of the holy martyrs of Centum Arbores, Vincentius and Centumarbor, and others.
EDCS-15900387.summary: This inscription is a dedication to martyrs, likely from the late antique period, commemorating Vincentius and Centumarbor.

EDCS-15900388.translation: To Quintus Memmius Rufus, honored man, flamen, and to Cilonia Saturnina, his wife, and to Quintus Memmius Memmius Florus, honored man, flamen, and to Quintus Memmius, equestrian, flamen, and to Quintus Sextius Pudens, son of Sextius Maximus, and to Memmia Processina, with her son Marcus Fulvius Honoratus, for her parents, erected and dedicated by Quintus Memmius Agatangelus, freedman of the Memmii.
EDCS-15900388.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to multiple members of the Memmii family, including flamines, with the dedication made by a freedman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900389.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Veritus, an equestrian of Stablis, lived for 22 years and 2 months. Aurelius Vitalis, exarch, made this memorial for his comrade.
EDCS-15900389.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aurelius Veritus, an equestrian from Stablis, who died at the age of 22, with the monument erected by his comrade, Aurelius Vitalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900391.translation: The names of the martyrs who confessed at Centum Arbores: thirty-six and Justus.
EDCS-15900391.summary: This inscription lists the names of martyrs who were recognized at Centum Arbores, dating likely to the late antique period.

EDCS-15900392.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius Aelianus, cornicularius of the Cohors Hispanorum, lived for 51 years and served for 26 years. Annius Dativus made this for his friend.
EDCS-15900392.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Aelianus, a cornicularius (clerk) of the Cohors Hispanorum, who died after 26 years of service, with the monument dedicated by his friend, Annius Dativus, likely from the Roman military context.

EDCS-16000020.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Egnatia Rufina Vernamiessa lived for 45 years and 10 months and 6 days. Caecilius Victor, vexillarius, son, made this for his most sweet mother.
EDCS-16000020.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Egnatia Rufina Vernamiessa, who died at the age of 45, with the monument erected by her son, Caecilius Victor, a vexillarius, likely from the Roman military context.

EDCS-16000023.translation: He made a gift to the god Mercury.
EDCS-16000023.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a gift made to the god Mercury, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16000024.translation: To the memory of Iulia Iusta, the mother of the family, for whom her sons made an eternal home. She lived more or less 85 years and departed on the 6th day before the Kalends of September in the 504th year of the province.
EDCS-16000024.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Iusta, erected by her sons, providing her age and the date of her death according to the provincial era, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16000026.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Lucius Porcius Porcianus, who served in the ala of the Thracians as a standard-bearer, lived for 50 years. Flavia Honorata, his wife, and Lucius Porcius, his son, made this for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16000026.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Porcius Porcianus, a standard-bearer in the ala of the Thracians who died at 50, erected by his wife and son, likely from the Roman military context.

EDCS-16000312.translation: To the Holy God Saturn Augustus, Lucius Iulius Kastus, priest, made and dedicated the temple at his own expense with all his children.
EDCS-16000312.summary: This is an inscription recording the dedication of a temple to the god Saturn Augustus by the priest Lucius Iulius Kastus at his own expense, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16100265.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Cassius Calpurnianus, an equestrian of the Thracian cavalry, an assistant to the record office, who lived 32 years, 9 months, 20 days, and 5 and a half hours. Calpurnia Ancilla placed here for her deserving son. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-16100265.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Cassius Calpurnianus, an equestrian, erected by his mother Calpurnia Ancilla.

EDCS-16100266.translation: Marcus Asclepiades, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, decurion of the colony of Caesarienses, erected this for himself and his children.
EDCS-16100266.summary: A funerary monument erected by Marcus Asclepiades, a decurion, for himself and his family.

EDCS-16100267.translation: In these estates of Cominius Montanus and Felicianus the younger and Felicianus their father, a bath and every comfort is provided in the urban style.
EDCS-16100267.summary: An inscription indicating the provision of a bath and amenities in the urban style on estates belonging to the Cominii family.

EDCS-16100336.translation: With the god, ask if the jars are beneficial.
EDCS-16100336.summary: A short, cryptic inscription, possibly a votive or a proverb related to jars and a deity.

EDCS-16100372.translation: To Titus Licinius Hierocles of the Clustumina tribe. Lucius Vinnius Fronto from the record office: Begin, little boy, to surpass your excellent parent in your studies and adorn your noble lineage with your own virtues.
EDCS-16100372.summary: An encouragement from Lucius Vinnius Fronto to a young boy named Titus Licinius Hierocles to excel in his studies and honor his family.

EDCS-16100373.translation: Here is the holy blood of Christ, of the martyr Eusebius.
EDCS-16100373.summary: A Christian inscription referring to the blood of the martyr Eusebius.

EDCS-16100374.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Lollius Saturninus, who was also called Bicchari, lived 85 years. Quintus Lollius Saturninus, who was also called Mercurius, made this for his dearest father.
EDCS-16100374.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Lollius Saturninus (aka Bicchari), erected by his son Quintus Lollius Saturninus (aka Mercurius).

EDCS-16100432.translation: By the indulgence of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the country, by order of Caius Asprius Sabinianus, procurator, the fort of Vartanense.
EDCS-16100432.summary: An inscription detailing the construction or maintenance of the fort of Vartanense by order of the Emperor and under the authority of procurator Caius Asprius Sabinianus.

EDCS-16200055.translation: To Fortune. Tiberius Claudius Perpetuus, procurator of the emperor.
EDCS-16200055.summary: A dedication to the goddess Fortune by Tiberius Claudius Perpetuus, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-16200056.translation: To the Father God Salutaris. Aemilius Pompeianus, decurion of the Thracian cavalry, commander, set up this vow.
EDCS-16200056.summary: A votive inscription to the god Salutaris by Aemilius Pompeianus, a decurion and commander.

EDCS-16200057.translation: Titus Flavius Priscus Gallonius Fronto Marcius Turbo, imperial procurator.
EDCS-16200057.summary: This is a personal inscription naming Titus Flavius Priscus Gallonius Fronto Marcius Turbo as an imperial procurator.

EDCS-16200058.translation: For Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, Invictus, Pius, Felix, Augustus; ten miles.
EDCS-16200058.summary: This is a milestone inscription marking ten miles for Emperor Aurelian, likely from the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-16200696.translation: He ordered new milestones to be set up, through Publius Flavius Claudius Ementus, his procurator; one mile.
EDCS-16200696.summary: This is a milestone inscription indicating the placement of one mile of new markers, commissioned by a procurator under imperial authority.

EDCS-16200697.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. His sons erected this for their son of good memory, who lived 50 years, 7 months, and 1 day.
EDCS-16200697.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a son, erected by his sons, providing his age at death and likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200698.summary: This inscription likely refers to a boxer named Rogatianus or belonging to him, possibly a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-16200701.translation: Caius Iulius Germanicus erected this for his most pious mother, a holy woman, priestess of Tellus, born to Marcus Cassius Proculus and Porcia Rogata, who lived 70 years, 7 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-16200701.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Porcia Rogata, erected by her son Caius Iulius Germanicus, detailing her parentage, priesthood, and lifespan.

EDCS-16200889.translation: Condonius, son of Coro, city treasurer and aedile of Oppidum Novum, was killed with five soldiers; Arius Celtius buried him here.
EDCS-16200889.summary: This epitaph records the death of Condonius, a local official, along with five soldiers, and identifies his burial by Arius Celtius.

EDCS-16200890.translation: I, the prefect of Iugmena, began the church, and God completed it in the name of the Holy Spirit, in the year 335 of the province; we Zabenses completed it.
EDCS-16200890.summary: This inscription records the founding and completion of a church by a prefect of Iugmena, with completion attributed to divine aid and local efforts in the provincial year 335 (likely 335 AD).

EDCS-16200960.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Pius, Felix, Augustus, father of the country, proconsul, from the Ala; seven miles.
EDCS-16200960.summary: This is a milestone inscription marking seven miles for Emperor Severus Alexander, likely from the Ala region, dating to his reign (222-235 CE).

EDCS-16200962.summary: This inscription likely refers to Quadratus Surus, an archer, possibly a tombstone or a dedication related to military service.

EDCS-16200963.translation: Publius Livius Phileros, freedman of Publius Eusextus, an oil merchant, lies here; he was 48 years old. Valeria made the tombstone for her husband, for his merits.
EDCS-16200963.summary: This is a tombstone erected by a wife for her deceased husband, an oil merchant, indicating his age and the wife's affection.

EDCS-16200965.translation: If the grace of living is restored to the shades, here is one who would have had his dwelling in the Elysian fields, to whom the Father from above granted many years; but the Fates forbade the father's wishes to go further. For the laudable boy lived only twenty years and one month before the impious fate of the Fates snatched him away. That you may know who rests in this urn, consider the verses generally, but first individually: Secundus.
EDCS-16200965.summary: This is a poetic epitaph for a young man named Secundus, who died at the age of 20, lamenting his early death and praising his character.

EDCS-16200966.translation: Table of Miginius, subdeacon, on the 3rd day before the Ides of June.
EDCS-16200966.summary: This is a brief inscription likely recording a memorial meal or offering for a deceased individual named Miginius, a subdeacon.

EDCS-16201031.translation: Titus Caecilius Honoratus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, dedicated this gift to the August Victory for the honor of his flaminate.
EDCS-16201031.summary: This is a dedication of a gift by Titus Caecilius Honoratus to the goddess Victoria, likely in thanks for or as part of his priestly office (flamen).

EDCS-16201180.translation: Fortune, may you bring good luck to the youth!
EDCS-16201180.summary: This is a short invocation to the goddess Fortuna for good luck, likely addressed to or for a group of young people.

EDCS-16201225.translation: Seek the table of the martyrs. Renatus and Optata suffered on the Nones of April and were crowned.
EDCS-16201225.summary: This inscription requests access to the 'table' (likely a tomb or altar) of martyrs, mentioning Renatus and Optata who suffered and were crowned on April 5th.

EDCS-16201553.translation: To Mauricis. Caius Stertinius Aemilianus, procurator of the Emperor. To Mauricis. Caius Stertinius Aemilianus, procurator of the Emperor.
EDCS-16201553.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or dedication repeated, by Caius Stertinius Aemilianus, an imperial procurator, possibly to a deity or person named Mauricis.

EDCS-16201627.summary: This is a genitive inscription, possibly referring to a group, family, or property associated with the Thybridii and Basilianus.

EDCS-16201629.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Clodia Saturnina, to her most loving wife, Honores.
EDCS-16201629.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Honores to his beloved wife, Clodia Saturnina, consecrated to the spirits of the departed.

EDCS-16201784.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Here I, unhappy, was received into the dire storehouses of Dis. I gave back my life and could not see my sons, according to my fate and the law allotted by the arbiters of the Fates. I looked upon the infernal seats and the upper world, and gave incense with hostile complaints to the gods above and to the grim altars, praying to the Spirits. I beg you, Bato, my dearest brother, if you have found my children entrusted to you, that the goddess Poverty, subject to her will, not be their memory. Do not let me be mocked, having suffered from accusations in the lowest infernal seats. Named Dalmatio, always loved. To Valerius Dalmatio, exarch of the Stablesian cavalry, from Bato, his parent.
EDCS-16201784.summary: This complex funerary epitaph is dedicated by Bato to his deceased adopted father, Valerius Dalmatio, an exarch, lamenting his own unhappy fate and seeking remembrance.

EDCS-16300158.translation: May you flourish with your clergy, as God willed this to happen; Florus and Matrona rendered their vow with all their sons.
EDCS-16300158.summary: A votive inscription from Florus and Matrona with their sons, dedicating a sacrifice or offering with the approval of God.

EDCS-16300566.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Marcus Clodius Pupienus Maximus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul, and for the Emperor Caesar Decimus Caelius Calvinus Balbinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul, and for Marcus Antonius Gordianus, the most noble Caesar, Augustus, Prince of Youth, grandson of the deified Gordiani, by the state of the colony of Nerviana Augusta Martialis Sitifensium.
EDCS-16300566.summary: A dedication by the state of the colony of Sitifis to the emperors Pupienus and Balbinus, and to Caesar Gordianus (later Gordian III), likely from around 238 CE.

EDCS-16300568.translation: For the health of the Roman people, Victorinus, the aedile, placed and dedicated the weigh-station at his own expense.
EDCS-16300568.summary: An inscription recording the public works project of an aedile named Victorinus, who funded the construction of a public weigh-station for the benefit of the community.

EDCS-16400310.translation: To the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, protector of the Thamascanians.
EDCS-16400310.summary: A dedication to an emperor, identified by his epithets, likely as a benefactor or protector by the people of Thamugadi.

EDCS-16400371.translation: Eutyches, the actor, with his people, restored the Springs and the Most Holy Nymphs with squared stonework from the ground up.
EDCS-16400371.summary: A dedication by Eutyches, an actor, who restored a sanctuary of springs and nymphs using carefully constructed stonework.

EDCS-16400372.translation: For the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Marcus Magnus Antoninus Augustus Britannicus Germanicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Severus Arabicus Adibeneus Parthicus Maximus, great-grandson of the deified Commodus and the deified Marcus Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, descendant of the deified Antoninus Pius and the deified Hadrianus, descendant of the deified Trajan and the deified Nerva, by the colony of Nerviana Augusta Martialis of the veterans of Sitifis, unit VIII.
EDCS-16400372.summary: A lengthy imperial titulature inscription from the colony of Sitifis, likely dating to the reign of Caracalla, listing numerous imperial ancestors.

EDCS-16400399.translation: For the welfare of the Empire of Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Caesar Augustus, for Sol Invictus, Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Luna Regina, Minerva, Victoria, Mercury, the Genius of the Place, and Fortuna the Propagator, and for all the gods, with good fortune.
EDCS-16400399.summary: A dedication to various gods and the emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius (likely Caracalla) for the well-being of the Roman Empire.

EDCS-16400400.translation: Here lie the remains of the martyr Vincent.
EDCS-16400400.summary: An inscription marking the burial place of the martyr Vincent.

EDCS-16400401.translation: Rejoice in the Lord and exult, you just, and let all the upright in heart glory; the good one inscribed this.
EDCS-16400401.summary: A religious inscription encouraging joy and glory in the Lord, attributed to a virtuous individual.

EDCS-16400402.translation: In God I will praise the word, in God I will praise the speech; in God I have hoped, I will not fear what a man may do to me. For his good presbyter Faustus, by whose initiative the church was built.
EDCS-16400402.summary: A religious inscription expressing faith in God and a desire not to fear human actions, dedicated to Presbyter Faustus, who initiated the building of the church.

EDCS-16400479.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Athonius Marcellus, an outstanding man, procurator of our Augustus and governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, and to his wife Manilia Septimia Crispina, by Lupercus, freedman of Augustus, from the record office.
EDCS-16400479.summary: This is a dedication to a governor and his wife, likely made by an official in the record office.

EDCS-16500201.translation: The place of the tomb of Saint Rogatianus the martyr.
EDCS-16500201.summary: This inscription marks the burial site of a Christian martyr.

EDCS-16500207.translation: Sacred to Caelestis, by Julius Victor, Julius Felix, and Julius Mono.
EDCS-16500207.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Caelestis by three individuals.

EDCS-16500221.translation: Sacred to the Lord God Holy Saturn Augustus, by Lucius Otacilius Candidus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, priest of the god Saturn, together with his wife Titiana Sabina, willingly fulfilled his vow. From the workshop of Lucius Aemilius Quartus.
EDCS-16500221.summary: This is a religious dedication to Saturn Augustus made by a priest and his wife, originating from a specific workshop.

EDCS-16500256.translation: To our lord Valerius Licinianus Invictus.
EDCS-16500256.summary: This is a dedication to an individual named Valerius Licinianus Invictus, referred to as 'our lord'.

EDCS-16500268.translation: Ceionius Caecina Albinus, most illustrious man, consular, holder of the sexfascalis office.
EDCS-16500268.summary: This inscription records the titles and status of Ceionius Caecina Albinus, a prominent Roman official.

EDCS-16700059.translation: Aemilius Brumasius, soldier of the III Augusta legion, lived 35 years. His colleagues made this.
EDCS-16700059.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aemilius Brumasius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his colleagues.

EDCS-16700129.translation: To Titus Caesernius Statio Quinctius, son of Titus, of the Palatina tribe, Macedo, procurator of Augustus, the gens Maurorum Maccuum, most innocent, his patron and governor.
EDCS-16700129.summary: This dedication is for Titus Caesernius Statio Quinctius, a procurator and patron, by the gens Maurorum Maccuum.

EDCS-16700130.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Zenon Ianuarius, procurator of our Augustus, governor.
EDCS-16700130.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Aurelius Zenon Ianuarius, who served as a procurator and governor.

EDCS-16700235.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, erected this, under the supervision of his procurator Lucius Catellius Livianus. 12 miles from Tecta, 18 miles from Altava.
EDCS-16700235.summary: This is a milestone inscription by Emperor Philippus, indicating distances from two locations, likely marking a road.

EDCS-16700249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph mentioning a table belonging to Mantilla, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-16700254.translation: Table of the Martyrs Donatus, Felix, Novici, and Baric, who suffered at Guruzis.
EDCS-16700254.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating martyrs named Donatus, Felix, Novici, and Baric, who died at Guruzis.

EDCS-16800204.translation: To Victoria Augusta for the health of our Lords Emperors Augusti and Aelius Peregrinus, our governor, Nonius Fortunatus, centurion of the Vth Cohort, IOI R [likely part of unit designation].
EDCS-16800204.summary: This is a dedication to Victoria Augusta, made by a centurion for the health of the emperors and the provincial governor.

EDCS-16800209.translation: To the goddess Bellona. Scantia Peregrina, daughter of Caius, priestess, by decree of the town council, built this temple from its foundations with her own money.
EDCS-16800209.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Bellona, funded by a priestess named Scantia Peregrina, in accordance with a town council decree.

EDCS-16800210.translation: To Aelia Flavia, wife of Classicus, procurator of the Emperor, a most virtuous woman, from Caninia Salsa, for her merits.
EDCS-16800210.summary: This is a dedication to Aelia Flavia, wife of an imperial procurator, made by Caninia Salsa in recognition of her merits.

EDCS-16800211.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Malia made this for her well-deserving husband, Attaeus Primitivus, an administrator's slave. He lived 24 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-16800211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Attaeus Primitivus, a slave who served as an administrator, erected by his wife Malia.

EDCS-16800212.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For the deceased Albinus, patron of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, and for the most distinguished boy Flavinus Valerius Festus, son of Lucius, and Lucius F(lavius) Melli Latronianus, by decree of the provincial council of Mauretania Caesariensis, their honor having been received, he undertook the expense.
EDCS-16800212.summary: This inscription honors Albinus, patron of Mauretania Caesariensis, and two young boys, Flavinus Valerius Festus and Lucius F(lavius) Melli Latronianus, as decreed by the provincial council.

EDCS-16800349.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Quetus Ulpius, a soldier of the Germanic reconnaissance unit, with 6 years of service, who lived 27 years. His second heir, Flavius Victorinus, saw to the arrangements for his burial.
EDCS-16800349.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quetus Ulpius, a soldier in a reconnaissance unit, whose burial was arranged by his heir.

EDCS-16800401.translation: The eternal vigilance strengthens the prudence of peace, faithfully protecting Roman affairs, and right by the river, a mountain strengthened by a fortification from which it took its name Petra, and moreover the neighbouring peoples come together in peace agreements at Sammac, so that virtue, accompanied by loyalty, may be in concord in every duty, always allied with Roman triumphs. Estate of Sammacis.
EDCS-16800401.summary: This inscription celebrates the strategic importance of a fortified mountain named Petra near the river Sammac, highlighting its role in securing peace and alliances for Rome.

EDCS-16900005.translation: In memory of sacred Robba, sister of Honoratus, bishop of Aquae Sirensis, who suffered martyrdom by betrayal. She lived 50 years and gave up her spirit on the eighth day before the Kalends of April in the 395th year [of the province? or Christian era?].
EDCS-16900005.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Robba, sister of a bishop, who is honored as a martyr, with details of her age and death date.

EDCS-16900024.translation: In memory of Mauricius, magister militum, who lived 55 years and rested in peace on the third day before the Kalends of April, in the 14th indiction.
EDCS-16900024.summary: This inscription is a tombstone commemorating Mauricius, a military official, and noting his lifespan and date of death.

EDCS-16900025.translation: In memory of Patricia, daughter of the glorious lord Mauricus, magister militum.
EDCS-16900025.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Patricia, daughter of Mauricus, a magister militum.

EDCS-16900026.translation: In memory of Constantina, daughter of the glorious lord Mauricus, magister militum, who restored the long-collapsed buildings to this holy basilica, she was laid to rest in peace at the age of 3 on the Kalends of November, in the 9th indiction.
EDCS-16900026.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Constantina, daughter of Mauricus, a magister militum, who also records her father's restoration of buildings.

EDCS-17000041.translation: Gaius Avianius Amandus, augur, gave and consecrated the lectica with its ornaments and base to the goddess Pedisequae of Virtue and Bellona.
EDCS-17000041.summary: This is a dedication by Gaius Avianius Amandus, an augur, offering a lectica and base to the goddesses Pedisequae, Virtue, and Bellona.

EDCS-17000059.translation: For Floras, son of Vitalis of Tipasa, and Marcia and Caesallia.
EDCS-17000059.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial for several individuals: Floras, Marcia, and Caesallia, with Floras identified as the son of Vitalis of Tipasa.

EDCS-17000064.translation: For Marcianus Donatus, son of Marcius Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, son of Sextus Marcius Marcianus, perpetual flamen, to his aedile due to the honor of his father, the citizens of Forenses.
EDCS-17000064.summary: This inscription is a dedication for Marcianus Donatus, honoring his father's offices and his own position as aedile.

EDCS-19400139.translation: To the eternal Augustus, 22 miles, and to the eternal Augusti and to Gratian, perpetual Augusti.
EDCS-19400139.summary: This inscription records distances to imperial figures, indicating milestones along a road.

EDCS-19400140.translation: From Tipasa, 4 miles, to our Emperors Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletian Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, and Emperor Caesar Aurelius Valerius Maximian Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, and the most noble Caesars Flavius Valerius Constantius and Galerius Valerius Maximian.
EDCS-19400140.summary: This inscription marks a distance of 4 miles from Tipasa, dedicated to the emperors Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Galerius Maximian.

EDCS-19400142.translation: From Tipasitani, 19 miles, to our lords Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius and Gaius Valens Hostilianus Quintus, most noble Caesars, sons of our lord the Emperor Trajan Augustus.
EDCS-19400142.summary: This inscription indicates a distance of 19 miles from Tipasa to the Caesars Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius and Gaius Valens Hostilianus, identifying them as sons of Emperor Trajan Augustus.

EDCS-23200001.translation: To Fabatia Polla, daughter of Lucius, a consular lady and torchbearer, Marcus Fabatius Domitius Pancratius, freedman and procurator of his most devoted patroness.
EDCS-23200001.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by a freedman and procurator to his patroness, Fabatia Polla, noting her status and relationships.

EDCS-23200003.translation: To the Genius of the municipality of Rusuccur: Gaius Julius Felix Rusuccuritanus, son of Rusticus, of the Quirina tribe, a decurion appointed by the order, prefect acting as praetor, and again appointed by the order as a praetor for two years, also a praetor for two years, quinquennial praetor, perpetual flamen of the Augusti, and augur, built and dedicated at his own expense from his old house a temple and a statue.
EDCS-23200003.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of a temple and statue to the Genius of Rusuccur by a local magistrate, Gaius Julius Felix Rusuccuritanus.

EDCS-23200004.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Victor, freedman of the emperor, made this for Aelia Magna, his freedwoman, and devoted wife, who lived 30 years, 3 months, and 16 days.
EDCS-23200004.summary: This funerary inscription records the devotion of a freedman, Victor, to his freedwoman and wife, Aelia Magna.

EDCS-23200008.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Hossidius Rogatus, most loving husband.
EDCS-23200008.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to a husband named Hossidius Rogatus.

EDCS-23200009.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Atirus, an eternal friend, well-deserving. Made for the above-mentioned, who lived 47 years.
EDCS-23200009.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by an unnamed person to their well-deserving friend, Atirus, who lived 47 years.

EDCS-23200011.translation: Gaius Anicius Firmus lies here. He lived 51 years.
EDCS-23200011.summary: This is a simple tomb inscription for Gaius Anicius Firmus.

EDCS-23200012.translation: For Bullatia Secunda, dutiful, who lived 35 years. Titus Us Ven lies here for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-23200012.summary: This is a tomb inscription from Titus Us Ven for his dutiful wife, Bullatia Secunda.

EDCS-23200013.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. A memorial tablet for Milcin Mioinedin, a prince from the fort of Tulei, who lived 71 years in the year 220.
EDCS-23200013.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Milcin Mioinedin, a prince from the fort of Tulei, in the year 220.

EDCS-23200014.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. A memorial tablet for Umatsinei Amdieuma, son of Nababo, a prince from the fort of Tulei, who lived 68 years.
EDCS-23200014.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Umatsinei Amdieuma, a prince from the fort of Tulei.

EDCS-23200016.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Marcus Aurelius Vindex, well-deserving veteran, formerly prefect, who lived 44 years.
EDCS-23200016.summary: This funerary inscription is for Marcus Aurelius Vindex, a veteran and former prefect.

EDCS-23200017.translation: Aurelia Honorata, well-deserving, dutiful, lived 33 years and six months.
EDCS-23200017.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Honorata.

EDCS-23200021.translation: For the eternal memory of my wife Petranilla, who lived 27 years, she is laid here.
EDCS-23200021.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating a wife, Petranilla, who died at age 27.

EDCS-23200025.translation: Marius Ianuarius, with his wife Omidia, happily fulfilled a vow to the Holy Divine Power of Victory.
EDCS-23200025.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Victoria by Marius Ianuarius and his wife Omidia.

EDCS-23200029.translation: To Pluto and the Deities Ceres, Dis, and the Holy Ones, Marcus Cornelius Crispinus, having held all honors, made and dedicated this altar with his own funds, which he had promised with the readiest goodwill, along with his wife Cominia Romana and their children, on the Kalends of March.
EDCS-23200029.summary: An altar dedication by Marcus Cornelius Crispinus, honoring chthonic deities, after holding public offices, with his family.

EDCS-23200030.translation: Sacred to Augustus Saturn. Lucius Clodius Campanus, Lucius Clodius Martialis, and Lucius Clodius Campanus, priests.
EDCS-23200030.summary: A dedication to Saturn Augustus by three priests, likely from the Clodius family.

EDCS-23200036.translation: Valeria Victorina fulfilled her vow to Virtue with a good will and mind.
EDCS-23200036.summary: A votive inscription by Valeria Victorina to the personified Virtue.

EDCS-23200051.translation: To the Divine Powers of the Successful, Victorious, Invincible Emperor Augustus.
EDCS-23200051.summary: A dedication to the imperial cult, specifically to Augustus, likely from a soldier or official.

EDCS-23200056.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Struck with terrible grief, snatched from me by the most unjust fortune, I restrained the tears that time brought forth. I have set up this monument and these inscriptions, for a fierce plague took two at once. These boys, this is Clemens and Vincentia. Stolen from us in their bloom, they were carried off. For us, Geminius Clemens, patron of the colony from military service, along with his wife Placentina and sons, made and dedicated this. Clemens lived 6 years, 6 months, 23 days. Vincentia.
EDCS-23200056.summary: A funerary monument set up by Geminius Clemens for his sons Clemens and Vincentia, who died young, likely due to plague.

EDCS-23200058.translation: For Maritas, most secret devotee of chastity and honorable reputation, who lived 30 years and 26 days without fevers. Quintus Pullaenius Donantius, her husband, a Roman knight. For Quintus Pullaenius Donatus, a Roman knight of honorable memory, who lived 58 years and 36 days, to his most dutiful father. A Roman knight.
EDCS-23200058.summary: A funerary inscription for Maritas and her son Quintus Pullaenius Donatus, set up by the husband and father, Quintus Pullaenius Donantius.

EDCS-23200059.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. In memory of Caecilius Rogatus, a veteran, who lived 80 years. Flavia Rustica, his wife, lived 50 years. Caecilius Sanniboi lived 27 years. Rogatus lived 20 years.
EDCS-23200059.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Caecilius Rogatus, his wife Flavia Rustica, and two sons, Caecilius Sanniboi and Rogatus.

EDCS-23200060.translation: Lucius Cassius Restutus, a veteran ex-decurion, and his wife Clodia Luciosa. For their well-deserving, most dutiful parents, Cassius Rogatus and Saturninus. Lucius Cassius Restutus, a veteran ex-decurion, by his will so commanded: 'The children whom I have by my wife Clodia Luciosa, I wish them not to lack... considering her affection... living, a fitting memorial of myself and my well-deserving wife Clodia Luciosa, and also for you, my descendants who will be future decurions, I wish them to perform the annual ceremonies. For these purposes you shall count from the sum of interest... and also on the Nones of August, my birthday, to be provided by the magistrates, for all the accompanying circumcisions of the year, 135 denarii shall be given. On the same day, before the third hour, sportulae shall be given to all my fellow decurions and to the two scribes, 1 denarius each. To my wife Clodia Luciosa, to my nieces Cassia Dulca and Cassia Restuta, for their upkeep, 2 denarii each, and 3 denarii for care, so that she may wipe, anoint, and crown my statue and that of my wife, and light two candles. Likewise on the Ides of January, my wife Clodia Luciosa's birthday, they shall be provided by the magistrates for all the accompanying circumcisions of the year, 135 denarii shall be given. On the same day, before the third hour, before the bases of my statue and that of my wife, sportulae shall be given to all my fellow decurions and to the two scribes, 1 denarius each. To my wife Clodia Luciosa, to my nieces Cassia Dulca and Cassia Restuta, for their upkeep, 2 denarii each, and 3 denarii for care, so that she may wipe, anoint, and crown my statue and that of my wife, and light two candles. I believe, however, that you, my descendants and yours, will gladly maintain this memorial of ours.'
EDCS-23200060.summary: A detailed funerary and endowment inscription by Lucius Cassius Restutus, a veteran ex-decurion, establishing perpetual provisions for the maintenance of his and his wife's memory, including religious ceremonies, distributions, and upkeep of statues.

EDCS-23200061.translation: Lucius Clodius Iustus, a veteran, made and dedicated this to his wife Caelia Processa and his mother Sallustia Ianuaría.
EDCS-23200061.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Clodius Iustus to his wife and mother.

EDCS-23200062.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Arsus set this up for Dassius Albinus, who deserved well of him, of the cohort of selected troops, aged 50.
EDCS-23200062.summary: A funerary inscription for Dassius Albinus, a soldier of the cohort of selected troops, set up by Arsus.

EDCS-23200063.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. In memory of Fulvius Quintulus, a centurion of the cohort of selected troops, who lived 46 years. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Fulvius Quintulus, a centurion of the cohort of selected troops, set this up for his deserving wife Iulia Saturnina, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-23200063.summary: A funerary inscription for Fulvius Quintulus, a centurion of the cohort of selected troops, and his wife Iulia Saturnina, likely set up by Quintulus himself.

EDCS-23200064.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Furfanius Donatus, a soldier of the cohort, lived 30 years, 2 months, 2 days, and 11 hours.
EDCS-23200064.summary: A funerary inscription for Furfanius Donatus, a soldier of the cohort, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-23200065.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Geminius Saturninus, a deputy centurion under a prefect, served for 19 years. While alive, he made this monument for himself and his wife Aufidia Donata, and for his sons Geminius Primulus, Cepio, Sabastena, and Sabastenus.
EDCS-23200065.summary: A funerary monument established during his lifetime by Geminius Saturninus for himself, his wife, and his children, recording his military service.

EDCS-23200066.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. In memory of Iulia Prima, daughter of Marcus Iulius, who lived 45 years, and her son Marcus Corentus Hospes, who lived 20 years. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Iulius, a soldier and deputy centurion of the cohort of selected troops, served for 20 years and lived 60 years. Iulia Prima made and dedicated this to her father and husband.
EDCS-23200066.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Prima and her son Marcus Corentus Hospes, and also for Marcus Iulius, a soldier and deputy centurion, likely set up by Iulia Prima.

EDCS-23200067.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Titus Ioincatius Sabinus, a soldier of the Divitiensis unit in Upper Germany, who served for 12 years. Ioincatius Severus, his brother, made and dedicated this, along with Quintus Victor, the second heir.
EDCS-23200067.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Ioincatius Sabinus, a soldier from Upper Germany, set up by his brother and heir.

EDCS-23200068.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. This monument for Itamon Ituveri, from the province of Upper Germany, of the Melenuensis unit, who served 14 years. The Concibones made and dedicated it.
EDCS-23200068.summary: A funerary inscription for Itamon Ituveri from Upper Germany, set up by the Concibones.

EDCS-23200069.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Sentia Fasta, a most innocent girl, who lived 11 years and 3 months. Caecilia Mallon, her mother, made this. Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caecilia Mallon made and dedicated this for her deserving husband Sentius Nampamon, a veteran, and for her deserving daughter Tullia Dulca.
EDCS-23200069.summary: A funerary monument established by Caecilia Mallon for her daughter Sentia Fasta, her husband Sentius Nampamon, and her daughter Tullia Dulca.

EDCS-23200072.translation: Caius Annius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, from Cittium, former duovir with curule office, also because of the office of duovir, which was conferred upon him with the people's most willing support beyond the legal amount, he made and dedicated a public exedra with a statue and its base with his own money.
EDCS-23200072.summary: A public dedication by Caius Annius of a public exedra with a statue, funded by him beyond the legally required amount as a former duovir.

EDCS-23200076.translation: To Quintus Clodius Victor, dispunctor of the colony, from Vitalis, to his Quintus Clodius Clodianus, patron of the colony.
EDCS-23200076.summary: This inscription is a dedication honoring Quintus Clodius Clodianus, patron and dispunctor of the colony, by Vitalis.

EDCS-23200078.translation: To Cornelia Octavia, a girl of most rare memory. Marcus Cornelius Crispinus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, who has held all honors, and Cominia Romana, pious parents, to their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-23200078.summary: This epitaph commemorates Cornelia Octavia, a young girl, and was erected by her parents, Marcus Cornelius Crispinus and Cominia Romana, who held civic honors.

EDCS-23200079.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fabius Ianuarius, a decurion of Gunugu, made and dedicated this cistern. He lived 52 years.
EDCS-23200079.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a cistern by Fabius Ianuarius, a decurion from Gunugu, who also specifies his lifespan.

EDCS-23200080.translation: In memory of Caelia Pietas, who lived 18 years. In memory of Rutilia Ianuaria, priestess, who lived 45 years. In memory of Romanus, who lived 28 years. Caelius Macrinus made this for his most holy wife and brother.
EDCS-23200080.summary: This monument was erected by Caelius Macrinus to honor his wife (Caelia Pietas), his sister-in-law (Rutilia Ianuaria), and his brother (Romanus), specifying their ages at death.

EDCS-23200081.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Iulius Martialis lived 49 years. Marius Victor, twice duumvir, lived 40 years. Claudius Onoratus, son of Pl..., lived 15 years. Aprilis lived 12 years. His mother made the monument for him.
EDCS-23200081.summary: This inscription lists the ages at death for several individuals (Marcus Iulius Martialis, Marius Victor, Claudius Onoratus, Aprilis), with the final monument erected by a mother for her son Aprilis.

EDCS-23200083.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Pompeius Saturninus, priest. Didia Caecina, his wife, dedicated this. He lived 88 years.
EDCS-23200083.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Didia Caecina to her husband, Pompeius Saturninus, a priest, and records his lifespan.

EDCS-23200084.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Servilius Secundus, priest, lived 85 years.
EDCS-23200084.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Servilius Secundus, a priest, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-23200086.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Aelia Honorata, dearest daughter. Laconius and Fausta, parents, dedicated this. (aged) 11 months, 5 days.
EDCS-23200086.summary: This epitaph, erected by parents Laconius and Fausta, commemorates their daughter Aelia Honorata, who died at 11 months and 5 days old.

EDCS-23200087.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Aelia Monna, sweetest wife. Claudius Florus established this. She lived 27 years, 6 months. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilia Maura, who was also called Mininna, lived 75 years. Aelius Silvanus, Saturninus, and Donatulus, sons, and their son-in-law, made this.
EDCS-23200087.summary: This inscription contains two separate funerary dedications: one by Claudius Florus to his wife Aelia Monna, and another by sons and a son-in-law to Aemilia Maura (Mininna).

EDCS-23200088.translation: By certain devotion, I release this sacred rite to the Shades. I, Novellus, to my mother and son, buried here. Valentina, to you, worthy and sweetest mother, your name thrives here, a clear gift of nature by deserving merit, as a sign from the poem. Felix, behold, the ground covers here two dear names. Extricatus, son, to the lamentations of his grieving grandmother. Aelia Valentina lived 55 years. Lucius Iulius Extricatus lived 12 years.
EDCS-23200088.summary: This complex funerary inscription commemorates Aelia Valentina (mother, 55 years) and Lucius Iulius Extricatus (son, 12 years), erected by Novellus and detailing familial grief and praise.

EDCS-23200089.translation: I repay this sacred offering with certain devotion for the hands [of my departed]. Thus Donatus, for his sweetest daughter Felicia and for his buried children Victoria and Julia, his sweetest daughter, her name lives on in this famous gift of nature on the monument, by the merits of her song, her sign. Behold, Felix alone covers the ground; here lie two dear names. In death, Felicia. VCIT F I.
EDCS-23200089.summary: A epitaph by Donatus for his deceased wife Felicia and children Victoria and Julia, mentioning his daughter Felicia again.

EDCS-23200090.translation: To the departed spirits. Terentius Donatus made and dedicated this tomb for Aelia Ulpia, his dearest wife, and for Terentius Octavianus, his dearest son.
EDCS-23200090.summary: A funerary monument (tomb) made and dedicated by Terentius Donatus for his wife and son.

EDCS-23200091.summary: The name of Anicius Barbarus, likely part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-23200092.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred to the memory of Apronius Piritis, an excellent young man, who lived 30 years and 9 months, and also to Apronius Secundus and Lucanus, his sons, who died at a bitter age. Apronius Nabor made and dedicated this for his most loving brother and sons, for their merits and devotion.
EDCS-23200092.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Apronius Nabor to his deceased brother and sons, honoring their merits and piety.

EDCS-23200100.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred to the memory of Publius Caelius Donatus, who lived 51 years and 8 months. Satria Satura made and dedicated this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-23200100.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Satria Satura to her husband, Publius Caelius Donatus.

EDCS-23200101.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Caelius Ianuarius lived 57 years. Caelia Monna, his daughter, [made this] for her father. To the departed spirits, sacred. Caelius Ianuarius the younger lived 22 years. Caelia Monna, his sister, made [this]. To the departed spirits, sacred. Ulpia Rogatina lived 100 years. Caelia Monna and Donata, daughters, made and dedicated [this].
