EDCS-23200101.summary: A complex epitaph with multiple dedications by Caelia Monna for her father, brother, and sister-in-law, and by Caelia Monna and Donata for Ulpia Rogatina.

EDCS-23200102.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Clodia Luciosa made this memorial for her mother Kalpurnia Luciosa, snatched from life. To the departed spirits, sacred. Clodia Luciosa made [this memorial] for her most worthy father Clodius Cito, snatched from life, who lived 65 years.
EDCS-23200102.summary: A funerary monument by Clodia Luciosa for her mother Kalpurnia Luciosa and her father Clodius Cito, both 'snatched from life'.

EDCS-23200103.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Caelius Rogatus lived 65 years. Aelia Hilara made and dedicated this for her dearest husband. Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Sucinus lived 75 years. Aelia Hilara made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23200103.summary: This epitaph records the lives and deaths of Gaius Caelius Rogatus and Valerius Sucinus, both commemorated by Aelia Hilara, likely his wife, who lived to a considerable age.

EDCS-23200104.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Kanni Victoris, who lived 45 years and 5 months. Vettia Domitia made and dedicated this for her husband.
EDCS-23200104.summary: This epitaph commemorates Kanni Victoris, who died at the age of 45 years and 5 months, and was set up by his wife, Vettia Domitia.

EDCS-23200105.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits for Claudia Satta, most dutiful. She lived 28 years.
EDCS-23200105.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Claudia Satta, who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-23200106.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Saturnina, daughter of Apollonius of Caesariensis, lived 35 years. May the earth lie lightly upon her.
EDCS-23200106.summary: This epitaph records the death of Claudia Saturnina, daughter of Apollonius of Caesariensis, at the age of 35.

EDCS-23200107.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodia Marsia lived 9 years. Caecilia Honorata made and dedicated this. Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodius Messor lived 70 years and 5 months. Caecilia Honorata made this for her husband.
EDCS-23200107.summary: This epitaph records the deaths of Clodia Marsia (aged 9) and her husband Clodius Messor (aged 70 years and 5 months), both commemorated by Caecilia Honorata.

EDCS-23200109.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Donatus lived 2 years and 8 months. Claudia Cornelia lived 3 years and 2 months. Cornelius Marcus lived 3 years and 2 months. Cornelius Placentinus made and dedicated this for his sons.
EDCS-23200109.summary: This epitaph commemorates the young deaths of Cornelius Donatus (2 years, 8 months), Claudia Cornelia (3 years, 2 months), and Cornelius Marcus (3 years, 2 months), set up by Cornelius Placentinus, likely their father.

EDCS-23200112.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Aucta, dutiful, lived 27 years. Flavius Rogatus.
EDCS-23200112.summary: This inscription records the death of Cornelia Aucta at the age of 27, with Flavius Rogatus as the dedicator, likely her husband or relative.

EDCS-23200113.translation: This is what you wished for, Donate, to lie here secure after the death of your loved ones; fate willed that you lie here. Gududius and Tullius, the managers, made and dedicated this. He lived 42 years.
EDCS-23200113.summary: This epitaph, likely for Donate, expresses a desire for peaceful repose after death, and was made by Gududius and Tullius, possibly freedmen or estate managers.

EDCS-23200114.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Flavius Namfamo, who lived 70 years and 10 months. His son Donatus made this. In memory of Herennia Siddina, who lived 120 years, 5 months, and 25 days. Her children Donatus and Ianuaria made this. Gargilius Felicus senior lived 60 years and 5 months. His son Siddinus made this.
EDCS-23200114.summary: This epitaph records the long lives of Flavius Namfamo (70 years, 10 months), Herennia Siddina (120 years, 5 months, 25 days), and Gargilius Felicus senior (60 years, 5 months), commemorated by their respective children.

EDCS-23200115.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Flavia Tertia and Flavius Quietus and Flavius Getulus, their young children who have died. Flavius Februarius, their father, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23200115.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates the young children Flavia Tertia, Flavius Quietus, and Flavius Getulus, and was dedicated by their father, Flavius Februarius.

EDCS-23200116.translation: Publius Aelius Calvus made and dedicated this to Fulvius Optatus and Aurelia Venusta and Fulvia Veneria, most remarkable women.
EDCS-23200116.summary: This is a dedication made by Publius Aelius Calvus to three women, likely family members, possibly on a tomb or a public monument.

EDCS-23200118.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Gargilius Marinus, Gargilia, Quintus Gargilius.
EDCS-23200118.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (spirits of the departed), listing several names, likely family members.

EDCS-23200120.translation: Brother Julianus made and dedicated this. Sextus Iulius Fortunatus lived for 10 years.
EDCS-23200120.summary: This is a funerary inscription where a brother, Julianus, dedicated a monument to Sextus Iulius Fortunatus, who lived for 10 years.

EDCS-23200121.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius ... lived for 15 years, 11 days. [Son?] ... made this.
EDCS-23200121.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Iulius and indicating that a son made the monument.

EDCS-23200125.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Fortunatus. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Iulius Fortunnatus lived for... Saturnius, a slave, lived for... Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Ianuarius lived for 60 years. Isteb Aecyptica lived for 40 years. He/She dedicated this to the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-23200125.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription listing multiple individuals and their lifespans, with at least one person dedicating the monument to the Manes.

EDCS-23200126.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Furnia Pusinna placed and dedicated this cupola to Iulius Hospes, her dearest husband, who lived for 45 years.
EDCS-23200126.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Furnia Pusinna dedicated a cupola (likely a tomb structure) to her husband Iulius Hospes.

EDCS-23200127.translation: Caius Iulius Milimon lived for 62 years. Iulia Iuliosa to her dearest husband, and Caius Iulius Iulianus to his father.
EDCS-23200127.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Caius Iulius Milimon and includes dedications from his wife Iulia Iuliosa and son Caius Iulius Iulianus.

EDCS-23200128.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Nampamo lived for 55 years.
EDCS-23200128.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the lifespan of Caius Iulius Nampamo.

EDCS-23200130.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Datina made this for her most beloved spouse, Sextus Iulius Saturninus, who lived for about 60 years.
EDCS-23200130.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Valeria Datina dedicated a monument to her husband Sextus Iulius Saturninus.

EDCS-23200131.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Saturninus, also called Cr[a]ssus, who lived for 47 years. Paccia Firmina made and dedicated this to her dearest husband, through your friends.
EDCS-23200131.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Paccia Firmina, through friends, dedicated a monument to her husband Iulius Saturninus (also known as Crassus).

EDCS-23200132.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Gaius Julius Saturninus, dearest father; his sons Gaius Julius Gitteus and Rogatianus established this tomb. He lived 107 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. To Aelia Matrona, most devoted mother; her sons Gaius Julius Gitteus and Julius Rogatianus made this statue. She lived 75 years.
EDCS-23200132.summary: This funerary monument commemorates Gaius Julius Saturninus and Aelia Matrona, with their sons acting as dedicators.

EDCS-23200133.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Victor lived 81 years. Julius Marius, his devoted son. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Saturnina lived 60 years. Julius Marius, her devoted son.
EDCS-23200133.summary: This inscription records the ages of Julius Victor and Aurelia Saturnina, with their son Julius Marius as the dedicant.

EDCS-23200134.translation: Julius Victor lived thirty years. Julius Donatus lived twenty-seven years.
EDCS-23200134.summary: This inscription records the lifespans of Julius Victor and Julius Donatus, likely on a shared monument.

EDCS-23200136.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Curvia lived 70 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-23200136.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Curvia, likely placed by a family member, asking for peaceful rest.

EDCS-23200137.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Iulia Gemina, who lived 75 years. In memory of Pullaena Mima, who lived 82 years. Julius Rogatianus made and dedicated this to his parents-in-law.
EDCS-23200137.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Julius Rogatianus to his parents-in-law, Iulia Gemina and Pullaena Mima.

EDCS-23200138.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Iulia Hospita, who lived 80 years. Feli made [this]. Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of most beloved father Caius Coelius Rogatus, who lived 85 years.
EDCS-23200138.summary: This inscription records the deaths of Iulia Hospita and Caius Coelius Rogatus, with dedications from Feli and an unnamed individual respectively.

EDCS-23200139.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Iulia Romana, who lived 72 years. Her son Iulius Cittinus dedicated [this]. Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Marcus Iulius Demetrius, who lived 45 years. Iulia Romana made [this] for her husband.
EDCS-23200139.summary: This inscription memorializes Iulia Romana, with a dedication from her son, and also commemorates her husband Marcus Iulius Demetrius, dedicated by her.

EDCS-23200140.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Iulia Sinta, his dearest virgin daughter, who lived 18 years. Her father Iulius Fortunatus, with his wife Occia Prima, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23200140.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Iulius Fortunatus and Occia Prima to their daughter Iulia Sinta, a virgin.

EDCS-23200142.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Liburnius Felicianus, who lived 50 years and 6 months. Liburnius Victorinus made [this] for his father. Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Iulia Victoria, who lived 45 years and 5 months. Liburnius Victorinus made [this] for his mother.
EDCS-23200142.summary: This inscription records the deaths of Liburnius Felicianus and Iulia Victoria, with Liburnius Victorinus dedicating monuments to both his parents.

EDCS-23200143.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Liburnius Felicianus the younger lived 50 years and 4 months. Liburnius Victorinus made [this] for his most devoted brother, and dedicated it willingly.
EDCS-23200143.summary: A funerary inscription by Liburnius Victorinus dedicating a monument to his brother, Liburnius Felicianus the younger.

EDCS-23200144.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Liburnius Victorinus lived 72 years. Liburnius Castus [made this] for his most worthy father, with good wishes.
EDCS-23200144.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Liburnius Victorinus and made by his son, Liburnius Castus.

EDCS-23200145.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Licinius Gallonius lived 35 years. Iulia Matrona, his mother, made this.
EDCS-23200145.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Licinius Gallonius and made by his mother, Iulia Matrona.

EDCS-23200146.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. In memory of Lucius Lucceius Lucianus, who lived 81 years. Lucius Lucceius Secundus, freedman of the emperor, made and dedicated this for his father.
EDCS-23200146.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Lucceius Lucianus and made by his son, Lucius Lucceius Secundus.

EDCS-23200147.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucilia Donata lived 40 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelius Zodorus lived 60 years.
EDCS-23200147.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of two individuals, Lucilia Donata and Aelius Zodorus.

EDCS-23200148.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Luciosa Pia lived 28 years.
EDCS-23200148.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Luciosa Pia.

EDCS-23200149.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Luria Rogata lived 29 years and 8 months. For the merits of her sons, Iulius Kapito made this for his wife.
EDCS-23200149.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Luria Rogata and made by her husband, Iulius Kapito, in recognition of their sons.

EDCS-23200150.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Magnia Ancilla and Geminius Nabor. Sertorius and Ianuaria, their children, made this. Here lies a young man, first in the flower of youth, fearless in war, as he went cautiously against the enemy, the envious will of the Fates always thus inclined, oppressed you, whom the mother deploys to the sepulchre, pitifully how she buries you in the earth. Geminius Victor made and dedicated this for his dearest brother, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-23200150.summary: A complex funerary epitaph for Geminius Nabor and his brother Geminius Victor, detailing their ages and recording sentiments about the boy's death in battle and the grief of their mother.

EDCS-23200151.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Manlius Barbarus. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sosius Sandarlus lived 12 years. Here lies Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Arissa Rufina.
EDCS-23200151.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of Sosius Sandarlus and mentioning Manlius Barbarus and Arissa Rufina.

EDCS-23200152.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Manilius Crescens, of good memory, lived 70 years. Manilius Calvus made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23200152.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Manilius Crescens and made by Manilius Calvus.

EDCS-23200153.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Modia Privata lived 80 years. Rogat made this.
EDCS-23200153.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Modia Privata and made by Rogat.

EDCS-23200154.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Namephamo lived one year and is buried here.
EDCS-23200154.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life and burial of Namephamo.

EDCS-23200155.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For the memory of Marcus Noleius Florentinus, his wife Iulia Supera lived 55 years.
EDCS-23200155.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Marcus Noleius Florentinus and records the lifespan of his wife, Iulia Supera.

EDCS-23200156.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Octavius Aelianus lived 20 years. Lucius Octavius Secundus made this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-23200156.summary: This tombstone records the death of Octavius Aelianus at age 20 and was dedicated by his father, Lucius Octavius Secundus.

EDCS-23200157.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Petronia Augurina lived 3 years. Annia Macia lived 36 years. Petronius Datus, husband, made and dedicated this, and Petronius Vitalis lived 11 years.
EDCS-23200157.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Petronia Augurina (age 3), Annia Macia (age 36), and Petronius Vitalis (age 11), with Petronius Datus identified as the husband and maker.

EDCS-23200158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For the memory and the snatched light of Plautia Donata, a good woman, who lived 38 years, 7 months, and 7 days. Antonius Saturninus made this for his sweetest wife, for her merits.
EDCS-23200158.summary: This funerary inscription honors Plautia Donata, a good woman, detailing her age at death and dedicated by her husband, Antonius Saturninus.

EDCS-23200159.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Plautia Monica lived 12 years. Her mother Aelia Aeliosa made and dedicated this.
EDCS-23200159.summary: This tombstone records the death of Plautia Monica at age 12 and was made by her mother, Aelia Aeliosa.

EDCS-23200160.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Plotius Pequarius lived 61 years. Mustela made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-23200160.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Plotius Pequarius at age 61 and was made by his wife, Mustela.

EDCS-23200161.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pompeius Blandus lived 60 years. Pomponius Placidus made this for his dearest brother. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pomponius Nasseus lived 14 years, 11 months. Antonia Beata for her sweetest son.
EDCS-23200161.summary: This inscription comprises two separate funerary notices: one for Pompeius Blandus (age 60), dedicated by his brother Pomponius Placidus, and another for Pomponius Nasseus (age 14), dedicated by his mother Antonia Beata.

EDCS-23200162.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For the good memory of Pullaenia Minucia, most dutiful matron and wife. Her husband Iulius Rusticus made and dedicated this, who lived 55 years.
EDCS-23200162.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Pullaenia Minucia, a dutiful matron and wife, by her husband Iulius Rusticus, and records her age at death.

EDCS-23200163.translation: Redducta lived 75 years. Quadratus made this for his wife.
EDCS-23200163.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Redducta at age 75 and was made by her husband, Quadratus.

EDCS-23200164.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Romana lived 95 years. Her sisters made this for their dearest mother. Sacred to the departed spirits. Seius Titus lived 70 years. He made this for his dearest husband.
EDCS-23200164.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Romana, who lived 95 years, and her husband, Seius Titus, who lived 70 years, with dedications from her sisters and his wife respectively.

EDCS-23200165.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rubria Kalpurnia lived 65 years. Caecilius Caecilianus and Rubrianus made this for their most devoted mother.
EDCS-23200165.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Rubria Kalpurnia at age 65, with a dedication from her sons.

EDCS-23200166.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ave Secundus, dearest to your parents, flower of youth, killed by barbarians at age 50. Mucia [made this] for her husband.
EDCS-23200166.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Ave Secundus at age 50, killed by barbarians, with a dedication from his wife Mucia.

EDCS-23200168.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Decimus Servilius Calvus.

EDCS-23200169.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Servilius Felix lived 25 years. Lucilla Lillosa made this for her dearest son and dedicated it. Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Romana.
EDCS-23200169.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Servilius Felix at age 25, with a dedication from his mother Lucilla Lillosa, and includes a separate inscription for Iulia Romana.

EDCS-23200171.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Tannonius Donatus who lived 60 years. Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Flavia Quarta who lived 50 years.
EDCS-23200171.summary: This epitaph commemorates two individuals, Tannonius Donatus (died at 60) and Flavia Quarta (died at 50), likely a couple.

EDCS-23200172.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Oh, my Tertulus, flower of youth, taken away at age 20. Aurelius Felix and Iulia Emerita made this for their dearest son.
EDCS-23200172.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Tertulus at age 20, with a dedication from his parents Aurelius Felix and Iulia Emerita.

EDCS-23200173.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Minucianus lived 44 years. His wife Sextia Lucilla made this.
EDCS-23200173.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Lucius Valerius Minucianus at age 44, with a dedication from his wife Sextia Lucilla.

EDCS-23200174.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Romana made this for her dearest son Sutorias Emeritus, and dedicated it. He lived 22 years and 6 months.
EDCS-23200174.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Sutorias Emeritas at age 22 years and 6 months, with a dedication from his mother Valeria Romana.

EDCS-23200175.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Dom[a] Ven [lived] 16 years.
EDCS-23200175.summary: This fragmentary funerary epitaph records the death of a person named Doma Ven at age 16.

EDCS-23200176.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, in memory of Caius Vibius Saturninus, a man of good memory, who lived 105 years.
EDCS-23200176.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Vibius Saturninus, who lived to be 105 years old.

EDCS-23200177.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, for Vibius Ianuaria, most loving. Cornelius Terentius made this. She lived 19 years.
EDCS-23200177.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibius Ianuaria, set up by Cornelius Terentius, who died at 19.

EDCS-23200178.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ulpius Vitalis lived XLI years.
EDCS-23200178.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpius Vitalis, who lived 41 years.

EDCS-23200179.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, for Ulpius Puteolanus, sweet pupil. He lived 6 years, 5 months, 11 days. Ulpia Puteolana, a deserving matron, made this.
EDCS-23200179.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Ulpius Puteolanus at the age of 6 years, 5 months, and 11 days, dedicated by Ulpia Puteolana.

EDCS-23200180.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, for Ulpia Hilaritas, most holy and sweet pupil. She lived 9 years, 4 months, 20 days. Ulpia Puteolana, deserving, made this.
EDCS-23200180.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Ulpia Hilaritas at the age of 9 years, 4 months, and 20 days, dedicated by Ulpia Puteolana.

EDCS-23200181.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, for Ulpia Romana, pious and most faithful wife. She lived 40 years. Marcus Geminius Romanus, her husband, made this.
EDCS-23200181.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpia Romana, a pious and faithful wife who lived 40 years, erected by her husband Marcus Geminius Romanus.

EDCS-23200184.translation: ..., lived 45 years. Made and dedicated by Victor, her most sweet husband.
EDCS-23200184.summary: This fragment of a funerary epitaph records a woman who lived 45 years, dedicated by her husband Victor.

EDCS-23200186.translation: at the highest hour of the first year, 8 months, and [unknown] days, born in the afternoon hours, as if he was 2 years old, was born there. D.B. D. pia lived 25 years, 4 months.
EDCS-23200186.summary: This inscription appears to record the birth and death details of two individuals, one who lived 25 years and 4 months and another whose age is partially specified.

EDCS-23200188.translation: Sacred to the holy god Saturn. Lucius Gargilius Crescens, the priest, together with his children, fulfilled his vow and dedicated this with a willing spirit.
EDCS-23200188.summary: This inscription records Lucius Gargilius Crescens, a priest, fulfilling a vow to the god Saturn along with his children.

EDCS-23200189.translation: In memory of Iulius Numerianus, decurion of the ala, most rare brother, most innocent name, who lived 43 years and 9 days, and of Omidia Extricata, dearest mother, and of Caelis Honorata, matron, of the same brother, and of Castus, his kinsman, a young student. Iulius Rusticus, a beneficiarius, made and dedicated this for his most worthy parents, above mentioned: for the good, well; and for the bad, well.
EDCS-23200189.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Iulius Rusticus to his brother Iulius Numerianus, his mother Omidia Extricata, his sister-in-law Caelis Honorata, and his kinsman Castus, with an unusual closing sentiment.

EDCS-23200191.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Domitius Secundus lived for 37 years. Lucius Domitius Faustus made this for his younger brother.
EDCS-23200191.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Domitius Secundus, erected by his brother Lucius Domitius Faustus.

EDCS-23200192.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavius Primus lived for 60 years.
EDCS-23200192.summary: This tombstone records the death of Flavius Primus, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-23200193.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Ianuarius lived for around 55 years.
EDCS-23200193.summary: This tombstone records the death of Iulius Ianuarius, who lived to be approximately 55 years old.

EDCS-23200194.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. In memory of Iulia Cittina, who lived for 9 years, 9 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-23200194.summary: This tombstone records the death of Iulia Cittina, who died at the age of 9 years, 9 months, and 15 days.

EDCS-23200195.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Cittina lived for 73 years. Exuper made this. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Granius Victor lived for 85 years. His heir and son made this.
EDCS-23200195.summary: This inscription records the deaths of Iulia Cittina, who lived to 73, and Granius Victor, who lived to 85, with Exuper and Granius Victor's son as the dedicator, respectively.

EDCS-23200196.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Misimon made this mausoleum for her son Iulius Umbrio Optatus, who lived for 44 years.
EDCS-23200196.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription where Iulia Misimon dedicated a mausoleum to her son Iulius Umbrio Optatus.

EDCS-23200197.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iuventius Citteus lived for 40 years and 3 months.
EDCS-23200197.summary: This tombstone records the death of Iuventius Citteus, who lived to be 40 years and 3 months old.

EDCS-23200198.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Rogato lived for 5 years.
EDCS-23200198.summary: This tombstone records the death of Rogato, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-23200199.translation: With certain piety, we dedicate this to the departed spirits, to our brothers Lucio Longaenio Victor and our father. Buried here, your memory lives on clearly in this inscription, worthy of you.
EDCS-23200199.summary: This inscription is a dedication by brothers to their father, Lucio Longaenio Victor, emphasizing his enduring memory and worthiness.

EDCS-23200200.translation: Obius Cal... son of Ianua, for his father.
EDCS-23200200.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a dedication by a son named Obius Cal... to his father.

EDCS-23200203.translation: G. P. A. S. Catellius Marinus willingly paid his vow and dedicated this also.
EDCS-23200203.summary: This is a dedication by Catellius Marinus, likely to a deity or for a public work, possibly related to a military context given the 'V' which might stand for 'virens' or a similar honorific.

EDCS-23200204.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Modius Spanus, a private individual.

EDCS-23200205.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Abillahas Rummei, a soldier of the II Cohort of Sardinians, lived for 55 years. Sextia Prima, his devoted wife, made this for her husband.
EDCS-23200205.summary: This is a tombstone for Abillahas Rummei, a soldier in the II Cohort of Sardinians, erected by his wife Sextia Prima.

EDCS-23200206.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aemilius Pudens, a veteran, served for 26 years and lived for 62 years. His wife Ata Namgedde made this monument for him in the year of the province.
EDCS-23200206.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Aemilius Pudens, a veteran, erected by his wife Ata Namgedde, dated to a provincial year.

EDCS-23200207.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Datus Felicis, a soldier of the II Cohort of Sardinians, served for 15 years and lived for 40 years. Donatus, his son, made this.
EDCS-23200207.summary: This is a tombstone for Datus Felicis, a soldier in the II Cohort of Sardinians, erected by his son Donatus.

EDCS-23200208.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Donatus Silvani, a veteran, lived for 47 years. Iulia Felicula, his devoted wife, made this for him, well-deserving.
EDCS-23200208.summary: This is a tombstone for Donatus Silvani, a veteran, erected by his devoted wife Iulia Felicula.

EDCS-23200209.translation: Favonius Donatus, a soldier of the II Cohort of Sardinians, lived for 30 years. His heir Tertula, devoted, made this.
EDCS-23200209.summary: This is a tombstone for Favonius Donatus, a soldier in the II Cohort of Sardinians, erected by his heir Tertula.

EDCS-23200210.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Licinius Licinianus, an equestrian of the Thracian Wing, lived for 20 years and served for 4 years. His father Licinius Frugi, devoted, made this.
EDCS-23200210.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Licinius Licinianus, an equestrian, erected by his father Licinius Frugi.

EDCS-23200211.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lollius Fronto, a sesquiplicarius, lived for 60 years. Latinia Fortunata, his wife, made this for his merits.
EDCS-23200211.summary: This is a tombstone for Lollius Fronto, a sesquiplicarius, erected by his wife Latinia Fortunata.

EDCS-23200212.translation: To the spirits of the departed. P. Quintillus, a veteran, lived for 70 years. His wife made this.
EDCS-23200212.summary: This is a tombstone for P. Quintillus, a veteran, erected by his wife.

EDCS-23200213.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Saturninus served as a soldier for 15 years, lived for 35 years, and is buried here.
EDCS-23200213.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Saturninus, a soldier who served for 15 years and died at age 35.

EDCS-23200214.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. A soldier of the first cohort lived for 30 years.
EDCS-23200214.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for an unnamed soldier of the first cohort who died at age 30.

EDCS-23200215.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Acceptus lived for 35 years, Gaetulicus lived for 6 years; they are buried here.
EDCS-23200215.summary: This tombstone records the deaths of Acceptus (aged 35) and Gaetulicus (aged 6).

EDCS-23200216.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annio Nam lived for 10 years. Annius made it.
EDCS-23200216.summary: This is a tombstone for Annio Nam, who died at age 10, made by Annius.

EDCS-23200217.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annius Quintio erected this. Clodius Satu lived for 1 year.
EDCS-23200217.summary: This tombstone was erected by Annius Quintio and records the death of Clodius Satu, who lived for 1 year.

EDCS-23200218.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annia Saturnina lived for 30 years.
EDCS-23200218.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Annia Saturnina, who died at age 30.

EDCS-23200219.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Venutus lived for 10 years. [The rest is illegible].
EDCS-23200219.summary: This tombstone records that Aurelius Venutus lived for 10 years, with the remainder of the inscription illegible.

EDCS-23200220.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabia Quintula lived for 60 years and 5 days. She placed this memorial to the spirits of the departed for her dearest daughter Fabiae Victorinae, who lived for 19 years.
EDCS-23200220.summary: This tombstone was placed by Fabia Quintula for her daughter Fabiae Victorinae, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-23200221.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Satura lived for 75 years and 9 months. Flavius Rufinus made this for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-23200221.summary: This tombstone was erected by Flavius Rufinus for his mother Flavia Satura, recording her age at death.

EDCS-23200222.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Rogat lived for 70 years. Iulius Felix S. [added the memorial].
EDCS-23200222.summary: This tombstone records the death of Iulia Rogat at age 70 and was erected by Iulius Felix.

EDCS-23200223.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Marcius Saturninus lived 6 years and 3 months. Marcia lived 7 years.
EDCS-23200223.summary: A funerary inscription recording the ages of two individuals, possibly siblings or children, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-23200224.summary: A brief funerary inscription recording the age of Publius Storicusius.

EDCS-23200225.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Alpennius Felix lived 70 years. Iulia Octavia made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-23200225.summary: A funerary monument set up by Iulia Octavia for her husband, Publius Alpennius Felix, who lived to be 70.

EDCS-23200226.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Praecilius Victor lived 27 years. Praecilius Saturninus, his brother, made this.
EDCS-23200226.summary: A funerary inscription where Praecilius Saturninus set up a monument for his brother, Quintus Praecilius Victor.

EDCS-23200228.summary: A funerary inscription stating the remarkable age of Satura, who lived to be 100 years and 8 months.

EDCS-23200229.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Septimia Tuta lived 21 years. Clodius Felix, her husband, made this for her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-23200229.summary: A funerary monument erected by Clodius Felix for his wife, Septimia Tuta, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-23400841.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ulpius Is. Iucunda, his wife, and Ulpius Hospes and Ius Petronia Saturnina, his wife, made this monument.
EDCS-23400841.summary: A funerary monument jointly made by family members, including Ulpius Is, his wife Iucunda, Ulpius Hospes, and his wife Ius Petronia Saturnina.

EDCS-23400842.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lavia Gemnaria lived 75 years.
EDCS-23400842.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Lavia Gemnaria, who lived to be 75.

EDCS-23400843.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Mulosim.
EDCS-23400843.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to an individual named Mulosim.

EDCS-23400844.summary: A fragmented inscription likely mentioning two sisters named Ignisoa and possibly another individual.

EDCS-23400848.summary: This is a fragment likely referring to an emperor, possibly part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-23400850.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Acilius Victorinus lived 30 years, 2 months, and 13 days. V. P. E. V.
EDCS-23400850.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age at death of Acilius Victorinus and a ritualistic formula.

EDCS-23400857.translation: To Publius Herennius, son of Publius Herennius Masculus, decurion of the Thracian cavalry wing. He lived one year and 64 days. These two slaves also died together for their master.
EDCS-23400857.summary: A funerary inscription for a young boy, the son of a decurion, and two slaves.

EDCS-23400859.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Seranus, son of Aurelius Tertius, lived 73 years and 10 months. They (the inscription) made it three times.
EDCS-23400859.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age at death of Aurelius Seranus, with a note about the inscription's creation.

EDCS-23400860.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Fabius Ingenuus lived 13 years. Octavia Quarta made this for her deserving son. He is buried here.
EDCS-23400860.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Fabius Ingenuus, set up by his mother Octavia Quarta.

EDCS-23400861.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Marcus Herennius Cassianus, for his third [term/period] in the province of Midiaecia.
EDCS-23400861.summary: A dedication possibly honoring Marcus Herennius Cassianus for his service in the province of Midiaecia.

EDCS-23400862.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vipsindir is buried here. He lived 60 years and 1 month. For [his] wife.
EDCS-23400862.summary: A funerary inscription for Vipsindir, set up by his wife.

EDCS-23500003.translation: To Alfidius Sabinus, quaestor, tribune of the plebs, legate of the proconsul of Sicily, praetorian legate of Meanius Lepidus, proconsul of Asia, patron, [from] the people of Rusgunium.
EDCS-23500003.summary: A dedication by the people of Rusgunium to Alfidius Sabinus, honoring his various political and administrative offices.

EDCS-23500004.translation: In memory of Flavius Ziperis, tribune of the first cohort of the Felicem Iustinianorum, who was laid to rest in peace while holding the tribunate in Rusgunium for 12 years.
EDCS-23500004.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Flavius Ziperis, who served as a tribune for 12 years in Rusgunium.

EDCS-23500005.translation: To Licinius Donatus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, decurion of the fatherland of Rusgunium, who was chosen for the province of Baetica to defend fiscal cases in the early years of his youth by the benefit of studies, and then promoted for his merits and actions to defend the people in the area of Saturn, and [promoted] to the sacred city [Rome] by Valeria Victorina of the province.
EDCS-23500005.summary: A detailed career inscription for Licinius Donatus, a decurion from Rusgunium, who held various administrative and legal positions.

EDCS-23500006.translation: To Lucius Tadius Rogatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, a decurion, aedile, and twice chief magistrate for five years, the people of Rusguniae and those residing there, with contributed funds, [honored him] for his merits because he brought grain and did not allow the price of grain to increase.
EDCS-23500006.summary: This is a public honorific inscription from Rusguniae, dating to the Roman imperial period, honoring a local magistrate for ensuring a stable grain supply.

EDCS-23500008.translation: To the departed spirits. Ausenia Oilla lived for 6 years and 1 month. Ausenius Ingenuus [dedicated this] to his most devoted daughter.
EDCS-23500008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the Roman imperial period, recording the short life of a child and dedicated by her father.

EDCS-23500009.translation: To the memory of Memmius Secundus, her good father, and Petelius Claudianus, her husband, and her sons, Memmia Secunda, his wife, built and dedicated this.
EDCS-23500009.summary: This funerary inscription from the Roman imperial period records a woman dedicating a tomb to her father, husband, and sons.

EDCS-23500010.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely from the Roman imperial period, indicating the maker of an object or structure.

EDCS-23500011.translation: Flavius Nuvel, son of Saturninus, a most accomplished man, one of the counts, and [son] of Colicia, a most honorable woman, and great-grandson of Elurius Laconi, dedicated this basilica, promised and offered by vow, with his wife Nonnica, [supported] by all his own people.
EDCS-23500011.summary: This is a dedication inscription from the late Roman period, recording Flavius Nuvel dedicating a basilica based on a vow, with familial ties mentioned.

EDCS-23500012.translation: Aphrodisius willingly paid his vow to Mithras.
EDCS-23500012.summary: This is a votive inscription from the Roman imperial period, recording a devotee fulfilling a vow to the god Mithras.

EDCS-23500013.translation: Lucius Caecilius Rufus Agilis, son [of the king], dedicated and had this made from his own funds, having completed all the highest honors of his father, King Juba.
EDCS-23500013.summary: This is an honorific inscription from the Roman imperial period, recording Lucius Caecilius Rufus Agilis dedicating a monument in honor of his father, King Juba.

EDCS-23500015.translation: To Publius Sittius Plocamianus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe. The council of Icosium [honored him]. Marcus Sittius Caecilianus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, waived the expenses for his most devoted son, having received the honor.
EDCS-23500015.summary: This is an honorific inscription from the Roman imperial period, detailing the council of Icosium honoring Publius Sittius Plocamianus, with his father waiving costs.

EDCS-23500016.translation: Calpurnius Martialis, son of Imilis, is buried here.
EDCS-23500016.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph from the Roman imperial period, indicating the burial place of Calpurnius Martialis.

EDCS-23500017.translation: Lucius Ennius Paullus, son of Caius, lived for 4 months and 4 days.
EDCS-23500017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the Roman imperial period, recording the very short life of a child.

EDCS-23500019.translation: To Julia Celsa, daughter of Lucius, Marcus Fadius Celer dedicated this to his excellent wife.
EDCS-23500019.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his wife, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23500020.translation: Quadratilla, daughter of Victor, lived for four years and is buried here; her heir [arranged this].
EDCS-23500020.summary: This is a short funerary epitaph for a young girl named Quadratilla, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23500026.translation: Peace to you entering this doorway, peace to you returning. Sabina, pleasing to our lord, rendered her vow to the martyr Lawrence the blessed, upon the completion of the building, on the 14th day before the Kalends of July. Here is the memorial of her son Ianuarius, who lived 47 years and 5 months. He rests in peace in the 6th year of the province. He requested to see Paradise of Light.
EDCS-23500026.summary: This is a dedication inscription for a building, likely a church or chapel, dedicated to Saint Lawrence by Sabina for herself and her son Ianuarius, dating to the provincial era 410 (which would place it in the 5th or 6th century CE).

EDCS-23500027.translation: May you fare well. I wish for you to dine well at Clundius'.
EDCS-23500027.summary: This is a short, informal inscription, possibly a graffiti or a casual note, expressing good wishes for a meal, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23500028.translation: In memory of Germanius and Donata, who labored well.
EDCS-23500028.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Germanius and Donata, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23500031.translation: Anibas, son of Salacs, I have [received] from my property a pregnant ass, which is present there.
EDCS-23500031.summary: This is a legal or administrative document, possibly related to a property transaction or inheritance, likely from a Roman province with local influences.

EDCS-23500032.translation: To the Divine Shades of the Venantii Basiliorum.
EDCS-23500032.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed members of the Venantii Basiliorum family, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23500033.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the age of Iulius Lucianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23500034.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sextus Summus Scipionis, son of Marcus, and his wife Cassia. He was a princeps, aged 80 years.
EDCS-23500034.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Summus Scipionis and his wife Cassia, indicating his status as a 'princeps', likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23500035.translation: Lucius [---] placed this altar for the safety of the Emperor [---].
EDCS-23500035.summary: This is a dedication inscription for an altar, set up by Lucius for the well-being of the Emperor, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23500036.translation: With our lords who now flourish, our Augustus Constantius, and our Caesar Constantius, the council, carrying out all commands, with the help of divine power, rebuilt the new city walls with the people and the entire state.
EDCS-23500036.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of city walls by the council and people, dedicated to Constantius Augustus and Constantius Caesar, likely in the 4th century CE.

EDCS-23500037.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits of Luria Repentina. Felix, her husband.
EDCS-23500037.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Felix to his wife, Luria Repentina.

EDCS-23500038.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmentary dedication or funerary text mentioning individuals named Longi, Arimus, and Casianus.

EDCS-23500040.translation: Often proven through many exiles and found worthy defender of the Catholic faith, he completed 18 years, 2 months, and 12 days in the episcopate and was killed in the war of the Moors and was buried on the 6th day before the Ides of May in the 346th year (of the province).
EDCS-23500040.summary: This inscription records the episcopate and death in battle of an unnamed bishop, who served for over 18 years and died in a war against the Moors in the 4th century CE.

EDCS-23500041.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription mentioning a bishop named Taciis.

EDCS-23500073.translation: F. Luktesia, DONE, daughter of CF.
EDCS-23500073.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary dedication, mentioning Luktesia and her father or lineage.

EDCS-23500074.translation: ...sius / ...enus Ae...milianus / on the occasion of / his aedileship / in the 170th year (of the province).
EDCS-23500074.summary: This inscription records a dedication for the honorable aedileship of Ae...milianus in the 170th year of the province, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23500075.translation: Marcianus and his brothers established and completed this temple of Aesculapius, which Labe, son of Marc, accepted as a site from the most distinguished council of Caesariensis, with its pronaos, pool, trees, marble, statues, and all decoration, with columns, with Publius Aelius Peregrinus dedicating it for IV.
EDCS-23500075.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of a temple to Aesculapius by Marcianus and his brothers, funded and accepted from the council of Caesariensis, and dedicated by Publius Aelius Peregrinus.

EDCS-23500076.translation: To Ceres Augusta. Amatia Africana. Who Canisius.
EDCS-23500076.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Ceres Augusta by Amatia Africana, with a possible mention of Canisius.

EDCS-23500078.translation: To the good god. Marcus Allecinus Athictus gave willingly from his own resources.
EDCS-23500078.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a deity by Marcus Allecinus Athictus, who gave voluntarily.

EDCS-23500079.translation: Aurelius Litua, a most distinguished man, governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, willingly dedicated this offering, giving thanks to Jupiter Best and Greatest and all the other Immortal Gods because, after the barbarians across the Tagus had been completely destroyed and a second successful plunder achieved, he returned safe and sound with all his soldiers under the rule of our Lords Diocletian and Maximian Augusti.
EDCS-23500079.summary: A dedication by the provincial governor Aurelius Litua to Jupiter and other gods, commemorating a military victory and his safe return, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-23500080.translation: To the god Liber, the state of Caesariensis, under the supervision of I. C. Le., official curator of the state of Caesariensis.
EDCS-23500080.summary: A dedication to the god Liber by the state of Caesariensis, mentioning a local official, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23500083.translation: Fausta, daughter of Lucius Geminius, from Saldis, willingly paid her vow to Saturn.
EDCS-23500083.summary: A votive dedication to Saturn by Fausta, daughter of Lucius Geminius, from Saldis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23500084.translation: Ingenuis Sutor paid his vow to Lord Saturn.
EDCS-23500084.summary: A votive dedication to Saturn by Ingenuis Sutor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23500085.translation: Secundio and Apicla willingly paid their vow to Saturn.
EDCS-23500085.summary: A votive dedication to Saturn by Secundio and Apicla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23500087.translation: Africanus the butcher willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-23500087.summary: A votive dedication by Africanus, identified as a butcher, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23500088.translation: Marcus Cerrinius, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, and Valeria Secundilla paid their vow.
EDCS-23500088.summary: A votive dedication by Marcus Cerrinius, a soldier of Legio III Augusta, and Valeria Secundilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23500089.summary: A dedication by Estricatus, a 10-year-old, possibly a votive offering, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23500090.translation: Felix willingly paid his vow to the god from his own resources.
EDCS-23500090.summary: A votive dedication to a god by Felix, made at his own expense, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23500091.summary: A votive dedication by Iulia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23500093.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Macer.

EDCS-23500094.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the short lifespan of Lucius Terentius Donatus.

EDCS-23500095.translation: Africanus gave two altars with their bases as a gift for P. Eoa; he made the new work at the age of 14.
EDCS-23500095.summary: This inscription records the dedication of two altars with bases by Africanus, likely a freedman, for P. Eoa.

EDCS-23500096.summary: This is a dedication or votive inscription for the well-being of Florus.

EDCS-23500099.translation: Aeschinus, freedman of King Iuba, aged 50 years and a half, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-23500099.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aeschinus, a freedman of King Iuba, providing his age and a common Latin funerary wish.

EDCS-23500100.translation: Tiberius Claudius Zena, brother of Tiberius Claudius Chresimus, is buried here.
EDCS-23500100.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Tiberius Claudius Zena and his relation to Tiberius Claudius Chresimus.

EDCS-23500101.translation: Caius Iulius Faustus, brother of Aeschinis, is buried here.
EDCS-23500101.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Caius Iulius Faustus and his relation to Aeschinis.

EDCS-23500102.translation: Iulia Faustilla, freedwoman of Helena, is buried here.
EDCS-23500102.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Iulia Faustilla and her former owner, Helena.

EDCS-23500103.translation: Daughter of King Iuba's freedman, aged 14 years, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-23500103.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the daughter of a freedman of King Iuba, specifying her age and including a common Latin funerary wish.

EDCS-23500104.translation: To Caelaetus, freedman of King Iuba, is buried here.
EDCS-23500104.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caelaetus, identified as a freedman of King Iuba.

EDCS-23500105.translation: Laetus, son of Acceptus, freedman of King Juba, snatched away by the cruelty of fate in his first year and eight days, lies here; as you pass by, it is your duty to say, may your bones rest well.
EDCS-23500105.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Laetus, a freedman of King Juba, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-23500106.translation: Philocalus, freedman of King Ptolemy's Pylades, lived for less than a year by fifteen days, lies here.
EDCS-23500106.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Philocalus, a freedman of King Ptolemy, who died at a young age.

EDCS-23500113.translation: To Publius Aelius Marcianus, son of Publius, of the Palatine tribe, tribune of the Aelian Cohort Expedita, prefect of the II Augusta Thracian Ala, prefect of the Syrian and Augusta Fleet, prefect of the Moesian Fleet, Caius Caelsius Marcellus, a veteran former decurion of the II Thracian Ala, [dedicated this].
EDCS-23500113.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Publius Aelius Marcianus, a Roman military officer of high rank, by Caius Caelsius Marcellus, a veteran.

EDCS-23500115.translation: To Publius Aelius Peregrinus, governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, a most perfect man, responsible for legal affairs of the Augusti, Tiberius Claudius Licinius, former prefect of the I Flavian Spanish Cohort, [dedicated this].
EDCS-23500115.summary: This is a dedication to Publius Aelius Peregrinus, the governor of Mauretania Caesariensis, by Tiberius Claudius Licinius, a former military officer.

EDCS-23500117.translation: To Alexander, freedman of our Emperor, procurator of the customs duties, Tiberius Claudius Sabinus, who is also called Amonius, patron of the province, [dedicated this].
EDCS-23500117.summary: This is a dedication to Alexander, an imperial freedman and procurator, by Tiberius Claudius Sabinus, patron of the province.

EDCS-23500120.translation: To Sextus Cornelius Clemens, son of Sextus, of the Palatine tribe, consul and leader of the three Dacian provinces, Avidius Valens, a centurion of the XIII Gemina Legion, formerly a staff officer of the consular governor, [dedicated this] as his candidate from his own municipality.
EDCS-23500120.summary: This is a dedication to Sextus Cornelius Clemens, who held high military and administrative positions, by Avidius Valens, a centurion.

EDCS-23500121.translation: To Gnaeus Haelius Diadumenianus, procurator of the Augusti of both Mauretanias, Tingitana and Caesariensis, Julius Valentinus, STA PR OI ordoru, patron, [dedicated this].
EDCS-23500121.summary: This is a dedication to Gnaeus Haelius Diadumenianus, an imperial procurator of the Mauretania provinces, by Julius Valentinus.

EDCS-23600809.summary: This is a brief dedication to the Manes, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-23600854.translation: In this place are the memorials of the holy martyrs Laurence, Hippolytus, Euphemia, Minna, and [the relics] deposited from the cross of the Lord, on the second day before the Nones of February in the 435th year of the province.
EDCS-23600854.summary: This inscription records the burial of several Christian martyrs and relics in a specific province, dated to 435 AD.

EDCS-23702048.translation: To Titus Licinius Hierocles, a distinguished man, procurator of our Emperor, most just governor with the sword of Emperor Severus, a most worthy patron, [dedicated this].
EDCS-23702048.summary: This is a dedication to Titus Licinius Hierocles, an imperial procurator and governor, by an unnamed patron.

EDCS-23702049.translation: Marius Domesticus, prefect of the Mauretanian Tibiscentian cavalry wing and Gaius Julius Italicus, son of Lucius, [from the records of the prefects] of the excellent prefects, patron of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, patron of the citizens of Nepete, priest and pontiff of the Laurentes Lavinates, [is honored here].
EDCS-23702049.summary: This inscription honors Marius Domesticus and Gaius Julius Italicus for their civic and religious roles in Mauretania Caesariensis and Nepete, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702054.translation: To [name] Augustus, freedman of Caesar, lived X years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-23702054.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedman of Augustus in Caesarea, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702055.translation: To Lucius Annius Fabianus, son of Caius, of the voting tribe Quirina, twice aedile, twice duovir, flamen, admitted into the quinque decuriis, prefect of the fourth cohort of Gauls in Raetia.
EDCS-23702055.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Annius Fabianus, detailing his political and military career in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702056.translation: To the divine shades. Auxesis, aged 14, for the sake of piety, belonging to Iulius Rufus, a veteran, [placed this memorial for] his native slave.
EDCS-23702056.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a veteran for his young, native slave girl, Auxesis, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702057.translation: To the divine shades. Gaius Cusonius Viator, soldier of the first legion Adiutrix, a Pannonia by birth, lived 28 years and 1 month.
EDCS-23702057.summary: This funerary epitaph records the details of Gaius Cusonius Viator, a soldier of Legio I Adiutrix from Pannonia, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702058.translation: Dazas, son of Scenius, a Maezian, cavalryman of the sixth cohort of Dalmatians, in the troop of Liccon, aged 27, with 10 years of service.
EDCS-23702058.summary: This inscription details the military service of Dazas, a cavalryman in the Cohors VI Dalmatarum, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702060.translation: To the divine shades. Insteius Victorinus, scribe of the Liburnian fleet Augusta, lived 45 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Trebia Mustia, his wife, had this made.
EDCS-23702060.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Insteius Victorinus, a scribe of the Roman fleet, with his wife as the dedicator, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702061.translation: To the divine shades. To Iulius Exoratus, a cavalryman of the Thracian wing, lived 18 years and 4 months. Iulius Valerius, a veteran and former beneficiary of the governor, [placed this monument for] his most distinguished son.
EDCS-23702061.summary: This funerary epitaph is a dedication by a veteran father for his son, Iulius Exoratus, a cavalryman who died at a young age, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702062.translation: To the divine shades, sacred. Sextus Iulius Iulianus, from Upper Germania, tribune of the Malvensian Syrian troop, is buried here, having led a thousand Bessian youths into the province of Tingitana, who lived 45 years. His brother and heir, Iulius Ingenuus, had the monument made, with Sacimathus, his freedman, overseeing it.
EDCS-23702062.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Sextus Iulius Iulianus, a tribune who led auxiliary troops into Tingitana, with his brother and heir as dedicator, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702064.translation: To Iulius [---], [son] of [---], [of the] cohort [---] of Severus's [troop].
EDCS-23702064.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely honoring a soldier or officer named Iulius, possibly associated with the Cohors IIII ARRI Severi, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23702065.translation: Liccaius, son of Carvus, of the Maezei tribe, a cavalryman in the Seventh Cohort of Dalmatians, lived for 30 years.
EDCS-23702065.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Liccaius, a cavalryman, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23702066.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucia Petronia dedicated this to her husband, Crescentius, a soldier of the Syrian fleet, who deserved well of her.
EDCS-23702066.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a wife for her deserving soldier husband, a sailor in the Syrian fleet.

EDCS-24400429.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Claudius Felicianus lived for 9 years and 15 days.
EDCS-24400429.summary: A brief funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a young individual named Claudius Felicianus.

EDCS-24400431.summary: A very short funerary epitaph for Cornelius, who died at the age of 24.

EDCS-24400433.translation: To our Emperor Caesar Flavius Constantinus, the Greatest, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, Augustus, chief priest, thrice Germanicus Maximus, thrice Sarmaticus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Carpicus Maximus, Arabicus Maximus, Medicus Maximus, Armenian Maximus, Gothicus Maximus, with the tribunician power for the 14th time, hailed as imperator for the 13th time, consul for the 4th time, father of his country, proconsul. Flavius Terentianus, a man of highest rank and governor of the province of Mauretania Sitifensis, always devoted to his divine majesty and power, dedicates this.
EDCS-24400433.summary: This is a lengthy dedication by the governor of Mauretania Sitifensis to Emperor Constantine the Great, highlighting his titles and virtues, likely from the 4th century AD.

EDCS-24400434.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Iulius Donatus lived for 75 years. Cecilia Boroc made this for her husband and will owe it to Cecilia Barag, who lived for [---] years.
EDCS-24400434.summary: A funerary inscription where Cecilia Boroc dedicates a tomb to her husband Marcus Iulius Donatus, with a mention of another individual, Cecilia Barag.

EDCS-24400436.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. C G S A.
EDCS-24400436.summary: A religious dedication to the god Mercury Augustus, followed by an abbreviated phrase.

EDCS-24400437.translation: Good Fortune. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Publius Substantius lived for 40 years for his dearest husband. Roscia Rufi and Publilia Monnina, his daughter, lived for 18 years. STV INT A.
EDCS-24400437.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to a husband by his wife, also mentioning the lifespan of their daughter, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-24400438.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Hostilia Octavia lived for 9 years. Aurelius Cirrus and Logus made this for their most devoted mother. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Aurelius Crailus lived for 80 years. Aurelius Cirrus and Logus made this for their most devoted father.
EDCS-24400438.summary: A funerary monument where Aurelius Cirrus and Logus honor their parents, Hostilia Octavia and Lucius Aurelius Crailus, with separate inscriptions.

EDCS-24400439.translation: Pompeia Victoria made this tomb for herself and her slave-girl. She lived for 75 years. May she rest in peace.
EDCS-24400439.summary: A tomb inscription made by Pompeia Victoria for herself and her slave, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24400440.summary: This inscription likely records the name and profession of Lucius P. Sorius, a calendary scribe.

EDCS-24400442.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Munius Victorinus, son of Caius, of the Papirian tribe, one of the Sitifian council, lived 55 years. He is buried here. His sons made this for their dearest father.
EDCS-24400442.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caius Munius Victorinus by his sons, mentioning his age and tribal affiliation, likely from Sitifis.

EDCS-24400443.translation: Caelia Thalassa, a Jewess, lived 20 years and 4 months. Marcus Avillius Ianuarius [made this] for his dearest wife.
EDCS-24400443.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caelia Thalassa, a Jewish woman, erected by her husband Marcus Avillius Ianuarius.

EDCS-24400444.translation: You will drink well today, tomorrow for free. Restuta Salvi ordered it to be made from 8 sesterces.
EDCS-24400444.summary: This inscription seems to be a humorous or promotional notice for a vendor, indicating free service on the second day after payment.

EDCS-24400447.summary: A brief Christian epitaph or dedication expressing hope in Christ.

EDCS-24400451.translation: The memoria of Saint Stephen, made on the 5th day before the Ides of December.
EDCS-24400451.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a memoria, likely a church or shrine, to Saint Stephen on a specific date.

EDCS-24400452.translation: To the eternal Divine Augusta, from the colony of Nerviana Augusta. Martialis of Sitifis erected this by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-24400452.summary: This public inscription records the erection of a monument to the Divine Augusta by Martialis of Sitifis, funded by the city council.

EDCS-24400453.translation: Sacred to Pluto and Ceres. Quintus M. Marinus gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-24400453.summary: A votive inscription by Quintus M. Marinus dedicating a shrine or offering to the gods Pluto and Ceres.

EDCS-24400457.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Lucius Iulius Petus, priest.
EDCS-24400457.summary: A dedication to Saturn Augustus by Lucius Iulius Petus, who identifies himself as a priest.

EDCS-24400458.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Iulius, priest.
EDCS-24400458.summary: A dedication to Saturn Augustus by a priest named Iulius.

EDCS-24400459.translation: Salvus Cassianus lived for 1 year. Flavia Syra, his mother, fulfilled her vow to Saturn, my departed ones; may they be safe.
EDCS-24400459.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Salvus Cassianus, with his mother Flavia Syra dedicating a vow to Saturn.

EDCS-24400461.translation: Marcus Caecilius Germanus, priest of Saturn.
EDCS-24400461.summary: This inscription records Marcus Caecilius Germanus as a priest of Saturn.

EDCS-24400462.summary: This inscription records Caius Iulius Martialis as a priest.

EDCS-24400463.translation: Caius Iulius, son of Caius, from the Arnensis tribe, Nundinarius, priest of the Lord Saturn.
EDCS-24400463.summary: This inscription identifies Caius Iulius Nundinarius as a priest of the Lord Saturn, noting his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-24400464.summary: This is a dedication or memorial to two individuals, Protogenis and Marcellus.

EDCS-24400465.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta. Marcus Longeius Silvanus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24400465.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Longeius Silvanus to Victoria Augusta.

EDCS-24400466.translation: To Victoria Augustorum Invictorum, to the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, and to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, and to Julia Augusta, mother of Caesar and of the whole divine house, Quintus Captius Martialis, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, [made this] on the occasion of his aedileship.
EDCS-24400466.summary: This is a dedication by Quintus Captius Martialis to Victoria and the imperial family, likely related to his aedileship.

EDCS-24400467.translation: Marcus Ulpius Avitus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, quaestor, aedile for the second time, a duovir, set up and dedicated [this] to Virtus Augusta for his honors.
EDCS-24400467.summary: This is an honorific inscription by Marcus Ulpius Avitus for holding magistracies, dedicated to Virtus Augusta.

EDCS-24400470.translation: Gargilius Iucus, son of Quintus, priest of the Lord Saturn, together with Victoria, his wife.
EDCS-24400470.summary: This inscription records Gargilius Iucus, priest of the Lord Saturn, and his wife Victoria.

EDCS-24400472.translation: Caius Talvonius Crescens, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, priest of the Lord Saturn and flamen.
EDCS-24400472.summary: This inscription identifies Caius Talvonius Crescens as a priest of the Lord Saturn and a flamen, noting his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-24400473.translation: Gaius Homullius Victor, son of Gaius, of the Arnensis tribe, a priest, along with his wife Maxima and his sons, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24400473.summary: A priest named Gaius Homullius Victor, with his family, fulfills a vow, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-24400474.translation: Ulpius, son of Marcus, of the Trepta tribe.
EDCS-24400474.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription or identification marker for Ulpius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-24400477.translation: Lucius Petronius Ianuarius, son of Marcus, of the Arnensis tribe, aedile, gave 6,000,000 sesterces from his own liberality for the adornment of the city, which he had promised beyond the legal amount for the honor of his aedileship, and dedicated it.
EDCS-24400477.summary: An aedile named Lucius Petronius Ianuarius donated a significant sum of money to the city for its adornment, possibly on a public building or monument, during the reign of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-24400479.translation: The colony of Sitifis [dedicated this] to Aurelius Hadrianus Antoninus, son of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, father of the country, by decree of the decurions, with public funds.
EDCS-24400479.summary: The colony of Sitifis erected a monument in honor of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (likely Marcus Aurelius), funded by the local council.

EDCS-24400480.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, father of the country, and for the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 6th time, father of the country, consul for the 2nd time, Marcus Cestius Regianus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, having pledged for the honor of the aedileship, dedicated [this] from his own money by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-24400480.summary: Marcus Cestius Regianus funded a dedication honoring both Emperor Septimius Severus and his son Caracalla, likely for his own aedileship, by decree of the local council.

EDCS-24400485.translation: Lucius Mamilius Castus and Lucius Aemilius Primus, duovirs, dedicated this sacred offering to the August Diana of the Moors.
EDCS-24400485.summary: Two local magistrates (duovirs) dedicated a sacred offering to the goddess Diana, likely on a temple or shrine.

EDCS-24400487.translation: Publius Arrius Ianuarius Mamertinus, heir from the will of Publius Herennius Mamertinus, set this up to Mars the August God and the Genius of the colony, and for the dedication he presented theatrical games and gave gifts to the decurions.
EDCS-24400487.summary: An heir named Publius Arrius Ianuarius Mamertinus set up a dedication to Mars and the Genius of the colony, and sponsored games and gifts for the local council.

EDCS-24400488.translation: Marcus Ulpius Andronicus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, quaestor, aedile, duovir, and quinquennial flamen, set this up at his own expense to Mars the August Victor.
EDCS-24400488.summary: Marcus Ulpius Andronicus, who held multiple civic and religious offices, dedicated this to Mars the August Victor at his own expense.

EDCS-24400854.translation: For the health of Serapis the Holy, Quintus Caecilius Temporinus.
EDCS-24400854.summary: Quintus Caecilius Temporinus makes a dedication for the health of the god Serapis, likely on a shrine or temple dedicated to him.

EDCS-24400864.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Iulius Cupitus lived 91 years.
EDCS-24400864.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Cupitus, who lived to the age of 91.

EDCS-24400865.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Vibia Aphrodisia, daughter, placed this for her most dutiful parents, Gaius Vibius Phaedrus and his wife Vibia Quanta.
EDCS-24400865.summary: This funerary inscription records a daughter dedicating a monument to her parents.

EDCS-24400870.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased Fufia Marciosa.

EDCS-24400872.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavia Victoria lived for [...] years.
EDCS-24400872.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the spirits of the underworld and records the incomplete lifespan of Flavia Victoria.

EDCS-24400873.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Neasulus lived for 70 years and [...] day.
EDCS-24400873.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Quintus Neasulus, who lived for 70 years and an unspecified number of days.

EDCS-24400874.translation: For the genius of the municipality of Satafens, from the will of Gaius Statulenius Martialis, perpetual flamen, his heir Gaius Statulenius Vitalis established a fund of 50,000 sesterces.
EDCS-24400874.summary: This inscription records a bequest from the will of a perpetual flamen for the benefit of the municipality of Satafens, established by his heir.

EDCS-24400876.translation: To Liber Pater, from his own property, Memorius.
EDCS-24400876.summary: This brief inscription records a dedication to the god Liber Pater, made by Memorius at his own expense.

EDCS-24400880.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld, to the honor and memory of Aper Namfamon, a soldier of the First Urban Cohort.
EDCS-24400880.summary: This funerary inscription honors the memory of Aper Namfamon, a soldier in the First Urban Cohort.

EDCS-24400881.translation: Marcus Annius Sacerdos, a Roman knight, patron of the municipality, curator and dispunctor, placed this table at his own expense.
EDCS-24400881.summary: This inscription records a public benefactor, Marcus Annius Sacerdos, who paid for a table at his own expense in his various official capacities.

EDCS-24400883.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aelia Urbia Anteiana lived for [...] years.
EDCS-24400883.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the spirits of the underworld and records the incomplete lifespan of Aelia Urbia Anteiana.

EDCS-24400884.translation: For the memory of Julia Hospita, our dearest wife, Caecilius Donatus together with my sons have placed this table.
EDCS-24400884.summary: This funerary inscription records a table placed by Caecilius Donatus and his sons in memory of Julia Hospita, his wife.

EDCS-24400885.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Sabina lived for 81 years.
EDCS-24400885.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulia Sabina, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-24400886.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Publius Lurius Primus, son of Publius, junior, lived for 14 years.
EDCS-24400886.summary: A funerary dedication for Publius Lurius Primus, recording his youth and lifespan.

EDCS-24400887.translation: Martialis made this from his own means.
EDCS-24400887.summary: An inscription recording a private dedication of a structure, possibly a wall or fortification.

EDCS-24400888.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Sittius Urbanus, junior, lived for 15 years.
EDCS-24400888.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Sittius Urbanus, recording his youth and lifespan.

EDCS-24400889.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Victor lived for 35 years. Maximus erected this for his brother.
EDCS-24400889.summary: A funerary dedication for Victor, erected by his brother Maximus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-24500086.translation: For the divine Publius Cornelius Licinius Valerianus, grandson of the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Augustus, brother of the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Augustus, the most noble Caesar Augustus Publius Cornelius Licinius Saloninus, by decree of the decurions and public money, from the veterans of the colonia Nerviana Augusta Martialis Sitifensium.
EDCS-24500086.summary: A dedication by the veterans of Sitifis in honor of the Caesar Valerianus, likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-24500087.translation: To our lord, the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Aurelius Diocletianus, the Invincible, Pious, Happy, Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the V time, consul for the III time, father of the fatherland, proconsul. Flavius Pecuarius, a most perfect man, governor of the province Mauretania Caesariensis, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-24500087.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Diocletian, set up by the governor of Mauretania Caesariensis, likely late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-24500088.translation: To the most happy and brave prince, our lord, Flavius Claudius Constantius, the most noble Caesar. Flavius Augustianus, a most perfect man, governor of the province Mauretania Sitifensium, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-24500088.summary: An inscription honoring Caesar Constantius, set up by the governor of Mauretania Sitifensium, likely early 4th century AD.

EDCS-24500090.translation: To the great and invincible prince, our lord, the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Happy, Always Augustus, chief priest, master of the Sarmatians, master of the Germans, master of the Goths, holding tribunician power for the X time, consul for the IIII time, imperator for the IX time, father of the fatherland, proconsul. Septimius Flavianus, a most perfect man, governor of the province Mauretania Sitifensis, always devoted to his divinity and majesty. Vota X, Multa X.
EDCS-24500090.summary: A grand inscription honoring Emperor Constantine I, detailing his titles and achievements, set up by the governor of Mauretania Sitifensis, likely around 317-324 AD.

EDCS-24500092.translation: To our lords, the Emperors Constantinus and Peregrinus, a most perfect man, governor of the province Mauretania Sitifensis.
EDCS-24500092.summary: A dedication to the Emperors Constantinus and the governor Peregrinus, likely from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-24500093.translation: For the prosperity of the times of our blessed lords Valentinian, Theodosius, and Arcadius, eternal princes, Flavius Maecius Constans, most perfect vir perfectissimus, governor of the province of Mauretania Sitifensis, under the care of the curator of the most splendid republic of the colony of Sitifis, reformed a public building, originally established by the decurions and principals and citizens who were suffering from severe hardship, which had been abandoned due to the imminent ruins of all its decorative work; having removed the old dirt and squalor, and adding new adornment, he had it decorated with brickwork and handed it over to the bakers for the public cooking of bread, and thus fed the people.
EDCS-24500093.summary: This inscription records the renovation of a public building for grain storage and baking by the governor of Mauretania Sitifensis, likely in the late 4th or early 5th century CE.

EDCS-24500094.translation: Valentinian and Theodosius, lords of our reign, have brought forth the arts of war from the earth and stars, like thunderbolts.
EDCS-24500094.summary: This inscription, likely part of a larger text, praises the military prowess of emperors Valentinian and Theodosius, possibly from the late 4th century CE.

EDCS-24500095.translation: Aurelius ... of our lord Julian, always Augustus, ... the amphitheater...
EDCS-24500095.summary: This fragment likely refers to a monument or action related to Emperor Julian and an amphitheater, possibly from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-24500097.translation: Titus Atilius, from the rationalibus summarum of the city of Rome, governor of the province of Mauretania Sitifensis, took care of this.
EDCS-24500097.summary: This inscription records the governor of Mauretania Sitifensis, Titus Atilius, as the responsible party for an unspecified work, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500100.translation: ... to Publius Mi[---]Moru, responsible for taxes and revenues in Mauretania Caesariensis, aged 65.
EDCS-24500100.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions an individual named Publius Mi[---]Moru, involved in fiscal administration in Mauretania Caesariensis, and records his age, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500101.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Soso, servant of Augustus, steward in Mauretania Caesariensis, lived 40 years. Felix Victor made this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-24500101.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and service of Soso, a steward in Mauretania Caesariensis, set up by Felix Victor, likely from the Roman imperial period.
