EDCS-24500102.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marinus, a Roman knight, most skilled in law, lived 25 years and 11 months; he is buried here. To the spirits of the departed, sacred. To Fabio Donatus, his son, a knight from the ranks of officials, lived 35 years. Marinus lived 55 years. Vettia Fortunata, daughter of Marcus Vettius Saturninus, made this for all with her own means and expenses during her lifetime.
EDCS-24500102.summary: This elaborate tomb inscription commemorates multiple family members, including a lawyer, an official, and Vettia Fortunata who commissioned it, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500103.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Valerius Valerianus made this memorial for his father, Valerius Marcellinus, a knight from under the command of Valerius, prefect of the Stablesian horsemen, who deserved well.
EDCS-24500103.summary: This funerary inscription records the memorial erected by Valerius Valerianus for his father, Valerius Marcellinus, a knight in a cavalry unit, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500106.translation: To Caius Coelius Felix, son of Caius Coelius Felix of Arna, Felix, as aedile, who lived 60 years, and to M. N. Quarta, who lived [---] years. Caius Coelius Victor, son of Marcus, made this for his most dutiful parents.
EDCS-24500106.summary: This inscription records a father and son, both named Caius Coelius Felix, one serving as an aedile, and their family, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500107.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. [---] Romano, who was also a decurion and prefect, a citizen of Neapolis. Maximia [---] his wife and Corneli [---] nina Victorinus [---] Sabinus for their dearest [---]
EDCS-24500107.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription honors a civic official named Romano from Neapolis and is dedicated by his family members, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-24500108.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Iulianus, having held all offices, lived 55 years.
EDCS-24500108.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Iulianus, who lived 55 years and had held various public offices.

EDCS-24500109.translation: Publius Octavius Laetus, perpetual priest of the emperors, made this for Iulia Maiora, his most rare betrothed and wife.
EDCS-24500109.summary: A funerary dedication by Publius Octavius Laetus to his wife Iulia Maiora.

EDCS-24500111.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Sitifensis is buried here for his most rare and dearest father, Publius Aelius Proclus, the physician.
EDCS-24500111.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Proclus, a physician, dedicated by his son.

EDCS-24500112.translation: Avilia Aster, a Jewess. Marcus Avilius Ianuarius, father of the synagogue, [made this] for his most sweet daughter.
EDCS-24500112.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Avilius Ianuarius to his daughter Avilia Aster.

EDCS-24500115.translation: Furfanius Fructus and his heir Flavia Primosa made the calling forth of Flavius Gaetulicus.
EDCS-24500115.summary: A dedication by Furfanius Fructus and Flavia Primosa, likely related to the invocation or memorial of Flavius Gaetulicus.

EDCS-24500117.summary: A very brief inscription identifying Tertullus as being from Arzni and associated with Sitifis.

EDCS-24500118.summary: A very brief inscription identifying Aemilius Aup as being from Sitifis.

EDCS-24500124.translation: May you do more, may you dedicate better, whoever enters here healthy. Miltiades with bellows, 55.
EDCS-24500124.summary: An unusual inscription possibly related to a medical or craft context, with a blessing and a mention of Miltiades and bellows.

EDCS-24500128.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aebutia Donata lived 17 years. Buried here.
EDCS-24500128.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aebutia Donata, who died at age 17.

EDCS-24500129.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Aufidianus lived 14 years. Publius Aelius Quintianus is buried here for his son.
EDCS-24500129.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Aufidianus, who died at age 14, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-24500130.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Aelius Donatus lived 66 years. Aelia Secunda made this for her dutiful husband and herself.
EDCS-24500130.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Aelius Donatus, set up by his wife Aelia Secunda.

EDCS-24500131.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Aelius Vindex the boy lived 16 years and 4 months.
EDCS-24500131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the boy Lucius Aelius Vindex.

EDCS-24500132.translation: For Aelia Major, his most dutiful wife. Publius Aemilius Donatus, son of Publius, son of Publius, for the memory of her life, lived X years.
EDCS-24500132.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Major, possibly set up by her husband Publius Aemilius Donatus.

EDCS-24500133.translation: In memory of Aemilius. He lived 23 years. Helvia Avi placed this for her sweetest [relation].
EDCS-24500133.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilius, erected by Helvia Avi.

EDCS-24500134.translation: For the memory of Aemilius Coddeus. Fabia Lautina, with her children, erected this for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-24500134.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilius Coddeus, erected by his wife Fabia Lautina and their children.

EDCS-24500135.translation: Lucius Aemilius Florus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 17 years and 7 months. Lucius Aemilius Primulus, his father, made this for his son. He lies here.
EDCS-24500135.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Aemilius Florus, erected by his father Lucius Aemilius Primulus.

EDCS-24500136.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aemilius for his son Florus lived 2 years. Aemilius Primus for his grandson lived 34 years.
EDCS-24500136.summary: This epitaph records the deaths of two Aemilii, Florus (a son) and Primus (a grandson), likely related.

EDCS-24500137.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aemilia Saturnina, daughter of Publius, lived 35 years. Caius Iulius Ingenuus for his wife.
EDCS-24500137.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia Saturnina, set up by her husband Caius Iulius Ingenuus.

EDCS-24500138.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Afredius Victor lived 164 years. He lies here.
EDCS-24500138.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Afredius Victor, whose age is likely exaggerated.

EDCS-24500139.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Annaeus Salvianus, also called Crementius, who lived 5 years. Salvianus, for his dearest son.
EDCS-24500139.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Annaeus Salvianus (Crementius), a young child, set up by his father Salvianus.

EDCS-24500140.translation: Antistia, daughter of Antistius, and Iulius Candidus, freedmen, XCV
EDCS-24500140.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antistia and Iulius Candidus, possibly freedmen, dated by the Roman numeral XCV.

EDCS-24500141.translation: To the divine shades. Publius Antonius Castresianus lived 25 years. Geminia Felicula [dedicated this] to her pious son.
EDCS-24500141.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Geminia Felicula to her son, Publius Antonius Castresianus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-24500142.translation: To the divine shades, sacred. Marcus Antonius Felix lived 110 years.
EDCS-24500142.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Felix, who lived to the age of 110.

EDCS-24500143.translation: To the divine shades, sacred. Antonius Priscus, aged 9, made [this]. His son, Ianus, [made this].
EDCS-24500143.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonius Priscus, who died at age 9, and was likely made by his son.

EDCS-24500144.translation: To the divine shades, sacred. Antonius, also called Sinus, also called Oniacus, lived harmoniously with all. While I was alive, he made [this] with his wife Veneria, who lived 40 years. His former wife Mustula lived 20 years.
EDCS-24500144.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonius (also known as Sinus and Oniacus) and his wives Veneria and Mustula, likely erected by Antonius himself.

EDCS-24500145.translation: Antonia Carosa lived 16 years. Novius Secundianus lived 73 years.
EDCS-24500145.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of Antonia Carosa (16 years) and Novius Secundianus (73 years).

EDCS-24500146.translation: The tomb of love for Arria Dativa, his concubine. Caius Simplicius lived 23 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-24500146.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arria Dativa, concubine of Caius Simplicius, who also records Simplicius's age and burial.

EDCS-24500147.translation: Aufidia Rogata lived 70 years. Coccaia Dativa made [this] for her mother from her own resources.
EDCS-24500147.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aufidia Rogata, erected by her daughter Coccaia Dativa.

EDCS-24500148.translation: To the divine shades, sacred. Aurelius Extricatus [made this] for his [wife/daughter/etc.], Nonnia Saturnina.
EDCS-24500148.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Aurelius Extricatus to Nonnia Saturnina.

EDCS-24500149.translation: In memory of Marcus Aurelius Maximianus, who lived...
EDCS-24500149.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph commemorating Marcus Aurelius Maximianus, recording his name and that he lived, but not for how long.

EDCS-24500150.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Prima lived 48 years. Eutyches her son-in-law and Saturnina her daughter made and dedicated this to their most dutiful mother.
EDCS-24500150.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Aurelia Prima and was dedicated by her son-in-law and daughter.

EDCS-24500151.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Victoria lived 17 years and 6 days. Son
EDCS-24500151.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Aurelia Victoria, who lived 17 years and 6 days, with the end of the text indicating a familial relationship.

EDCS-24500152.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cae Valentinus lived 7 years. Cae Ianuarius [buried?] his dearest son here.
EDCS-24500152.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cae Valentinus at the age of 7, and was likely set up by his father, Cae Ianuarius.

EDCS-24500154.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cecilia Donata lived 70 years. Marcus Octavius Felix made this for his dearest mother. She is buried here.
EDCS-24500154.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cecilia Donata at the age of 70 and was erected by her son, Marcus Octavius Felix.

EDCS-24500155.summary: This incomplete inscription is a dedication to a wife, possibly from a funerary monument.

EDCS-24500156.translation: Caelia Lauti lived 17 years. Her partner, Carus Victoricus.
EDCS-24500156.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caelia Lauti at the age of 17 and was set up by her partner, Carus Victoricus.

EDCS-24500157.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cale Minici lived 12 years and 7 days.
EDCS-24500157.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cale Minici at the age of 12 years and 7 days.

EDCS-24500158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Kalpurnius, son of Quintus, lived 105 years, and Cassia Ingenua his wife lived 75 years.
EDCS-24500158.summary: This funerary inscription records the long lives of Quintus Kalpurnius (105 years) and his wife Cassia Ingenua (75 years).

EDCS-24500159.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Carchedonius lived [X] years.
EDCS-24500159.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription records the death of Carchedonius, with the duration of his life missing.

EDCS-24500160.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cassia Fausta formerly and Monna Sancta.
EDCS-24500160.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Cassia Fausta and Monna Sancta.

EDCS-24500161.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Piriaiti Cedalo made this for her husband. Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cinneus Victor lived 47 years. Felicianus made this for his dearest father.
EDCS-24500161.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of two individuals, one made by the deceased's wife and the other by a son for his father.

EDCS-24500162.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Clodius Crescens, also called Vigilantius, lived 12 years, 11 months, 13 days. Caius Clodius Processus lived 19 years, 1 month, 6 days. Ianuarius and Clodia Ianuaria lived 17 years, 8 months. Their father [made this].
EDCS-24500162.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages at death of three young individuals, likely siblings, with their father identified as the dedicator.

EDCS-24500163.translation: For the memory of Claudia Maria, who lived 50 years. Numisius Primulus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-24500163.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of Claudia Maria and identifies her husband, Numisius Primulus, as the dedicator.

EDCS-24500164.translation: Quintus Considius Firmianus lived 23 years, 7 months, 15 days. He lies here.
EDCS-24500164.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of Quintus Considius Firmianus and states that his body is interred here.

EDCS-24500165.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Considius Fortunatus lived 70 years, bequeathed by his will. And Caelia Maior, his wife, lived 52 years. They lie here.
EDCS-24500165.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of a married couple, Quintus Considius Fortunatus and Caelia Maior, with provisions made by the deceased husband's will.

EDCS-24500166.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Noreius lived 16 years. Sammia Emerita made this for her son with her own money.
EDCS-24500166.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young man, Cornelius Noreius, and identifies his mother, Sammia Emerita, as the dedicator.

EDCS-24500167.translation: Lived 20 years. Lucius Decrius made this for his wife Dicua.
EDCS-24500167.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of an individual and identifies Lucius Decrius as the dedicator for his wife.

EDCS-24500168.translation: Lucius Domitius Daphnus made this for his son Petronianus.
EDCS-24500168.summary: This inscription records Lucius Domitius Daphnus dedicating a monument to his son, Petronianus.

EDCS-24500169.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Domitius Saturninus lived 45 years.
EDCS-24500169.summary: This funerary inscription records the life span of Quintus Domitius Saturninus.

EDCS-24500170.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Domitia Cina lived 70 years with her husband. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Ianuarius lived 70 years.
EDCS-24500170.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of two individuals, Domitia Cina and Caius Iulius Ianuarius, both of whom lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-24500171.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Dor lived 15 years. Here lies Titus Aurelius Ianuarius, father of his sweetest and most devoted daughter.
EDCS-24500171.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a young girl, Dor, and commemorating her father, Titus Aurelius Ianuarius.

EDCS-24500172.translation: To the good memory of Lucius Ennius Restutianu, a well-deserving, good, sweetest, and admirable boy, whom cruel fate snatched away in his seventh year, his loving father [erected this].
EDCS-24500172.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy, Lucius Ennius Restutianu, erected by his father.

EDCS-24500173.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Fabius Faustus, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, lived 46 years. Here lies Antonia Maura, to her dearest husband. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fabius Victorinus lived 30 years.
EDCS-24500173.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Caius Fabius Faustus and his wife Antonia Maura, and separately Fabius Victorinus.

EDCS-24501183.summary: A very brief epitaph, likely for Primigenius Neronius, possibly a slave or freedman given the context of similar inscriptions.

EDCS-24700625.translation: Here lie the remains of the saints Stephen and Julianus. Placed on the 12th day before the Kalends of April, near the tomb of Nabor and Saint Stephen.
EDCS-24700625.summary: An inscription recording the deposition of relics or burials of saints Stephen and Julianus, possibly in relation to earlier saints.

EDCS-24700626.summary: A simple epitaph for Mensa Suduitus, identified as a grandmother.

EDCS-24700627.translation: Here lies the holy bishop Novatus, who held his see for 37 years. He departed on the 10th day before the Kalends of September in the 401st year of the province.
EDCS-24700627.summary: An epitaph for Novatus, a holy bishop, recording his years of service and date of death within a provincial dating system.

EDCS-24700628.translation: Adrianus the faithful lived in peace for 7 years and 37 days.
EDCS-24700628.summary: A funerary epitaph for Adrianus, a faithful individual, likely a child given the short lifespan.

EDCS-24700629.translation: Here lies Broccu the innocent, who lived more or less 3 years.
EDCS-24700629.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Broccu, emphasizing their innocence.

EDCS-24700631.translation: Here lies Crisacius the foreigner, who lived more or less 35 years. In the year 366 of the province.
EDCS-24700631.summary: A funerary epitaph for Crisacius, identified as a foreigner, and dated by the provincial era.

EDCS-24700632.translation: Constantius the transmarine rests here, who lived twenty-seven years more or less in the year of the province 366.
EDCS-24700632.summary: This epitaph records the death of Constantius, a 'transmarine' (possibly indicating origin or status), who died in provincial year 366.

EDCS-24700633.translation: The memorial of innocent Istablicius, who is also called Donatus, his brother Peregrinius erected, who is from the Mosattes, of your Jews, EIV DALVIR E.
EDCS-24700633.summary: This inscription is a memorial erected by Peregrinius for his brother Istablicius (also known as Donatus), identifying them as Jews from Mosattes and dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-24700634.translation: The memorial of the innocent Iustus, who lived two years, died on the eleventh day before the Kalends of October in the year 380.
EDCS-24700634.summary: This epitaph records the short life of Iustus, who died on September 21st in the provincial year 380.

EDCS-24700635.translation: Ireneus the transmarine rests here, who lived forty years more or less in the year of the province 366.
EDCS-24700635.summary: This epitaph records the death of Ireneus, a 'transmarine' individual, who died in provincial year 366.

EDCS-24700636.translation: The memorial of Iulius Seccundius, who lived twenty-seven years; Iulius Primus [erected it] for his most beloved son.
EDCS-24700636.summary: This epitaph is a memorial erected by Iulius Primus for his son Iulius Seccundius, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-24700637.translation: Modanius, aged 18, and his grandmother Viatorina, aged 60, rested in peace in the year of the province 367.
EDCS-24700637.summary: This inscription records the peaceful rest of Modanius (18 years old) and his grandmother Viatorina (60 years old) in the provincial year 367.

EDCS-24700638.translation: Help us, Ianri, Stefane, Paulus, in God, may he live.
EDCS-24700638.summary: This is a short devotional or prayer invoking Ianri, Stefane, and Paulus, possibly saints or important figures, asking for help and wishing life in God.

EDCS-24700639.translation: The memorial of Aptacida; Vincentius [erected it as] father.
EDCS-24700639.summary: This is a memorial for Aptacida, erected by his father, Vincentius.

EDCS-24700640.translation: For Sertoria the chaste matron, Salvianus [erected it] for his most loving wife, who lived forty-four years, 4 months, and 17 days.
EDCS-24700640.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by Salvianus to his wife Sertoria, a chaste matron, detailing her lifespan.

EDCS-24700641.translation: Victoria the transmarine rests here, who lived more or less 31 years; Eulogius the transmarine rests here, who lived a number of 53 years, in the year 366.
EDCS-24700641.summary: This inscription records the deaths of two individuals, Victoria and Eulogius, both identified as 'transmarine', who died in provincial year 366.

EDCS-24700642.translation: Vitivulfus lies here; he lived for fifty years, more or less, in the 376th year of the province.
EDCS-24700642.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Vitivulfus, with a dating reference to the 376th year of the province.

EDCS-24700643.translation: Untancus and Innocens, of the Trigari tribe, lie here.
EDCS-24700643.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Untancus and Innocens, identified by their tribal affiliation.

EDCS-24700644.translation: Bonifatia, faithful in peace, lived for 27 years.
EDCS-24700644.summary: This tombstone records the life of Bonifatia, who lived for 27 years.

EDCS-24700646.translation: Andisvove lies here; he lived for 9 years, of Count Paconianus.
EDCS-24700646.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Andisvove, who lived for 9 years and was associated with Count Paconianus.

EDCS-24700813.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Caritu lived for 35 years; she is buried here. Publius Aelius Victorinus, her husband, sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-24700813.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits of Iulia Caritu, who lived for 35 years, and her husband Publius Aelius Victorinus.

EDCS-24700814.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Colonica lived for 40 years.
EDCS-24700814.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits of Iulia Colonica, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-24700815.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Felicia lived for 4 years; she is buried here. Caius Iulius Successus, her father, made this for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-24700815.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits of Iulia Felicia, who lived for 4 years, and was made by her father Caius Iulius Successus.

EDCS-24700817.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Lucentina lived for 25 years.
EDCS-24700817.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits of Iulia Lucentina, who lived for 25 years.

EDCS-24700818.translation: To Iulia Nina, dearest wife, who lived for 50 years. Her husband, Flavius Donatianus, made the cupola.
EDCS-24700818.summary: This tombstone commemorates Iulia Nina, who lived for 50 years, and was made by her husband Flavius Donatianus.

EDCS-24700819.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Quinta lived for 70 years; the heir of Lucius Pompeius Maximus made this memorial. He lies here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Pompeius Maximus lived for [---] years.
EDCS-24700819.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the departed spirits of Iulia Quinta, who lived for 70 years, and Lucius Pompeius Maximus, made by the heir of Lucius Pompeius Maximus.

EDCS-24700820.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Quinta lived 12 years, pious.
EDCS-24700820.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Iulia Quinta, who lived 12 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700821.translation: For the memory of Quintus Faustus. Iulia Victoria, his wife, and Iunius Aemilianus made this.
EDCS-24700821.summary: This tombstone inscription was erected by Iulia Victoria and Iunius Aemilianus for Quintus Faustus, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700822.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sweet Iunius Nicii [---] his [---]
EDCS-24700822.summary: This is a fragmented tombstone inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades, mentioning Iunius Nicii, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700823.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iunia Matrona lived 29 years. Caius Iulius Iulianus [dedicated this] to his dearest wife.
EDCS-24700823.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Iunia Matrona, aged 29, dedicated by her husband Caius Iulius Iulianus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700824.translation: To the Divine Shades. Licinius lived 70 years; he rests here.
EDCS-24700824.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Licinius, who lived 70 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700826.translation: Marcus Mai Felix the younger lived twenty-three years. Maius Felix dedicated this to his most loving son.
EDCS-24700826.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Marcus Mai Felix the younger, aged 23, and was dedicated by his father Maius Felix, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700827.translation: To the Divine Shades. Mamilia daughter of Lucius Maior lived 35 years; she rests here.
EDCS-24700827.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Mamilia Maior, daughter of Lucius, who lived 35 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700828.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Manilius Urbanus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 23 years; he rests here. [---] us cor anus lived 25 years; [he] rests.
EDCS-24700828.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Quintus Manilius Urbanus, aged 23, and another individual aged 25, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700829.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcius Martialis lived 38 years. Marcia Donatula dedicated this.
EDCS-24700829.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Marcius Martialis, aged 38, and was dedicated by Marcia Donatula, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700830.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Marcius Martialis [lived]. To the Divine Shades. Caius Marcius Donatus [dedicated this].
EDCS-24700830.summary: This fragmented tombstone inscription mentions Lucius Marcius Martialis and Caius Marcius Donatus, both dedicated to the Divine Shades, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-24700831.translation: To the divine shades. Quinta Cessa Matronica lived 8 years. Her dearest [father/mother/etc.] made this.
EDCS-24700831.summary: A funerary dedication for a child named Quinta Cessa Matronica, who died at the age of 8.

EDCS-24700832.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Marcus Messius Saturninus lived 29 years and 7 months. He is buried here.
EDCS-24700832.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Messius Saturninus, who died at the age of 29 years and 7 months.

EDCS-24700833.translation: To Lucius Octavius Saturninus, [dedicated by his] most dutiful husband.
EDCS-24700833.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Octavius Saturninus, presumably made by his husband.

EDCS-24700834.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Lucius Otacilius Commodus lived 3 years and 2 months. He is buried here.
EDCS-24700834.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Otacilius Commodus, who died at the age of 3 years and 2 months.

EDCS-24700835.translation: To the divine shades. Lucius Paconius Victor, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 82 years.
EDCS-24700835.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Paconius Victor, who lived to be 82 years old.

EDCS-24700836.translation: To the divine shades. Felix, aged 60. Pompeius Quintus, from Ompe, lived [---] years.
EDCS-24700836.summary: A funerary dedication for Felix (aged 60) and Pompeius Quintus, with incomplete lifespan information for the latter.

EDCS-24700837.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Pompeia Issula lived two years, 4 months, and 5 days. Lucius Pompeius Avus made this.
EDCS-24700837.summary: A funerary dedication for Pompeia Issula, who died at the age of 2 years, 4 months, and 5 days, made by Lucius Pompeius Avus.

EDCS-24700838.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Quintus Publilius Romulus lived 34 years. His brother, Publilius Rufus, made this.
EDCS-24700838.summary: A funerary dedication for Quintus Publilius Romulus, who died at the age of 34, made by his brother Publilius Rufus.

EDCS-24700839.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Titus C. Cicero [lived] 12 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-24700839.summary: A funerary dedication for Titus C. Cicero, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-24700840.translation: He lived 95 years. He is buried here. Lucius Quintitius Uninitus lived 88 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-24700840.summary: A funerary dedication for an unnamed individual who lived 95 years and Lucius Quintitius Uninitus who lived 88 years.

EDCS-24700841.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aldus lived 85 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Flora, his wife, lived 85 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Quintius Primitivus lived... Sacred to the departed spirits. Alfia Donatula, to her wife, lived... Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-24700841.summary: This inscription is a collection of funerary dedications to the departed spirits, recording the ages of several individuals, likely from a single burial context.

EDCS-24700842.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Sabinius Vetus lived 50 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Quinta lived 60 years; she is buried here. Quintus Sabinus Silvanus made this for his dearest mother.
EDCS-24700842.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument recording the lives of Quintus Sabinius Vetus and Iulia Quinta, and a dedication by Quintus Sabinus Silvanus to his mother.

EDCS-24700843.translation: Of the Saediorum, son of Volus and Faoniana, and her son.
EDCS-24700843.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment naming individuals, likely related to a family or property, possibly indicating parentage or inheritance.

EDCS-24700846.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Sempronius Maximus lived 6 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24700846.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the early death and burial of a child, Caius Sempronius Maximus.

EDCS-24700847.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Caius Sempronius Modestus, uncle, who lived 63 years.
EDCS-24700847.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Caius Sempronius Modestus, identified as an uncle.

EDCS-24700848.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Setidia Urbana made this monument for herself and for her daughter Cormon while she was alive.
EDCS-24700848.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Setidia Urbana for herself and her daughter, Cormon.

EDCS-24700849.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Tanonius Pamphilus lived 25 years.
EDCS-24700849.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Publius Tanonius Pamphilus at death.

EDCS-24700850.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tranquillus lived 4 years. Firmus lived 3 years.
EDCS-24700850.summary: This funerary inscription records the very young ages of two individuals, Tranquillus and Firmus.

EDCS-24700851.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Varius Fuscus lived 96 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24700851.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age and burial of Quintus Varius Fuscus.

EDCS-24700852.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Mutrarpius lived 70 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-24700852.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age and burial of Marcus Ulpius Mutrarpius.

EDCS-24700853.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Urbanus lived 32 years, is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Urbanus the Younger lived 4 years, 5 months, is buried here.
EDCS-24700853.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Ulpius Urbanus and his son, Marcus Ulpius Urbanus the Younger.

EDCS-24700854.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ulpia Pecuaria lived 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-24700854.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Ulpia Pecuaria.

EDCS-24700855.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Volcacia Paulina lived 55 years. Manilius Rusticus and Donatus made this for their dearest mother.
EDCS-24700855.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Volcacia Paulina, made by her sons Manilius Rusticus and Donatus.

EDCS-24700865.translation: Flavius Innocentius, a clerk, tiled (this) for his own health and that of all his family.
EDCS-24700865.summary: This inscription records Flavius Innocentius tiling a space for his health and that of his family.

EDCS-24700866.translation: In this holy place, the relics of Saint Lawrence the martyr have been deposited. On the 3rd day of May, in the consulship of Herculanus, a most distinguished man, on the Lord's day, dedicated by Lawrence, a venerable man and priest, after the death of our Lord, in the year of the province 413. Amen.
EDCS-24700866.summary: This inscription records the deposition of the relics of Saint Lawrence the martyr on May 3rd, 413 provincial era, during the consulship of Herculanus.

EDCS-24701396.translation: I dedicated. Lived 65 years. Flavia Prima, his wife, lived...
EDCS-24701396.summary: This incomplete inscription records the life span of a man and his wife, Flavia Prima, and includes a dedication.

EDCS-24701397.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunatus, who is also called Dacus, lived 22 years, 10 months, 20 days. May his bones rest well here.
EDCS-24701397.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Fortunatus (also known as Dacus) and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-24701398.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Fortunata, who is buried here.
EDCS-24701398.summary: This funerary inscription is a memorial for Fortunata, recording her burial.

EDCS-24701399.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gargilla Saturnina lived 47 years. Caius Aemilius Furninus to his mother.
EDCS-24701399.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gargilla Saturnina, to whom her son Caius Aemilius Furninus dedicated it.

EDCS-24701400.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gemina Optata lived 19 years.
EDCS-24701400.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gemina Optata.

EDCS-24701401.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Geminia Primigenia lived 63 years and Columbula, her grandson, lived 7 years.
EDCS-24701401.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages of Geminia Primigenia and her grandson Columbula.

EDCS-24701402.translation: Fortune suddenly snatched away the joys she had given and turned the hopes of her parents into bitter grief, for this small boy, snatched from the threshold of life, Ginga, is placed in the tomb, the unworthy wounds of his father. Oh sorrow and groans and mocked hopes of your loved ones, nevertheless you go not to the shades but to the stars of heaven, Ginga by name.
EDCS-24701402.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph lamenting the premature death of a young boy named Ginga, emphasizing the sorrow of his parents and his ascent to heaven.

EDCS-24701403.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Helvidia Monnis lived 31 years; here she lies. Marcus Ulpius Ingenuus [made this] for his most sweet wife. Marcus Ulpius Ingenuus lived [X] years.
EDCS-24701403.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Helvidia Monnis and includes a dedication from her husband, Marcus Ulpius Ingenuus, who also likely has his lifespan recorded.

EDCS-24701404.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Hortensia Euhodia lived 60 years.
EDCS-24701404.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Hortensia Euhodia.

EDCS-24701405.translation: Hostilia Prima lived 30 years. Iulius Aurelius [made this] for his dearest wife.
EDCS-24701405.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Hostilia Prima and is a dedication from her husband, Iulius Aurelius.

EDCS-24701406.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Callistianus lived 40 years; here he lies.
EDCS-24701406.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Caius Iulius Callistianus and noting his burial place.

EDCS-24701407.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caio Iulio Pulicioni lived 50 years. Iulius Donatus made [this] for his father.
EDCS-24701407.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Caio Iulio Pulicioni and is a dedication from his son, Iulius Donatus.

EDCS-24701408.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Iulius Quietianus lived 24 years, 9 months, and 24 days.
EDCS-24701408.summary: This funerary epitaph records the precise lifespan of Gaius Iulius Quietianus.

EDCS-24701409.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Gaius Rogatus.
EDCS-24701409.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Gaius Rogatus, likely marking his burial.

EDCS-24701410.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Victor lived 2 years, 1 month, and 1 day. Iulia Monnula lived 3 years. Rufus, the father, made [this]. Iulia Spes lived 3 years, 1 month, and 2 days. Rufus made [this].
EDCS-24701410.summary: This funerary epitaph records the short lifespans of Caius Iulius Victor, Iulia Monnula, and Iulia Spes, with dedications from their father, Rufus.

EDCS-24900482.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Comidius Caelius lived 55 full years. For the sake of memory, Comidius Tertius lived 70 years. His brother Comidius made this.
EDCS-24900482.summary: A tombstone set up by a brother for Comidius Caelius and Comidius Tertius, dated by the mention of the life spans of the deceased.

EDCS-24900484.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Atupinius lived [number] years.
EDCS-24900484.summary: A tombstone for Flavius Atupinius, mentioning his lifespan, the exact duration of which is partially effaced.

EDCS-24900488.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Bamuliusarius lived [number] years.
EDCS-24900488.summary: A tombstone for Bamuliusarius, recording a lifespan that is incomplete.

EDCS-24900489.translation: Millialius was laid to rest on the twelfth day before the Kalends of October for his Fabianae.
EDCS-24900489.summary: A burial record for Millialius, dated by the Roman calendar and a reference to someone named Fabianae.

EDCS-24900492.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anius Primus lived 65 years. Caia.
EDCS-24900492.summary: A tombstone for Anius Primus, noting his age, and also mentioning Caia.

EDCS-24900493.summary: A tombstone for Aufidius Bacuarius, with an incomplete record of his lifespan.

EDCS-24900494.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Matroi lived 65 years. Lucius Annius Saturus lived 30 years.
EDCS-24900494.summary: A tombstone commemorating Iulia Matroi and Lucius Annius Saturus, with their respective ages.

EDCS-24900495.translation: Table of Ianuarianus and Urbanais. V.
EDCS-24900495.summary: An inscription possibly related to a dining table or a memorial, associated with Ianuarianus and Urbanais.

EDCS-24900497.translation: Oria Felicie was laid to rest on the fourteenth day before the Kalends of March in the 409th year of the province. Burial of RIS LIRI XV AE.
EDCS-24900497.summary: A burial record for Oria Felicie, dated by the provincial era and containing abbreviated details about the burial.

EDCS-24900498.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly a name or part of a larger text, referring to Marcus Cervulus.

EDCS-24900499.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all the other gods and goddesses, for the safety and well-being of our victorious and most holy lord, the Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, and of his wife, our Empress Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta, and of their entire divine household, the castellans of B have dedicated this from their own money.
EDCS-24900499.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and other gods for the safety of Emperor Gordian III and his wife, made by castellans.

EDCS-24900502.translation: To the Emperors, Caesars, Flavius Valerius Constantinus and Valerius Licinianus Licinius Invicti Semper Augusti, Septimius Flavianus, a most accomplished man, governor of the province of Mauretania Sitifensis, constructed and dedicated the centenarium of Sol from the ground up and has always been most devoted to their divine majesty and numen.
EDCS-24900502.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of a centenarium for Emperors Constantine and Licinius by Septimius Flavianus, governor of Mauretania Sitifensis.

EDCS-24900503.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. C. Rufila and Lucius Artidius Muttun lived 75 years.
EDCS-24900503.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages of C. Rufila and Lucius Artidius Muttun.

EDCS-24900504.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cecilius PR lived 3 years.
EDCS-24900504.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of a child, Cecilius PR.

EDCS-24900505.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Kariola and Aelius Mutus lived 63 years.
EDCS-24900505.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages of Aelia Kariola and Aelius Mutus.

EDCS-24900506.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Julius Mico lived 75 years.
EDCS-24900506.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Gaius Julius Mico.

EDCS-24900507.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Julius Rufinus lived 75 years. Lucius Julius Saturninus lived 85 years.
EDCS-24900507.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages of Lucius Julius Rufinus and Lucius Julius Saturninus.

EDCS-24900508.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Julius Rusticus lived 86 years. His heirs made this.
EDCS-24900508.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Gaius Julius Rusticus and notes that his heirs erected it.

EDCS-24900509.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Julius Saturninus the pious lived 60 years.
EDCS-24900509.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Gaius Julius Saturninus.

EDCS-24900510.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Rogata lived 75 years.
EDCS-24900510.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Iulia Rogata.

EDCS-24900511.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Omidius Sedot lived 35 years.
EDCS-24900511.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Omidius Sedot, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-24900512.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Pes Iovinus lived 65 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Lan lived 40 years.
EDCS-24900512.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Publius Pes Iovinus and Publius Lan, who lived 65 and 40 years respectively.

EDCS-24900514.translation: T. Baugus lived 74 years. Ianuarius lived 40 years.
EDCS-24900514.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of T. Baugus and Ianuarius, who lived 74 and 40 years respectively.

EDCS-24900515.translation: Viteli Getuli lived 70 years. Tullia Victorina lived 65 years.
EDCS-24900515.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Viteli Getuli and Tullia Victorina, who lived 70 and 65 years respectively.

EDCS-24900516.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Vaica Ulpia lived 102 years.
EDCS-24900516.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Vaica Ulpia, who lived 102 years.

EDCS-24900521.translation: Donata, who lived 61 years, Iulius Berectus, a grandson of 3 years, Muttum Gududus.
EDCS-24900521.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Donata (aged 61) and Iulius Berectus (a 3-year-old grandson).

EDCS-24900524.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulius Getulicus lived 45 years.
EDCS-24900524.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Getulicus, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-24900525.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marius Rullus lived 85 years.
EDCS-24900525.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Marius Rullus, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-24900526.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Netia Fecunda lived 63 years. Quintus Iulius Paulinus lived 85 years. The heirs made and dedicated this.
EDCS-24900526.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Netia Fecunda and Quintus Iulius Paulinus, who lived 63 and 85 years respectively, and was erected by their heirs.

EDCS-24900527.translation: ENISED XII PRE. Quadratus made [it]. PR
EDCS-24900527.summary: This inscription appears to record a construction or dedication made by someone named Quadratus, with some measurements or other details indicated by abbreviations.

EDCS-24900528.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Sallustius Cerialis lived 75 years. The guild and his heirs made this for a well-deserving person.
EDCS-24900528.summary: This is a funerary monument for Publius Sallustius Cerialis, erected by his guild and heirs.

EDCS-24900529.translation: Julius Cittinus lived 20 years. Julius Severus lived 20 years. Julius Silvanus lived 20 years. Julius Bonosus lived 12 years. Julius Fortunatus lived 10 years. Julius Ianuarius lived 12 years. Julius Spica lived 4 years. Mici
EDCS-24900529.summary: This inscription records the ages of death for several individuals named Julius, likely family members, probably on a tombstone.

EDCS-24900530.translation: For the memory of Titinia Donata, who lived 13 years and 10 days.
EDCS-24900530.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Titinia Donata and her lifespan.

EDCS-24900531.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tullia Donata lived 75 years.
EDCS-24900531.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tullia Donata, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-24900532.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Valerius Baricis lived 91 years.
EDCS-24900532.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Valerius Baricis.

EDCS-24900533.translation: Gaius Vinnus, 50 years, the hairy one.
EDCS-24900533.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Vinnus, noting his age and a nickname.

EDCS-24900534.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Volussenius Maximus lived 68 years.
EDCS-24900534.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Volussenius Maximus.

EDCS-24900536.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iostara lived 55 years. Visurcuris made this.
EDCS-24900536.summary: This is a funerary monument for Iostara, erected by Visurcuris.

EDCS-24900538.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Faonia Amia lived 20 years.
EDCS-24900538.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Faonia Amia.

EDCS-24900539.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Papirius Quadratus lived 70 years. Dativa, his wife, lived [unknown age].
EDCS-24900539.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Papirius Quadratus and his wife Dativa, recording the known lifespan of the former.

EDCS-24900540.translation: The house of Pomponius Nigrinus lived.
EDCS-24900540.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the death or a brief biographical note of Pomponius Nigrinus.

EDCS-24900542.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibius Sergius Victor lived 78 years, priest of Saturn.
EDCS-24900542.summary: A funerary inscription for Vibius Sergius Victor, who lived 78 years and served as a priest of Saturn.

EDCS-24900544.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Marcus lived 85 years and 4 months. His heir made and dedicated this.
EDCS-24900544.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Marcus, stating his age at death and that his heir erected the monument.

EDCS-24900547.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Ilius Madis lived 30 years and 4 months.
EDCS-24900547.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Ilius Madis, recording his age at death.

EDCS-24900548.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fundonata lived 75 years, her heirs made this. Sacred to the departed spirits. Egnatius Geminus lived 95 years, his heirs made this.
EDCS-24900548.summary: A funerary inscription for Fundonata and Egnatius Geminus, whose heirs erected the monuments after their deaths.

EDCS-24900550.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Quintus lived [for...] years, his heirs [made this].
EDCS-24900550.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Iulius Quintus, indicating his heirs were responsible for the monument.

EDCS-24900551.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Nina lived 70 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Celius Arisus lived 74 years, his heirs made this.
EDCS-24900551.summary: A funerary inscription recording the ages of Iulia Nina (70) and Celius Arisus (74), with the latter's heirs responsible for the monument.

EDCS-24900552.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilius Macedo lived 60 years. Ioagima. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-24900552.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for Aemilius Macedo who lived 60 years, with another name (Ioagima) also present.

EDCS-24900553.translation: In the fourth year, the Ides of May. Twelve days before the Kalends of July. Four days before the Ides of February. [He/She] died on the tenth day before the Kalends of February. Sperantia, the Ides of July. Victorinus, the Nones of September. Victoria, the seventh day before the Kalends of November.
EDCS-24900553.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of significant dates or obits, possibly for a family or community, with multiple individuals and specific dates mentioned.

EDCS-24900554.translation: Table of Silarus, the best faith which rejoices
EDCS-24900554.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, possibly related to a table or a dedication of good faith, with incomplete words.

EDCS-24900555.summary: This inscription records a table dedicated to Numerius and Saculcius.

EDCS-24900556.translation: Table of Iulius. He lived 75 years.
EDCS-24900556.summary: This inscription records a table for Iulius, noting his lifespan of 75 years.

EDCS-24900557.translation: Felicis Fallenis made this table for Silve Dertenellorteus; may he live with the god.
EDCS-24900557.summary: This inscription records a table made by Felicis Fallenis for Silve Dertenellorteus, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-24900559.summary: This inscription records a table dedicated to Fidentius and Sabina.

EDCS-24900562.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Iulius Salo, aide to a tribune, lived 80 years.
EDCS-24900562.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Gaius Iulius Salo, an aide to a tribune, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-24900563.translation: Table of Saturninus, stone from Caesarea, province of Sitifensis. He died in the Paludensis year, the Nones of March, in the provincial year 380.
EDCS-24900563.summary: This inscription records the death of Saturninus, noting his origin from Caesarea and the date of his death in provincial year 380.

EDCS-24900565.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Volusia Saturnina lived 38 years. Lucius Rod Felix, her husband, made this.
EDCS-24900565.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Volusia Saturnina at age 38, made by her husband.

EDCS-24900567.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Valeria Victorina lived [life]. Iulius Clarus, her husband, made this for his deserving wife from his own property and dedicated it happily.
EDCS-24900567.summary: This funerary inscription records that Iulius Clarus dedicated a monument to his deserving wife, Valeria Victorina.

EDCS-24900574.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Valeria Pudentila lived 30 years. Sentius Iulianus, standard-bearer, for his wife.
EDCS-24900574.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Valeria Pudentila at age 30, dedicated by her husband Sentius Iulianus.

EDCS-24900575.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Calpurnius lived [life] years.
EDCS-24900575.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Calpurnius, though the number of years is missing.

EDCS-24900576.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Celsus lived for 16 years. Donatus lived for 12 years.
EDCS-24900576.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the ages at death of Celsus and Donatus.

EDCS-24900577.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Trania, wife of Aurelius Donatus, lived for 31 years.
EDCS-24900577.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Iulia Trania, dedicated by her husband Aurelius Donatus.

EDCS-24900578.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sosius Ianuarius lived for 60 years.
EDCS-24900578.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Sosius Ianuarius.

EDCS-24900579.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tannia Fortuna lived for six years and four months.
EDCS-24900579.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Tannia Fortuna.

EDCS-24900580.translation: Ulpia Faustina lived for 7 years. F. Vusnus Marcus Leti Ans.
EDCS-24900580.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Ulpia Faustina, with additional names that are difficult to fully interpret.

EDCS-24900584.translation: Gaius Julius Hospes made this sacred offering to the Third Augustan Legion.
EDCS-24900584.summary: This is a dedication to the Third Augustan Legion by Gaius Julius Hospes.

EDCS-24900596.translation: This city of Nova Iustiniana Zabi was built from the foundations during the reign of our most pious and invincible lord.
EDCS-24900596.summary: This inscription records the founding of the city of Nova Iustiniana Zabi during a specific imperial period.

EDCS-24900598.translation: To the Holy God Hercules, Gaius Fabius Donatus, Lucius Syrtius Narcissus, Marcus Julius Castus, Titus Pompeius Saturninus (as duovirs), Gaius Egnaitus Maximus, and Gaius Stertinius Donatus (as perpetual priests) erected and dedicated this altar at their own expense.
EDCS-24900598.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Hercules by a group of local magistrates and priests, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25001016.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Aemilius, son of Caius, of the Horatia tribe, Felix, lived for 10 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25001016.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Caius Aemilius Felix, who is buried here.

EDCS-25001017.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cornelius, son of Publius, of the Arnensis tribe, Rogatus, lived for 75 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25001017.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Lucius Cornelius Rogatus, who is buried here.

EDCS-25001019.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gargilia Prisca lived 73 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Clodius Secundus lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001019.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and ages of two individuals, Gargilia Prisca and Gaius Clodius Secundus.

EDCS-25001021.translation: Iulia Donata, daughter of Publius, lived 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001021.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and age of Iulia Donata.

EDCS-25001022.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Fundana, daughter of Caius, lived 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001022.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and age of Iulia Fundana.

EDCS-25001024.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aulus Iulius Ilaricus, son of Caius, lived 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001024.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and age of Aulus Iulius Ilaricus.

EDCS-25001025.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Iulius Meridianus lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001025.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and age of Gaius Iulius Meridianus.

EDCS-25001026.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Iulius Peregrinus lived 61 years.
EDCS-25001026.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Marcus Iulius Peregrinus.

EDCS-25001027.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Priscus, son of Iulius from Arnens.
EDCS-25001027.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and filiation of Priscus.

EDCS-25001028.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Lucceius Victor, son of Publius, lived 13 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001028.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and age of Marcus Lucceius Victor.

EDCS-25001029.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maenia Maxima, daughter of Sextus, lived 75 years. Gaius Iulius Donatus lived 75 years.
EDCS-25001029.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespans of Maenia Maxima and Gaius Iulius Donatus.

EDCS-25001030.translation: Annius Victor Martialis, son of Publius, lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001030.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial and age of Annius Victor Martialis.

EDCS-25001031.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Messia Rogata lived 65 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25001031.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Messia Rogata.

EDCS-25001032.translation: To the departed spirits. Nice Mellite lived 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25001032.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Nice Mellite.

EDCS-25001033.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Numisius Felix lived 7 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25001033.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Marcus Numisius Felix, a child.

EDCS-25001034.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Caius Octavius Felix.

EDCS-25001035.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Percennia Romana, a dutiful and most devout woman, lived 32 years.
EDCS-25001035.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and virtues of Percennia Romana.

EDCS-25001036.translation: Percennia made this sacred offering to the departed spirits. Iulia Secunda lived 100 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25001036.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Iulia Secunda, erected by Percennia.

EDCS-25001037.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rutilia Marcia, daughter of Marcus, lived 90 years.
EDCS-25001037.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Rutilia Marcia, noting her parentage.

EDCS-25001038.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tadia Extricata lived 10 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25001038.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Tadia Extricata, a child.

EDCS-25001040.translation: [...]llis lived 100 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25001040.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of an individual, likely named 'llis'.

EDCS-25001042.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Priscus, identifying him as a son.

EDCS-25001048.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Valerius Modestus, son of Marcus, lived 35 years. Marcus Valerius Crescens placed his most devoted brother here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25001048.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Valerius Modestus, set up by his brother Marcus Valerius Crescens, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001057.translation: The boundary of lands was publicly set up by Gargilius Goddeus, a decurion, by order of the most excellent vir perfectissimus Iucundus Peregrinus, our governor, between the territory of Aurelium and a private estate in the provincial year 100.
EDCS-25001057.summary: This inscription records the public demarcation of land boundaries, likely for administrative or tax purposes, during the Roman Imperial period, possibly in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25001058.translation: The boundary lines of the Matidia estates are assigned to the colonists of the castle of Turra by order of the excellent vir egregius Axius Aelianus, procurator of the private rationes of our lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, through the land surveyor Caecilius Martialis.
EDCS-25001058.summary: This is an imperial rescript assigning land boundaries for colonists, related to the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, likely in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25001059.translation: By the generosity of Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, the boundaries have been assigned to the Numidian people through Gaius Petronius Celer, procurator of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis.
EDCS-25001059.summary: This inscription records the assignment of territorial boundaries to the Numidian people, authorized by Emperor Hadrian and administered by his procurator, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25001060.translation: By the generosity of Emperor Caesar Hadrian Augustus, the boundaries have been assigned to the Numidian people through Gaius Petronius Celer, procurator of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis.
EDCS-25001060.summary: This inscription records the assignment of territorial boundaries to the Numidian people, authorized by Emperor Hadrian and administered by his procurator, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25001061.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Aufidius Valerianus lived 73 years in the provincial year 260. Puoma Quintilla lived 75 years in the provincial year 288. The children made this.
EDCS-25001061.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Aufidius Valerianus and Puoma Quintilla, likely a married couple, with dates given in provincial years, suggesting a Roman North African context.

EDCS-25001062.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Clemens, perpetual flamen Augusti, lived 64 years. Flavia Secunda lived 51 years. Their son Aurelius Emeritus dedicated this.
EDCS-25001062.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Aurelius Clemens and Flavia Secunda, set up by their son, indicating their civic and religious status in a Roman provincial town.

EDCS-25001063.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelia Mazica, daughter of Titus, lived 16 years. A virgin, buried here.
EDCS-25001063.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young, unmarried woman named Aelia Mazica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001064.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Felix lived 95 years. Claudia Saturnina lived 55 years.
EDCS-25001064.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aemilius Felix and Claudia Saturnina, likely a couple, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001065.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Arrania Fausta lived 90 years. Rozona Crescens lived 61 years. Cai Rozoni Calvi lived 83 years. Rozoni Macrini lived [X] years.
EDCS-25001065.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespans of at least three individuals, possibly related, from a Roman provincial context, with incomplete information for the last person.

EDCS-25001066.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Surilla lived.
EDCS-25001066.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelia Surilla.

EDCS-25001067.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Aurelius Fortunatus lived 85 years.
EDCS-25001067.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Aurelius Fortunatus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25001068.translation: M. Ancirenius lived 42 years in the 242nd year of the province.
EDCS-25001068.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of M. Ancirenius and dating it to the 242nd year of the province.

EDCS-25001069.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Velia Felix lived 21 years.
EDCS-25001069.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Velia Felix, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-25001073.translation: Gaius Numinius Secundus willingly fulfilled his vow to the divine powers.
EDCS-25001073.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Numinius Secundus.

EDCS-25001075.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ciratis.
EDCS-25001075.summary: This is a very brief funerary dedication to Ciratis.

EDCS-25001076.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Appius Crescens lived 115 years.
EDCS-25001076.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Appius Crescens, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25001078.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Zaio Sturnini lived 103 years.
EDCS-25001078.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Zaio Sturnini, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25001079.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Victor lived 110 years. Rhodope, his daughter, lived 30 years. Maurosa placed this in memory of them.
EDCS-25001079.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Maurosa for Victor and Rhodope, recording their lifespans.

EDCS-25001080.translation: Lucius Percinius Stellatina Rogatus, son of Lucius, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25001080.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Percinius Stellatina Rogatus.

EDCS-25001084.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaius Julius Honoratus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, a Roman knight, lived 23 years.
EDCS-25001084.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Julius Honoratus, a Roman knight, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-25001085.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulius Nitlarcy, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, honored with the public horse, lived 23 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001085.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Iulius Nitlarcy, who was honored with the public horse and died at the age of 23.

EDCS-25001086.translation: To Sextus Lucretius Rogatus, son of Sextus, of the Romilia tribe, a decurion promoted to the senatorial order, according to his will, his freedman Lucretus Felix and his heir placed and dedicated this with 6,000 sesterces.
EDCS-25001086.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by a freedman and heir to Sextus Lucretius Rogatus, a decurion who was promoted to a higher rank, funded by his will.

EDCS-25001089.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Acria Tertia, daughter of Quintus, lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001089.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Acria Tertia, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-25001091.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Aemilius Felix, son of Lucius, lived 15 years.
EDCS-25001091.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Publius Aemilius Felix at the age of 15.

EDCS-25001092.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Aemilius ER HO, son of Lucius, lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001092.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Lucius Aemilius, who lived to be 75 years old.

EDCS-25001093.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Aemilius Honoratus, son of Publius, of the Horatia tribe, lived 29 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001093.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Lucius Aemilius Honoratus, who died at the age of 29.

EDCS-25001094.translation: Gaius Aemilius Hordotus, son of Lucius, lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001094.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Gaius Aemilius Hordotus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-25001095.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Aemilius Kapito, son of Lucius, of the Horatia tribe, lived 25 years; he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25001095.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Gaius Aemilius Kapito, who died at the age of 25, with a common epitaph wishing him peace.

EDCS-25001096.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aemilia Donata, daughter of Publius, lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001096.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Aemilia Donata, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-25001097.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aemilia, daughter of Publius, Sadufa, lived 10 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001097.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Aemilia Sadufa, who died at age 10.

EDCS-25001098.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Annius Felix, son of Marcus, of the Arnensian tribe, lived sixty-five years.
EDCS-25001098.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Gaius Annius Felix, who died at age 65.

EDCS-25001099.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Annius Fundanus, son of Marcus, lived 73 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001099.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Publius Annius Fundanus, who died at age 73.

EDCS-25001100.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Annonia Paula, his wife, an example to women, who died at only 33 years of age, leaving her husband in deep mourning, is buried here.
EDCS-25001100.summary: A funerary epitaph lamenting the early death of Annonia Paula, wife of the dedicator, who died at age 33.

EDCS-25001101.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aufustia Secunda lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001101.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Aufustia Secunda, who died at age 55.

EDCS-25001102.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Caecilius Crescens, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 3 years.
EDCS-25001102.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Marcus Caecilius Crescens, who died at age 3.

EDCS-25001103.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caecilius Felix, son of Marcus, of the Arnensian tribe, lived 60 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Modia Sura lived 33 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001103.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lives and burials of two individuals, Caecilius Felix and Modia Sura.

EDCS-25001104.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Caecilius Urbanus, son of Marcus, lived 57 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001104.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Lucius Caecilius Urbanus, who died at age 57.

EDCS-25001105.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caecilia Secunda, daughter of Marcus, lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001105.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Caecilia Secunda, who died at age 75.

EDCS-25001106.translation: Caesulenia Optata, daughter of Titus, lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001106.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life and burial of Caesulenia Optata, who died at age 40.

EDCS-25001107.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caprilia Donata lived for [some number of] years.
EDCS-25001107.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caprilia Donata, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-25001108.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Carfundana, daughter of Marcus, lived for 36 years.
EDCS-25001108.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Carfundana, daughter of Marcus, specifying her age at death.

EDCS-25001109.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Katlius, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-25001109.summary: This funerary epitaph identifies Publius Katlius, son of Publius, and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-25001110.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Cominia Prima lived for [some number of] years.
EDCS-25001110.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Cominia Prima, indicating her lifespan.

EDCS-25001111.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Cornelius Crescens, a freedman from Arnensium, lived for 36 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001111.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Publius Cornelius Crescens, a freedman from Arnensium, including his age and burial location.

EDCS-25001112.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Decimia Galla Palatina lived for 107 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001112.summary: This funerary epitaph for Decimia Galla Palatina states her advanced age of 107 years and her burial place.

EDCS-25001113.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quinta, daughter of Quintus, lived for 95 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001113.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Quinta, daughter of Quintus, as 95 years, and notes her burial.

EDCS-25001114.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Victoria, daughter of Quintus, lived for 60 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001114.summary: This funerary epitaph for Flavia Victoria, daughter of Quintus, states she lived for 60 years and is buried in this location.

EDCS-25001115.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Urbana, daughter of Publius, lived for 57 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001115.summary: This funerary epitaph for Flavia Urbana, daughter of Publius, records her age at death as 57 years and her burial site.

EDCS-25001116.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. In these places, the bones of Florus are buried, snatched away by a pitiable death in his first youth, taken to the lower realms of Dis and the groves of the blessed, whom the learned Thalia had adorned with studies; and when he had twice already reached almost twenty years, lest Lachesis might break the short threads of the spindle; alas, why is it permitted to no one to break the harsh decrees of the Fates or escape their cruel destinies? He is buried here.
EDCS-25001116.summary: This complex funerary epitaph laments the death of Florus, a young man known for his studies, who died before reaching his 40th birthday, and states his burial here.

EDCS-25001117.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Fonteius.
EDCS-25001117.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the underworld by Caius Fonteius.

EDCS-25001118.translation: Furdana lived for 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001118.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Furdana, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-25001119.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Furnia Fortuna, daughter of Quintus, lived for 13 years and 2 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001119.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Furnia Fortuna, daughter of Quintus, at the age of 13 years and 2 months.

EDCS-25001120.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gargilia.

EDCS-25001121.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Heudes lived for 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001121.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Heudes, who lived for 25 years.

EDCS-25001122.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Iulius Crescens, son of Caius, lived for 46 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001122.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Iulius Crescens, son of Caius, who lived for 46 years.

EDCS-25001123.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Iulius Martialis, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 55 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001123.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Iulius Martialis, son of Caius and from the Quirina tribe, who lived for 55 years.

EDCS-25001124.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Felicitas lived for 45 years.
EDCS-25001124.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Iulia Felicitas, who lived for 45 years.

EDCS-25001125.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Flora Rogata lived.
EDCS-25001125.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the spirits of the underworld and records the name of Iulia Flora Rogata.

EDCS-25001126.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Maenius Felix, son of Publius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived for 5 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001126.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Caius Maenius Felix, son of Publius and from the Arnensis tribe, who lived for 5 years.

EDCS-25001127.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Marcius Victor, son of Marcus, of the Arnensian tribe, lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001127.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Marcus Marcius Victor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001128.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Maxi, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001130.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Minucia Faustina, daughter of Caius, lived 66 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Felix, son of Quintus, of the Fabian tribe, lived 73 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001130.summary: A double funerary epitaph recording the lifespans and burials of Minucia Faustina and Quintus Iulius Felix, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001131.translation: [...lia], daughter of Lucius, Mustia, lived 25 years.
EDCS-25001131.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Mustia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period, with a partially lost name.

EDCS-25001132.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Nonius Crescens, son of Marcus, of the Arnensian tribe, lived 19 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001132.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Marcus Nonius Crescens, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001133.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Percenia, daughter of Caius, of the Ponhoe tribe, lived 62 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001133.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Percenia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001134.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Petronius Nepos, son of Lucius, of the Cornelia tribe, lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001134.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Lucius Petronius Nepos, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001135.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Petronius Pacatus, son of Lucius, of the Cornelia tribe, lived 78 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25001135.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Quintus Petronius Pacatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001136.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Petronia Domnula, daughter of Lucius, lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001136.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Petronia Domnula, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001137.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Petronia, daughter of Lucius, Fu, lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25001137.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Petronia Fu, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001138.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Pomus Secundus lived 53 years.
EDCS-25001138.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life span of Gaius Pomus Secundus.

EDCS-25001139.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pompeia Postuma Servata, daughter of Marcus, lived 40 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001139.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life span and burial place of Pompeia Postuma Servata.

EDCS-25001140.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pomponia Donata lived 25 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001140.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life span and burial place of Pomponia Donata.

EDCS-25001141.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pontia Tertia, daughter of Publius, lived 65 years and is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
