EDCS-25001141.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life span and burial place of Pontia Tertia, with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-25001142.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. She was named Papinia Quinta, beloved daughter of Papinius, whom her mother bore, joined to an exceptional husband, from whom, a descendant of a Roman equestrian, Julia shone; when she reached thirty years of age, she yielded to fate and holds this tomb.
EDCS-25001142.summary: This is a poetic funerary epitaph commemorating Papinia Quinta, detailing her family connections and lifespan.

EDCS-25001143.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Sempronius Libertio lived 50 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001143.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life span and burial place of Quintus Sempronius Libertio.

EDCS-25001144.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tadia Quinta, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-25001144.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tadia Quinta, daughter of Caius, likely incomplete.

EDCS-25001145.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tadia Honorata, daughter of Sextus, lived 25 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001145.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life span and burial place of Tadia Honorata.

EDCS-25001146.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Turius Nepotianus, son of Caius, lived 4 years and 6 months and is buried here.
EDCS-25001146.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life span and burial place of Gaius Turius Nepotianus.

EDCS-25001147.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Viator lived 18 years and is buried here.
EDCS-25001147.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life span and burial place of Viator.

EDCS-25001148.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Victoria lived 45 years.
EDCS-25001148.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Victoria, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-25001149.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Vetustina lived 40 years; she rests here.
EDCS-25001149.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Ulpia Vetustina, who lived 40 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25001150.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Vo[---] Caius [---].
EDCS-25001150.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning Gaius Vo[---] Caius [---].

EDCS-25100006.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Eanda lived 75 years; she rests here.
EDCS-25100006.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Eanda, who lived 75 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25100007.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] lived 100 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25100007.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of an individual who lived 100 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25100008.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Evus lived 15 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25100008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Evus, who lived 15 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25100009.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Arnensis lived 10 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25100009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Arnensis, who lived 10 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25100012.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [My] son lived 8 years.
EDCS-25100012.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a son who lived 8 years.

EDCS-25100013.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---]us lived 52 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25100013.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of an individual named [---]us who lived 52 years and is buried here.

EDCS-25100017.translation: To the spirits of the departed. IIII M Dionia, daughter of Aulus, I RO [---].
EDCS-25100017.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph identifying Dionia, daughter of Aulus, possibly with a numerical indicator and place name.

EDCS-25100019.translation: To Sit and the Arnensian Pere.
EDCS-25100019.summary: This inscription is a dedication to deities or individuals, likely from an altar or shrine, with the names Sit, Arnensi, and Pere.

EDCS-25100020.translation: To the spirits of the departed Chei.
EDCS-25100020.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the deified spirits of the deceased named Chei.

EDCS-25100021.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed; Caius Varitidius Donatus lived 45 years.
EDCS-25100021.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Varitidius Donatus, who lived for 45 years.

EDCS-25100023.translation: Sextus Cornelius Dexter, son of Sextus, of Arnensium, legal official of Alexandria, dedicated this gift to Neptune Augustus.
EDCS-25100023.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Neptune Augustus by Sextus Cornelius Dexter, a legal official in Alexandria, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25100025.translation: To King Ptolemy, son of King Juba.
EDCS-25100025.summary: This inscription is a dedication to King Ptolemy, son of King Juba, likely from the Roman period when the kingdom of Mauretania was influenced by Rome.

EDCS-25100028.translation: To the divine spirit of Augustus, Caesar, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of the Parthians, greatest of the Britons, greatest of the Germans, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul for the fourth time, [in the province of] Mauretania Caesariensis in the year...
EDCS-25100028.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicating honors to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, likely a milestone or a dedication from the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, dating to his fourth consulship (around 161-180 CE).

EDCS-25100029.translation: To the most divine and blessed prince, our lord Flavius Julius Constantius, most noble Caesar; Flavius Terentianus, most excellent governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis and Sitifensis, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-25100029.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Flavius Julius Constantius, the Caesar, by Flavius Terentianus, governor of Mauretania Caesariensis and Sitifensis, likely dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-25100030.translation: To the most felicitous and blessed prince, our lord Flavius Julius Constantius, most noble Caesar; Flavius Terentianus, most excellent governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis and Sitifensis, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-25100030.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Flavius Julius Constantius, the Caesar, by Flavius Terentianus, governor of Mauretania Caesariensis and Sitifensis, likely dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-25100032.translation: To Sextus Cornelius Dextro Maximus, son of Sextus, of Arnensium, procurator of Asia, legal official of Alexandria, procurator of Neapolis and Mausoleum, prefect of the Syrian fleet, honored with military gifts by the deified Hadrian for the Jewish War with a pure spear and vexillum, prefect of the I Augusta Gemini twin cavalry, tribune of the VIII Augusta legion, prefect of the V Raetorum cohort, prefect of the Fabri (engineers) three times, patron of the colony; Publius Blaesius Felix, centurion of the II Traiana Fortis legion, devoted to his most pious relative for his merits.
EDCS-25100032.summary: This extensive inscription honors Sextus Cornelius Dextro Maximus for his extensive military and administrative career, culminating in his patron status of the colony, and records the dedications made by Publius Blaesius Felix, a centurion and relative.

EDCS-25100033.translation: Sextus Cornelius Dexter Maximus, son of Lucius, of Arnensium, Roman eques, who had discharged all the honors of the fatherland, and Sextus Cornelius Dexter Petronianus, son of Sextus, of Arnensium, moved equestrian statues, collapsed from the forum due to their uncle's old age, to adorn the temple, and with permission of the town council, restored them with their own money and dedicated them.
EDCS-25100033.summary: This inscription records the restoration and rededication of two equestrian statues in the forum by Sextus Cornelius Dexter Maximus and Sextus Cornelius Dexter Petronianus to adorn a temple, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25100034.translation: To Julius Martialis, son of Caius, scribe of the aediles' decuria, who lived 25 years, a citizen of V A, to my sweetest one.
EDCS-25100034.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Julius Martialis, a scribe of the aediles' decuria, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-25100036.translation: Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus, son of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, erected this for his most dutiful mother, Aurelia Laidis, freedwoman of Augustus, in a spot granted by the order; for this dedication, he distributed three victoriati to the decurions and Roman knights and presented circus games to the people.
EDCS-25100036.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus for his mother, Aurelia Laidis, including public benefactions, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25100037.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Petronius Modestus, son of Marcus, of the Stellatina tribe, having attained all honors, lived 91 years.
EDCS-25100037.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Marcus Petronius Modestus, who lived 91 years and had achieved many honors.

EDCS-25100038.translation: Quintus Pomponius Crispinus, son of Marcus, of the Collina tribe, twice chief magistrate, prefect of the second court, quinquennial magistrate, military tribune, lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25100038.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Quintus Pomponius Crispinus, detailing his various public offices and his age at death, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-25100039.translation: Marcus Pomponius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Collina tribe, twice chief magistrate, quinquennial pontiff, lived 32 years.
EDCS-25100039.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Marcus Pomponius Maximus, detailing his public offices and his age at death, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-25100040.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Antonius Similis, son of Marcus, lived 7 years. Merianus is buried here.
EDCS-25100040.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Lucius Antonius Similis, who died at the age of 7.

EDCS-25100041.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Maxsima, daughter of Caius, lived 4 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25100041.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Aurelia Maxsima, who died at the age of 4.

EDCS-25100042.translation: To the departed spirits. Blaesius Ianuarius lived 6 years.
EDCS-25100042.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Blaesius Ianuarius, who died at the age of 6.

EDCS-25100043.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Caecilius Mercurius lived 53 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25100043.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Marcus Caecilius Mercurius, who lived 53 years.

EDCS-25100044.translation: Publius Clodius Felix lived 85 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25100044.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Publius Clodius Felix, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-25100045.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clodia Maiora lived for 1 year.
EDCS-25100045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia Maiora, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-25100046.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cominia Postuma, daughter of Lucius, lived for 11 years.
EDCS-25100046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cominia Postuma, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-25100047.translation: Lucius Cornelius Gemellus, son of Caius, of the Collina tribe, lived for 71 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25100047.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius Gemellus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25100048.translation: Cornelia Secunda, daughter of Lucius, lived for 5 years.
EDCS-25100048.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Secunda, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-25100049.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cornificius Victor, son of Quintus, lived for 11 years.
EDCS-25100049.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornificius Victor, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25100050.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Crescens, recording part of his lifespan.

EDCS-25100051.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Curius Castus lived for 71 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25100051.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Curius Castus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25100052.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Decius Martialis, son of Marcus, of the Falerna tribe, lived for 23 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25100052.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Decius Martialis, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25100053.translation: Iruius Felix lived for 4 years. Titus Fa...
EDCS-25100053.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Iruius Felix, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25100054.translation: To the memory of Quintus Fundilius Saturninus.
EDCS-25100054.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the memory of Quintus Fundilius Saturninus.

EDCS-25100055.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Herennius Achilleus lived 52 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25100055.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Lucius Herennius Achilleus, who lived 52 years.

EDCS-25100056.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Fortuna lived 85 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25100056.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Iulia Fortuna, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-25100057.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lelia Urbana, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-25100057.summary: A funerary inscription dedicating a burial to Lelia Urbana, daughter of Publius.

EDCS-25100058.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Licarius Mesor lived 1 year.
EDCS-25100058.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Quintus Licarius Mesor, who lived 1 year.

EDCS-25100059.translation: Lucretia Theodora, daughter of Antipater, lived 14 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25100059.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Lucretia Theodora, who lived 14 years.

EDCS-25100060.translation: To the Divine Shades. Octiana lived.
EDCS-25100060.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the name Octiana.

EDCS-25100061.translation: Orchivia, daughter of Lucius, Teria, lived 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25100061.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Orchivia Teria, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-25100062.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sextus Petronius Modestus, son of Publius, lived 65 years.
EDCS-25100062.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Sextus Petronius Modestus, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-25100063.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Pomponius Aemilia Crescens, son of Publius, lived 80 years. He is buried.
EDCS-25100063.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Marcus Pomponius Aemilia Crescens, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-25100064.translation: Pomponia Crispina, daughter of Quintus, lived 17 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25100064.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Pomponia Crispina, who lived 17 years.

EDCS-25100065.translation: Tia Prisca lived for 76 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25100065.summary: This is a tombstone for Tia Prisca, who lived for 76 years.

EDCS-25100066.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextilia Cae, daughter of Quintus, lived for 20 years.
EDCS-25100066.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Sextilia Cae, daughter of Quintus, who lived for 20 years.

EDCS-25100067.translation: Gnaeus Terentius Secundus, son of Gnaeus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for 83 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25100067.summary: This is a tombstone for Gnaeus Terentius Secundus, who lived for 83 years.

EDCS-25100068.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tongilia Laeta, daughter of Caius, lived for 85 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25100068.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Tongilia Laeta, daughter of Caius, who lived for 85 years.

EDCS-25100069.translation: Lucius Valerius Exoratus, son of Publius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived for...
EDCS-25100069.summary: This is an incomplete tombstone for Lucius Valerius Exoratus, son of Publius, of the Arnensis tribe.

EDCS-25100070.translation: Quintus Vatronius Rogatus, son of Quintus, of the Arnensis tribe, lived for 23 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25100070.summary: This tombstone is for Quintus Vatronius Rogatus, son of Quintus, of the Arnensis tribe, who lived for 23 years.

EDCS-25100071.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Veiania Satura, daughter of Publius, lived for 50 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25100071.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Veiania Satura, daughter of Publius, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-25100072.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vicellia Exorata lived for 30 years.
EDCS-25100072.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Vicellia Exorata, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-25100073.translation: ...niae, daughter of Quintus, his wife, to [---] worthy. ...
EDCS-25100073.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely related to a funerary dedication to a wife.

EDCS-25100075.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that includes a possible name (Iuratina) and numerical information.

EDCS-25100077.translation: Laen, daughter of Sa, his wife.
EDCS-25100077.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the relationship of a woman named Laen to her father Sa and her husband.

EDCS-25100082.translation: Vercien, son of Nerdoc, a princeps, lived...
EDCS-25100082.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life of Vercien, son of Nerdoc, who held the title of princeps.

EDCS-25100083.translation: Quintus Asellius Cassianus erected this, fulfilling a vow to Holy Hygieia.
EDCS-25100083.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Hygieia, set up by Quintus Asellius Cassianus to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-25100085.translation: To the departed spirits. Crepereia Flora lived for 73 years, 6 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-25100085.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Crepereia Flora, detailing her lifespan.

EDCS-25100086.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Iulius Primus, also called Iamcar, lived for 47 years.
EDCS-25100086.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Primus, also known as Iamcar, detailing his lifespan.

EDCS-25100087.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Secunda lived for 51 years.
EDCS-25100087.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Secunda, detailing her lifespan.

EDCS-25100088.translation: To the departed spirits. Modi, son of Secundus Latatis, erected this for his deserving father, who lived for 20 years.
EDCS-25100088.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Modi for his father, detailing the father's lifespan and Modi's filial piety.

EDCS-25100090.translation: Julius Anabus fulfilled his vow to the god Charon.
EDCS-25100090.summary: This is a votive inscription by Julius Anabus to the god Charon.

EDCS-25100091.translation: Magius Maximus, a classicus centurion, lived for 60 years and 2 months, and Rogata Fabricia, daughter of Proculus, wife of Magius Maximus, a centurion from Caesariensis, are buried here. Magius Maximus for himself and his wife...
EDCS-25100091.summary: This is a joint funerary inscription for a centurion, Magius Maximus, and his wife Rogata Fabricia, indicating it was set up by the husband for them both.

EDCS-25100092.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely refers to a military unit or individual within a military context.

EDCS-25100093.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Petronius Vales, veteran, lived 80 years. Longinia Ammonus [made this] for her most devoted uncle and son.
EDCS-25100093.summary: This is a tombstone for Petronius Vales, a veteran who lived 80 years, with a dedication by Longinia Ammonus.

EDCS-25100094.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Plotius Modestus, decurion of the Miliaria cavalry wing, lived 23 years and served 4 years. His father and mother, both Spanish, made this monument for their most devoted son. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25100094.summary: This tombstone records the life and service of Lucius Plotius Modestus, a decurion in the cavalry, and was erected by his parents.

EDCS-25100097.translation: In memory of her husband, Valerius Silvanus, trierarch. Celia Monnata [erected this] for her surviving husband; his pyre lived 41 years, 5 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-25100097.summary: This epitaph commemorates Valerius Silvanus, a trierarch, and records his age at death, erected by his wife Celia Monnata.

EDCS-25100098.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Vereius Victor, soldier of the fourth cohort of Sugambri, a singular foot soldier in the centuria of Florus, served 7 years, lived 30 years. Cornelius Maximus and Quintus Vereius Viator, his heirs, [made this] for the well-deserving.
EDCS-25100098.summary: This tombstone records the military service and life span of Vereius Victor, a soldier in the fourth cohort, and was erected by his heirs.

EDCS-25100099.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Ulpius Abelas, veteran, lived 75 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25100099.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Ulpius Abelas, a veteran who lived 75 years.

EDCS-25100101.translation: To the commander of Legion A
EDCS-25100101.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a commander of Legion A, likely a dedication.

EDCS-25100102.translation: Nico Crescen [erected this] for his most innocent husband, curator of the estate of R.N.M.A. He served 5 years.
EDCS-25100102.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Nico Crescen to his husband, who was an estate curator and served for 5 years.

EDCS-25100103.translation: Domitius Alexander of Caesarea, having been honored with the public horse.
EDCS-25100103.summary: This inscription records the honor bestowed upon Domitius Alexander of Caesarea, likely a political or military distinction.

EDCS-25100104.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Junius Asclepiades, priest, made [this] for his sons.
EDCS-25100104.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Junius Asclepiades, a priest, for his sons.

EDCS-25100105.translation: To Gaius Egnatius Quirinus Claudianus, decurion of the colony of Claudius Caesariensium, lived 42 years. Caesenia Mar [erected this].
EDCS-25100105.summary: This tombstone records the life of Gaius Egnatius Quirinus Claudianus, a decurion, and was erected by Caesenia Mar.

EDCS-25100106.translation: To Enio Fatalis, son of Caius, decurion of the most splendid colony of Caesarea, a pious priest of the most holy Spirit of the Mother of the Gods, Dendrophorus, most worthy.
EDCS-25100106.summary: This is a dedication to Enio Fatalis, a decurion and priest, likely from Caesarea.

EDCS-25100107.translation: To Gaius Julius Victor, son of Gaius, of the tribe Quirina, aedile, twice chief magistrate, twice quinquennial chief magistrate, by his friends with money collected, for his merits.
EDCS-25100107.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Julius Victor, who held multiple civic offices, erected by his friends.

EDCS-25100108.translation: Iulia Maximilla, daughter of Gaius, Flavia.
EDCS-25100108.summary: This is a fragmented inscription likely commemorating Iulia Maximilla, daughter of Gaius.

EDCS-25100109.translation: To Marcus Junius Modestus, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, aedile, chief magistrate, and priest of Augustus.
EDCS-25100109.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Junius Modestus, who served as aedile, chief magistrate, and priest of Augustus.

EDCS-25100110.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundinus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, decurion of the people of Caesarea, honored with the public horse, and having performed the rites of the Lupercalia, by Antonia Rufina, to her dearest son.
EDCS-25100110.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Antonia Rufina to her beloved son, Lucius Licinius Secundinus, a decurion of Caesarea.

EDCS-25100111.translation: To Lucius Licinius Secundinus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, decurion of the people of Caesarea, honored with the public horse, and having performed the rites of the Lupercalia, to his cousin.
EDCS-25100111.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Licinius Secundinus, a decurion of Caesarea, from his cousin.

EDCS-25100113.translation: To Proculus, son of Caius, of the tribe Quirina, honored with the public horse, by Iulia Lucilla, his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-25100113.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Iulia Lucilla to her son, Proculus, who was honored with the public horse.

EDCS-25100114.translation: To Sextus Valerius Munitus, son of Lucius, of the tribe Quirina, honored with the public horse, priest of the province, by the cultivators of the Father Liber, to their patron, for his merits, the innkeepers.
EDCS-25100114.summary: This inscription honors Sextus Valerius Munitus, a provincial priest and patron, for his merits, and was set up by innkeepers.

EDCS-25100115.translation: To the most excellent father, our father.
EDCS-25100115.summary: This is a fragmented dedication to an esteemed father figure.

EDCS-25100119.translation: Quintus Balienus arranged for this to be made: [---] died at the age of 50 years and 5 days. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-25100119.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Balienus, detailing his age at death and expressing a wish for peace.

EDCS-25100129.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aemilia Verna, of Tingis, lived 27 years. Avellius Flavus placed here his most devoted wife. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25100129.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aemilia Verna, erected by her husband Avellius Flavus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100130.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caecilia Iuliana, daughter of Maximi, of Gunugita, lived 15 years and 4 months. Quintus Memmius Exoratus made this for his excellent wife, who is placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25100130.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Iuliana, erected by her husband Quintus Memmius Exoratus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100131.translation: Marcus Cassius Severinus of Icositanus lived for...
EDCS-25100131.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for Marcus Cassius Severinus, providing his name and origin, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100132.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Claudio Publicio Fortunato, the temple attendant, lived 60 years. His wife Attica and his children Crescens, Ianuarius, and Fortunata placed here their most excellent father. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25100132.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudio Publicio Fortunatus, erected by his wife and children, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100135.translation: Iulia Mimesis, costume designer for the Ionicus and the Canonis, who lived 48 years, be placed here. May the earth lie lightly upon you and yours.
EDCS-25100135.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Mimesis, a theatre costume designer, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100136.translation: Faustus, a freedman, to Namphamon, freedman of Annobalis, the butcher, his highly deserving patron.
EDCS-25100136.summary: A dedication from a freedman to his patron, who was also a butcher, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100137.translation: Saburio, son of Antas, a glassmaker, lived six sweet months, is placed here.
EDCS-25100137.summary: A funerary epitaph for Saburio, son of Antas, a glassmaker, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100138.translation: Subsac, mother of Eutice, a chambermaid, is placed here.
EDCS-25100138.summary: A funerary epitaph for Subsac, mother of Eutice, a chambermaid, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100139.summary: An inscription on an ossuary identifying the deceased as Vitlius, a sausage-maker, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100140.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aelius Aquestor lived 18 years, 11 months, 5 days, and 3 hours. Aelius Fructuosus lived 17 years and 46 days, until the first hour of the day. Twin brothers who loved each other. Zeta, father, for his sons.
EDCS-25100140.summary: A funerary epitaph for twin brothers Aelius Aquestor and Aelius Fructuosus, mentioning their father Zeta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100141.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Processianus, freedman of our emperors, lived 16 years, 2 months, and 25 days. Publius Aelius Pecuarius laid here his most dutiful son, may the earth lie lightly on him.
EDCS-25100141.summary: A tombstone for Publius Aelius Processianus, set up by his father, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100142.summary: A dedication to Aelia Fausta and Secunda, likely daughters, possibly from a tombstone, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100143.translation: To Aelia Ingenua, who lived 2 years, 11 months, and 16 days. Aelius Decimus [set this up] for his sweetest daughter.
EDCS-25100143.summary: A tombstone for Aelia Ingenua, set up by her father Aelius Decimus, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100144.translation: Aelius Silvanus made this for his most cherished sister, the most innocent Aelia Silvana, a virtuous girl, for her outstanding devotion to him, a well-deserving [sister].
EDCS-25100144.summary: A tombstone for Aelia Silvana, set up by her brother Aelius Silvanus due to her devotion, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100145.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Aemilius Putator lived 48 years. Bassia Bassina [set this up] for her most worthy husband. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Aemilius Bassinus Putator the younger lived 16 years, 7 months, and 3 days. Bassia Bassina [set this up] for her sweetest son.
EDCS-25100145.summary: A tombstone for Caius Aemilius Putator and his son Caius Aemilius Bassinus Putator the younger, set up by Bassia Bassina, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100146.translation: Be happy, you who speak to Ammon. Greetings, may the earth lie lightly on you forever, so that your bones may rest gently.
EDCS-25100146.summary: A funerary epigram invoking Ammon, likely from a tomb, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100147.translation: Lucius Annius Optatus for his son from Numidia.
EDCS-25100147.summary: A dedication by Lucius Annius Optatus for his son from Numidia, possibly from a tombstone, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100148.translation: Atilia Chloe, mother, for her son Redemptus, lived eight months.
EDCS-25100148.summary: A tombstone for Redemptus, who lived eight months, set up by his mother Atilia Chloe, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100149.translation: Atm Baribal, son, lived five [years?]; he was snatched away from his own [people].
EDCS-25100149.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription for Atm Baribal, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100153.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Philumene made this for her daughter Aurelia Maura, who lived 14 years, 9 months, and 16 days, a well-deserving [daughter].
EDCS-25100153.summary: A tombstone for Aurelia Maura, set up by her mother Aurelia Philumene, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25100154.translation: Marcus Barbatius Pandarus, freedman of Marcus, is laid here.
EDCS-25100154.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Barbatius Pandarus, identified as a freedman.

EDCS-25100155.translation: Beatilla, daughter of Messius, aged 25, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-25100155.summary: This is a tombstone for Beatilla, daughter of Messius, who died at the age of 25, with a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-25100156.translation: Amicus, son of Bostaris, lived 4 years.
EDCS-25100156.summary: This inscription records the short life of a child named Amicus, son of Bostaris, who died at the age of 4.

EDCS-25100157.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Quintus Carusius Valens, most innocent son. Caius Carusius Valens, father, and Iulia Cara, mother, [erected this] for Cuccuri.
EDCS-25100157.summary: This funerary dedication by parents mourns their innocent son, Quintus Carusius Valens, and likely mentions another individual, Cuccuri.

EDCS-25100159.translation: Cinnamius, son of Cinnamius, most sweet, lived 1 year, 9 months, and 26 days. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-25100159.summary: This is a tombstone for Cinnamius, son of Cinnamius, detailing his lifespan of just under two years and expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-25100161.translation: Claudia Agape lived 2 years, 4 months, and 12 days. She is laid here. Claudius Faustus, freedman, and Aelia Aurelia [erected this] for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-25100161.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young girl, Claudia Agape, and was erected by her presumed parents or guardians, Claudius Faustus and Aelia Aurelia.

EDCS-25100162.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Coelius Marcellinus lived 50 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-25100162.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Coelius Marcellinus, who lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-25100163.translation: To the Divine Manes. Coelia Restituta lived 75 years. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-25100163.summary: This funerary dedication is for Coelia Restituta, who lived 75 years, with a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-25100164.translation: Publius Cornelius Dammaeus lived 90 years. He is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. His wife, with his children, made [this] for their husband who well deserved of them.
EDCS-25100164.summary: This tombstone commemorates Publius Cornelius Dammaeus, who lived to 90, and was erected by his wife and children in recognition of his merits.

EDCS-25100165.translation: Crestus, decurion of the guard corps. Aulus' wife set [this] up for him for his merits.
EDCS-25100165.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by the wife of Aulus, honoring Crestus, a decurion of the guard corps, for his merits.

EDCS-25100166.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Divitia lived 37 years, 4 months. Iunia Ianuaria, her mother, made this for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-25100166.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Divitia, set up by her mother, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100167.translation: Domitius Montanus lived 52 years, 6 months, 6 hours.
EDCS-25100167.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the lifespan of Domitius Montanus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100168.translation: For the well-deserving Domitia Honerata, who lived 6 years, 8 months, 16 days, and for Iulia Primosa, for Domitia Rustica, who lived 16 years, 11 months, 25 days. Domitius Honoratus, father, for his most dutiful daughters.
EDCS-25100168.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the deaths of two young girls, Domitia Honerata and Iulia Primosa, set up by their father Domitius Honoratus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100170.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Euschemon Phosphoros, his most devoted grandson.
EDCS-25100170.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicating a funerary monument to Euschemon Phosphoros, the grandson of the dedicator, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100171.translation: Eutactus made this for Tertia, his wife, for her merits.
EDCS-25100171.summary: A tombstone inscription recording Eutactus' memorial to his wife Tertia, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100173.translation: Statu, freed by his father, remitted.
EDCS-25100173.summary: A legal inscription recording the emancipation of a slave named Statu by his father, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100175.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Faustus rests here. He lived 1 year, 1 month.
EDCS-25100175.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Faustus at a very young age, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100176.translation: Flavius Clemens for his son A.
EDCS-25100176.summary: A fragmentary tombstone inscription indicating Flavius Clemens dedicated it to his son A., dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100178.translation: In this tomb is placed Ingenua, daughter of Flora, who lived 9 months, and for whom the funeral rites that were due to a child born to a parent were performed; her father, in adverse circumstances, himself gave these. May the earth lie light and fertile upon the bones, and may the ashes of the infant not burn with weight.
EDCS-25100178.summary: A tombstone inscription for a baby named Ingenua, composed by her grieving father, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100180.translation: Gallia Natalis lived 14 years. She rests here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25100180.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the death of Gallia Natalis at age 14, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100181.translation: To the Divine Manes. Geminius Priscinus lived for 5 years. Verius Priscus made this for his son. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-25100181.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a child, Geminius Priscinus, and made by his father, Verius Priscus.

EDCS-25100182.translation: She lived for 50 years and 19 days. Her most devoted husband made and dedicated this for Herennia Felicia.
EDCS-25100182.summary: A funerary epitaph for Herennia Felicia, commissioned by her husband.

EDCS-25100185.translation: To the Divine Manes. Honoratus made this for his most dutiful father, Honoratus. He lived for 49 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-25100185.summary: A funerary epitaph for Honoratus, made by his son, also named Honoratus.

EDCS-25100186.translation: Damianus for his incomparable wife, Honorata, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-25100186.summary: A dedication from Damianus to his wife, Honorata.

EDCS-25100187.translation: Aged 3 years, 5 months, and 10 days. The brothers Isentii Hyas and Se[--] made this for their most excellent sister.
EDCS-25100187.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl, made by her brothers.

EDCS-25100190.translation: To the Manes. Nic[--] for his most sweet mother, Iulia Lae. Felicianus and Iulius Cosconianus, sons.
EDCS-25100190.summary: A funerary dedication to Iulia Lae, made by her sons Felicianus and Iulius Cosconianus.

EDCS-25100191.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Julius Castus lived for 40 years, 10 months, and 8 days. Valeria Sympherusa, his wife, made this for him as one well-deserving. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-25100191.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Castus, erected by his wife Valeria Sympherusa.

EDCS-25100192.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Julius C[--]finus lived for 75 years. Iulius Aufidius, his brother, made this.
EDCS-25100192.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius C[--]finus, erected by his brother Iulius Aufidius.

EDCS-25100195.translation: Caius Iulius Prudens for [himself as] one well-deserving.
EDCS-25100195.summary: A funerary dedication for Caius Iulius Prudens, likely a self-funded epitaph.

EDCS-25100196.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Julius Severus lived for 50 years, 5 months, and 18 days. Caius Julius Ingenuus, his father, made this for his most dutiful son. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-25100196.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Severus, erected by his father Caius Julius Ingenuus.

EDCS-25100197.translation: Iulia Hesperis, freedwoman of Eleuternis, lived for 50 years. Eros made this monument for himself and his wife.
EDCS-25100197.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Eros for himself and his wife, commemorating Iulia Hesperis, his freedwoman, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-25100198.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Marina lived for 20 years and 6 days. Crepreius Ci made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-25100198.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Crepreius Ci for his wife, Iulia Marina, who died at the age of 20 years and 6 days.

EDCS-25100199.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Monnica lived for 29 years, 3 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-25100199.summary: This funerary dedication records the lifespan of Iulia Monnica, who lived for 29 years, 3 months, and 15 days.

EDCS-25100200.translation: Iulia Stacte made this monument for my husband Rufus, who lived for 30 years; he is buried here; I ask you, passerby, may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-25100200.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Iulia Stacte for her husband Rufus, who lived 30 years, with a plea for a light earth.

EDCS-25100201.translation: For lae, freedwoman, here is her... [---]
EDCS-25100201.summary: This fragment of an inscription likely commemorates a freedwoman named 'lae'.

EDCS-25100202.translation: Libosus lived for 2 years and 5 months.
EDCS-25100202.summary: This inscription records the short lifespan of a person named Libosus, who died at 2 years and 5 months.

EDCS-25100203.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary marker from Lucia Te to someone named Iuliae S.

EDCS-25100206.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Marcius Crescens lived for 93 years, 5 months, and 13 days; he is buried here; may the earth be light upon you. Marcia Benedicta and Cornelia Veneria made this for their most devoted father.
EDCS-25100206.summary: This funerary monument, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE, was dedicated by Marcia Benedicta and Cornelia Veneria to their father, Quintus Marcius Crescens, who lived 93 years, 5 months, and 13 days.

EDCS-25100208.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Aquilius Proculus to his most desired wife, Messia, daughter of Marcus, named Honorata. She lived in peace for sixty years.
EDCS-25100208.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Caius Aquilius Proculus to his wife, Messia Honorata, recording her lifespan of sixty years.

EDCS-25100209.translation: Neritus, clerk of Trophimus, lived for 28 years. His devoted wife Iucunda made this monument for him.
EDCS-25100209.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Iucunda for her husband, Neritus, who served as a clerk and lived for 28 years.

EDCS-25100210.translation: To the Divine Shades. Nigrinus Valens lived 21 years. His mother made this for her devoted son.
EDCS-25100210.summary: A funerary epitaph for Nigrinus Valens, set up by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100211.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Philocomas made this for his devoted wife Pamphila.
EDCS-25100211.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pamphila, set up by her husband Philocomas, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100212.translation: For Philo, slave of Lucius Vibius Secundus. Vibius Gemellus made this for his son. This stone covers the remains of miserable Philo, whom [---] took away when he had barely reached his fifth year. Read these few verses, alas!
EDCS-25100212.summary: A funerary epitaph for Philo, a young slave, set up by his owner Vibius Gemellus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100213.translation: May the earth be light upon you. May rest be pleasant for you, Echi and Plata, my sister. She lived 3 years and [---] months.
EDCS-25100213.summary: A funerary epitaph for Plata, likely a child, with a wish for peaceful rest, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100214.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quartinus, indicating his age at death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100215.translation: For Restitutus and Julia, 7 months. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-25100215.summary: A funerary epitaph for Restitutus and Julia, indicating their age in months, with a wish for peaceful rest, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100216.translation: 8 months and 7 days. Rogatus, the father, and Salvia, the mother.
EDCS-25100216.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child, indicating age at death, set up by parents Rogatus and Salvia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300002.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. C. Ma[---]. He lived 36 years. His husband placed this.
EDCS-25300002.summary: A funerary epitaph for C. Ma[---], set up by her husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300003.translation: For Bona, in good memory. L. L. made this, devoted.
EDCS-25300003.summary: A funerary epitaph for Bona, set up by L. L., dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300004.translation: To the Divine Shades. M. PA. MAI. TIA. VIVNCO. SIT.
EDCS-25300004.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades, possibly for an individual named TIA VIVNCO, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300007.translation: The second cohort of Cirtensians, the century of Vitellius.
EDCS-25300007.summary: This inscription likely marks a military unit or subdivision, specifically the second cohort of Cirtensians and the century of Vitellius.

EDCS-25300008.translation: A centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, he served for 25 years, lived for 56 years. Cecilia Ianuaria placed this memorial for her father.
EDCS-25300008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Cecilia Ianuaria for her father, a centurion in the Fifth Macedonian Legion, detailing his military service and lifespan.

EDCS-25300009.translation: To the memory of Maecius Rusticus, dearest son. Maecius Africanus has passed away.
EDCS-25300009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Maecius Africanus to his son, Maecius Rusticus.

EDCS-25300010.translation: Maxillus Iagumus lived for 50 years, served for 36 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25300010.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maxillus Iagumus, recording his lifespan and military service.

EDCS-25300011.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Cecilius Pr...
EDCS-25300011.summary: This is a funerary dedication, beginning with 'Dis Manibus sacrum', for Lucius Cecilius Pr...

EDCS-25300014.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Victorina is buried here, delivered to her husband. Cruel fate! She was with her husband for 2 years, 2 months, and 15 days. Her father, Iulius Victor, has renewed this.
EDCS-25300014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Victorina, who died young after 2 years and a short period with her husband, set up by her father, Iulius Victor.

EDCS-25300015.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Superius Flavianus is buried here. [---]
EDCS-25300015.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Superius Flavianus, beginning with 'Dis Manibus sacrum'.

EDCS-25300017.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph, likely for M. Masesu, stating his age at death.

EDCS-25300018.translation: Traveler, please pause a little as you pass and read what you cannot read without tears. For here, in this tomb, I lie, most unhappy. My father, a duovir and quaestor, was not permitted to see me wearing the toga praetexta except for one day, the Kalends of January. From then, received into bed, returned to funeral rites twenty days later. I passed through life for 16 years, 10 months, and 10 days. My grieving mother, with sister, brother, and father, left me in sorrow. This was made by Gaius Coelius Donatus for his son Gaius Coelius Sedatus.
EDCS-25300018.summary: This is a poignant funerary epitaph set up by Gaius Coelius Donatus for his son Gaius Coelius Sedatus, detailing the son's short life, his father's magistracies, and the family's grief.

EDCS-25300019.translation: For Gaius Ulpius Maternus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, twice aedile, twice duovir, duovir quinquennalis, who held all the offices, princeps loci, with money contributed by the town of Oppido Novo.
EDCS-25300019.summary: This is a dedication honoring Gaius Ulpius Maternus for his public service and various magistracies in the town of Oppido Novo, funded by communal contributions.

EDCS-25300021.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Flavia Honerata, wife of S DI Q Ra Hono, who lived 6 months. To her husband.
EDCS-25300021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Honerata, mentioning her husband and her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25300022.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Iulius Totnus made this for his most beloved wife, Iulia Aemilia, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-25300022.summary: This funerary inscription records the deceased Iulia Aemilia and her grieving husband, Caius Iulius Totnus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25300024.translation: Lucius, son of Secundianus, freedman of Marcus, lived 13 years.
EDCS-25300024.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius, the son of Secundianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25300025.summary: This is a dedication to the god Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300026.translation: Sextus Domitius Laetus and Puden, descendants of Socrates, set this up by the command of the Augustan Numina.
EDCS-25300026.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Sextus Domitius Laetus and Puden, following divine instruction, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25300027.translation: To Pertinax Augustus, pontifex maximus, consul for the second time, proconsul. Publius Licinius Selinus by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25300027.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Pertinax, likely set up by Publius Licinius Selinus on behalf of the local council, dating to his reign (193 CE).

EDCS-25300028.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Lucius Aelius Urbanus, an evocatus.
EDCS-25300028.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aelius Urbanus, identified as an evocatus (a veteran recalled to service), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300029.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Caesennius Iuvenis, a soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, with 14 years of service, from the centuria of Iulius Quirinalis. Caedicius Ianuarius, his heir, saw that it was made.
EDCS-25300029.summary: This funerary inscription honors Titus Caesennius Iuvenis, a soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, detailing his service and the heir who commissioned the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300030.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aelius Albanus, heir, placed this for Lucius Cassius Verus, a soldier of the I Minervia legion, with 18 years of service.
EDCS-25300030.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Cassius Verus, a soldier of the I Minervia legion, with his service years noted, and indicates his heir commissioned it, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300031.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Caius Domitius Macrinus, soldier of the XXII Primigenia Pia Fidelis legion, who lived 45 years, with 16 years of service. The heir saw that it was made.
EDCS-25300031.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Domitius Macrinus, a soldier of the XXII Primigenia Pia Fidelis legion, noting his age and service years, and that his heir commissioned it, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300032.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Domitius Severus, soldier of the 22nd Legion Primigenia Pia Fidelis, of the century of Niustius Florus, having served 13 years, who lived 40 years. His heir arranged for this to be made according to his will.
EDCS-25300032.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Domitius Severus, a soldier who served for 13 years and lived to 40, erected by his heir.

EDCS-25300033.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His heir dedicated and arranged for this for Genialis, an equestrian of the Ala Flavia Superior.
EDCS-25300033.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Genialis, an equestrian, erected by his heir.

EDCS-25300196.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Flavius Donatus, who lived 74 years, 10 months, and a few days, and his wife Agria Extricata, who lived 72 years, 3 months, and a few days, and their grandchildren Flavia Extricata, aged 15 years and 3 months, and Flavia Donata, aged 13 years, and Iulia, who lived 10 years, and Quintus, who lived 12 years, and Donata, who lived 8 years. After the death of their father, the children made this.
EDCS-25300196.summary: This is a collective funerary epitaph for Quintus Flavius Donatus, his wife Agria Extricata, and several grandchildren, erected by the children after their father's death.

EDCS-25300197.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Geminia Lucida, who lived 25 years; Flavius Cocceianus, aged 7 years; Flavius Quintilianus, aged 5 years; Flavia Optata, aged 2 years; and his daughter Arruntia Ianuaria, aged 25 years.
EDCS-25300197.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Geminia Lucida and several children, including Flavius Cocceianus, Flavius Quintilianus, Flavia Optata, and Arruntia Ianuaria.

EDCS-25300198.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aconia Maior, who lived 27 years with her sons; Aconia Berecta, who lived 18 years; Flavius Quintilianus, aged 13 years; Flavia Extricata, aged 9 years.
EDCS-25300198.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Aconia Maior and several other individuals, including Aconia Berecta, Flavius Quintilianus, and Flavia Extricata.

EDCS-25300199.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Herennius Nundinarius, who lived 80 years.
EDCS-25300199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Herennius Nundinarius, who lived to the age of 80.

EDCS-25300200.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Ulpius Marcellus, priest, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-25300200.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Ulpius Marcellus, a priest, who lived to the age of 60.

EDCS-25300201.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Erucia Quinta, who lived 57 years. To the spirits of the departed. For Celius Silus, who lived 62 years. His heir made this.
EDCS-25300201.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Erucia Quinta (57 years) and Celius Silus (62 years), with Celius Silus's heir arranging the monument.

EDCS-25300202.translation: To the glory and courage of the Triumvirs of the Parthorian War, to our lord Flavius Claudius Constantius, perpetual and most victorious Augustus. Claudius Helpidius, most perfect man, governor of the province of Mauretania Sitifensis, devoted to his divine majesty and power.
EDCS-25300202.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Emperor Constantius II, erected by Claudius Helpidius, the governor of Mauretania Sitifensis.

EDCS-25300203.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aelia Victoria, who lived 60 years, 5 months, and 3 days. Caius Iulius Lucianus made this for his mother. From the province, 192 [miles?].
EDCS-25300203.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Victoria, erected by her son Caius Iulius Lucianus.

EDCS-25300204.translation: Fabia Audicaena, mother of the sacred rites, lived 65 years. Fulvia Melessa dedicated this to her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-25300204.summary: A funerary monument set up by Fulvia Melessa for her mother, Fabia Audicaena, who was a mother of the sacred rites and lived 65 years.

EDCS-25300205.translation: Dearest mother, Sallustius Honoratus [dedicated this] to Claudia Extrikata, his dearest mother. For what you owed, having left me and my children miserable and begging, and what you should have done, I tempered my ink with tears as I wrote this. Dearest mother, greetings to your sons. Why do you stand and recite the inscription of my monument? I lived 45 years for you; I died in my 46th year, when it was given.
EDCS-25300205.summary: A funerary inscription by Sallustius Honoratus for his mother, Claudia Extrikata, lamenting her death and expressing his grief; she lived 45 years and died in her 46th.

EDCS-25300206.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Sapancio, son of Claudia Graecina. He lived 10 years, 2 months, and 1 day. Claudius Seneca made this monument for his most devoted household slave. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25300206.summary: A funerary monument for Sapancio, son of Claudia Graecina, who died at age 10 years, 2 months, and 1 day, dedicated by his master Claudius Seneca.

EDCS-25300207.translation: Saturninus Magonus, son, lived 1 year, 6 months, and 1 or 2 hours.
EDCS-25300207.summary: A funerary inscription for Saturninus Magonus, son, recording his lifespan of 1 year, 6 months, and 1-2 hours.

EDCS-25300208.summary: A simple epitaph stating that Secundio is buried here.

EDCS-25300209.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sedatus lived 35 years, 3 months, and 20 days. Sossia Crescentina arranged for this monument to be made for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-25300209.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Sossia Crescentina to her husband Sedatus, who lived 35 years, 3 months, and 20 days.

EDCS-25300210.summary: A tombstone with only the name Titus Septimius Faustus, likely indicating a burial spot.

EDCS-25300211.translation: Sergius, as soon as he began to grow into manhood, obeyed Sulpicius with a loving disposition, with his father rejoicing, in the better way, Festus, endowed with great hope from his father, with his obedience, with beautiful integrity, to love his parents in all things and obey their commands with wondrous honor. If it had been permitted to enjoy this piety longer, alas, but a crude, unworthy, and sad crime for all; for not yet having enjoyed 16 years, he perishes and destroys, and bereaves his father of a support in old age. This pious populace wept for him, these miserable parents groaned, struck by long grief and perpetual sadness, returning with the miserable father the testimonies of a good life. He who placed the remains of his beloved pledge in this sacred tomb, the unhappy Sulpicius, lamenter of an adult age.
EDCS-25300211.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sergius, who died shortly after reaching adulthood at age 16, lamenting his lost potential and the grief of his parents, particularly his father Sulpicius, who dedicated the monument.

EDCS-25300212.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Thadius Fortunus, the husband with whom life was pleasant, religious, frugal, honest, faithful, and disciplined. Lucius Caecilius Honoratus, called Thaumantus, arranged for this. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25300212.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Caecilius Honoratus, called Thaumantus, to his wife Thadius Fortunus, praising her virtues.

EDCS-25300213.translation: Tedasin M la Lucius Monumenus made [this] for his patron.
EDCS-25300213.summary: A funerary monument set up by Lucius Monumenus for his patron, Tedasin.

EDCS-25300214.translation: Lucius Valerius Saturninus, son of Spurius, is buried here.
EDCS-25300214.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Valerius Saturninus.

EDCS-25300215.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Veneria and her son Quintus, dearest, aged 21.
EDCS-25300215.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Veneria and her son Quintus, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-25300216.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Vinicia Karpimae made this for Vinicia Quita, most dutiful daughter, who lived 29 years and 7 days.
EDCS-25300216.summary: A funerary inscription by Vinicia Karpimae for her daughter Vinicia Quita, detailing her age at death.

EDCS-25300221.translation: [...]us Aricius, to his own [...]. ollia lived here and is buried.
EDCS-25300221.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription, likely recording the burial of someone named ollia and possibly dedicating the tomb to Aricius.

EDCS-25300222.summary: An inscription identifying the freedman of Causius Aelius.

EDCS-25300225.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the burial of someone identified by the initials LE.

EDCS-25300226.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning that someone named eticius lived.

EDCS-25300231.translation: To [his son] cri- [filius], most deserving, most dutiful.
EDCS-25300231.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription likely dedicated to a son, praising his character.

EDCS-25300249.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, mentioning Gaius and praising him.

EDCS-25300250.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Philar.

EDCS-25300254.translation: Marcus Ilius lived for 9 years. To Pacio, most sweet.
EDCS-25300254.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Marcus Ilius, dedicated to Pacio, likely a beloved relative.

EDCS-25300272.translation: To the deserving (deceased), a brother and son.
EDCS-25300272.summary: This is a funerary dedication for someone honored as a deserving brother and son.

EDCS-25300273.translation: Euelpius, a cultivator of the word, donated this forecourt and built the cella with his own expenses for all his family, leaving this as a memorial to the holy church. Greetings, brothers, with a pure and simple heart. Euelpius greets you. The church of the brethren restored this marble inscription in the first year of the most illustrious Severianus, from the design of Asterius.
EDCS-25300273.summary: This is a funerary and church dedication by Euelpius, who built a cella and donated land for a church, restored by the church of the brethren in the year of Severianus.

EDCS-25300274.translation: In memory of those whose bodies are buried in this place of reclining: Alcimus, of Caritas and Juliana, and Rogata, mother of Victor the priest, who made this place for all the brethren.
EDCS-25300274.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating those buried in a specific resting place, established by Victor the priest and others.

EDCS-25300276.summary: A very brief funerary inscription for Donata, wishing her peace.

EDCS-25300278.translation: In memory of Iulia Tutta. Here she rests in peace.
EDCS-25300278.summary: A simple funerary inscription commemorating Iulia Tutta.

EDCS-25300281.translation: IOD... on the Nones of December, obtained ... and was in the world for 9 days from the day of obtaining until the 7th Ides of December, and died on the 7th D... husband TT not except of himself.
EDCS-25300281.summary: This inscription records a person's lifespan, possibly related to a spiritual or monastic attainment, with specific dates noted in December.

EDCS-25300288.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Annia Paulina lived for 20 years.
EDCS-25300288.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades for Annia Paulina, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-25300289.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cai F. V., former centurion, lived for 50 years.
EDCS-25300289.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades for Cai F. V., a former centurion who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-25300291.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, mentioning the name C. Sius Honoratus.

EDCS-25300292.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius L. Rogatus lived 25 years.
EDCS-25300292.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius L. Rogatus.

EDCS-25300293.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Leburnius Donatus lived 55 years.
EDCS-25300293.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Leburnius Donatus.

EDCS-25300294.translation: To the departed spirits. Milesi lived well 70 years; his heir erected this.
EDCS-25300294.summary: A funerary epitaph for Milesi, stating his lifespan and that his heir set it up.

EDCS-25300297.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Libosus, a priest, gladly and with a willing spirit fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25300297.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by a priest named Libosus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-25300298.translation: For the perpetual safety and well-being of the divine house of the emperors, the worshippers of Pluto, Publius S.
EDCS-25300298.summary: An inscription dedicating something for the well-being of the imperial family, by devotees of Pluto.

EDCS-25300303.translation: Marcus Seleu Aurelius NVCFV, standard-bearer of the armored troops of Aurelius Masucanus, prefect ENTIMADI, with...
EDCS-25300303.summary: A fragment of an inscription naming military officials including a standard-bearer and a prefect, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-25300304.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius V. Iucundus, miles esar V.
EDCS-25300304.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius V. Iucundus, a soldier whose rank is partially preserved.

EDCS-25300305.translation: From the will of Marcus Ulpius Crescentis, a veteran and signifer of the Thracian ala. Gentianus Ulpius Marcianus, his son, and fellow soldiers dedicated this.
EDCS-25300305.summary: A dedication made by fellow soldiers and the son of a deceased veteran named Marcus Ulpius Crescentis, a standard-bearer.

EDCS-25300306.translation: To Manlia Secundilla, daughter of Lucius, sister of excellent men and Roman equites, Quintus Herennius Rufus, her husband, an Roman eques, and Severa, their daughter, placed this.
EDCS-25300306.summary: A funerary monument set up by a husband and daughter for Manlia Secundilla, noting her distinguished family and her husband's equestrian status.

EDCS-25300307.translation: To Quintus Pompeius Clemens, son of Gnaeus, of the Quirina tribe, a former aedile and duumvir, from the will of his brother Quintus Pompeius Rogatus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe. Pompeia Nabra, daughter of Quintus, placed this.
EDCS-25300307.summary: A funerary monument erected by a sister from the will of her deceased brother, who had held local magistracies (aedile and duumvir).

EDCS-25300308.translation: Bodmilkaris, the physician Hippocrates, made this.
EDCS-25300308.summary: This is a dedication made by Bodmilkaris for the physician Hippocrates, likely an occupational marker or a dedication of a tool.

EDCS-25300309.translation: To the departed spirits. Aemilius Celsus lived 25 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Iulia Honorata, his wife, made this monument.
EDCS-25300309.summary: This is a tombstone for Aemilius Celsus, who died at 25, erected by his wife Iulia Honorata.

EDCS-25300311.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Soricus lived 9 years and 2 months. Aurelius Victor lived 21 years and 6 months.
EDCS-25300311.summary: This is a tombstone recording the short lives of two individuals, Aurelius Soricus (9 years, 2 months) and Aurelius Victor (21 years, 6 months).

EDCS-25300312.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Iulia Ianuaria lived 20 years. Iulius Pressus made this monument for his most cherished wife with a willing heart.
EDCS-25300312.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Iulius Pressus for his wife Iulia Ianuaria, who died at 20.

EDCS-25300314.translation: Munina, daughter of Valerius Rogatus, while she was still alive, made this for him from her own limited means.
EDCS-25300314.summary: This is a dedication by Munina, daughter of Valerius Rogatus, made during her lifetime from her personal resources.

EDCS-25300387.translation: Corentus the builder, for the repository, lived 45 years. He is buried here. Rosa, his wife, made this for his merits.
EDCS-25300387.summary: This is a tombstone for Corentus, a builder, who died at 45, erected by his wife Rosa.

EDCS-25300388.translation: The associates made this monument for Felici, the paste-maker, from their own funds. He lived 30 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25300388.summary: This is a tombstone for Felici, a paste-maker, who died at 30, erected by his colleagues ('sodales').

EDCS-25500475.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Caius Iulius Primus, soldier of the XXII Primigenia Pia Fidelis Legion, of the century of Antonius Martialis, served 14 years, lived 35 years. The heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-25500475.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Iulius Primus, a soldier who served 14 years and died at 35, with his heir arranging the monument.

EDCS-25500476.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Primus, soldier of the XXII Primigenia Pia Felix Legion. The heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-25500476.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Iulius Primus, a soldier of the XXII Primigenia Pia Felix Legion, whose heir arranged for its creation.

EDCS-25500477.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulius Quirinalis of the II Adiutrix Pia Felix Legion. Iulius Marinus placed this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-25500477.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Iulius Marinus to Iulius Quirinalis of the II Adiutrix Pia Felix Legion.

EDCS-25500478.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Saturninus Marinus served as a soldier for 25 years and lived for 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25500478.summary: A tombstone recording the military service and lifespan of Saturninus Marinus, a soldier.

EDCS-25500479.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Servilius Bassilas, a soldier of the I Minerva legion, with 22 years of service, lived for 55 years. His heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-25500479.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Servilius Bassilas, a soldier, indicating his service length, lifespan, and that his heir erected it.

EDCS-25500480.translation: To Gaius Fulcinius Optatus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, Augustan priest, twice duovir for five years, pontiff, twice duovir, augur, aedile, quaestor, who defended the colony during the incursion of the Baquates; awarded by decree of the town council and people of Carthago Nova, and by the inhabitants, with the first public contribution ever made to him.
EDCS-25500480.summary: A dedication honoring Gaius Fulcinius Optatus for his civic and religious offices and his defense of the colony of Carthago Nova, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25500481.translation: To Gaius Fulcinius Optatus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, with the public equestrian status of an aedile. Marcus Fulcinius Maximus, his brother.
EDCS-25500481.summary: A dedication by Marcus Fulcinius Maximus to his brother, Gaius Fulcinius Optatus, mentioning his equestrian status.

EDCS-25500483.translation: Lucius Terentius Crescens and Lucius V, with the authority of an aedile, have made equal and dedicated the oil measures from their own expenses.
EDCS-25500483.summary: A dedication for the equalization and dedication of oil measures by Lucius Terentius Crescens and Lucius V, acting with aedilic authority.

EDCS-25500486.translation: The Fortia and Felicia Granaries of Cassius Divitianus and Cassius Marianus.
EDCS-25500486.summary: An inscription identifying the owners of the Fortia and Felicia granaries as Cassius Divitianus and Cassius Marianus.

EDCS-25500487.translation: In this tomb is buried Syra, who, before a full two years had been completed, separated from her marital bond by the sudden intervention of death, died at the age of 17 years, 3 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-25500487.summary: A tombstone for Syra, recording her premature death shortly after marriage at the age of 17.

EDCS-25500488.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Faustus lived for 27 years and 15 hours.
EDCS-25500488.summary: A tombstone for Aemilius Faustus, noting his lifespan including hours, likely indicating a very young death.

EDCS-25500489.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudius Mercurius lived for 54 years, 5 months, and 3 days. Varia Urbana [erected this] for her husband.
EDCS-25500489.summary: A tombstone for Claudius Mercurius, erected by his wife Varia Urbana, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25500490.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudia Saturnina lived for 7 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudius Vagalitanus lived for [---] year. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudius Rogatianus lived for 4 years.
EDCS-25500490.summary: A tombstone recording the short lifespans of three individuals: Claudia Saturnina, Claudius Vagalitanus, and Claudius Rogatianus.

EDCS-25500491.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Titus Flavius Mauritius; his heir had this made.
EDCS-25500491.summary: This funerary inscription records that the heir of Titus Flavius Mauritius commissioned the monument.

EDCS-25500492.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Honorata lived 59 years, 3 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-25500492.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Flavia Honorata.

EDCS-25500493.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Herculanus lived 14 years.
EDCS-25500493.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Herculanus.

EDCS-25500494.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Lucanus lived 32 years and 7 days.
EDCS-25500494.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Iulius Lucanus.

EDCS-25500495.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iunior lived 2 years and 2 months.
EDCS-25500495.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Iunior.

EDCS-25500496.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Manilius Tiruntius lived 22 years, 5 months, and 11 days. Pescen (dedicated this) to her son.
EDCS-25500496.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Manilius Tiruntius and is dedicated by his mother, Pescen Iulia.

EDCS-25500497.translation: To Lucius Nemonius Pastor, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, a most innocent youth, according to the terms of his will, Baebia Domitiana (dedicated this) to her most longed-for son.
EDCS-25500497.summary: This funerary inscription records Baebia Domitiana dedicating a monument to her son Lucius Nemonius Pastor as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-25500499.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Seius Ianuarius lived 35 years and 10 months.
EDCS-25500499.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Seius Ianuarius.

EDCS-25500500.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Arcius Severus the younger lived 37 years.
EDCS-25500500.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Quintus Arcius Severus the younger.

EDCS-25500501.translation: Lucius Sulpicius Quadratus made this monument for his son Saturninus, who died at the age of 11, and for himself when the time comes.
EDCS-25500501.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Sulpicius Quadratus commissioning a monument for his son Saturninus and for himself.

EDCS-25500502.translation: Tullia Ingens lived 80 years, 3 months, and 11 days. [For] Iucundus Perennis.
EDCS-25500502.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tullia Ingens, giving her age at death, likely set up by Iucundus Perennis.

EDCS-25500503.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Valerius Urbanus lived 45 years. A sacred offering to the departed spirits. Varia Honorata, a beautiful and innocent maiden, who died near her new bed, lived 21 years.
EDCS-25500503.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Urbanus and Varia Honorata, giving their ages at death, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-25500504.translation: A sacred offering to the departed spirits. Valeria Donata lived 48 years for her dearest mother.
EDCS-25500504.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Donata, giving her age at death, and likely dedicated by her mother.

EDCS-25500505.translation: A sacred offering to the departed spirits. Marcus Virius set [this up] for his wife Scipio.
EDCS-25500505.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Scipio, set up by her husband Marcus Virius.

EDCS-25500506.translation: A sacred offering to the departed spirits. Marcus Virius Seleucus Pedacius lived 49 years, 7 months, and 8 days. Whose [memory?]...
EDCS-25500506.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Virius Seleucus Pedacius, giving his age at death.

EDCS-25500507.translation: A sacred offering to the departed spirits. My husband departed to the grim Underworld; now he who, at the end of years, gave sacred rites to Proserpina, is revealed from the twelfth number. Nor could he win [his release] by prayers and tears, but if something is destined, no piety resists. Pious Juno SE...
EDCS-25500507.summary: This is a funerary epitaph likely for a husband, lamenting his death and expressing the futility of prayers against fate, possibly with a dedication to Juno.

EDCS-25500508.translation: My mother asks you, my son, to receive me with you.
EDCS-25500508.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from a mother to her son, asking to be received into his afterlife.

EDCS-25500509.translation: Egusa Satta, mature in Christ, and Simplicia, meeting in peace.
EDCS-25500509.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Egusa Satta and Simplicia, identifying them as Christians.

EDCS-25500510.translation: To the good memory of Rozon, the physician. He lived 70 years and 20 days. He preceded us in peace on the 16th day before the Kalends of May in the provincial year 492. Gaia made [this] for her dearest husband.
EDCS-25500510.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rozon, a physician, giving his age at death and the date of his passing, set up by his wife Gaia.

EDCS-25500511.translation: [The memory] of... Abo, the faithful, made [this] happy.
EDCS-25500511.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, likely commemorating a faithful individual named Abo.

EDCS-25500516.translation: To the memory of Gaius Julius Extricatus, his most sweet and innocent son, Gaius Julius Honoratus, first citizen of his fatherland, steward of the public affairs of the Quizensians and patron of the province.
EDCS-25500516.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Julius Honoratus for his son, Gaius Julius Extricatus, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500517.translation: In the 123rd year of the province, Gaius Longinius Severus and Geminius Iulianus were duovirs.
EDCS-25500517.summary: This inscription records the names of two duovirs, Gaius Longinius Severus and Geminius Iulianus, serving in the 123rd year of the province, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500518.translation: In the 213th year of the province, Marcus Longius Diomedes was duovir.
EDCS-25500518.summary: This inscription records Marcus Longius Diomedes as duovir in the 213th year of the province, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500519.translation: In the 278th year of the province, Quintus Aulius.
EDCS-25500519.summary: This inscription records the name Quintus Aulius and the year 278 of the province, likely during the Roman imperial period, possibly as an official or a dedication.

EDCS-25500520.translation: In the name of the Lord the savior, with God willing, the holy Vitalianus the bishop completed [this] in Ulpiana with his [people].
EDCS-25500520.summary: This is a Christian dedication by Bishop Vitalianus in Ulpiana, dated to the Late Roman or Byzantine period.

EDCS-25500521.translation: An altar to the eternal god.
EDCS-25500521.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication of an altar to an eternal god, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500522.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aelius Gallus, veteran, lived 85 years, served 20 years in Mesopotamia. Anicia Iulia made [this] for her husband.
EDCS-25500522.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aelius Gallus, a veteran who served in Mesopotamia, set up by his wife Anicia Iulia, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500523.translation: Caius Silius Ec Numerius Suaro lived 21 years. Magnia Extricata, his wife, made [this].
EDCS-25500523.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Silius Ec Numerius Suaro, who died at the age of 21, set up by his wife Magnia Extricata, likely from the Roman imperial period.
