EDCS-25500524.summary: This is a brief inscription identifying a workshop belonging to the 'Sodales', likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500526.translation: Here rests our father, Reperatus the bishop, of holy memory, who served in the priesthood for 9 years and 11 months, and departed from us in peace on the eleventh day before the Kalends of August in the 436th year [of the province].
EDCS-25500526.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Reperatus, a bishop, recording his death date and length of service, dated to the 436th year of the province, indicating a post-Roman or Late Roman Christian context.

EDCS-25500528.translation: Saposus of good memory preceded us in peace, resting on the third day before the Nones of November, and was buried on the Nones of November, in the 320th year of the province and the Nones.
EDCS-25500528.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and burial dates of Saposus in the 320th year of the province.

EDCS-25500529.translation: In memory of the holy martyr Iesus, who suffered, blessed and most holy Ae. The martyrdom was proclaimed. In memory of the apostles Peter and Paul, suffered on the first day before the Nones of May, in the year, by the will of God and Christ.
EDCS-25500529.summary: This inscription commemorates martyrs, possibly including Iesus, and references the apostles Peter and Paul, with a date in May during a specific year.

EDCS-25500533.translation: In memory of Reperatus. Arellius Faunus departed in the 375th year of the province.
EDCS-25500533.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Reperatus and Arellius Faunus in the 375th year of the province.

EDCS-25500538.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly related to a city or imperial dedication.

EDCS-25500541.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Domitius Celerinus lived 60 years. Domitia Celerina to her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-25500541.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domitius Celerinus, erected by his sister Domitia Celerina.

EDCS-25500542.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aelia Processa, most innocent daughter, lived 4 months and 7 days. Aelius Processus and Ulpia Mica, her most sweet parents, [set this up] for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-25500542.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the infant Aelia Processa, set up by her parents Aelius Processus and Ulpia Mica.

EDCS-25500543.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaddala lived approximately 20 years. Pious [she/he] lived [---] years.
EDCS-25500543.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaddala, recording an approximate age at death.

EDCS-25500545.translation: [---] his wife Tilabir N lived 65 years.
EDCS-25500545.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of a wife named Tilabir, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-25500546.translation: In memory of Bonifatius, the priest. He died on the 12th day before the Kalends of October in the 422nd year of the province.
EDCS-25500546.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Bonifatius, a priest, in the 422nd year of the province.

EDCS-25500547.translation: Gaius Iunius [---] son of Simio lived 30 years. He died in the 430th year of the province.
EDCS-25500547.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Iunius Simio at the age of 30, in the 430th year of the province.

EDCS-25500548.translation: In memory of Propercia Gududia, faithful, lived 60 years. Received on the third day before the Kalends of April in the 432nd year of the province.
EDCS-25500548.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Propercia Gududia, indicating her lifespan and the date of her reception.

EDCS-25500549.translation: To the memory of Austa, lived 75 years. Heirs made this from the will in the 440th year of the province.
EDCS-25500549.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Austa, erected by her heirs according to her will.

EDCS-25500550.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Domitia Ferca, lived 70 years, in the 444th year of the province.
EDCS-25500550.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the memory of Domitia Ferca.

EDCS-25500551.translation: Termineroga, in the 115th year of the province.
EDCS-25500551.summary: This inscription seems to record a place name or a boundary marker from the 115th year of the province.

EDCS-25500555.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Aelia Fortunata, my incomparable mother, lived 71 years.
EDCS-25500555.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Aelia Fortunata by her child.

EDCS-25500556.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gabinius Saturninus, lived more or less 65 years.
EDCS-25500556.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gabinius Saturninus, noting his approximate lifespan.

EDCS-25500557.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Julius Zodorus, incomparable husband, lived 70 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. To Secundina, incomparable mother-in-law, lived 70 years. Claudia Donatula dedicated this.
EDCS-25500557.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudia Donatula to her husband Julius Zodorus and her mother-in-law Secundina.

EDCS-25500561.translation: In memory of Manno, lived 80 years, in the 530th year of the province, and Octava. Died.
EDCS-25500561.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and lifespan of Manno and mentions Octava.

EDCS-25500562.translation: Lived 40 years, departed. Proculla Quitrasil es.
EDCS-25500562.summary: This inscription records the death and lifespan of an individual named Proculla Quitrasil.

EDCS-25500564.translation: To the divine spirit and genius of the river.
EDCS-25500564.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the numen and genius of a river.

EDCS-25500565.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Magnius Felicianus, a duplicarius of an ala miliaria, lived 30 years, 10 months, and 21 days. Magius Ermeritus and Magia Mosa, children, made this for their deserving father. In the provincial year [date missing].
EDCS-25500565.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Magnius Felicianus by his children, indicating his military rank and lifespan.

EDCS-25500566.translation: In memory of Aelius Mugiaris, who preceded us in the Lord's peace. He lived 15 years. He died in the year 390. And his son, [name missing], lived more or less [duration missing]. He died on the Ides of March. Aelius Sossanus, along with his fathers, erected this monument in the year 403.
EDCS-25500566.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Aelius Mugiaris and his son, likely set up by Aelius Sossanus, with dates in the provincial era.

EDCS-25500567.translation: To the memory of Iulia Pullae, who preceded us in the Lord's peace. She lived more or less 15 years and died on the Ides of August in the provincial year 411. To the memory of Depusinna, who preceded us in the Lord's peace and lived more or less 22 years and died on the Ides of August in the provincial year 400 and...
EDCS-25500567.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Iulia Pullae and Depusinna, with dates referring to the provincial era and specific death days.

EDCS-25500568.translation: To Juno Regina. By the will of Marcus Valerius Geminus. Valeria Honorata, his daughter, and Junius Geminus, the heirs, erected this.
EDCS-25500568.summary: A dedication to Juno Regina made by heirs according to the will of Marcus Valerius Geminus.

EDCS-25500570.translation: Alexand [illegible]. Clodius Largus, centurion, commander of the B and D frontier, and Callimorphus Liberalis, deputy dispensator of Augusti, [illegible] him.
EDCS-25500570.summary: An inscription likely related to military command and duties on a frontier, mentioning Clodius Largus and Callimorphus Liberalis.

EDCS-25500572.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Severus Augustus, brother of Antoninus Augustus. By the will of Sextus Cornelius Honoratus, procurator of their [illegible] of excellent memory. Marcus Caecilius Caecilianus, his heir, [erected this].
EDCS-25500572.summary: A posthumous dedication to Geta Caesar, brother of Caracalla, by his heir Marcus Caecilius Caecilianus, on behalf of the procurator Sextus Cornelius Honoratus.

EDCS-25500575.translation: To Sextus Cornelius Sexti filius Quirina Honoratus of Portus Magnus, honored with equestrian military careers, a procurator of sixty years, a man of excellent memory, in the province of Mesopotamia. Marcus Caecilius Caecilianus, his heir, by his will.
EDCS-25500575.summary: A funerary monument honoring Sextus Cornelius Honoratus, a procurator in Mesopotamia, erected by his heir, Marcus Caecilius Caecilianus.

EDCS-25500576.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Publius Crescentinius Flavius Saturninus of Siscia, soldier of the XI Claudia legion, in the century of Ulpius Victor, with 16 years of service. He lived 37 years. Flavia Ianuaria, his daughter, had this made.
EDCS-25500576.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Crescentinius Flavius Saturninus, a soldier of the XI Claudia legion, erected by his daughter.

EDCS-25500577.translation: Iulius Vales, soldier of the IIII Flavia legion, with 18 years of service. He lived 36 years. Publius Culileius Ianius and Lucius Lollius made this in honor.
EDCS-25500577.summary: An honorary inscription for Iulius Vales, a soldier of the IIII Flavia legion, erected by two other individuals.

EDCS-25500578.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Licinius Julianus, an eques from the office of the singulares. He lived 26 years, 8 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-25500578.summary: A funerary inscription for Licinius Julianus, an eques serving in the singulares, indicating his age and service.

EDCS-25500579.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Faustianus, a clerk of the ala Brittonum veteranorum, after 12 years of service, lived for 32 years. His betrothed set this up for him, her most beloved and deserving husband.
EDCS-25500579.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Faustianus, a clerk, set up by his betrothed, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25500580.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Silmius, a soldier of the legion, after 17 years of service. Taurus Senecio, his heir, made this for the deserving man.
EDCS-25500580.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Silmius, a soldier, made by his heir Taurus Senecio, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25500582.translation: The legates [Cornelius, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, Cat[---]; [---]vilius, son of Marcus, of the tribe Quirina, Pat[---]; [---]arisi, of the tribe Quirina, Iolitan[---] completed this.
EDCS-25500582.summary: This inscription records the completion of a task by legates, including Cornelius and others, possibly related to a public building or military project.

EDCS-25500583.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Magnius Iulius, a priest, lived for 80 years. He is buried here. Pudens and Onoratus made this for their devoted father.
EDCS-25500583.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Magnius Iulius, a priest, set up by his sons Pudens and Onoratus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601088.translation: They restored the camp bathhouse under the care of Caecilius Iovinus.
EDCS-25601088.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a bathhouse in a military camp, overseen by Caecilius Iovinus, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601089.translation: For the memory of Antonius Donatus, the innocent. He lived for 3 years and 10 days. Antonius Ianuarius, prefect of the cavalry, made this for his most loving son.
EDCS-25601089.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonius Donatus, a young child, set up by his father Antonius Ianuarius, a cavalry prefect, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601090.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius Datus lived for 25 years. His father made for him an eternal dwelling.
EDCS-25601090.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Datus, set up by his father, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601091.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Emerita lived for 85 years. Her sons and grandsons made for her an eternal home in the year 430 of the province.
EDCS-25601091.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Emerita, set up by her descendants, dated to the provincial year 430, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601092.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Donata lived for 18 years. Her husband Cricinus made this for her, the well-deserving wife.
EDCS-25601092.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Donata, set up by her husband Cricinus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601093.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Artemius [made this for] his dearest son Atarbio, a messenger. As for you and me, and as for me and all [---], he is buried here.
EDCS-25601093.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atarbio, a messenger, set up by his father Artemius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601095.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Emerita lived 25 years. Her husband... [built her?] an eternal home.
EDCS-25601095.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aurelia Emerita, who lived 25 years, and mentions her husband.

EDCS-25601096.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Calpurnia Iucunda lived 14 years and 6 months. Gaius Ceselius Sui, with her brother, made an eternal home [for her].
EDCS-25601096.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Calpurnia Iucunda, who lived 14 years and 6 months, and states that Gaius Ceselius Sui and her brother provided her eternal home.

EDCS-25601097.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Calpurnia Prima lived 20 years. Ia[---]pernia made an eternal home for her in the year of the province.
EDCS-25601097.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Calpurnia Prima, who lived 20 years, and states that Ia[---]pernia provided her eternal home.

EDCS-25601098.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To the deserving Constantius, Crepereus Catullianus, who lived 58 years, made [this].
EDCS-25601098.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Crepereus Catullianus, who lived 58 years, and was erected by Constantius.

EDCS-25601099.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Geminius Ieiviiens lived ten years. His parents made [this].
EDCS-25601099.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Geminius Ieiviiens, who lived ten years, and was erected by his parents.

EDCS-25601102.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Erucinus lived more or less 80 years. His son made him an eternal home in the year of the province 311.
EDCS-25601102.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Erucinus, who lived approximately 80 years, and states that his son provided him an eternal home in the provincial year 311.

EDCS-25601103.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Iadir lived 70 years. His sons made him an eternal home in the year of the province 315.
EDCS-25601103.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Iadir, who lived 70 years, and states that his sons provided him an eternal home in the provincial year 315.

EDCS-25601104.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Iuba lived...
EDCS-25601104.summary: This incomplete funerary epitaph records the name of Iulius Iuba and his lifespan is to be inferred.

EDCS-25601106.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Bonosa lived more or less 87 years. Her sons and grandsons made her an eternal home in the year of the province 316.
EDCS-25601106.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulia Bonosa, who lived approximately 87 years, and states that her sons and grandsons provided her an eternal home in the provincial year 316.

EDCS-25601108.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Caecilia lived more or less 60 years. Her husband and sons made her an eternal home in the year of the province 378.
EDCS-25601108.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulia Caecilia, who lived approximately 60 years, and states that her husband and sons provided her an eternal home in the provincial year 378.

EDCS-25601109.translation: Iulia Caecilia lived for [number] years and 6 months; her husband and daughters made this eternal home.
EDCS-25601109.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband and daughters for Iulia Caecilia, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-25601110.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Getula lived for 80 years; her son made this eternal home in the 517th year of the province.
EDCS-25601110.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Getula at age 80 and commemorates the construction of her eternal home by her son in 517 Anno Provinciae.

EDCS-25601111.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Materna lived for 70 years and 70 days (or 70 years and 7 months) of the province.
EDCS-25601111.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Iulia Materna, recording her lifespan in provincial years and days/months.

EDCS-25601113.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Quieta lived for 9 years and [restored number] months.
EDCS-25601113.summary: This funerary inscription records the short lifespan of Iulia Quieta, with a partially restored age.

EDCS-25601114.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Secunda lived for more or less 10 years; her daughters erected this eternal home in the 500th year of the province.
EDCS-25601114.summary: This funerary monument records the death of Iulia Secunda at approximately 10 years old and the construction of her eternal home by her daughters in 500 Anno Provinciae.

EDCS-25601115.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fabadduar lived for 78 years; his son made this eternal home in the 512th year of the province.
EDCS-25601115.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Fabadduar at age 78 and the construction of his eternal home by his son in 512 Anno Provinciae.

EDCS-25601116.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, for Quintus Maecius Rusticus, who died by the sword, who lived 43 years, 3 months, 21 days, and 5 hours; Maecii Africanus and Donatus [erected this] for their most innocent brother.
EDCS-25601116.summary: This funerary inscription honors Quintus Maecius Rusticus, who died violently, and details his age and the dedicators as his brothers, Maecii Africanus and Donatus.

EDCS-25601117.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Marius Namphamo lived for 85 years, 2 months; his mother and [relationship] for her well-deserving son made this eternal home.
EDCS-25601117.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Marius Namphamo and was erected by his mother and another relative.

EDCS-25601118.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Naistor lived.
EDCS-25601118.summary: This is a minimal funerary dedication for Naistor, only recording his name and the phrase indicating his death.

EDCS-25601119.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Nonnea Prima lived for 80 years; her son made this eternal home in the 518th year of the province.
EDCS-25601119.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Nonnea Prima at age 80 and the construction of her eternal home by her son in 518 Anno Provinciae.

EDCS-25601120.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Octavia Pollis lived for more or less 25 years; her betrothed made this eternal home for her in provincial year 483.
EDCS-25601120.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavia Pollis, set up by her betrothed.

EDCS-25601121.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Pomponius Sisemes lived for 85 years; his children, honoring his merits, made him a well-deserved eternal home while they were still alive.
EDCS-25601121.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pomponius Sisemes, set up by his children.

EDCS-25601122.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. [She] lived for 30 years and 2 months, Pomponia Psalis, his wife.
EDCS-25601122.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pomponia Psalis, identified as a wife.

EDCS-25601123.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Felicianus, his son, made this for his deserving father, Rogatus, who lived for 1 year, and his deserving mother, Ulpia Contentae, who lived for 80 years and 7 months.
EDCS-25601123.summary: A funerary epitaph for Rogatus and Ulpia Contentae, set up by their son Felicianus.

EDCS-25601124.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Rogatus lived for 70 years; his daughters made this eternal home for their deserving daughter in provincial year 481.
EDCS-25601124.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Rogatus, set up by his daughters.

EDCS-25601125.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. [Name illegible] Romanus lived for 70 years; his sons made [this] for him.
EDCS-25601125.summary: A funerary epitaph for Romanus, set up by his sons.

EDCS-25601126.translation: [Name illegible] lived for [number illegible] years; Decimus Rufinus made [this] for his daughter.
EDCS-25601126.summary: A funerary epitaph for an unnamed daughter, set up by Decimus Rufinus.

EDCS-25601127.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Trebius Zabullus lived for 45 years; Marcus Trebius Ianuarius made [this] for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-25601127.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Trebius Zabullus, set up by his brother Marcus Trebius Ianuarius.

EDCS-25601128.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Valerius Donate lived for 50 years; his sons and grandsons made this eternal home for him in provincial year 516.
EDCS-25601128.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Donate, set up by his sons and grandsons.

EDCS-25601129.translation: Dedicated to the Divine Spirits. Valerius Emeritus lived for more or less 25 years; his brothers made this eternal home for him in provincial year 597.
EDCS-25601129.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Emeritus, set up by his brothers.

EDCS-25601130.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Istefanus lived about [X] years; his nephews made him an eternal home in the 50th year of the province.
EDCS-25601130.summary: A funerary monument recording the life of Valerius Istefanus and commissioned by his nephews.

EDCS-25601131.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Mannica lived 45 years; C. Gener and Flavius Felix made her an eternal home in the 518th year of the province.
EDCS-25601131.summary: A funerary monument recording the life of Valeria Mannica and commissioned by C. Gener and Flavius Felix.

EDCS-25601132.translation: Valerius Mat lived [X] years; his sons and grandsons made him an eternal home in the 499th year of the province.
EDCS-25601132.summary: A funerary monument recording the life of Valerius Mat and commissioned by his sons and grandsons.

EDCS-25601133.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Matrona lived 35 years; her husband made her an eternal home in the 591st year of the province.
EDCS-25601133.summary: A funerary monument recording the life of Valeria Matrona and commissioned by her husband.

EDCS-25601134.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Sardoi lived 70 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Ianuarius lived 30 years. Bari.
EDCS-25601134.summary: A funerary monument recording the lives of Valeria Sardoi and Aurelius Ianuarius, likely in Bari.

EDCS-25601135.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Valeria] Vera made an eternal home in the 400th year.
EDCS-25601135.summary: A funerary monument recording the construction of an eternal home, possibly for Valeria Vera, in the 400th year.

EDCS-25601136.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vespasianus Certa lived 21 years; his son made him an eternal home in the 432nd year of the province.
EDCS-25601136.summary: A funerary monument recording the life of Vespasianus Certa and commissioned by his son.

EDCS-25601137.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Honorata daughter lived 4 years and 2 months. Marble.
EDCS-25601137.summary: A funerary monument for Ulpia Honorata, a young child.

EDCS-25601141.translation: To Victoria Augusta, conqueror of the Sarmatians and Germans, her husband Dececius [erected this]...
EDCS-25601141.summary: An inscription dedicated to the goddess Victoria, likely by Dececius, celebrating military victories.

EDCS-25601142.translation: Sacred. An altar was set up in fulfillment of a promise and vow of Lentinus Priscianus, father of the Mavors [family?], under the supervision of Caecilius Maximus, princeps.
EDCS-25601142.summary: An altar dedicated by Lentinus Priscianus and supervised by Caecilius Maximus, likely related to a vow or religious promise.

EDCS-25601146.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Donatus, priest, who lived more or less 105 years, departed on the 7th day before the Kalends of June. Flavius Monimus Faustus, his sons, established the Romulan house for their dearest and well-deserving father in the 363rd year of the province.
EDCS-25601146.summary: A funerary inscription from the 363rd year of the province recording the establishment of a funerary monument by sons for their father, Flavius Donatus, a priest.

EDCS-25601147.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Germanus, ordained, lived 58 years, departed on the 6th day before the Ides of April. Valerius Constantinus, his brother, and Valerius SVICF Donatus, his sons, established the Romulan house for their dearest and well-deserving father in the 314th year of the province.
EDCS-25601147.summary: A funerary inscription from the 314th year of the province, recording the establishment of a funerary monument by sons and brother for Valerius Germanus.

EDCS-25601149.translation: Dolentulus together with Aelia Secunda, his mother, established the Romulan house in the year of the province.
EDCS-25601149.summary: An inscription recording the establishment of a funerary monument by Dolentulus and his mother Aelia Secunda.

EDCS-25601150.translation: Aerennius Caicrialis the cruel, lived 30 years, departed on the 2nd day before the Ides of April. To his dearest and well-deserving brother, Aerennius Constantius.
EDCS-25601150.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Aerennius Caicrialis at the age of 30 and dedicated to his brother.

EDCS-25601151.translation: For the dearest brother Anicius Glabrio Faustus, they established the Romulan house in the 360th year of the province.
EDCS-25601151.summary: A funerary inscription from the 360th year of the province, recording the establishment of a funerary monument for Anicius Glabrio Faustus.

EDCS-25601152.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Galla, who lived approximately [---] years, departed [---].
EDCS-25601152.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Galla.

EDCS-25601153.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Aemilius lived 75 years, departed on the Nones of June. Iulius Donatus Honorius for his deserving father, the Romulan house.
EDCS-25601153.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Iulius Aemilius at age 75 and the establishment of a funerary monument by his son.

EDCS-25601154.translation: Iulius Saturus, son, for [his] father, successfully established the Romulan house [---].
EDCS-25601154.summary: A fragmented inscription likely related to the establishment of a funerary monument by Iulius Saturus for his father.

EDCS-25601155.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Secundus lived 70 years and departed on the 10th day before the Kalends of February. Iulius Fronto, his son, established the Romulan house for his dearest and well-deserving father in the 353rd year of the province.
EDCS-25601155.summary: A funerary inscription from the 353rd year of the province, recording the establishment of a funerary monument by a son for his father, Iulius Secundus.

EDCS-25601156.summary: A heavily fragmented inscription mentioning Iulius Victor.

EDCS-25601157.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Aurelia, who lived about 74 years, departed on the 22nd of September. Domitus Romanus Victor established this home for his dearest and well-deserving daughter in the 359th year of the province.
EDCS-25601157.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Aurelia, set up by her father, dating to 359 years after the province's establishment.

EDCS-25601158.translation: Iulia Aelia, grandmother, died on the 3rd of November.
EDCS-25601158.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Iulia Aelia, mentioning her status as grandmother and death date.

EDCS-25601159.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For my dearest wife, Iulia Marcia, who lived about 34 years. Claudius Felix made this home in the 350th year of the province.
EDCS-25601159.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Marcia, set up by her husband Claudius Felix, dating to 350 years after the province's establishment.

EDCS-25601161.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintius Victor, cruel, lived 35 years. Iulius Lelius established this home in place of his father in the 325th year of the province.
EDCS-25601161.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintius Victor, established by Iulius Lelius, dating to 325 years after the province's establishment.

EDCS-25601163.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Iulia, dearest mother, who lived 65 years. Her sons Tannoni Monimus, Ianuarius, and Victor set up this monument with grief in the 310th year of the province.
EDCS-25601163.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Iulia, set up by her sons, dating to 310 years after the province's establishment.

EDCS-25601165.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. The brothers Ulpii, sons of Ulpius Cocceius, Deacentes and Cocceius the younger, established this marble monument and a square stone altar for their kinsman Ulpius Cocceius.
EDCS-25601165.summary: A funerary monument inscription dedicated to Ulpius Cocceius by his brothers and their cousin.

EDCS-25601166.summary: A very fragmentary epitaph, likely funerary, recording a person's name (partially preserved) and age.

EDCS-25601167.translation: Iulius [...] may your bones rest well. His sister established this home in the 372nd year of the province.
EDCS-25601167.summary: A funerary epitaph, part of which is reconstructed, dating to 372 years after the province's establishment.

EDCS-25601516.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Aemilius Felix, pious, lived 40 years.
EDCS-25601516.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Aemilius Felix, recording his name and age.

EDCS-25601517.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cestronia Victoria, pious, lived 42 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25601517.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cestronia Victoria, recording her name, age, and burial place.

EDCS-25601518.translation: Pious Furia Quintula lived 90 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25601518.summary: A tombstone recording the death and age of Furia Quintula.

EDCS-25601568.translation: For the departed spirits of Caecilia Saturnina. Quintus Caecilius Aemilianus the younger [dedicated this].
EDCS-25601568.summary: A funerary dedication to Caecilia Saturnina by Quintus Caecilius Aemilianus the younger.

EDCS-25601569.translation: For the departed spirits. Cesonia Bebia lived 105 years.
EDCS-25601569.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Cesonia Bebia.

EDCS-25601570.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Severinus lived 29 years. [Dedicated by] Claudius Sceinus to his most innocent brother.
EDCS-25601570.summary: A tombstone for Tiberius Claudius Severinus, set up by his brother Claudius Sceinus.

EDCS-25601571.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Emilia lived about 24 years. Honoratus made [this] for his wife.
EDCS-25601571.summary: A tombstone for Cornelia Emilia, made by her husband Honoratus.

EDCS-25601572.translation: [Dedicated by] Eros to his most dutiful mother, and also to Clodia Iunior, his most indulgent grandmother.
EDCS-25601572.summary: A dedication by Eros to his mother and grandmother.

EDCS-25601573.translation: Iulius Victor lived 18 years. Iulia Stacidia made [this] for her dearest brother.
EDCS-25601573.summary: A tombstone for Iulius Victor, made by his sister Iulia Stacidia.

EDCS-25601574.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Issula lived 43 years, 4 months, and 10 days. Iulius Florus made [this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-25601574.summary: A tombstone for Licinia Issula, made by her husband Iulius Florus, recording her age and lifespan.

EDCS-25601575.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maria Quinta lived [number] years. Honoratus and Anni [made this] for their mother.
EDCS-25601575.summary: A funerary inscription for Maria Quinta, set up by her sons Honoratus and Anni.

EDCS-25601576.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Metilius Ingenuus lived 45 years. Publius Metilius Ingenianus and Metilius Ingenuus [dedicated this] to their most devoted father.
EDCS-25601576.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Metilius Ingenuus, erected by his sons.

EDCS-25601577.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Ulpia Tyche, the pious, lived 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25601577.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpia Tyche, recording her death and burial.

EDCS-25601578.translation: [This inscription is too fragmented to provide a meaningful translation or summary].
EDCS-25601578.summary: This inscription is too fragmented to provide a meaningful translation or summary.

EDCS-25601579.translation: In memory of Paul the innocent.
EDCS-25601579.summary: A short inscription commemorating an individual named Paul.

EDCS-25601580.translation: To Diana the Victorious. Gaius Julius Maximus, procurator of the Emperor, commander of the frontier.
EDCS-25601580.summary: A dedication to Diana the Victorious by Gaius Julius Maximus, an imperial official.

EDCS-25601581.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Quintus Maximus, commander of the frontier.
EDCS-25601581.summary: A dedication to Diana Augusta by Quintus Maximus, a frontier commander.

EDCS-25601584.translation: Fabia Germana, who passed into the peace of the Lord, who lived 9 years. Aperius, her father, together with his wife...
EDCS-25601584.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabia Germana, who died at the age of 9, erected by her father.

EDCS-25601587.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, for the third time consul. Quintus Iulius Ianuarius, a veteran decurion, placed this silver image weighing three pounds at his own expense, in honor.
EDCS-25601587.summary: A dedication of a silver image to Emperor Septimius Severus by the veteran decurion Quintus Iulius Ianuarius.

EDCS-25601588.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Romanius Victorinus, a soldier of Germania Inferior, with 13 years of service, who lived 32 years. Priscus Secundus, the heir, saw to the construction of his burial.
EDCS-25601588.summary: A funerary epitaph for Romanius Victorinus, a soldier, whose burial was arranged by his heir.

EDCS-25601589.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aurelius Honorius lived 11 years and 15 days. Aurelius AbiginaeUS, decurion, [dedicated this] to his sweetest son.
EDCS-25601589.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Honorius, who died at age 11, erected by his father.

EDCS-25601590.translation: In memory of Avius Safareni, son of Volu, grandson of Cudai, his brother, the prefect Safari, who lived for [---] years.
EDCS-25601590.summary: This funerary inscription honors Safari, identified as the brother and prefect of Avius Safareni, providing details about their family relations.

EDCS-25601591.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Aselius lived for 70 years. Erenius Silvanus, the second heir, made this for him, at a cost of 500,000 sesterces. He is buried here.
EDCS-25601591.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Aselius, detailing his age, the cost of his monument, and the second heir who commissioned it.

EDCS-25601592.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Ianata lived for 3 years, 4 months, and 12 days. Aurelius Marinus made this for his dearest sister.
EDCS-25601592.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Aurelia Ianata, detailing her young age at death and its dedication by her brother, Aurelius Marinus.

EDCS-25601593.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Donata Vestannis lived for 94 years. Aulus S. M. made this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-25601593.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Donata Vestannis at the age of 94 and its erection by Aulus S. M. as a tribute to her deserving character.

EDCS-25601594.translation: In memory of Evasse, who lived for about 23 years and departed in peace in the 379th year [of some era].
EDCS-25601594.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Evasse, noting his approximate age at death and a date likely tied to a local era.

EDCS-25601595.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Furnius Secundus lived for 45 years. Munatia Ianuaria made this for her husband.
EDCS-25601595.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Furnius Secundus at age 45 and its dedication by his wife, Munatia Ianuaria.

EDCS-25601596.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Geminia Secunda, pious, lived for 20 years. Lucius Munatius S. N. A. Mascal lived for 40 years. Mascavar [lived] for 35 years. Lucius Salitorius and Munatius Nata are buried here.
EDCS-25601596.summary: This complex funerary inscription records the deaths and burials of multiple individuals, including Geminia Secunda, Lucius Munatius Mascal, Mascavar, Lucius Salitorius, and Munatius Nata, with varying ages.

EDCS-25601597.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Harenia Luciosa lived for 80 years. Harenia Luciosa made this for her mother.
EDCS-25601597.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Harenia Luciosa at age 80 and its dedication by her daughter, also named Harenia Luciosa.

EDCS-25601599.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Apronius, pious, lived for 66 years. He is buried here; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-25601599.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Iulius Apronius, detailing his age and including a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-25601600.translation: In memory of Iulius Emeritus, who lived for about 30 years and departed in peace.
EDCS-25601600.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Iulius Emeritus, noting his approximate age at death and his peaceful passing.

EDCS-25601601.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Secunda lived 21 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25601601.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulia Secunda, who lived 21 years.

EDCS-25601602.translation: Tulia made this for her sister Iulia Sepul in peace.
EDCS-25601602.summary: A funerary dedication made by Tulia for her sister Iulia Sepul.

EDCS-25601603.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maric Garuatis lived 50 years. Bona lived 40 years. For the well-deserving.
EDCS-25601603.summary: A funerary dedication recording the lives of Maric Garuatis and Bona.

EDCS-25601604.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marius Considius lived 40 years. Vipinicosa [lived X years].
EDCS-25601604.summary: A funerary dedication for Marius Considius, who lived 40 years, with a partial mention of Vipinicosa.

EDCS-25601605.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Mesuleolus Resus Ponponius, who lived more or less 41 years, and departed in the peace of the Lord, and passed away on the seventh day before the Kalends of August in the 405th year of the province.
EDCS-25601605.summary: A funerary dedication for Mesuleolus Resus Ponponius, detailing his lifespan and date of death in the provincial era 405.

EDCS-25601606.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Munatius Cutai lived 25 years. His wife Bonosa made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-25601606.summary: A funerary dedication made by Bonosa for her husband Munatius Cutai, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-25601607.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Naevius Narcissus lived 25 years. Naevius Narcissus, his father, made this for the well-deserving son.
EDCS-25601607.summary: A funerary dedication made by Naevius Narcissus for his son, also named Naevius Narcissus, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-25601608.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Rutilius Felix, father, and Elia Sigesai, mother. Rutilius, their son, made this.
EDCS-25601608.summary: A funerary dedication made by Rutilius for his parents, Rutilius Felix and Elia Sigesai.

EDCS-25601609.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Rutilius Felix and Rutilius Munianus, brothers. Rutilius, their brother, made this.
EDCS-25601609.summary: A funerary dedication made by Rutilius for his brothers, Rutilius Felix and Rutilius Munianus.

EDCS-25601610.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Salvidenius Maximus lived 16 years. Salvidenius Felix, his father, made this.
EDCS-25601610.summary: A funerary dedication made by Salvidenius Felix for his son Salvidenius Maximus, who lived 16 years.

EDCS-25601611.translation: Oria, wife of Vicatus, lived about 15 years and departed in peace.
EDCS-25601611.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Oria, likely from the 3rd-4th century CE, recording her age at death.

EDCS-25601612.translation: Domitia Seasla, may the earth lie lightly upon you, Domitius [set this up].
EDCS-25601612.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domitia Seasla, erected by Domitius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601614.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Simplicisus. P. M. [presided over].
EDCS-25601614.summary: This is a dedication to the Manes (underworld spirits) for Simplicisus, with an indication of a public official's involvement, date uncertain.

EDCS-25601619.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Aurelius Siona, sesquiplicarius of the Osdroeni, [dedicated this] for his son Aurelius Donatus, who died aged 26 years and after 7 years of service. MORINOABAVR.
EDCS-25601619.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Donatus, set up by his father Marcus Aurelius Siona, a soldier of the Osdroeni, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601620.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Antoninus and Bilisarius.
EDCS-25601620.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Antoninus and Bilisarius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601621.translation: To Diana, goddess of the groves, victorious over wild beasts, I, Fannius Iunianus, prefect of the II Cohort of Sardinians, gave annual vows.
EDCS-25601621.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Diana, dedicated by Fannius Iunianus, prefect of a Sardinian cohort, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601625.translation: For the health and safety of King Masuna, ruler of the Moors and Romans, the fortress was built by Masgivinus, prefect, and Iider, procurator, of Safar. Castra Severiana, which Masuna Altava set up and Maximus, procurator of Altava, completed in the 468th year of the province.
EDCS-25601625.summary: This is a dedication inscription for the construction of a fortress, dedicated to King Masuna and completed in the provincial year 468 (likely 468 AD based on the province of Africa).

EDCS-25601628.translation: Aurelius Proximus, a cavalryman of the ala Parthorum, lived [for?] 8 years of service.
EDCS-25601628.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Proximus, a cavalryman, recording his service length, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601631.translation: In memory of Atilius Renovatus, who lived 32 years, departed on the Nones of April in the provincial year [date missing].
EDCS-25601631.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atilius Renovatus, recording his age at death and date of departure, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601632.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Atilia Ingenna lived 60 years, 5 days before the Ides of November. Stlaccius Victor set this up for his wife in the provincial year 450.
EDCS-25601632.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atilia Ingenna, erected by her husband Stlaccius Victor, recording her age at death and the provincial year 450 (likely 450 AD).

EDCS-25601633.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aufidia Satura lived 9 years and [some] months.
EDCS-25601633.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Aufidia Satura.

EDCS-25601634.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Altavesis [dedicated this] to his most sweet daughter Saturina, who lived 5 years, 6 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-25601634.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Saturina, dedicated by her father Aurelius Altavesis.

EDCS-25601635.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Magus lived 2 years, 8 months, and 27 days; his father [dedicated this] to his most loving son.
EDCS-25601635.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Aurelius Magus, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-25601637.translation: In memory of Aurelius Sammac, who lived 55 years and departed on the 2nd day before the Nones of May; his most sweet brother made this in the 322nd year of the province.
EDCS-25601637.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and death date of Aurelius Sammac, dedicated by his brother and dated to the provincial era.

EDCS-25601638.translation: In memory of Aurelius Victor, who lived 15 years and departed on the 10th day before the Kalends of July in the 354th year of the province.
EDCS-25601638.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and death date of Aurelius Victor, dated to the provincial era.

EDCS-25601639.translation: In memory of Aurelia Emerita, who lived 21 years and departed on the Kalends of [A]RIS in the 360th year of the province.
EDCS-25601639.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and death date of Aurelia Emerita, dated to the provincial era.

EDCS-25601642.translation: In memory of Caecilius Iulianus, who lived 80 years and departed on the 3rd day before the Ides of April in the 414th year of the province.
EDCS-25601642.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and death date of Caecilius Iulianus, dated to the provincial era.

EDCS-25601643.translation: Caelius Honoratus, aged 27 years; Cornelia Iuliana made this.
EDCS-25601643.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Caelius Honoratus, made by Cornelia Iuliana.

EDCS-25601644.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Kalventius Malsamo lived 70 years.
EDCS-25601644.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Kalventius Malsamo.

EDCS-25601647.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fanius Africanus and his heirs made this; he lived 80 years and 7 months. Fanius Restutus lived 4 years.
EDCS-25601647.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Fanius Africanus and his heir Fanius Restutus, made by Fanius Africanus and his heirs.

EDCS-25601648.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Faonius Felix lived for 16 years. Publius Faonius Silvanus made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-25601648.summary: A funerary epitaph for Faonius Felix, set up by his father Publius Faonius Silvanus.

EDCS-25601649.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavia Rogata lived for 4 years and died on the 18th of December in the 391st year of the province.
EDCS-25601649.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Rogata, recording her age at death and date of decease, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-25601650.translation: In memory of Florentia Maxima, who lived for 15 years and died on the 24th of February in the 323rd year of the province.
EDCS-25601650.summary: A funerary epitaph for Florentia Maxima, stating her age at death and date of decease, likely from the Roman province of Africa.

EDCS-25601653.translation: Granius Isteius Martialus, dearest, lived for 1 year.
EDCS-25601653.summary: A funerary epitaph for the infant Granius Isteius Martialus.

EDCS-25601654.summary: A fragment of a funerary epitaph for ntius Onoratus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-25601655.translation: In memory of Ianuarius, who lived for 80 years, was struck down, and died on the 21st of May in the 380th year of the province.
EDCS-25601655.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ianuarius, detailing his age at death, the date of his demise, and the cause of death (violent).

EDCS-25601656.translation: In memory of Istlacius Cerialis, who lived for 20 years, died in June in the 410th year of the province, having been struck down by the sword.
EDCS-25601656.summary: A funerary epitaph for Istlacius Cerialis, recording his age at death, the date of his demise, and the cause of death (killed by sword).

EDCS-25601659.translation: In memory of Iulius Donatus, head of the household, for whom his sons made an eternal home. He lived for about 75 years and died on the 5th of November in the 497th year of the province.
EDCS-25601659.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Donatus, a pater familias, erected by his sons, and dating to the late Roman period.

EDCS-25601660.translation: In memory of Iulius Germane, head of the household, for whom his sons and grandsons made an eternal home. He lived for about 70 years and died in peace on the 27th of November in the 554th year of the province.
EDCS-25601660.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Germane, a pater familias, erected by his descendants, and dating to the late Roman period.

EDCS-25601662.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iulius Saturus, innocent, lived for 11 years and died on the 28th of December in the 353rd year of the province. His dearest father made this.
EDCS-25601662.summary: A funerary epitaph for the child Iulius Saturus, set up by his father, recording his age and date of death.

EDCS-25601664.translation: To the memory of Julia Ianuaria, a most innocent woman, who lived 40 years. Iulius Crepereius Pontianus, a veteran, to his sweetest wife.
EDCS-25601664.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a veteran for his wife, Julia Ianuaria, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-25601665.translation: Iulia Monnina lived 3 years and died on the 2nd day before the Kalends of February in the 353rd year of the province. Her dearest father made this.
EDCS-25601665.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Iulia Monnina, erected by her father, dating to the 353rd year of the province.

EDCS-25601666.translation: To the memory of Iulius Aunna, who lived 60 years and died on the Kalends of February in the 441st year of the province.
EDCS-25601666.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Aunna, who died at the age of 60 in the 441st year of the province.

EDCS-25601667.translation: To the memory of Iunia Fortuna, who lived more or less 90 years and died on the 6th day before the Kalends of August in the 413th year of the province.
EDCS-25601667.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iunia Fortuna, who died at approximately 90 years of age in the 413th year of the province.

EDCS-25601668.translation: To the memory of Iunius Titurus, also called Maccalis, who lived 80 years and died on the 18th day before the Kalends of February in the 351st year of the province. His dearest sons made this.
EDCS-25601668.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iunius Titurus (Maccalis), erected by his sons, who died at the age of 80 in the 351st year of the province.

EDCS-25601669.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maenia Saturnina made this for her husband Sextus Hor Tertellus.
EDCS-25601669.summary: This funerary inscription records that Maenia Saturnina dedicated a monument to her husband, Sextus Hor Tertellus.

EDCS-25601670.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Martia lived 85 years.
EDCS-25601670.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits of Martia, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-25601671.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maximus Surugis lived 43 years.
EDCS-25601671.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits of Maximus Surugis, who lived 43 years.

EDCS-25601672.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Omidia Septimina lived 25 years and died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of October. Her mother and husband made this for their dearest.
EDCS-25601672.summary: This funerary inscription records that Omidia Septimina died at 25 years old, and it was erected by her mother and husband.

EDCS-25601673.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Popilia Casta, mother, lived 75 years. Her sons made this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-25601673.summary: This funerary inscription records that Popilia Casta, a mother who lived 75 years, was honored by her sons.

EDCS-25601674.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Saturninus, dearest son, lived for [some duration].
EDCS-25601674.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a son named Saturninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25601676.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Sempronius Donatus, his elder grandson, who lived for more or less [some duration].
EDCS-25601676.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Sempronius Donatus, the elder grandson of the dedicator.

EDCS-25601677.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sulpicia Honerata lived for 70 years and passed away on the 7th day before the Ides of May in the 381st year of the province.
EDCS-25601677.summary: This epitaph records the death of Sulpicia Honerata, including her age and the date of death in provincial years, likely from Roman North Africa.

EDCS-25601681.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Virius Martialis lived for 41 years. Quarta made this for her husband.
EDCS-25601681.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Virius Martialis, dedicated by his wife Quarta.

EDCS-25601684.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. He lived for more or less 18 years and passed away on the 7th day before the Kalends of July in the 312th year of the province. His dearest father made this.
EDCS-25601684.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates a young man who died at 18, dedicated by his father, with the date given in provincial years.

EDCS-25601685.translation: To Decima. His most loving wife made this in the 423rd year of the province.
EDCS-25601685.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Decima by her loving wife, dated by provincial years.

EDCS-25601687.translation: In memory of T.R.L. He lived for 50 years and passed away on the 14th day before the Kalends of February in the 354th year of the province. His dearest sons made this.
EDCS-25601687.summary: A memorial inscription for someone identified by initials, erected by their sons and dated in provincial years.

EDCS-25601698.translation: For Sextus Valerius Maximus, son of Sextus Valerius, Marcus Valerius Saturninus, his paternal uncle, by his will.
EDCS-25601698.summary: A funerary dedication erected by a paternal uncle for his nephew, Sextus Valerius Maximus, according to the deceased's will.

EDCS-25601701.translation: For Mundicia Saturnina, daughter of Quintus. Lucius Valerius Saturninus for his mother and Sextus Valerius Maximus for his grandmother.
EDCS-25601701.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Mundicia Saturnina by her son Lucius Valerius Saturninus and grandson Sextus Valerius Maximus.

EDCS-25601702.translation: For Quintus Valerius, son of Sextus Quirinus, former aedile, former flamen, former quinquennalis. Lucius Valerius Saturninus for his father and Sextus Valerius Maximus for his grandfather.
EDCS-25601702.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Quintus Valerius Quirinus, who held several public offices, dedicated by his son and grandson.

EDCS-25601703.translation: To Marcus Valerius Gauds, son of Quintus, of the tribe Quirina, Lucius Valerius Saturninus, for his brother, and Sextus Valerius Maximus, for his uncle.
EDCS-25601703.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Lucius Valerius Saturninus and Sextus Valerius Maximus to their deceased relatives, Marcus Valerius Gauds, his brother, and their uncle.

EDCS-25601704.translation: To Annia Maxima, his wife, Lucius Annius.
EDCS-25601704.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Lucius Annius to his deceased wife, Annia Maxima.

EDCS-25601705.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Annius Honoratus, for his dearest daughter, Annia Nonnosa.
EDCS-25601705.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Lucius Annius Honoratus to his beloved deceased daughter, Annia Nonnosa.

EDCS-25601706.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Antonina Saturnina, his dearest wife, Marcus Valerius Flaccus.
EDCS-25601706.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Marcus Valerius Flaccus to his beloved deceased wife, Antonina Saturnina.

EDCS-25601708.translation: SODRECA, priest; ODRECASS, priest; DRECASSVS, priest; RECASSVNVS, priest; ECASSVNINUS, priest; ASSVNIRARINVS, priest; ASSVNIRARINVS, priest; SSVNIRA Marcianus, priest; ASSVNIRARINVS, priest; ASSVNIRARINVS, priest; ECASSVNINUS, priest; RECASSVNVS, priest; DRECASSVS, priest; ODRECASS, priest; SODRECA, priest.
EDCS-25601708.summary: This inscription lists numerous priests, likely from a religious college or local priesthood, possibly arranged in a circular or repeating fashion, potentially with some textual corruption or repetition.

EDCS-25700421.translation: To Apollo Augustus, and to the Genius of the municipium of Tucca.
EDCS-25700421.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Apollo Augustus and the Genius of the local community of Tucca.

EDCS-25700422.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the camp and of Augustus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25700422.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Julia Augusta, likely a prominent imperial woman, by order of the town council.

EDCS-25700423.translation: To Gaius Julius Sodalis, of the tribe Papiria, sodalis, IIIvir, quaestor, perpetual flamen, Marcus Julius Sodalis and Lucius Julius Crescens, for their deserving grandfather.
EDCS-25700423.summary: This is a dedication from Marcus Julius Sodalis and Lucius Julius Crescens to their grandfather, Gaius Julius Sodalis, honoring his various public and religious offices.

EDCS-25700424.translation: Contractor, lived 47 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25700424.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph recording the occupation and lifespan of an individual.

EDCS-25700427.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Numisius Nundanus lived 7 years.
EDCS-25700427.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Marcus Numisius Nundanus for a child who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-25700428.translation: To the Emperors Caesars Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletian and Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximian, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate Augusts, and to Constantius and Maximian, most noble Caesars, Titus Aurelius Litua, a most distinguished man, governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, restored the Aqua Frigida district and rebuilt it into a better form, may our lords live for many happy years.
EDCS-25700428.summary: This inscription records the restoration of the Aqua Frigida district by Titus Aurelius Litua, governor of Mauretania Caesariensis, under the reigns of Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Maximian, likely in the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-25700429.translation: Vincentia has gladly fulfilled her vow to Saturn.
EDCS-25700429.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating Vincentia's fulfilled vow to the god Saturn, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25700431.translation: Selvale, governor of the province of Mauretania Sitifensis, along with...
EDCS-25700431.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Selvale, governor of Mauretania Sitifensis, with an incomplete dedication or record.

EDCS-25700432.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mevia Crescentina, from her peculium, for her well-deserving master, Iniara SCA, aged 24.
EDCS-25700432.summary: This funerary inscription records Mevia Crescentina's dedication from her personal savings to her deserving master Iniara SCA, who died at age 24, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25700433.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Terentia Augusta, my most rare wife, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-25700433.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Terentia Augusta, the wife of the dedicator, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-25700435.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. V Paris Fortuva, aged 15.
EDCS-25700435.summary: This is a funerary inscription for V. Paris Fortuva, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-25700436.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tiberius Granius Emilius, who lived 85 years.
EDCS-25700436.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Tiberius Granius Emilius, who lived to the age of 85.

EDCS-25700437.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sabinia Mai a, who lived 115 years.
EDCS-25700437.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Sabinia Mai a, who lived to the exceptional age of 115.

EDCS-25700439.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Clintus Silvanus, who lived 30 years and served as a soldier for 15 years, is buried here.
EDCS-25700439.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and military service of Clintus Silvanus, who died at age 30 after serving for 15 years.

EDCS-25700440.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mariurex, who lived 20 years. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25700440.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Mariurex at the age of 20, with a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-25700441.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Iulius Patr Fortunatus lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25700441.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Patr Fortunatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25700443.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Latia V V Tessisiae, 3 years. Fortunatus lived.
EDCS-25700443.summary: A funerary epitaph for Latia V V Tessisiae, a child, mentioning Fortunatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25700444.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. [He/She] lived 68 years.
EDCS-25700444.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25900040.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, commander-in-chief, most brave, most fortunate, with tribunician power for the 6th time, emperor for the 12th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country; and to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Germanicus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, nephew of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-great-nephew of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, commander-in-chief, most brave, most fortunate, the colonia Nerviana Augusta Martialis of the veterans of Sitifensium, at public expense, two miles from Sitifi, to our lord Flavius Claudius Iulianus, perpetual always Augustus.
EDCS-25900040.summary: A milestone inscription dedicated to Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), and also mentioning Emperor Flavius Claudius Iulianus, likely dating to the 3rd or 4th century AD, indicating road maintenance near Sitifis.

EDCS-25900042.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, son of the deified Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Augustus, and of Iulia Domna Augusta, mother of the camp and senate and country, nephew of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus, Germanicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 18th time, emperor for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, proconsul, the state of the Nervian Augusta Antoninianorum Sitifensium established this milestone.
EDCS-25900042.summary: A milestone inscription dedicated to Emperor Caracalla, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD, set up by the municipality of Sitifis.

EDCS-26000937.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Clodius Pupienus Maximus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul; and to the Emperor Caesar Decimus Caelius Calvinus Balbinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul; and to Marcus Antoninus Gordianus, most noble Caesar Augustus, nephew of the deified Gordians, the state of Nerviana Augusta Martialis of the veterans of Sitifensium.
EDCS-26000937.summary: An inscription honoring the emperors Pupienus, Balbinus, and Caesar Gordian, likely dating to 238 AD, likely related to the veterans of Sitifis.

EDCS-26000938.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Clodius Pupienus Maximus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, father of the country; and to the Emperor Caesar Decimus Caelius Calvinus Balbinus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-26000938.summary: An inscription honoring the short-lived co-emperors Pupienus and Balbinus, dating to 238 AD.

EDCS-26000942.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Antoninus Augustus Britannicus Germanicus Parthicus Maximus, nephew of the deified Severus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, great-nephew of the deified Commodus and the deified Marcus Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-great-nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius and the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan and the deified Nerva, Augusta Martialis of the veterans of Sitifensium, three miles.
EDCS-26000942.summary: A milestone inscription, likely referring to Emperor Gordian III, dating to the mid-3rd century AD, marking three miles for the veterans of Sitifis.

EDCS-26000947.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, father of the country, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, emperor for the 4th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, restored the milestones through Cnaeus Nunnium Martialem, his procurator, from Sitifi, two miles, to our lords Flavius Valentinianus and Flavius Valens Caesars.
EDCS-26000947.summary: A milestone restoration inscription by Emperor Septimius Severus, dating to between 193-211 AD, also referencing later emperors Valentinian and Valens, likely from the region of Sitifis.

EDCS-26000949.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, commander-in-chief, most brave, most fortunate, with tribunician power for the 6th time, emperor for the 12th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country; and to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, nephew of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva; and to Lucius Septimius Geatea, most noble Caesar Augustus, the colonia Nerviana Augusta Martialis of the veterans of Sitifensium, two miles from Sitifi.
EDCS-26000949.summary: A milestone inscription dedicated to Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Caesar Geta, likely dating to 202-205 AD, indicating road distance near Sitifis.

EDCS-26000952.translation: To Flavius Claudius Julianus, perpetual Augustus, for the fourth time.
EDCS-26000952.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius Claudius Julianus, who held the consulship for the fourth time, likely from the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-26000955.translation: The Republic of the Sitifensian Antoninian Tenth Legion established milestones for the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, favored of the divine Septimius Severus Pius, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, and for Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the camps, of the senate, and of the fatherland; for the son of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus, Germanicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 18th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul.
EDCS-26000955.summary: This is a milestone inscription from the colony of Sitifis, dating to the reign of Caracalla (indicated by his extensive titulature), recording the establishment of boundary markers.

EDCS-26000956.translation: The Republic of the Martialis established milestones for Quintus Vettius Decius Invictus Pius Felix, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul. Distance: 2,000 paces.
EDCS-26000956.summary: This milestone inscription from the Martialis area records the establishment of road markers for a high-ranking official, likely during the late 3rd or early 4th century.

EDCS-26000957.translation: From Sitifi, 4,000 paces, through Gnaeus Nunnies Martialis, his procurator.
EDCS-26000957.summary: This inscription indicates a distance of 4,000 paces from Sitifi, managed by the procurator Gnaeus Nunnies Martialis, likely related to road construction or maintenance.

EDCS-26000958.translation: The colony of Nerviana Augusta Martialis of veterans of Sitifis, with public funds, established [this road] for the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the divine Commodus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, most brave and most fortunate, holding tribunician power for the 6th time, Imperator for the 12th time, Consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland; and for the Emperor Caesar, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, his son, son of the divine Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus; and for Lucius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, Pius Felix; [for] 2,000 paces.
EDCS-26000958.summary: This is a milestone inscription from the colony of Sitifis, dating to the joint reign of Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, recording the establishment of road markers with public funds.

EDCS-26000961.translation: The Republic of the colony of Nerviana Augusta Sitifensium established 9,000 paces for the Emperor Caesar Marcus Clodius Pupienus Maximus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Consul for the 2nd time, Proconsul, Father of the Fatherland; and for the Emperor Caesar Decimus Caelus Calvinus Balbinus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Consul, Proconsul, Father of the Fatherland; and for Marcus Antoninus Gordianus, most noble Caesar, Pius Felix, grandson of the divine Gordians.
EDCS-26000961.summary: This milestone inscription from Sitifis dates to the joint reign of Pupienus and Balbinus, with Gordian III as Caesar, and records the establishment of 9,000 paces.

EDCS-26000963.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Marcus Valerius Diocletian Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul; and for the Emperor Caesar Aurelius Valerius Maximian Augustus; 9,000 [paces].
EDCS-26000963.summary: This milestone inscription records the establishment of 9,000 paces for Emperors Diocletian and Maximian, dating to their joint rule in the late 3rd or early 4th century.

EDCS-26000964.translation: To Claudius Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-26000964.summary: This is a dedication to Claudius Constantinus, who held the title of Caesar, likely from the period of the Tetrarchy or early Constantinian dynasty.

EDCS-26000973.translation: Dedicated to our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-26000973.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Flavius Valerius Constantinus, addressed as 'our lord', likely from the early 4th century.

EDCS-26100139.translation: And for the most noble Caesars Flavius Claudius Constantinus, [for] 2,000 paces.
EDCS-26100139.summary: This inscription records a distance of 2,000 paces for the Caesars Flavius Claudius Constantinus, likely from the early 4th century.

EDCS-26100140.translation: To Valerius Licinianus Licinius the younger, most noble Caesar, 12 miles.
EDCS-26100140.summary: This inscription records a distance of 12 miles, dedicated to Valerius Licinianus Licinius the younger, a noble Caesar, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26100148.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, from Aras, 4 miles.
EDCS-26100148.summary: This inscription records a distance of 4 miles from Aras, dedicated to Emperor Gordian III, likely dating to his reign (238-244 CE).

EDCS-26100149.translation: Under the supervision of the proconsul, by Sallustius Victor, his procurator, from Aras, 6 miles.
EDCS-26100149.summary: This inscription records a distance of 6 miles from Aras, overseen by the proconsul and administered by Sallustius Victor, his procurator, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100150.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 18th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, from Auzia, 3 miles.
EDCS-26100150.summary: This inscription records a distance of 3 miles from Auzia, dedicated to Emperor Antoninus Pius and lists his extensive imperial titles, dating to 155 CE.

EDCS-26100154.translation: To our lord the Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, 9 miles.
EDCS-26100154.summary: This inscription records a distance of 9 miles, dedicated to Emperor Aurelian, likely dating to his reign (270-275 CE).

EDCS-26100156.translation: And to Flavius Valerius Constantius and Galerius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesars, 18 miles.
EDCS-26100156.summary: This inscription records a distance of 18 miles, dedicated to the future emperors Constantius I and Galerius, likely dating to the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26100161.translation: To our lord the Emperor Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Augustus, from Zuccabar, 4 miles.
EDCS-26100161.summary: This inscription records a distance of 4 miles from Zuccabar, dedicated to Emperor Aurelian, likely dating to his reign (270-275 CE).

EDCS-26100162.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, from Caesarea, 6 miles.
EDCS-26100162.summary: This inscription records a distance of 6 miles from Caesarea, dedicated to Emperor Caracalla, listing his imperial titles, likely dating to his reign (198-217 CE).

EDCS-26100163.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, grandson of the deified Gordians, from Caesarea, 6 miles.
EDCS-26100163.summary: This inscription records a distance of 6 miles from Caesarea, dedicated to Emperor Gordian III, listing his titles and lineage, likely dating to his reign (238-244 CE).

EDCS-26100164.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, and to Marcia Otacilia Severa Augusta, mother of the camps, from Caesarea, 3 miles.
EDCS-26100164.summary: This inscription records a distance of 3 miles from Caesarea, dedicated to Emperor Philip the Arab and his wife Marcia Otacilia Severa, likely dating to their reign (244-249 CE).

EDCS-26100165.translation: To our lords Severus and Maximini, most noble Caesars, six Roman miles.
EDCS-26100165.summary: This is a milestone inscription dedicating six miles to the emperors Severus and Maximini, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26100168.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Decius Trajanus, Invictus, Pius, Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul, proconsul, from the public body of Portus Magnus, two [Roman miles].
EDCS-26100168.summary: This is a milestone inscription dedicating two miles to Emperor Decius, likely from his reign (249-251 CE), erected by the community of Portus Magnus.

EDCS-26100170.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, Pius, Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul, from Portus Magnus, [some number of] Roman miles.
EDCS-26100170.summary: This is a milestone inscription dedicating an unspecified number of miles to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely from his reign (235-238 CE), from the community of Portus Magnus.

EDCS-26100171.translation: To the Lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Antoninus Gordianus, Invictus, Pius, Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, twice father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, grandson of the deified Gordians, [some number of] Roman miles.
EDCS-26100171.summary: This is a milestone inscription dedicating an unspecified number of miles to Emperor Gordian III, likely from his reign (238-244 CE), referencing his imperial lineage.

EDCS-26100172.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, grandson of the deified Severus, son of the deified Antoninus, set up new milestone markers for two hundred and two Roman miles through Titus Aelius Decrianus, his procurator.
