EDCS-26100172.summary: This is a milestone inscription detailing the erection of 202 Roman miles of road markers by Emperor Caracalla, administered by the procurator Titus Aelius Decrianus, likely from his reign (198-217 CE).

EDCS-26100174.translation: New milestone markers through Titus Aelius Decrianus, procurator.
EDCS-26100174.summary: This is a fragment of a milestone inscription indicating the work of Titus Aelius Decrianus as procurator in establishing new road markers.

EDCS-26100176.translation: Grandson of the deified Severus, he set up new milestone markers for fourteen Roman miles through Titus Aelius Decrianus, his procurator, from the public body of Pomarium.
EDCS-26100176.summary: This is a milestone inscription dedicating fourteen Roman miles of road markers by Titus Aelius Decrianus, acting as procurator for the community of Pomarium and honoring his imperial lineage back to Septimius Severus.

EDCS-26100177.translation: To Felix Augustus, with tribunician power for the seventh time, proconsul, [great-grandson] of the deified Septimius Severus, [son] of the emperor Augustus.
EDCS-26100177.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to Emperor Caracalla, mentioning his seventh tribunician power and his relation to Septimius Severus and possibly Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-26100372.translation: To the All-Powerful Gods, Sallustius, procurator.
EDCS-26100372.summary: This is a dedication to the 'All-Powerful Gods' by Sallustius, who held the position of procurator, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26100374.translation: To the Mauri Conservators and the Genii of Satafis, Sallustius Saturninus, a beneficiarius duplicarius from the quaestorship, adorned the temple from his own means, he fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-26100374.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sallustius Saturninus, a military official, dedicating and embellishing a temple to local deities (Mauri Conservatores and Genii Satafis), likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26100375.translation: Sacred to the divine power of the Emperors of the Moors. Gaius Julius Novellus, his son, for the honor of being a patrician, with his own money, along with whitewashing, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-26100375.summary: This inscription records a private dedication by Gaius Julius Novellus to the divine power of the Emperors, likely associated with a public honor he received, funded by himself and approved by local officials.

EDCS-26100376.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Publius Aelius Annius Secundus, priest, and Caecilia Primosa, his wife.
EDCS-26100376.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Saturn Augustus, made by a priest and his wife.

EDCS-26100378.translation: Lucius Herennius Victor, priest, and Silia Victoria, his wife, made (this) with their own money.
EDCS-26100378.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a priest and his wife, funded by themselves.

EDCS-26100379.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Gaius Julius Neptunalis fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-26100388.translation: For the prosperity of the merciful ages of our lords Gratian, Valentinian, and Theodosius. Flavius Felix Gentilis, the most accomplished governor of the province, patron, instituted, completed, and dedicated the aqueduct of the baths, recently constructed with rotten timbers, but now constructed with marvelous work and perfection, from the expenses of his brothers Honoratus and Nasamon, with their consent and will, assisted by Opilius Felix, curator of the republic.
EDCS-26100388.summary: This extensive inscription records the reconstruction and dedication of a bathhouse aqueduct by the provincial governor Flavius Felix Gentilis, funded by his brothers, during the reign of Emperors Gratian, Valentinian, and Theodosius.

EDCS-26100389.translation: After its flames and ashes, the phoenix knows how to rise anew; now, so that the beautiful structure may be renewed, you, Quintinus, make it. For you, whose noble body and strength produced praise, by which you excel in all things, born from the Roman lineage of fathers, the prefecture of the Moors allowed you with distinguished honor. This Iones will rise again, whom the city of Satafis in the province rejoices to have nourished for herself. Year 322.
EDCS-26100389.summary: This poetic inscription praises Quintinus, a prefect of the Moors, for renewing a structure, possibly a bathhouse, in the city of Satafis, likening him to a phoenix, and dating it to the provincial year 322.

EDCS-26100390.translation: Marcus Annius Sacerdos, Roman equestrian, patron of the municipality, curator and dispunctor, placed (this) from his own funds, under the care of Praesidius, his son.
EDCS-26100390.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Marcus Annius Sacerdos, a prominent local official and Roman equestrian, funded by himself and supervised by his son.

EDCS-26100391.translation: Lucius Marius Ecc, curator of the Republic, placed (and) restored (this).
EDCS-26100391.summary: This inscription records the placement and restoration of something by Lucius Marius Ecc, identified as a curator of the Republic.

EDCS-26100396.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aulus Valerius Urbicus, the younger, a junior student, died aged 24.
EDCS-26100396.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Aulus Valerius Urbicus the Younger, a young student who died at the age of 24.

EDCS-26100397.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Aelius Primus lived for 35 years.
EDCS-26100397.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the death of Publius Aelius Primus at the age of 35.

EDCS-26100401.translation: Claudius Victor and Cluentia Siddina made and dedicated this.
EDCS-26100401.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Claudius Victor and Cluentia Siddina, likely for a public or religious purpose.

EDCS-26100402.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Domitius Usuctor lived 11 years.
EDCS-26100402.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Quintus Domitius Usuctor at the age of 11.

EDCS-26100404.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Furius Maximus the younger lived 8 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26100404.summary: This tombstone records the death of Furius Maximus the younger at the age of 8 and indicates his burial place.

EDCS-26100405.summary: This tombstone records the death of Iulia Carosa at the age of 36.

EDCS-26100406.translation: Quintus Lurius Rogatus (dedicated this) to his parents and to his wife of most noble memory, (building) them everything from the ground up.
EDCS-26100406.summary: This inscription records Quintus Lurius Rogatus dedicating a monument to his parents and wife, implying he built it for them.

EDCS-26100407.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Octavia Peregrina lived 35 years; her heir made this.
EDCS-26100407.summary: This tombstone records the death of Octavia Peregrina at the age of 35 and states that her heir commissioned the monument.

EDCS-26100408.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Petronia Guduia, wife (dedicated this); Marcus Cordius, husband (dedicated this).
EDCS-26100408.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the departed, made by both wife Petronia Guduia and husband Marcus Cordius.

EDCS-26100409.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Rusticeia Matrona lived 25 years. The cause of my death was childbirth and a malign fate. But you, my dearest husband, cease weeping, and our sons, preserve our shared love. My spirit has passed to the stars, married to the heavens.
EDCS-26100409.summary: This tombstone laments the death of Rusticeia Matrona at 25 due to childbirth, addressed to her husband and sons.

EDCS-26100410.translation: To Quintus Sittius Avitus, my brother.
EDCS-26100410.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial to Quintus Sittius Avitus, from a sibling.

EDCS-26100411.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial to Sittius Maximus, from a child.

EDCS-26100412.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Sittius Verus.

EDCS-26100415.translation: Her husband L. Arus placed this here.
EDCS-26100415.summary: This funerary epitaph records that a husband named L. Arus placed a monument for his wife.

EDCS-26100420.translation: For the memory of the deposition of the presbyter Securus, placed by his brothers Fatale and Flora. He lived 56 years in the 324th year of the province. The deposition of his brother Bassus, on the 7th day before the Ides of October.
EDCS-26100420.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of the presbyter Securus and his brother Bassus, providing their ages and dates in relation to the provincial era, likely dating to the 4th century CE.

EDCS-26100423.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Valentina, well-deserving of her husband Claudius Sapos, departed this life without sorrow, favored by the god. She lived 60 years in the 313th year of the province. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-26100423.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Aemilia Valentina, recording her age and death date in relation to the provincial era, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-26100427.translation: For the memory of Julia Innocentissima, a most innocent woman, who lived 2 years. Her daughter Julia Honorata placed and dedicated this monument.
EDCS-26100427.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Julia Innocentissima, noting her age and that the monument was erected by her daughter.

EDCS-26100428.translation: In memory of Julia Donata, who lived 3 years.
EDCS-26100428.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the short lifespan of Julia Donata.

EDCS-26100429.translation: In memory of Preiectus, who lived 22 years. Felicianus in the 312th year of the province.
EDCS-26100429.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Preiectus and provides a date based on the provincial era, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-26100431.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription that mentions the name Victor.

EDCS-26100432.summary: This fragmented inscription likely refers to a funerary monument or dedication, mentioning a date and the name Victor.

EDCS-26100433.translation: For the memory of Ulpius Saturninus. His son Ovinia Credula placed [this] for her son, who lived 15 years.
EDCS-26100433.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ulpius Saturninus, who lived 15 years, and was commemorated by his son Ovinia Credula.

EDCS-26100435.translation: Iulia Felicia, his daughter, made this table of navigation, 580 of the province.
EDCS-26100435.summary: This inscription records a table of navigation, dedicated by Iulia Felicia.

EDCS-26100437.translation: Gaius Antonius Saturninus, having made this vow, willingly paid it from his own money, 20 nummi.
EDCS-26100437.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Gaius Antonius Saturninus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100439.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibia Gaetula lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26100439.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Vibia Gaetula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100456.translation: Aulia Pudentina lived 75 years. Her husband Fl [---] made this sacred offering to the departed spirits.
EDCS-26100456.summary: This funerary inscription records Aulia Pudentina's life and is dedicated by her husband.

EDCS-26100457.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, and to Aurelius Valerius Maximianus Pius Felix Flavius Valerius Constantius.
EDCS-26100457.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman Emperors Diocletian and Maximianus, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-26100459.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Laelius Maure lived 70 years. Iulia Monnina made this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-26100459.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Monnina to her husband Lucius Laelius Maure.

EDCS-26100463.translation: From the sons of Hermogenes and Victor.
EDCS-26100463.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a dedication or ownership by the sons of Hermogenes and Victor.

EDCS-26100467.translation: For the memory of Aurelius Rufus, a soldier of the First Urban Cohort. Cresius Peregrinus and Aurelius Bariccio, soldiers of the First Urban Cohort, arranged this. Aurelius L IESL made this from his own money.
EDCS-26100467.summary: This inscription honors Aurelius Rufus, a soldier, and records arrangements made by fellow soldiers and a financial contribution, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100468.translation: The standard-bearer of the Legio VII Gemina made this.
EDCS-26100468.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the signifer (standard-bearer) of the Legio VII Gemina, a Roman legion.

EDCS-26100469.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, under the supervision of Victor, a beneficiarius. Ferianus the excellent, a sexagenarian, lived 37 years.
EDCS-26100469.summary: This funerary inscription honors Ferianus, likely a soldier or official, and mentions Victor as a supervisor.

EDCS-26100476.translation: Publius Aelius Sitifensis set this up for his wife.
EDCS-26100476.summary: This is a funerary monument for Publius Aelius Sitifensis' wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100477.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Victoria lived 17 years.
EDCS-26100477.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Victoria, recording her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100479.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Lollius Rogatus lived 80 years. His sons Gallonius and Ianuarius Lollius made this for their father and mother. He is buried here.
EDCS-26100479.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Lollius Rogatus, set up by his sons, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100480.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Geminius Datulus, son of Publius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived [---] years. His son Gemunus made this for his beloved son. He is buried here.
EDCS-26100480.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Geminius Datulus, set up by his son, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100481.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Helvia Marcia, daughter of Lucius, lived 21 years. She is buried here. Caecilia Honorata made this for her pious daughter.
EDCS-26100481.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Helvia Marcia, set up by her mother, recording her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100482.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Iulius Valens the younger lived 20 years. He is buried here. Gaius Iulius Valens made this for his son.
EDCS-26100482.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Valens the younger, set up by his father, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100483.translation: Quintus Iulius Urbanus, son of Quintus Iulius, lived 8 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-26100483.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Urbanus, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100484.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Rosula, mother of my children, always thrifty and good, lived 36 years. Marcus Caelius Titus made this for his wife.
EDCS-26100484.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Rosula, set up by her husband, recording her age at death and praising her character, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26100485.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Lurius Servilius, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 80 years. Caius Lurius Ianuarius, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, made this.
EDCS-26100485.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Lurius Servilius, set up by his son, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200014.summary: This inscription is a simple name, possibly a funerary marker or the name of a donor, with no further context, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200281.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Anigra, daughter of Quintus, lived 45 years and is buried here.
EDCS-26200281.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Anigra, daughter of Quintus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200282.translation: Lucius Annius Felix of the Palatina tribe lived 75 years and is buried here.
EDCS-26200282.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Annius Felix, a Roman citizen, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200283.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Annia Donata, daughter of Lan, lived 65 years and is buried here.
EDCS-26200283.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia Donata, daughter of Lan, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200284.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Antonius Maurus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 27 years and is buried here.
EDCS-26200284.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Maurus, son of Lucius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200285.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Rogata, daughter of Quintus, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 21 years.
EDCS-26200285.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Rogata, daughter of Quintus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200286.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cat, daughter of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 75 years.
EDCS-26200286.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cat, daughter of Lucius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200287.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cornelius Honoratus lived 81 years.
EDCS-26200287.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius Honoratus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200288.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fadia Quinta lived 87 years.
EDCS-26200288.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fadia Quinta, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200289.translation: Q. Fla[v]ia lived 101 years and is buried here.
EDCS-26200289.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Q. Flavia, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200290.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Flavius Felix, son of Marcus, of the Arnensis tribe, lived [---] years and is buried here.
EDCS-26200290.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Flavius Felix, son of Marcus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200291.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Fonteius Rogatus lived 44 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Fundana, with her husband, lived 50 years.
EDCS-26200291.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespans of Publius Fonteius Rogatus and Aurelia Fundana, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200292.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Grania Fundana, daughter of Caius, lived 25 years.
EDCS-26200292.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Grania Fundana, daughter of Caius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200293.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Quinta, daughter of Quintus, lived 61 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200293.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Iulia Quinta, daughter of Quintus, and indicating her burial place, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200294.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Lucretius Romilia Saturus lived 81 years.
EDCS-26200294.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Quintus Lucretius Romilia Saturus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200295.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maria Laeta, daughter of Marcius, lived 85 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200295.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Maria Laeta, daughter of Marcius, and indicating her burial place, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200296.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Modius Fundanus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 47 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200296.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Caius Modius Fundanus, son of Lucius and from the Arnensis tribe, and indicating his burial place, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200297.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Oppia Novella, daughter of Caius, lived 40 years.
EDCS-26200297.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Oppia Novella, daughter of Caius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200298.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Petronius Senecio, son of Caius, lived 18 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200298.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Lucius Petronius Senecio, son of Caius, and indicating his burial place, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200299.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Phalerius, son of Sextus, of the Arnensis tribe, lived seventy-five years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200299.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Quintus Phalerius, son of Sextus and from the Arnensis tribe, and indicating his burial place, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200300.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Popilius Secunda, daughter of Publius, lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200300.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Popilius Secunda, daughter of Publius, and indicating her burial place, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26200301.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Servilia Castula lived 65 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200301.summary: A tombstone for Servilia Castula, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-26200302.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Tadius Saturninus, son of Lucius, of the Aemilia tribe, made this monument; he lived 70 years.
EDCS-26200302.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Tadius Saturninus, recording his age at death and that he constructed his own monument.

EDCS-26200303.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titinia Secunda, daughter of Marcus, lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200303.summary: A tombstone for Titinia Secunda, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-26200304.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Ulpius Cabiresis lived 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200304.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Ulpius Cabiresis, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-26200305.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Ulpius Fatalis, son of Marcus, lived 66 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200305.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Ulpius Fatalis, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-26200306.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Ulpius Onesimus lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200306.summary: A tombstone for Ulpius Onesimus, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-26200307.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Zosimus, Roman equestrian, lived 65 years.
EDCS-26200307.summary: A tombstone for Zosimus, a Roman knight, recording his age at death.

EDCS-26200308.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sexta Flavia Maxima, daughter of Caius, lived 100 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200308.summary: A tombstone for Sexta Flavia Maxima, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-26200310.translation: To our lord Gaius Flavius Valerius Constantius, most noble Caesar, the colonists of Colonia Iulia Augusta Saldensium of the Seventh Legion, immune.
EDCS-26200310.summary: An inscription from the colonists of Colonia Iulia Augusta Saldensium to the Emperor Constantius.

EDCS-26200311.translation: Horatius Marcianus, to Annius Postumus, his most indulgent friend, procurator of the Emperor, in charge of the libraries, procurator of the twentieth tax, master of the procurators, procurator of the Emperor for the grain supply at Ostia, procurator of Lower Pannonia, for the benefits which he conferred upon him, erected and dedicated.
EDCS-26200311.summary: A dedication by Horatius Marcianus to Annius Postumus, a high-ranking imperial official, honoring him for his benefits.

EDCS-26200312.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Herennius Martialis, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, decorated with public equestrian rank, lived 37 years, died in office as tribune; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200312.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Herennius Martialis, a man decorated with equestrian rank who died while serving as a tribune.

EDCS-26200313.translation: Mausoleum. Herennia Tertulla, daughter of Marcus, priestess of Ceres, lived [number] years, held the priesthood for [number] year.
EDCS-26200313.summary: A funerary inscription for Herennia Tertulla, a priestess of Ceres, recording her lifespan and the duration of her priesthood.

EDCS-26200314.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Adiutor lived 1 year; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200314.summary: A brief funerary inscription for a child named Adiutor who died at the age of one.

EDCS-26200315.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Blaesius Vitalis, son of Publius, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 7 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200315.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Blaesius Vitalis, a child who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-26200316.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Blaesius Satiara lived 75 years.
EDCS-26200316.summary: A funerary inscription for Blaesius Satiara, recording her age at death.

EDCS-26200317.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Secunda lived 45 years.
EDCS-26200317.summary: A funerary inscription for Caecilia Secunda, recording her age at death.

EDCS-26200318.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cladianus Arator, son of Decimus, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 47 years.
EDCS-26200318.summary: A funerary inscription for Cladianus Arator, recording his age at death.

EDCS-26200319.translation: Publius Secundus, son of Publius, of the Fabia tribe, lived 7 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200319.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Secundus, a child who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-26200320.summary: A funerary inscription for Havia Laurentina, recording her age at death.

EDCS-26200321.translation: Caius Iulius Arator lived 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200321.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Iulius Arator, recording his age at death.

EDCS-26200322.translation: Vrta Honorata, of the Quirina tribe, lived 81 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200322.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vrta Honorata, recording her name, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-26200323.translation: To the Divine Manes, Lucius Iulius (dedicated this) for Arnensis.
EDCS-26200323.summary: This is a dedication to the gods of the underworld, likely a tombstone, set up by Lucius Iulius for someone named Arnensis.

EDCS-26200324.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia Matrona, daughter of Quintus, lived [X] years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200324.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Matrona, daughter of Quintus, recording her name, parentage, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-26200325.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publius Petronius Cerialis, son of Publius, of the Fabia tribe, lived 16 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200325.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Petronius Cerialis, recording his name, parentage, tribe, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-26200326.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Petronia Anulla lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200326.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petronia Anulla, recording her name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-26200327.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Setertuia lived 4 years.
EDCS-26200327.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Setertuia, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-26200329.translation: Baquaxs, daughter of Lucius, of the Arnensis (tribe?), lived 21 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200329.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baquaxs, recording her name, parentage, possible tribal affiliation, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-26200330.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Via, daughter of Marcus, lived [X] years.
EDCS-26200330.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Via, daughter of Marcus, recording her name, parentage, and age at death.

EDCS-26200332.translation: Lucius Annius Modestus (erected this) to his most dutiful father, Lucius Annius Maximus, son of Modestus, Roman knight, aedile, quaestor, and holder of the duumviral priesthood.
EDCS-26200332.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Annius Modestus to his father, Lucius Annius Maximus, detailing the father's career and Roman social status.

EDCS-26200664.translation: Datus made (this) for Mercury Augustus from a lot.
EDCS-26200664.summary: This is a dedication of a votive object to the god Mercury Augustus by a man named Datus, possibly related to a drawing of lots.

EDCS-26200665.translation: Sacred to Imperial Saturn. Quintus Alienius Fabullus, priest.
EDCS-26200665.summary: A dedication to Imperial Saturn by the priest Quintus Alienius Fabullus.

EDCS-26200666.translation: Sacred to Imperial Saturn. Quintus Caelius Felix, priest, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26200666.summary: A vow fulfilled by Quintus Caelius Felix, a priest of Imperial Saturn.

EDCS-26200667.translation: Cassius Donatus, priest, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26200667.summary: A vow fulfilled by Cassius Donatus, a priest.

EDCS-26200672.translation: Sacred to Imperial Saturn. Lucius Licinius Getulus, priest, gladly fulfilled his vow on the Ides of January.
EDCS-26200672.summary: A vow fulfilled on the Ides of January by Lucius Licinius Getulus, a priest of Imperial Saturn.

EDCS-26200673.translation: Sacred to Imperial Saturn. Lucius Licinius Rufus.
EDCS-26200673.summary: A dedication to Imperial Saturn by Lucius Licinius Rufus.

EDCS-26200676.translation: Sacred to Imperial Saturn. Quintus SSV Quintilianus gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26200676.summary: A vow fulfilled by Quintus SSV Quintilianus, a priest of Imperial Saturn.

EDCS-26200677.translation: Sacred. Marcus Rubrius Felix fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26200677.summary: A vow fulfilled by Marcus Rubrius Felix.

EDCS-26200683.translation: To Aemilia Honorata, most excellent lady, mother of Coelius Martialis and Coelius Senecio, most excellent men, and of Gnaeus Coelius Seneca, their brother, an eques Romanus.
EDCS-26200683.summary: A funerary dedication to Aemilia Honorata, honoring her sons and brother.

EDCS-26200684.summary: A tombstone recording the life of Aemilia Venusta, who died at age 25.

EDCS-26200685.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Avianius Silianus lived 80 years. Apponia Sissoi lived 75 years.
EDCS-26200685.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Avianius Silianus and Apponia Sissoi, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-26200686.translation: Consecrated to the departed spirits. Mag. Caecilius Urbanus lived for XX years; for Publia Aelia Garamantis, his wife, lived for LX years; deposited on the Ides of November. And for Fabia Maiosa, his wife, lived for I year; C. P. Nap. Publius lived for V years.
EDCS-26200686.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages and death dates of several family members, including wives of Mag. Caecilius Urbanus.

EDCS-26200687.translation: Consecrated to the departed spirits. Gaius Aelius Felix lived for XL years. Reia Pacata, his wife, and Aelius Felicianus Namphamo Gudullus, his sons and heirs, [erected this] to their most devoted father.
EDCS-26200687.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by the sons and heirs to their devoted father, Gaius Aelius Felix, also recording the wife Reia Pacata.

EDCS-26200688.translation: Consecrated to the departed spirits. Lucius Crepereius Secundus lived for LXX years; he is buried here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-26200688.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Lucius Crepereius Secundus and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-26200689.translation: Lucius Didius Quetus lived for LXXX years. Decria Rogata, his wife, aged LXV years.
EDCS-26200689.summary: This is a funerary epitaph that records the ages of Lucius Didius Quetus and his wife, Decria Rogata.

EDCS-26200690.translation: Consecrated to the departed spirits. Marcus Gargilius Pastor lived for LXX years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26200690.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Marcus Gargilius Pastor lived for 70 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-26200691.translation: Consecrated to the departed spirits. H. H. lived for LX years. R. V. lived for [..] years. Sextus Helvius [N]orius lived for XVI years. Sextus Helvius Secundinus lived for LV years. Rubria Secunda lived for [..] years. Sextus Helvius Sextianus lived for XXVIII years. The Helvii, Marcus Secundinus and Sextia, set this up for themselves and for their parents.
EDCS-26200691.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lives of several family members, including Sextus Helvius Secundinus and Sextus Helvius Sextianus, and indicates it was set up by Marcus Secundinus and Sextia for themselves and their parents.

EDCS-26200692.translation: Iulia Rogata, daughter-in-law, lived for [..] years. Modia Sapida, daughter-in-law, lived for [..] years. Ancillus, daughter-in-law, lived for [..] years.
EDCS-26200692.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing the ages of three women identified as daughters-in-law, with missing age information.

EDCS-26200693.translation: Marcus Laelius Didius Particus lived for LXX years, and Aelia Quinta lived for [..] years. In memory of Laelius Didius Particus, his sons Quietus and Publius erected this monument to their most devoted father.
EDCS-26200693.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Quietus and Publius in memory of their father, Marcus Laelius Didius Particus, also recording the age of Aelia Quinta.

EDCS-26200694.translation: Consecrated to the departed spirits. Munatia Rogata lived for LXX years. Munatius Ianuarius lived for [..] years.
EDCS-26200694.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of Munatia Rogata and Munatius Ianuarius, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-26200695.translation: Consecrated to the departed spirits. Gaius Numonius Secundus lived for LXXV years. Fabia Rogata, his wife, lived for LXV years. Buried here.
EDCS-26200695.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lives of Gaius Numonius Secundus and his wife Fabia Rogata, stating they are buried here.

EDCS-26200697.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Rutilius Victor lived [X] years. Claudia Satura, his wife, lived [X] years.
EDCS-26200697.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Rutilius Victor and his wife Claudia Satura, recording their lifespans.

EDCS-26200698.translation: of her husband C.V.L.I. Secundus, and her son Secundus, and her son-in-law Publius Aelius Victorinus, and Maurus and Solutor, for their merits.
EDCS-26200698.summary: This is a dedication, possibly by a wife or daughter, honoring her husband, son, and sons-in-law for their merits.

EDCS-26200699.translation: To the Divine Spirits. M. C. F. G. Saturnina lived 25 years and Emeritus lived 16 years.
EDCS-26200699.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Saturnina and Emerius, recording their lifespans and likely their relationship.

EDCS-26200700.summary: This fragment is part of a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a son.

EDCS-26200702.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning a daughter of Gnaeus.

EDCS-26200703.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, possibly funerary, recording a lifespan.

EDCS-26200704.summary: This is a short inscription, likely Christian, possibly an epitaph or a dedication, referencing 'Arcadius'.

EDCS-26200706.translation: XL. This table is an eternal home and perpetual happiness. Of all my people, this alone is mine. Aper, faithful, lived 65 years. His deposition was on the 10th day before the Kalends of September in the 321st year of the province.
EDCS-26200706.summary: This is a Christian funerary epitaph for Aper, detailing his lifespan, the date of his burial, and expressing ideas of eternal life and personal possession.

EDCS-26200710.translation: Flavia Amanda lived 35 years. Hospia, her daughter, made this table.
EDCS-26200710.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Amanda, made by her daughter Hospia, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-26200716.translation: Daniel in the den of lions.
EDCS-26200716.summary: This inscription, likely Christian, refers to the biblical story of Daniel in the lions' den, possibly as a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-26200719.translation: By the benevolence of the new age of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus, restorer of the world, the fort of Thibi, which was previously enclosed by a narrow wall, has now, with repaired and strengthened forces and trusting in peace, been extended to represent a larger place, under the care of Faltonius Restitutianus, an outstanding man and governor, and of Aurelius Felix, procurator of our Emperor.
EDCS-26200719.summary: This inscription records the expansion and repair of the fort of Thibi during the reign of Gordian III, overseen by the governor Faltonius Restitutianus and the procurator Aurelius Felix.

EDCS-26200720.translation: To the departed spirits. Patiua Urbana lived 70 years. Caius Fut, son of Cai, 1 year.
EDCS-26200720.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the ages of Patiua Urbana and Caius Fut.

EDCS-26200721.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Barbara lived 40 years.
EDCS-26200721.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Claudia Barbara.

EDCS-26200722.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. C. Metunus lived 75 years, 7 months. MF (fecit?).
EDCS-26200722.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age and month of death for C. Metunus.

EDCS-26200723.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rogata lived 17 years.
EDCS-26200723.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Rogata.

EDCS-26200724.translation: Iulius Satur lived 35 years. Iulia Maio lived 60 years.
EDCS-26200724.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the ages of Iulius Satur and Iulia Maio.

EDCS-26200728.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Aelius Quintus lived 60 years. Aurelia Prima lived 75 years.
EDCS-26200728.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the ages of Titus Aelius Quintus and Aurelia Prima.

EDCS-26200729.translation: To the departed spirits. Fadia Barin, 70 years. VIV (?).
EDCS-26200729.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age of Fadia Barin, with an unclear abbreviation.

EDCS-26200731.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Herennius Aufidius lived 80 years. Iulia Megum lived 50 years.
EDCS-26200731.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the ages of Herennius Aufidius and Iulia Megum.

EDCS-26200733.translation: Iulia Victorina lived 70 years. Cre[...] lived [...] years.
EDCS-26200733.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the age of Iulia Victorina and part of another person's name and age.

EDCS-26200734.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Longinia Saturnina lived 50 years. Her son made this.
EDCS-26200734.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Longinia Saturnina, erected by her son.

EDCS-26200735.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Memmius Saturninus lived 75 years. Iulia Victoria, with her heirs while alive, dedicated this monument.
EDCS-26200735.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life spans of Memmius Saturninus and Iulia Victoria, and was dedicated by Iulia Victoria and her heirs.

EDCS-26200736.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextilius Ianuarius lived 60 years. Gargilia S BA lived 4 years.
EDCS-26200736.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the ages of Sextilius Ianuarius and Gargilia S BA.

EDCS-26200737.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Suleia Sicina lived 65 years. Cornelius Namfamo lived 105 years.
EDCS-26200737.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of Suleia Sicina and Cornelius Namfamo.

EDCS-26200738.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Valerius Victor lived 50 years. Saturnina his wife lived 40 years.
EDCS-26200738.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Victor and his wife Saturnina.

EDCS-26200739.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. M. C. Victorina lived 71 years. Caius Pomponius Honoratus her dutiful son lived 80 years.
EDCS-26200739.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of M. C. Victorina and her son Caius Pomponius Honoratus.

EDCS-26200740.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, to the memory of Ulpia Urbana who lived 85 years. Claudia Barice made this for her dearest deserving son.
EDCS-26200740.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Ulpia Urbana and was made by Claudia Barice for her son.

EDCS-26200741.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. M. Caius lived 20 years.
EDCS-26200741.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for M. Caius, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-26200743.translation: In memory of Gitel who lived 55 years.
EDCS-26200743.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Gitel.

EDCS-26200745.translation: Secundus, from Africa, with his wife Iunia.
EDCS-26200745.summary: This inscription likely records the names of a couple, Secundus and Iunia, possibly indicating a shared monument or family connection.

EDCS-26200747.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavius Datullus lived 75 years, 4 months, 6 hours. His heirs and sons made this.
EDCS-26200747.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavius Datullus, recording his lifespan and stating that his heirs and sons erected it.

EDCS-26200749.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Victorina lived for an unspecified large number of years.
EDCS-26200749.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Victorina, noting her lifespan, although the exact number of years is unusual and likely symbolic or corrupt.

EDCS-26200750.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Victorina lived 85 years; her father Geminius made this. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Geminius lived 95 years; Geminius made this.
EDCS-26200750.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph recording the lives of Iulia Victorina and Geminius, both erected by Geminius, likely her father and then himself.

EDCS-26200752.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Cesia Maria lived 77 years.
EDCS-26200752.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cesia Maria, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-26200753.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publicius Primus lived 38 years. Arellia Polla lived 35 years. Their elder brother Publici Sul made this.
EDCS-26200753.summary: This is a shared funerary epitaph for Publicius Primus and Arellia Polla, erected by their elder brother.

EDCS-26200756.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Saturnina, wife of Cominatus the veteran, lived 65 years.
EDCS-26200756.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Saturnina, identifying her as the wife of a veteran named Cominatus and recording her lifespan.

EDCS-26200760.translation: Aemilius Caius lived 62 years. Patilia Maior lived 89 years.
EDCS-26200760.summary: This inscription records the lifespans of Aemilius Caius and Patilia Maior, likely a shared funerary monument.

EDCS-26200762.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Nina, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-26200763.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Iulius Secundus lived 81 years.
EDCS-26200763.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Secundus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-26200764.translation: Caius C. Ianuarius lived 95 years. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Longinia Nina lived 85 years.
EDCS-26200764.summary: This inscription records the lifespans of Caius C. Ianuarius and Longinia Nina, likely a shared funerary monument dedicated to the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-26200765.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Estrikata lived for 51 years.
EDCS-26200765.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Estrikata, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-26200766.translation: Iulius lived for 70 years, well and good.
EDCS-26200766.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius, recording his lifespan and a positive sentiment.

EDCS-26200767.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Saluss lived for 71 years. With his wife Citina, he lived for 65 years.
EDCS-26200767.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Saluss, recording his lifespan and the duration of his marriage.

EDCS-26200768.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for Iulia Valeria, stating her age at death.

EDCS-26200770.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Novius Secundinus lived for 63 years.
EDCS-26200770.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Novius Secundinus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-26200771.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Omidius.
EDCS-26200771.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely for Omidius.

EDCS-26200772.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Omidia Namgedde, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-26200773.translation: To the gods. Rogatus lived for 35 years.
EDCS-26200773.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rogatus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-26200774.translation: Sempronius, at his own expense, for himself and his husband, a monument.
EDCS-26200774.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Sempronius funded a monument for himself and his husband.

EDCS-26200775.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Sopitius Armutius lived for 52 years.
EDCS-26200775.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sopitius Armutius, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-26200776.translation: To Lucius Tirillarius, who lived 41 years.
EDCS-26200776.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Tirillarius.

EDCS-26200777.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ummidia Paulina lived 75 years.
EDCS-26200777.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Ummidia Paulina.

EDCS-26200782.translation: To our lords, the undefeated, eternal, greatest, victorious, and triumphant Emperors Valens, Gratian, and Valentinian, ever August, Flavius Victorinus, most excellent man of the first rank, count of Africa, ever devoted to your divine will, dedicated the camp.
EDCS-26200782.summary: An inscription recording the dedication of a camp by Flavius Victorinus, count of Africa, to the emperors Valens, Gratian, and Valentinian, dating to their joint reign (367-375 AD).

EDCS-26200783.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Fuscus lived 47 years. [---] lived [---] years.
EDCS-26200783.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulius Fuscus, with a partially preserved second entry.

EDCS-26200784.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulianus.

EDCS-26200785.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Clodius Saturninus, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, lived 81 years. Avilia Bona lived 35 years. His most worthy son Saturninus made this; he rests here.
EDCS-26200785.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Clodius Saturninus and Avilia Bona, set up by their son, Saturninus.

EDCS-26200786.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Ulpius Castro, best son, and for Ulpia Syrilla, his wife, Caius Calpurnius Felix. [---] lived 18 years.
EDCS-26200786.summary: A funerary epitaph possibly for Ulpius Castro and Ulpia Syrilla, set up by Caius Calpurnius Felix.

EDCS-26200790.translation: To the holy goddess Venus, Quintus Iulius Gemellinus, a litter-bearer, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26200790.summary: A votive inscription to the goddess Venus by Quintus Iulius Gemellinus, a lecticarius.

EDCS-26200793.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tarracius the slave, a tax collector, lived 10 years, 10 months, and 22 days.
EDCS-26200793.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and occupation of Tarracius, a slave and tax collector.

EDCS-26200795.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aelius Nabor lived 90 years. His son made this.
EDCS-26200795.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Aelius Nabor, set up by his son.

EDCS-26200796.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Annius Albucia.
EDCS-26200796.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Annius Albucia, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-26200797.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Antistius Antistianus lived for 53 years. Furnia Ianuaria erected this for her husband.
EDCS-26200797.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Antistius Antistianus, who lived to 53, and was erected by his wife, Furnia Ianuaria.

EDCS-26200798.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Antonia Sedula, daughter of Antonius, lived for 10 or 11 years.
EDCS-26200798.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life of Antonia Sedula, who died at the age of 10 or 11.

EDCS-26200799.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marco Caecilio Rogato, a dutiful husband, lived for 50 years. Agria Saturnina made this.
EDCS-26200799.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marco Caecilio Rogato, who lived to 50, and was created by his wife, Agria Saturnina.

EDCS-26200800.translation: Cesellia [---] lived for 35 years. Caius Volussius Felix, her husband, made this.
EDCS-26200800.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cesellia, who lived to 35, and was erected by her husband, Caius Volussius Felix.

EDCS-26200801.translation: To Helvia Briseis, from Firmus, her patron, for his excellent wife.
EDCS-26200801.summary: This is a dedication from a patron, Firmus, to his wife, Helvia Briseis, praising her.

EDCS-26200805.translation: Mulleia Saturnina, her nurse, lived for 85 years. Dedicated by the good by good will.
EDCS-26200805.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Mulleia Saturnina, who lived to 85, and was dedicated by her nurse.

EDCS-26200806.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Geminius for himself and for his wife Mevia Rogata, who lived for 72 years.
EDCS-26200806.summary: This funerary inscription records Caius Geminius dedicating a monument for himself and his wife Mevia Rogata, who lived to 72.

EDCS-26200807.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Iulius [---] V.
EDCS-26200807.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Caius Iulius.

EDCS-26200808.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Iulius Asellus lived for 17 years and 8 months.
EDCS-26200808.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Iulius Asellus, who died at the age of 17 years and 8 months.

EDCS-26200809.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ond Donata lived 82 years.
EDCS-26200809.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Ond Donata, who lived to be 82 years old.

EDCS-26200811.translation: Rufus Arnensis [lived] for himself and his wife, having lived 60 years.
EDCS-26200811.summary: This is a funerary epitaph of Rufus Arnensis, who lived 60 years and dedicated it for himself and his wife.

EDCS-26200812.translation: A sacred memorial from the wood of the cross from the land of promise where Christ was born, the names of the apostles Peter and Paul, of the martyrs Datianus, Donatianus, Cyprianus, Nemesianus, Citinus, and Victoria. Benenatus and Pequaria Victorinus Miggin placed [it] in the 320th year of the province, the seventh day before the Ides of September.
EDCS-26200812.summary: This inscription commemorates relics from the Holy Land and the cross, mentioning apostles and martyrs, and was dedicated by Benenatus and Pequaria Victorinus Miggin in the provincial year 320.

EDCS-26200815.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Rogatus, son of Gaius Julius Iovinus, from Lemelefensis, made and dedicated this tomb monument for himself and his wife, Iulia Sica.
EDCS-26200815.summary: This is a funerary epitaph and a dedication for a tomb monument made by Gaius Julius Rogatus for himself and his wife, Iulia Sica.

EDCS-26200816.translation: Maria, daughter of Peter, lived [X] years.
EDCS-26200816.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for Maria, daughter of Peter, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-26200818.translation: Sacred to Saturnus Augustus. Marcus Caecilius Sabinus, the priest, and his wife Iulia Emerita.
EDCS-26200818.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Saturnus Augustus by Marcus Caecilius Sabinus, a priest, and his wife Iulia Emerita.

EDCS-26200819.translation: To our Lords forever Emperors Flavius Constantinus Maximus, and Flavius Claudius Constantinus the Younger, and Flavius Constantius, most noble Caesars, for the good of the state.
EDCS-26200819.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Constantinus Maximus, Constantinus the Younger, and Constantius, likely for the benefit of the state, possibly related to a public building or honor.

EDCS-26200820.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antistia Rufina lived 13 years, 3 months, and 15 days; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200820.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antistia Rufina, detailing her age at death and burial.

EDCS-26200821.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cornelia Fortunata.
EDCS-26200821.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Fortunata.

EDCS-26200822.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Fabius Demetrius lived 55 years.
EDCS-26200822.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Fabius Demetrius, recording his lifespan of 55 years.

EDCS-26200823.translation: To Titus Flavius Victorinus the younger, his nephew, Titus Flavius Honoratus [dedicated this].
EDCS-26200823.summary: This is a dedication to Titus Flavius Victorinus the younger by Titus Flavius Honoratus, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-26200826.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Asiatica lived 39 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26200826.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Iulia Asiatica at age 39.

EDCS-26200827.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Longinus lived sixty years. [Name of person who arranged the burial] saw to his burial.
EDCS-26200827.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Longinus at age 60, with a mention of the person who arranged the burial.

EDCS-26200828.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sicinius Faus [dedicated this].
EDCS-26200828.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sicinius Faus.

EDCS-26200829.translation: Flavia Emerita made this for her parents, Marcus Virius Pacatus and Iulia Asiatica, his wife.
EDCS-26200829.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Flavia Emerita for her parents, Marcus Virius Pacatus and Iulia Asiatica.

EDCS-26200830.translation: The table of angels of Donata and Secundus.
EDCS-26200830.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication related to a communal meal or religious gathering, possibly funerary, for Donata and Secundus.

EDCS-26200831.translation: Iulius Verus and Felix and Faustinia [dedicated this] to the divine power of the province in the 373rd year of the province, in the 161st year of the province. D(edicated) D(evoted) T(hanks) V(overso).
EDCS-26200831.summary: This inscription is a dedication to provincial deities by Iulius Verus, Felix, and Faustinia, dated by provincial years, likely a religious or administrative monument.

EDCS-26200832.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Pompeius Felix lived 45 years.
EDCS-26200832.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Caius Pompeius Felix at age 45.

EDCS-26200833.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. M. V. Q. Vigilius lived 35 years. Celia Donata lived 41 years.
EDCS-26200833.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Vigilius at age 35 and Celia Donata at age 41.

EDCS-26200835.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Ulpius Soricus lived 95 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Istaberia Secunda lived 40 years.
EDCS-26200835.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Marcus Ulpius Soricus at age 95 and Istaberia Secunda at age 40.

EDCS-26200837.translation: The annual festival, which Jupiter and Genius Vanisnesi commanded, and the gods Ingirozoglezim commanded.
EDCS-26200837.summary: This inscription seems to be a religious invocation or dedication, possibly related to a festival or ritual, mentioning several divine entities.

EDCS-26200839.translation: From the Emperors Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletian Pius Felix Invictus Augustus and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximian Pius Felix Augustus and Flavius Valerius Constantius and Galerius Valerius Maximian, most noble Caesars.
EDCS-26200839.summary: This inscription lists the titles of the Tetrarchic emperors, Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Galerius, likely part of a larger official monument from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-26200840.translation: Under the consulship of Licinius, our lords Constantius and Constantine the Younger, most noble Caesars, X miles.
EDCS-26200840.summary: This inscription records a distance of 10 miles, dedicating it to the emperors Constantius and Constantine the Younger during the consulship of Licinius, likely a milestone marker.

EDCS-26200841.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Kurenia lived for years.
EDCS-26200841.summary: This is a funerary inscription, a dedication to the Manes (departed spirits), recording the name Kurenia and her lifespan.

EDCS-26200842.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Here lies Licinia, daughter of Publius, Maximilla, who lived 27 years, to his most dutiful wife. Marcus Ulpius Ingenuus [dedicated this].
EDCS-26200842.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Ingenuus to his devoted wife, Licinia Maximilla, recording her name, parentage, and age at death.

EDCS-26200843.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Victorinus lived 95 years. Quintus Cornelius Saturninus, also called Ba, lived 115 years.
EDCS-26200843.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the ages at death of two individuals, Victorinus and Quintus Cornelius Saturninus (also known as Ba).

EDCS-26200844.translation: [Dedicated] by the living man, to himself and his wife, he lived 92 years.
EDCS-26200844.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that the deceased, who lived 92 years, made provision for himself and his wife during his lifetime.

EDCS-26200845.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Name] lived 20 years.
EDCS-26200845.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, a dedication to the Manes, recording the lifespan of an individual who lived 20 years.

EDCS-26200847.translation: Here rests Romanilla, an honourable woman, who lived 42 years, departed in peace on the day before the Nones of July in the provincial year 442. Here rests Emerita, an honourable woman, who lived 75 years, departed in peace on the 5th day before the Kalends of August in the provincial year 428.
EDCS-26200847.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lives and peaceful passing of two women, Romanilla and Emerita, including their ages and the provincial year of death, dating to the 5th century CE.

EDCS-26200849.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mecius Primus lived 80 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vaesiddina lived 85 years.
EDCS-26200849.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates two separate individuals, Mecius Primus and Vaesiddina, to the Manes, recording their respective ages at death.

EDCS-26200850.translation: To Emperors Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletian and Maximian (Galerius and Constantius are not mentioned here, it should be Maximian and Diocletian) forever Augusti, vows for 20 years, and to Claudius Flavius Constantius the noblest, (vows) for 30 years.
EDCS-26200850.summary: This inscription records vows made for the emperors Diocletian and Maximian (or possibly Galerius and Constantius based on context), celebrating their vicennalia (20th anniversary of reign).

EDCS-26200851.translation: To our Lords Emperors Constantinus and Constantius, forever Augusti, vows for 20 years, many vows for 30 years; to Constantinus, forever Augustus, the noblest Caesar; to our Lords Flavius Julius Crispus and Claudius Flavius Constantius, born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-26200851.summary: This inscription records vows for the emperors Constantinus and Constantius, as well as for Caesar Crispus and Claudius Flavius Constantius, celebrating their vicennalia and acknowledging their noble status and dedication to the republic.

EDCS-26200852.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Marcius Victor, son of Quintus, decurion, elected aedile, twice chief magistrate, flamen, father of Roman knights and military commands, lived 75 years, is buried here.
EDCS-26200852.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and achievements of Lucius Marcius Victor, a prominent local official and military figure, who lived to be 75 years old.

EDCS-26200865.translation: From the Faurian estates, tile-making work, by Calpetanus Verna.
EDCS-26200865.summary: This is a maker's mark on pottery, indicating that the tiles were produced from the Faurian estates by Calpetanus Verna.

EDCS-26200866.translation: Tile-making work from the estates of our Augustus, by Caius Cominius Sarinianus.
EDCS-26200866.summary: This is a maker's mark on pottery, indicating that the tiles were produced from the estates of 'our Augustus' (likely referring to the emperor's property or a high-ranking official) by Caius Cominius Sarinianus.

EDCS-26200867.translation: Tile-making work from the estates of our Augustus, by Caius Cominius Sarinianus.
EDCS-26200867.summary: This is a maker's mark on pottery, indicating that the tiles were produced from the estates of 'our Augustus' by Caius Cominius Sarinianus.

EDCS-26200868.translation: Tile-making work from the estates of our Augustus, by Caius Cominius Sarinianus.
EDCS-26200868.summary: This is a maker's mark on pottery, indicating that the tiles were produced from the estates of 'our Augustus' by Caius Cominius Sarinianus.

EDCS-26200888.summary: This inscription indicates that lamps were sold by Assenus, likely marking goods for sale or a vendor's stall.

EDCS-26200889.translation: From the workshop of Assenus, filtered lamps.
EDCS-26200889.summary: This inscription indicates that filtered lamps were produced in the workshop of Assenus.

EDCS-26200890.summary: This inscription is an advertisement encouraging the purchase of filtered lamps from Assenus.

EDCS-26200891.summary: This is a commercial inscription advertising lamps for sale, likely from a vendor named Assenus.

EDCS-26200892.summary: This is a commercial inscription directing people to buy lamps sourced from Assenus.

EDCS-26300001.translation: Ma[e]nica lived 60 years. M. Aemilius Rufinus lived 115 years.
EDCS-26300001.summary: This epitaph records the lifespans of two individuals, Ma[e]nica and M. Aemilius Rufinus.

EDCS-26300002.translation: Namphamon lived 85 years. Memmia Siddina, a pious wife.
EDCS-26300002.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Namphamon and describes his wife, Memmia Siddina.

EDCS-26300003.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Octavius Potitus, standard-bearer, lived 40 years.
EDCS-26300003.summary: This funerary inscription honors Octavius Potitus, a standard-bearer, and records his age at death.

EDCS-26300004.translation: Lived 27 years. Lived 48 years. Petilius Felix, a son of good memory, for his father.
EDCS-26300004.summary: This inscription seems to record the ages of two individuals, with the latter part honoring a father by his son, Petilius Felix.

EDCS-26300006.translation: Flauta, daughter, for her son Rogatus, for her son Musincius.
EDCS-26300006.summary: This inscription indicates a dedication by Flauta, a mother, on behalf of her sons Rogatus and Musincius.

EDCS-26300007.translation: Valerius [---] and Vagnienus, the pious Verianus, [---].
EDCS-26300007.summary: This inscription appears to record names, possibly associated with a dedication or funerary context, with pious epithets.

EDCS-26300008.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Valerius Crescens of Emerita lived 50 years. Masof, his sister, made this.
EDCS-26300008.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Valerius Crescens and was made by his sister, Masof.

EDCS-26300010.translation: Lived 60 years. Vibius Secundus, his son, made this for his merits.
EDCS-26300010.summary: This inscription records a lifespan of 60 years and states that Vibius Secundus, the son, erected it, possibly in honor of a parent or benefactor.

EDCS-26300011.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades in memory of Victor, son of AMS S M. He lived 12 years. Codva Conge, living, made and dedicated it to Fortuna.
EDCS-26300011.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Victor, who lived 12 years, and was dedicated by Codva Conge to Fortuna.

EDCS-26300012.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ulpius Vitalis lived 25 years. To the Divine Shades. Ulpius Fron [lived some years].
EDCS-26300012.summary: This fragmentary epitaph records the lifespans of Ulpius Vitalis (25 years) and Ulpius Fron.

EDCS-26300013.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ulpia Ingenua, pious.
EDCS-26300013.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for Ulpia Ingenua.

EDCS-26300014.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ulpia Cacia, matrona, lived 55 years. Valerius Quintassus made and dedicated it.
EDCS-26300014.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Ulpia Cacia, who lived 55 years, and was dedicated by Valerius Quintassus.

EDCS-26300015.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Unertus Felix lived [some years]. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Ninus.
EDCS-26300015.summary: This fragmentary epitaph likely commemorates Unertus Felix and Iulius Ninus.

EDCS-26300018.translation: To the Divine Shades. [Name] lived 70 years. [Name] lived 18 years. [Name] lived 10 years.
EDCS-26300018.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespans of three individuals, likely family members, who lived 70, 18, and 10 years respectively.

EDCS-26300020.translation: In memory of Lucius Longaenius Victor. Iunia Aelia Emerita [dedicated this] to her husband. He lived 56 years.
EDCS-26300020.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Iunia Aelia Emerita to her husband, Lucius Longaenius Victor, who lived 56 years.

EDCS-26300021.translation: Sacred to the Divine. Maximus, son of Ingenuus, lived fifteen years [illegible].
EDCS-26300021.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Maximus, son of Ingenuus, lived fifteen years.

EDCS-26300022.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [Name] lived 30 years. C.
EDCS-26300022.summary: This fragmentary epitaph records the lifespan of an individual who lived 30 years.

EDCS-26300025.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurus Romanus lived 46 years.
EDCS-26300025.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Aurus Romanus, who lived 46 years.

EDCS-26300027.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. L. Ke. Pacatus' daughter, N.X., daughter of Fabius Petronius Namphamon, lived 45 years.
EDCS-26300027.summary: A funerary dedication for a woman named N.X., daughter of L. Ke. Pacatus and Fabius Petronius Namphamon, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-26300031.translation: In most fortunate and blessed times, Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Aurelius Diocletianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, and Flavius Valerius Constantius and Galerius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesars, restored to its original state from the foundations the municipium of Rapidum, which had been captured and destroyed many times by the incursions of rebels, under the supervision of Ulpius Apollonius, a most perfect man, governor of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, devoted to their divinity and majesty.
EDCS-26300031.summary: An inscription detailing the restoration of the town of Rapidum by the emperors Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Galerius, under the governor Ulpius Apollonius, likely in the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-26300032.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius A. lived, served, and made this in his lifetime. SELVA.
EDCS-26300032.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius A., who set up the monument during his lifetime.

EDCS-26300033.translation: Cilia A. lived 60 years. Vitalis lived 80 years. APPON and Caecilius Vitalis made this for their well-deserving parents.
EDCS-26300033.summary: A funerary monument set up by Vitalis for his parents, Cilia A. and Caecilius Vitalis, who lived to be 60 and 80 years old respectively.

EDCS-26300034.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Sentia Luclosa lived 75 years, through Claudius Rogatus.
EDCS-26300034.summary: A funerary dedication for Sentia Luclosa, who lived 75 years, arranged by Claudius Rogatus.

EDCS-26300035.translation: For 60 years. [Someone] made this for his well-deserving mother, father, and brother.
EDCS-26300035.summary: A funerary dedication for a mother, father, and brother, likely by their son or another relative, possibly indicating a lifespan of 60 years for one of them.

EDCS-26300038.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Victoria lived 60 years. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Ianuarius lived 30 years.
EDCS-26300038.summary: Two separate funerary dedications, one for Iulia Victoria who lived 60 years, and another for Iulius Ianuarius who lived 30 years.

EDCS-26300041.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the god Silvanus. Titus Aelius Zabidus made and dedicated the vexillum.
EDCS-26300041.summary: A dedication of a military vexillum (standard) to Jupiter and Silvanus by Titus Aelius Zabidus.

EDCS-26300042.translation: Anta, daughter of Sulla, willingly paid her vow to Saturn.
EDCS-26300042.summary: A votive inscription recording Anta, daughter of Sulla, fulfilling a vow to the god Saturn.

EDCS-26300334.summary: An incomplete inscription, likely the beginning of a name or dedication, possibly for Lucius Publius.

EDCS-26300342.summary: This inscription appears to be a personal name, possibly a tombstone or dedication, belonging to Lucius Rasinius Pisanus.

EDCS-26300375.summary: This inscription refers to 'Silbanus, an honorable man', likely part of a larger funerary or honorific text.

EDCS-26300386.summary: This inscription names Quintus Varius Hiberus, probably as the deceased or the subject of an honorific text.

EDCS-26300390.translation: Marcus VeItidius Philadelphus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, served in the legion for 2 years, lived for 4 years, his servant Sura, lived for 3 years, lived for 9 months. His brother Marcus VeItidius Crispus, a soldier of the Augustan legion, centuria of Gaius Princeps, undertook the care of the monument. He lived for 20 years.
EDCS-26300390.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus VeItidius Philadelphus, detailing his short life and military service, with his brother Marcus VeItidius Crispus dedicating the monument.

EDCS-26300394.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Aemilia Honorata, his most dutiful wife. Lucius Valerius Saturninus, centurion of the praetorian cohort, [erected this] because of her merits. She lived 30 years.
EDCS-26300394.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Lucius Valerius Saturninus to his 'most dutiful wife', Aemilia Honorata, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-26300395.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Quintus Annia Honorata and Aufidia Maxima and Aemilius Fortunatus, his most dutiful parents.
EDCS-26300395.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by an individual to their 'most dutiful parents', Quintus Annia Honorata, Aufidia Maxima, and Aemilius Fortunatus.

EDCS-26300396.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Cae Iugurta, my most loving father. I made this monument. He lived 90 years.
EDCS-26300396.summary: This funerary inscription records the maker of the monument, who dedicated it to their 'most loving father', Cae Iugurta, who lived to 90.

EDCS-26300397.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Egnatius Saturninus, for his well-deserving wife Saturnina. He made [this]. She lived 68 years.
EDCS-26300397.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Egnatius Saturninus to his 'well-deserving wife', Saturnina, who lived 68 years.

EDCS-26300399.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Galla, my incomparable wife. [Erected by] A. T. M. M. She lived 47 years.
EDCS-26300399.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to 'Galla, my incomparable wife', who lived 47 years, with the donor's initials A. T. M. M.

EDCS-26300401.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Iulius [BONI?] C. Rusuccuru lived for...
EDCS-26300401.summary: This funerary inscription names Lucius Iulius and C. Rusuccuru, but the age is incomplete.

EDCS-26300407.translation: Severus placed and dedicated this to his most loving father, Crescentius.
EDCS-26300407.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Severus to his father, Crescentius.

EDCS-26300408.translation: To the divine shades. Gaius Minius Bassus, aged 70.
EDCS-26300408.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Gaius Minius Bassus.

EDCS-26300411.translation: Ilioda lived 80 years. Iulius Modestus, his grandson, made this.
EDCS-26300411.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of Ilioda and made by his grandson, Iulius Modestus.

EDCS-26300412.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Matrona placed and dedicated this to her son, Vpator Pmasde, on the eleventh day before the Kalends of April.
EDCS-26300412.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Matrona to her son, Vpator Pmasde, and dated to March 22nd.

EDCS-26300414.translation: To Caelestis, the August goddess, Restorer of our household, who found Caius Iulius Victoricus and Caecilia Namphamina, his parents, safe and sound, Gaius Iulius Libosus, together with his wife Ulpia Dativa and their children, has donated this altar of squared stone masonry, and willingly fulfilled the vow made abroad, happily.
EDCS-26300414.summary: This inscription records the donation of an altar to the goddess Caelestis by Gaius Iulius Libosus and his family, fulfilling a vow made abroad.

EDCS-26300419.translation: To Victoria Augusta. Gaius Caesius Celsus, aedile, willingly fulfilled his vow, 213.
EDCS-26300419.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius Caesius Celsus, an aedile, to Victoria Augusta.

EDCS-26300420.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Mallon made and dedicated this to Sentius Rogatus, his dearest and most deserving son, Roman knight.
EDCS-26300420.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Mallon to his son, Sentius Rogatus, a Roman knight.

EDCS-26300421.translation: To Quintus Gargilius Martialis, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, veteran, perpetual flamen of the colony, patron, curator and inspector of public affairs, and to Iulia Prima, his wife, Quintus Gargilius Martialis, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Roman knight, candidate for military office, patron of the colony, their son, [erected this] to his most worthy parents.
EDCS-26300421.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Quintus Gargilius Martialis to his parents, Quintus Gargilius Martialis and Iulia Prima, who held various important civic and religious positions in the colony.

EDCS-26300422.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Iulius Honoratus, a beneficiary, [erected this] to Pompeia Profutura, his most dutiful daughter, who lived 8 years, 6 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-26300422.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius Honoratus' daughter, Pompeia Profutura, and her age at death.

EDCS-26300423.translation: From the cohort of the I Aelia Singulares. Similis, the standard-bearer, his heir, placed this for [his] merits, [he] lived 40 years.
EDCS-26300423.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a standard-bearer named Similis from the I Aelia Singulares cohort and was erected by his heir.

EDCS-26300424.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Invves, soldier of the 35th century of the cohort of Gaius Iulius, centurion, 1st rank, from the 2nd cohort.
EDCS-26300424.summary: A funerary dedication by a soldier for a fellow soldier, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26300426.translation: And Claudia, his wife, and his mother dedicated the four steps and Rufina, daughter of Gellus, and Vicianus with money for the baths in Madaurensis PVF.
EDCS-26300426.summary: A dedication of bathing facilities by a woman and her mother, possibly in Madaura, around the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26300427.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Caefalius, an exceptionally praiseworthy youth. What was possible to return was only after his death: a tomb. Pitying the misfortunes of his country and his fellow citizens, he entered by blocked roads on a distant course, whom neither the weary age of his father nor filial piety could hold back from dangers. What remains is the conscience of us all. Why he suffered such a death and grief, while he wanted to bring relief from tributes to the citizens, he met an unhappy fate. Julius Kapito made this for his son Julius Kapitolinus. He lived 44 years, 8 months.
EDCS-26300427.summary: A funerary monument erected by a father for his son, who died seeking to relieve the burdens of citizens, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26300428.translation: Furfanius Honoratus, a Jew, lived for approximately 45 years. Claudia Honorata, his mother, made this.
EDCS-26300428.summary: A funerary inscription for Furfanius Honoratus, set up by his mother, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-26300429.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Aelius Rufinus, dutiful, lived for 30 years. His dutiful mother made this.
EDCS-26300429.summary: A funerary dedication for Titus Aelius Rufinus, erected by his mother, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-26300430.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelius Victoricus lived for 35 years.
EDCS-26300430.summary: A funerary inscription for Aelius Victoricus, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-26300431.translation: Aelia Calvina made this for Caius [Dum] while he lived, for himself and his wife. He lived 55 years. Fabius Ospitalis made and dedicated this. He lived 52 years. Calvina made this for her husband.
EDCS-26300431.summary: A funerary monument for Aelia Calvina and her husband Caius, with separate dedications by their wives, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.
