EDCS-26300432.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Artoria Ianuaria lived 80 years. Honoratus, her son, made this. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Julius Calvus, son, lived 40 years.
EDCS-26300432.summary: A funerary monument with two separate dedications: one for Artoria Ianuaria by her son, and one for Julius Calvus by himself, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-26300433.translation: To Africanus and Magudianus, his fathers.
EDCS-26300433.summary: A dedication to two fathers by their sons, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-26300434.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Made for Bato, son of Muganius. He lived 50 years.
EDCS-26300434.summary: A funerary inscription for Bato Muganii, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-26300435.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Kalpurnius Fortunatus lived 47 years. Plautia Simplicia, with her sons Evassus and Fortunatus, made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-26300435.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Gaius Kalpurnius Fortunatus, erected by his wife and sons.

EDCS-26300436.translation: To Gaius Kalpurnius Maximus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, a boy of good disposition. Gaius Kalpurnius Maximus and Caelia Iulia to their sweetest son.
EDCS-26300436.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by parents to their young son, Gaius Kalpurnius Maximianus.

EDCS-26300437.translation: Cecilia Urbana lived 30 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius Urbanus lived 80 years, and Aelia Quarta lived 3 years, and OI FON Donatus lived 16 years. Cecilia Urbana.
EDCS-26300437.summary: A funerary monument commemorating multiple individuals, including Cecilia Urbana, Aelius Urbanus, Aelia Quarta, and OI FON Donatus.

EDCS-26300439.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Petronia the pious lived 40 years.
EDCS-26300439.summary: A funerary monument for Claudia Petronia, who lived 40 years and was described as pious.

EDCS-26300440.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Egnatia Rogata lived 40 years. Bombia Cenis made this.
EDCS-26300440.summary: A funerary monument for Egnatia Rogata, erected by Bombia Cenis.

EDCS-26300441.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Flavius Felix, who lived 52 years and some months.
EDCS-26300441.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Flavius Felix, who lived 52 years and some months.

EDCS-26300442.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Paulinus made this tomb for his dearest son Geminio, who lived 22 years. Flavius Paulinus made this tomb for his dearest daughter Flavia Victorina, who lived 17 years. Flavius Paulinus made this tomb for his dearest daughter Flavia Paulina, who lived 11 years. N R VA
EDCS-26300442.summary: A funerary monument erected by Flavius Paulinus for three of his children, who died at ages 22, 17, and 11.

EDCS-26300443.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. FMINIA Aufi dedicated [this tomb] at 26 years old, the pious [woman]. Her husband.
EDCS-26300443.summary: A funerary monument for FMINIA Aufi, dedicated by her husband when she was 26 years old.

EDCS-26300444.translation: There would have been love between us if fate had allowed. Our union was sweet for two years. Marcus Bulocinus made and dedicated this for his dearest wife Flavia Camerina, province 315, who lived 35 years. He drank for
EDCS-26300444.summary: A funerary monument erected by Marcus Bulocinus for his wife Flavia Camerina, who died after two years of marriage and lived 35 years.

EDCS-26300445.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaiutas lived 20 years.
EDCS-26300445.summary: A funerary monument for Gaiutas, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-26300447.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Gargilius Gududus, who lived 80 years. Julia Matrona, his wife, made this.
EDCS-26300447.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gargilius Gududus, set up by his wife.

EDCS-26300448.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For the Bassus Rogatus, Salgan's son, Julius Privatus, his father, made this.
EDCS-26300448.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Bassulus Rogatus, set up by his father.

EDCS-26300450.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Felix, deceived by a fraud, lived 1 year, 10 months, 12 days. Iulius Martialis, deceived by a fraud, lived 2 years, 7 months.
EDCS-26300450.summary: This funerary epitaph records the short lives of two individuals, Iulius Felix and Iulius Martialis, both victims of fraud.

EDCS-26300451.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Privatus made this for Felix, his son, who lived 21 years, and for Tanonia Ianuaria, his wife. Privata... [text incomplete]
EDCS-26300451.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by Iulius Privatus for his son Felix and his wife Tanonia Ianuaria.

EDCS-26300452.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Iulius Quartus, who lived 45 years. Esia Vicio, his dearest husband, made and dedicated this.
EDCS-26300452.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Quartus, set up by his wife Esia Vicio.

EDCS-26300453.translation: Publius Iulius Valentinus, who lived 6 years and 6 months. Valentinus, his son, made this.
EDCS-26300453.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Iulius Valentinus, set up by his son Valentinus.

EDCS-26300454.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Augenda, who lived 40 years, 3 months, 5 days. Terentius made this for his dearest mother. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caelia Malavia, dearest wife, who lived 31 years, 5 months, 7 days. Gi Faustinus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-26300454.summary: This funerary monument contains two epitaphs: one for Iulia Augenda, set up by her son Terentius, and another for Caelia Malavia, set up by her husband Gi Faustinus.

EDCS-26300455.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Gudula, who lived 70 years. May your bones rest well. Her son made this.
EDCS-26300455.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Gudula, set up by her son.

EDCS-26300456.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Iulia Hospita, his dearest wife, who lived 23 years, 11 months, 5 days. Publius Aelius Marianus [made this] for his deserving wife.
EDCS-26300456.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Hospita, set up by her husband Publius Aelius Marianus.

EDCS-26300457.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely recording the name of the deceased and their age, with a maker identified.

EDCS-26300458.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Labenia Cogutan lived 103 years. Secunda, her daughter, made this.
EDCS-26300458.summary: A funerary epitaph for Labenia Cogutan, erected by her daughter Secunda.

EDCS-26300459.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Longinia Zocabne lived about 80 years. Loizi Domitia, her daughter, made this for her most rare mother.
EDCS-26300459.summary: A funerary epitaph for Longinia Zocabne, erected by her daughter Loizi Domitia.

EDCS-26300460.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For his most pious wife Luriae Virginosae and his dutiful daughter Umbriae Vitali. Marcus Umbrius Prota made this. Virginosa lived 45 years. Vitalis lived 4 years and 11 months.
EDCS-26300460.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Marcus Umbrius Prota for his wife Luriae Virginosae and daughter Umbriae Vitali.

EDCS-26300461.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Luxurus, son of Secundus, lived 8 years. Aurelius Honoru made and dedicated this for his dearest. Sacred to the departed spirits. In memory of Secundinus Ariel, who lived 45 years. Aril Honoru made and dedicated this for her husband.
EDCS-26300461.summary: Two funerary epitaphs, one for Luxurus set up by Aurelius Honoru, and another for Secundinus Ariel set up by his wife Aril Honoru.

EDCS-26300462.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marius Castus the younger lived 26 years and 5 months. Marius Castus, his father, made this. Sacred to the departed spirits. Maria Rustica lived 16 years and 11 months. Marius Castus dedicated and made this.
EDCS-26300462.summary: Two funerary epitaphs, one for Marius Castus the younger set up by his father, and another for Maria Rustica dedicated by Marius Castus.

EDCS-26300463.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Maria Nampame who worked well. Primus made this tomb. She lived about 85 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. For his father Primo who worked well. Primus, his son, made this tomb. He lived about 95 years.
EDCS-26300463.summary: Two funerary epitaphs for Maria Nampame and her husband Primo, both erected by their son Primus.

EDCS-26300464.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quinta Midia Rogata lived 71 years. Aegamius dedicated this for his worthy mother-in-law. Modius Iucundus lived 60 years. Q. Rogata, his wife, made this.
EDCS-26300464.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quinta Midia Rogata and Modius Iucundus, set up by their relatives.

EDCS-26400642.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, father of the fatherland, conqueror of Arabia and Adiabene, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, Imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul. Gaius Julius Honoratus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, [---] according to the decree of the council, from the highest honor of his flamenship, furthermore added from his own generosity money to the value of 8,000 sesterces.
EDCS-26400642.summary: A dedication of funds by Gaius Julius Honoratus, likely for public works or religious purposes, honoring Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-26500040.translation: Within these properties of Marcus Cincius Hilarianus, son of Marcus, perpetual flamen of Augustus, and of Vetidia Impetrata, his wife.
EDCS-26500040.summary: A property inscription identifying the owners as Marcus Cincius Hilarianus and Vetidia Impetrata.

EDCS-26500045.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Augustus, father of the fatherland. Vibia Celerina, wife of Lucius Valerius Rufus, duovir quinquennalis, and mother of Lucius Valerius Scipio, Valeria Scipionilla, and Valeria Celerina, gave this gift. Moved from unsanitary places.
EDCS-26500045.summary: A dedication to Juno Regina for the safety of the Emperor Augustus, made by Vibia Celerina, wife of a duovir, and mother of three children.

EDCS-26500046.translation: Atelius Frugi and Cornelia Antestia gladly paid their vow to the god Saturn.
EDCS-26500046.summary: This dedication to Saturn was made by Atelius Frugi and Cornelia Antestia.

EDCS-26500047.translation: Cerdo gladly paid his vow to Saturn.
EDCS-26500047.summary: This is a votive dedication to Saturn by Cerdo.

EDCS-26500048.translation: Gorgene the *estiarius* gladly paid her vow to Saturn.
EDCS-26500048.summary: This votive dedication to Saturn was made by Gorgene, an *estiarius* (possibly a type of entertainer or vendor).

EDCS-26500049.translation: He gladly paid his vow to Lord Saturn.
EDCS-26500049.summary: This votive dedication to Saturn was made by an unnamed individual.

EDCS-26500051.translation: Marcus Iulius Rogatus gladly paid his vow to the Lord.
EDCS-26500051.summary: This votive dedication to an unspecified 'Lord' was made by Marcus Iulius Rogatus.

EDCS-26500053.summary: This is a dedication by Quintus Pomp Tert.

EDCS-26500065.translation: For the procurator of Augustus, T. Constantius, 40 years of service.
EDCS-26500065.summary: This inscription honors T. Constantius, a procurator of Augustus, likely commemorating 40 years of service.

EDCS-26500077.translation: You who pass by and see my monument, look with indignation: here lies Tiberius Claudius, freedman of Augustus and a clerk from the accounts department, whom Cannutia, his deserving wife, placed here after his death; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-26500077.summary: This tombstone records the death of Tiberius Claudius, a freedman and clerk for Augustus, and was dedicated by his wife Cannutia.

EDCS-26500078.translation: To Fortunatus, most innocent freedman of Augustus, [from] Heliodorus, the prefect.
EDCS-26500078.summary: This dedication honors Fortunatus, a freedman of Augustus, and was made by Heliodorus, a prefect.

EDCS-26500079.translation: To the Divine Shades: Auximus, freedman of Augustus and procurator of Augustus, [dedicated this] to his deserving wife, Ulpia Hermiona.
EDCS-26500079.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Ulpia Hermiona by her husband Auximus, who was both a freedman and a procurator of Augustus.

EDCS-26500080.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lona Adiutor, mother, made this for her devoted son. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26500080.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26500083.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Acrio V, a soldier of Syrian origin, served for 19 years and lived for 47 years. A.B. E Silva made this.
EDCS-26500083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a Syrian soldier named Acrio V, recording his service and age.

EDCS-26500084.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aemilius of the 52nd cohort.
EDCS-26500084.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Marcus Aemilius, a soldier identified by his cohort.

EDCS-26500085.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonius Karus, duplicarius of the fleet, made this for his well-deserving brother, Antonius Avitus, a soldier of Syrian origin, who lived 21 years and served for 6 years.
EDCS-26500085.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a fleet officer to his brother, a Syrian soldier.

EDCS-26500086.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Appeus Celer Memmi served for 32 years. Lollia Saturnina had this made for her husband. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26500086.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by a wife for her soldier husband, Appeus Celer Memmi.

EDCS-26500087.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Atellia Accepta lived for 55 years and 6 months. Her son, Aelius Felix, a cavalryman of Ques origin, erected this for his most beloved mother for her merits.
EDCS-26500087.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Atellia Accepta and was dedicated by her son, a cavalryman.

EDCS-26500088.translation: Avitius S, soldier of the 1st Legion TER.
EDCS-26500088.summary: This is a very brief inscription identifying Avitius S as a soldier of the 1st Legion.

EDCS-26500089.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Vincentius, a soldier of the third praetorian cohort, Maximinus' century, served for 5 years in the eleventh Claudian Legion and 11 years in the praetorian guard, and lived for 40 years. He was a Thracian citizen. His fellow citizens made this for him, well-deserving of his deeds.
EDCS-26500089.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Aurelius Vincentius, a soldier with service in both a legion and the praetorian guard, erected by his fellow citizens.

EDCS-26500090.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Cartellus Rufus, a veteran, who lived 60 years, and for his daughter, Cartilia Munatia, who lived 18 years. Ulpia Urbana made this for her well-deserving husband and stepson. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26500090.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Ulpia Urbana to her husband, a veteran, and her daughter.

EDCS-26500091.translation: Tiberius Claudius Aquilus, a veteran, lived 70 years. He is buried here. His freedwoman, Claudia Fortunata, who made this monument for him, is heir.
EDCS-26500091.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the freedwoman Claudia Fortunata for her former master and veteran, Tiberius Claudius Aquilus.

EDCS-26500092.translation: Tiberius Claudius Congonetiacus, a cavalryman of the II Thracum ala, of Biturix nationality, aged 60, with 32 years of service, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Tiberius Claudius Viator, Tiberius Claudius Clemens, and Claudia Maurica, his children, had this erected in accordance with his will.
EDCS-26500092.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Congonetiacus, a Roman auxiliary cavalryman, erected by his children.

EDCS-26500093.translation: Lucius Iulius Saturninus, son of Caius, of the Fabia tribe, and Marcus Antonius Heracla, trierarch, erected this for Tiberius Claudius Eros, freedman of Augustus, trierarch of the Nile liburnian, commander of the Augustan fleet at Alexandria, and his heirs.
EDCS-26500093.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Eros, a naval officer in Egypt, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-26500094.translation: To Claudius Gemellus, son of Rogatus, a cavalryman of the Thracum ala, serving in Atticus' turma, served for 12 years, lived for 30 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-26500094.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Gemellus, a Roman auxiliary cavalryman, likely erected by his family.

EDCS-26500095.translation: Tiberius Claudius Lucretius, freedman, son of a veteran, lived for 20 years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. His mother had this made.
EDCS-26500095.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Lucretius, son of a veteran, erected by his mother.

EDCS-26500097.translation: Flavia Ianuaria erected this monument for her husband Longinus Muleruna, a sesquiplicarius of the Miliaria ala, serving in Peticus' turma, served for 21 years, lived for 40 years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-26500097.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Longinus Muleruna, a Roman auxiliary cavalryman, erected by his wife Flavia Ianuaria.

EDCS-26500098.translation: Flavia Saturnina had this made from her own funds for her husband Lauros, a cavalryman of the II Thracum ala, serving in Sulpicius' turma, served for 27 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you, my beloved husband.
EDCS-26500098.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lauros, a Roman auxiliary cavalryman, erected by his wife Flavia Saturnina.

EDCS-26500099.translation: Marcus Furius Herennus, of the Papiria tribe, from Astigi, son of a veteran, is buried here. Baetica bore me. I came eager to know the boundaries of fertile Libya and Caesar's realm; eager for the fates, they snatched me away. I considered myself my own, though I lived among strangers for 50 years. While life remained, I lived dear to my loved ones, pious, and skilled in all things. Go, my friends, without me to your own homes. I ask, passerby, as you speak your farewell, may the earth rest lightly upon you, and may my bones rest gently.
EDCS-26500099.summary: This is a long and poetic funerary epitaph for Marcus Furius Herennus, a veteran's son, likely from Spain, who died at 50.

EDCS-26500100.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gargilia Honora of Salditana lived for years without any fault. Live, mortals, I warn you, death awaits all. Learn, you who read this. I, Herennius Rogatus, sesquiplicarius of the fleet, made this in sorrow for my worthy and deserving wife Gargilia. May the earth rest lightly upon you, Gargilia.
EDCS-26500100.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gargilia Honora, erected by her husband Herennius Rogatus, a naval officer, who urges mortals to remember death.

EDCS-26500101.translation: To Quintus, son of Lucius Gavius Libo, of the cohort I Pannoniorum, to his most dutiful son.
EDCS-26500101.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary or commemorative, to Quintus, son of Lucius Gavius Libo, a soldier in the I Pannoniorum cohort.

EDCS-26500102.translation: To Quintus Gavius Frontinus, son of Sextus, of the Collina tribe, centurion of the singulares. Sextus Gavius Paulus, his father, [erected this] for his son, who served for 3 years, 7 months, and 22 days, and lived for 28 years and 18 days.
EDCS-26500102.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Gavius Frontinus, a centurion of the singulares, erected by his father.

EDCS-26500103.translation: Titus Herculianus Clemens, prefect of the thousand-strong cavalry wing of the Maria, to his excellent wife, Modesta, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-26500103.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a cavalry prefect to his wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26500104.translation: Iulius, son of Iulius, aedile, duovir, prefect of the cavalry of the III Ulpian Cohort of Paphlagonians, tribune of the III Cyrenaica Legion, prefect of the Flavian Wing of Gallic cavalry, his wife Saturnina, heir by will.
EDCS-26500104.summary: This is a complex official career inscription listing numerous posts held by Iulius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26500105.translation: Caius Iulius Dapnus served in the Cohort of Syrians for 30 years, was granted retirement, and made this for his merits. Iulia Vartinigig made this for her husband for his merits; he is buried here.
EDCS-26500105.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Iulius Dapnus, a soldier who served in the Cohort of Syrians, erected by his wife.

EDCS-26500107.translation: Licaus, son of Iauletis, a soldier of the VII Dalmatian Cohort, in the troop of Annius, aged 27, with 11 years of service, is buried here. His heir made this by will.
EDCS-26500107.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licaus, a soldier in the VII Dalmatian Cohort, erected by his heir.

EDCS-26500108.translation: Licco, son of Burnio, a Pannonian, a cavalryman of the Pannonian Cohort, lived 27 years. Dexter, centurion, and Breucus, son of Laudion, took care to have this made.
EDCS-26500108.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Licco, a cavalryman from Pannonia, erected by a centurion and another soldier.

EDCS-26500109.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Manilius Rufus served for 26 years, lived for 59 years, is buried here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-26500109.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Manilius Rufus, a soldier who served for 26 years and lived to 59.

EDCS-26500110.translation: To the departed spirits. Saggin, a sesquiplicarius of the Ala Sebastenian, made this monument for his most worthy wife Atellia Terefana. She lived 35 years. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-26500110.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Saggin, a soldier of the Ala Sebastenian, for his wife Atellia Terefana.

EDCS-26500111.translation: Saturnina, daughter of Saturninus VeIteranus, aged 3 years, is here.
EDCS-26500111.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young child named Saturnina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26500113.translation: Terentia, daughter of Caius Terentius, lived 28 years, is buried here. Quintus Marcius, a soldier of the III Legion, made the monument for her merits.
EDCS-26500113.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Terentia, erected by Quintus Marcius, a soldier of the III Legion, in recognition of her merits.

EDCS-26500115.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Valerius Bassus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix Legion, with 19 years of service, lived 40 years. His heir made this for the deserving man.
EDCS-26500115.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Valerius Bassus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix Legion, erected by his heir.

EDCS-26500116.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Valerius Lascivus, an equestrian of the II Flavia Hispanorum cavalry, lived for 35 years and served for 13 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-26500116.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Lascivus, an equestrian soldier, indicating his age and service length.

EDCS-26500117.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Valerius Longus, a veteran from Alexandria, lived for 60 years. His heirs had this made. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26500117.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Valerius Longus, a veteran from Alexandria, stating his age and that his heirs erected the monument.

EDCS-26500118.translation: Verzo, son of Dasius, a soldier from the cohort.
EDCS-26500118.summary: An inscription identifying Verzo, son of Dasius, as a soldier in an unspecified cohort.

EDCS-26500119.translation: IAAIANTIES, soldier of the XXX Ulpia Victrix legion, with 17 years of service, lived for 40 years. His heir had this made, being of Italian origin.
EDCS-26500119.summary: A funerary epitaph for IAAIANTIES, a soldier of the XXX Ulpia Victrix legion, detailing his service length, age, and origin.

EDCS-26500121.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ae, freedwoman of a primipilaris, who lived for 18 years, 2 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-26500121.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ae, a freedwoman of a primipilaris, specifying her age at death.

EDCS-26500122.translation: EL MAI of the XIIII Gemina legion. He is buried here.
EDCS-26500122.summary: An inscription identifying EL MAI as belonging to the XIIII Gemina legion and stating he is buried there.

EDCS-26500123.translation: Freedman of ME, of the Dalmatae. He dedicated this for himself and [his family/his freedmen].
EDCS-26500123.summary: An inscription by a freedman of ME, from the Dalmatae, dedicating a monument for himself and others.

EDCS-26500125.summary: An inscription fragment identifying ILA as belonging to an unspecified cohort.

EDCS-26500126.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning Ameanti, a veteran, followed by illegible text.

EDCS-26500127.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sacred to Aemilia Severia, daughter of Aemilius Severinus the decurion, who lived for 5 and a half years. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iulia Veria her grandmother and Antistia Lucilla her mother had this made.
EDCS-26500127.summary: A funerary epitaph for young Aemilia Severia, daughter of a decurion, erected by her grandmother and mother.

EDCS-26500129.translation: Lucius Domitius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, formerly a decurion in the Parthian cavalry, quaestor, aedile, and duovir, had this monument erected by his heirs according to his will.
EDCS-26500129.summary: This inscription records the funerary monument of Lucius Domitius Felix, a former military officer and local magistrate, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-26500131.translation: Posis erected this monument for Fausto, her husband, because of his devotion.
EDCS-26500131.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Posis for her devoted husband, Fausto.

EDCS-26500132.translation: Iulius Mustarius, a dendrophorus (tree-bearer, possibly religious or related to timber trade).
EDCS-26500132.summary: This very brief inscription identifies Iulius Mustarius as a dendrophorus.

EDCS-26500133.translation: Labericia is buried here; her associates erected this monument.
EDCS-26500133.summary: This inscription records the burial of Labericia, with the monument erected by her associates.

EDCS-26500135.translation: Sacred to the Dis Manes. She lived 3 years, 4 months, and 10 days. IA, freedwoman of the public of Caesariensis, daughter of CCCMN.
EDCS-26500135.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of IA, a freedwoman and daughter, who died at a young age.

EDCS-26500141.translation: Gaius Secundus Marcus Portius, in honor of DIVS.
EDCS-26500141.summary: This inscription records an honorific dedication by Gaius Secundus Marcus Portius to someone named DIVS.

EDCS-26500143.summary: This inscription indicates that the object it is on comes from the workshop of Murisius.

EDCS-26500144.summary: This inscription identifies Muccosus as the praepositus (overseer) of Claudius Sabinus.

EDCS-26500148.translation: Alypias, freedman of Iuba, made this for Alypiades, his son, who lived one year, 5 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-26500148.summary: This epitaph records the monument erected by Alypias for his son Alypiades, who died in infancy.

EDCS-26500149.translation: Dinamis, his mother, erected this monument at her own expense for Iulia Clita, freedwoman of Epaphra, freedman of King Iuba, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-26500149.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Dinamis for her daughter Iulia Clita, who was a freedwoman of freedmen, at her own expense.

EDCS-26500150.translation: Iulia Fastila, freedwoman of Iuba, lies here; she was 22 years old.
EDCS-26500150.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of a freedwoman named Iulia Fastila.

EDCS-26500151.translation: Iulia Phiale, freedwoman of Iuba, lived for 30 years; she lies here.
EDCS-26500151.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of a freedwoman named Iulia Phiale.

EDCS-26500153.translation: Sincerus, freedman of Iuba, lies here in the tomb; he wove the vines, do not grieve for the funeral.
EDCS-26500153.summary: This funerary inscription describes Sincerus, a freedman of Iuba, and offers a message of solace to mourners.

EDCS-26500156.translation: Iulia Prima, wife, made this for Gaius Julius Montanus, freedman of King Ptolemy.
EDCS-26500156.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulia Prima to her husband, Gaius Julius Montanus, a freedman of King Ptolemy.

EDCS-26500158.translation: Iulia Pieris, freedwoman of King Ptolemy, [for] Marcus Casineius Serpulus, her husband.
EDCS-26500158.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Iulia Pieris, a freedwoman of King Ptolemy, to her husband Marcus Casineius Serpulus.

EDCS-26500159.translation: Fausta, freedwoman of King Myrrepsus, is the wife of Librarius.
EDCS-26500159.summary: This inscription identifies Fausta as the freedwoman of King Myrrepsus and the wife of Librarius.

EDCS-26500160.translation: Anteros, the piper, Vedianus Molpus, lies here.
EDCS-26500160.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Anteros, identified as a piper (hypaules).

EDCS-26500161.translation: Fadianus Bubbal, physician, lived for 62 years; he lies here.
EDCS-26500161.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Fadianus Bubbal, a physician.

EDCS-26500162.translation: Martialis lies here; he lived for 2 years. Semnos the musician made this.
EDCS-26500162.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Martialis, made by Semnos, identified as a musician (symphoniacus).

EDCS-26500163.translation: Flavinus, son of Flavius Clovii, from Saldita, lived for 19 years; he lies here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-26500163.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Flavinus, son of Flavius Clovii, from Saldita, and includes a common epitaph wish.

EDCS-26500164.translation: To the departed spirits. Latisius Macutus of Caesariensis lived for 26 years; he is buried here. Latidia Hygia, his mother, to her most longed-for son.
EDCS-26500164.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Latisius Macutus, who died at age 26, and was erected by his mother.

EDCS-26500165.summary: This inscription appears to be a very abbreviated funerary marker, possibly for Lesbia Myndia, with 'Rhodo' potentially being a name or description.

EDCS-26500166.translation: Gaius Mimmius Capito Alexandrus lived for 50 years; he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26500166.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaius Mimmius Capito Alexandrus, who died at age 50.

EDCS-26500167.translation: Marcus Sestius Florus lived for 4 years and 6 months. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sestia Cerialis of Caesariensis arranged for this monument to be made for her son.
EDCS-26500167.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Marcus Sestius Florus, who died at age 4 years and 6 months, and was erected by his mother Sestia Cerialis.

EDCS-26500170.translation: Aelius Ulpius Alexandrinus [lived for] 6 years, to [his] most dutiful [son].
EDCS-26500170.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary epitaph for Aelius Ulpius Alexandrinus, who died at age 6, likely erected by a parent.

EDCS-26500171.translation: Silius Mazicus, Julius QV, [and his] brother.
EDCS-26500171.summary: This abbreviated inscription likely records names of individuals, possibly for a joint funerary monument or a family record.

EDCS-26500173.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius, freedman of the Augusti.
EDCS-26500173.summary: This inscription records Aelius, a freedman of the emperors (Augusti), dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-26500174.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Aelius [died at age] 4 [something].
EDCS-26500174.summary: This fragmented inscription is a dedication to the departed spirits for Publius Aelius, possibly recording his age at death.

EDCS-26500176.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Aelius Ampliatus, [his] father. Aelius Ampliatus, [his] son, lived for 10 years.
EDCS-26500176.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aelius Ampliatus at age 10, erected by his father of the same name.

EDCS-26500177.translation: Aelia Victorina to her most dutiful husband. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26500177.summary: This funerary epitaph records Aelia Victorina dedicating a burial place to her devoted husband.

EDCS-26500178.translation: To the Divine Shades. Her sons, daughters, and sons-in-law arranged to have this made for Aemilia Iucunda, their mother.
EDCS-26500178.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated to Aemilia Iucunda by her children and sons-in-law.

EDCS-26500179.translation: To the Divine Shades. He made this for Afrania Victorina, who deserved well.
EDCS-26500179.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated to Afrania Victorina by an unnamed individual, stating she was deserving.

EDCS-26500180.translation: She lived 3 months and 8 days. He lived 5 years, 9 months, and 24 days. Amandus and Fortunula made this for their sweetest children. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-26500180.summary: This epitaph records the lifespans of two children and was set up by their parents, Amandus and Fortunula.

EDCS-26500181.translation: To the Divine Shades. Annia Flora made this for her well-deserving husband, Annius Abellas, who lived 35 years. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-26500181.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Annia Flora to her deserving husband, Annius Abellas.

EDCS-26500182.translation: To the Divine Shades. Annius Quintillus made this for his most devoted son, Annio Felici, who lived 16 years, 4 months. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-26500182.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Annius Quintillus to his devoted son, Annio Felici.

EDCS-26500183.translation: To the Divine Shades. For his well-deserving freedman, Annio Hermeti, who lived 52 years and 6 months.
EDCS-26500183.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespans of Annio Hermeti, a freedman, and was likely dedicated by his former master.

EDCS-26500184.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aelia Flavia for her most longed-for son, Antonio Iulio, who lived 7 years, 10 months, and 28 days. He is buried here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-26500184.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Aelia Flavia to her son, Antonio Iulio, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-26500185.translation: To the Divine Shades. May the earth be light for you. For Marcus Arrius Silvanus, son of Arrius, who lived one year, 4 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-26500185.summary: This funerary epitaph records the short life of Marcus Arrius Silvanus, son of Arrius.

EDCS-26500186.translation: To the Divine Shades. Arrius Victor and Flavia Severa for their most longed-for daughter, Arria Fructuosa, who lived 13 years, 8 months, and 6 days.
EDCS-26500186.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Arrius Victor and Flavia Severa to their daughter, Arria Fructuosa, noting her age.

EDCS-26500187.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Attius Apollonius for his protégés, Attia Caelestina, who lived 3 years, 11 months, and 19 days, and Attia Crescentia, who lived 1 year.
EDCS-26500187.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Quintus Attius Apollonius for two young protégés, Attia Caelestina and Attia Crescentia, recording their ages.

EDCS-26500188.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Augustalis lived 20 years and 3 months; his most devoted mother [set this up].
EDCS-26500188.summary: A funerary epitaph for Augustalis, commemorating his age and erected by his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26500190.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Crispinus lived 4 years, 4 months, and 16 days; his mother Aurea Cornelia [set this up] for her dearest son.
EDCS-26500190.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Crispinus, detailing his young age and erected by his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26500191.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia D lived 25 years and X days. Chrysogonus [set this up] for his well-deserving friend.
EDCS-26500191.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia D, commemorating her age and erected by her friend Chrysogonus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26500192.translation: Ausesis the virgin, aged 15. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26500192.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ausesis, a young virgin, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26500193.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Baucia lived 17 years, 4 months, and 19 days. Publius Aelius Primitivus and Aelia Tigris [set this up] for their most devoted brother.
EDCS-26500193.summary: A funerary epitaph for Baucia, detailing her age and erected by her siblings Publius Aelius Primitivus and Aelia Tigris, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26500195.translation: Bervius, son of Berulus, is laid here; he lived 1 year and 2 months.
EDCS-26500195.summary: A funerary epitaph for Bervius, a child, detailing his age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800202.summary: A very short inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership mark, related to Quintus Asinius Marcellus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800739.translation: At the request of the creator God and Christ, and desiring the memory of Saints Peter and Paul, Onesta the mother, with Gratia, Petronius Cassius, and Patricius with their families, set this up in this sanctuary for their prayer.
EDCS-26800739.summary: A Christian dedication mentioning the creators God and Christ, Saints Peter and Paul, and a group including Onesta, Gratia, Petronius Cassius, and Patricius, likely from the Late Roman or Early Byzantine period.

EDCS-26800740.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sacred. Aemilius Donatus lived [X] years.
EDCS-26800740.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for Aemilius Donatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800741.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sacred. Pompeius Urbanus [set this up].
EDCS-26800741.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph, possibly mentioning Pompeius Urbanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800747.translation: From the gods given on these estates, Fabi Sulpicius Crisogonus and his son Fasius Crisogonus undertook the construction from the foundations and brought it to dedication.
EDCS-26800747.summary: This is a dedication inscription by Fabi Sulpicius Crisogonus and his son for the construction of buildings on their estates.

EDCS-26800749.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Satura, mother of Valerius Tannatus and Pudentius, lived here for 32 years and 10 days.
EDCS-26800749.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Satura, recording her lifespan and dedicating the monument to the Divine Shades.

EDCS-26800751.translation: Aurelius Masfelus, scout and standard-bearer of the Ala Getula, of Saturninus's turma, made this, having lived for more or less 69 years, 3 months, and [? days].
EDCS-26800751.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Masfelus, a soldier in the Ala Getula, recording his service and lifespan.

EDCS-26800753.translation: Siliqua, frequent in my limbs in the bathing place for 15 [days/years?].
EDCS-26800753.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that may record a person named Siliqua and a duration of stay or activity in a bathhouse.

EDCS-26800755.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcella made this with her own money for her father, who lived for 83 years.
EDCS-26800755.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Marcella for her father, who lived to be 83 years old.

EDCS-26800758.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Veranus, a veteran, made this for his son Iactorensis.
EDCS-26800758.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a veteran named Veranus for his son Iactorensis.

EDCS-26800759.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Salustia Felicia lived for 12 years.
EDCS-26800759.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Salustia Felicia, recording her young age at death.

EDCS-26800760.translation: Veranus made this for his most beloved wife Victorina.
EDCS-26800760.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Veranus to his wife, Victorina.

EDCS-26800764.translation: Aurelius [---]gasen, who lived for 5 years and [? months/days], made this mausoleum.
EDCS-26800764.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Aurelius [---]gasen, who died at the age of five and for whom a mausoleum was constructed.

EDCS-26800765.summary: This is a short religious inscription expressing enduring hope in God.

EDCS-26800766.translation: To the memory of M. Matiga, whom Nezrifa, a matron, erected for her father Marganau Magirsumai.
EDCS-26800766.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a daughter for her father.

EDCS-26800767.translation: To the departed spirits. Aelius Buranus, a veteran, lived 60 years, and Aelius Burdanus lived 19 years. Aufidia Tertia erected this monument for the well-deserving.
EDCS-26800767.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of two individuals, Aelius Buranus and Aelius Burdanus, and was erected by Aufidia Tertia.

EDCS-26800768.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Mustius Annianus, dutiful, lived 30 years and 40 days. Mustius Alcimus erected this for his dearest son.
EDCS-26800768.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a father, Mustius Alcimus, to his son, Mustius Annianus.

EDCS-26800769.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Quintus Marcus Tannonius, soldier of the III Augustan Legion, I spent my own money to erect this for Quintus Tannonius Minensis, my dearest father, aged 50. He lived 75 years.
EDCS-26800769.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by a soldier of the III Augustan Legion for his father, Quintus Tannonius Minensis.

EDCS-26800770.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Iulius Iidir lived about 50 years and departed in peace on the 13th day before the Kalends of May. His brothers erected this monument upon his death.
EDCS-26800770.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius Iidir, who lived approximately 50 years, and was erected by his brothers.

EDCS-26800771.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Iulia Crescensa lived about 65 years and departed in peace on the eighth day before the Kalends of February. Her sons and grandsons erected this monument upon her death in peace.
EDCS-26800771.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Crescensa, who lived approximately 65 years, and was erected by her sons and grandsons.

EDCS-26800772.translation: To the departed spirits. To whoever passes by and travels beyond this tomb and says 'Hail Aufidia', may the air be light for you, and may it be light for you too upon your death. He lived 72 years.
EDCS-26800772.summary: This is a tomb inscription by Aufidia, who lived 72 years, invoking blessings upon those who greet her tomb.

EDCS-26800773.translation: In memory of Aurelia Felicia, who lived 70 years and departed on the 3rd day before the Ides of December in the year 438.
EDCS-26800773.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life and death of Aurelia Felicia in the year 438.

EDCS-26800774.translation: In memory of Iulius Donatus, who lived 20 years and died on the 15th day before the Kalendas of August in the provincial year 470.
EDCS-26800774.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Iulius Donatus in the provincial year 470.

EDCS-26800775.summary: This inscription records the erection of something by Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus.

EDCS-26800779.translation: Memorial of Iulius Sandalius. He lived 73 years. He was accepted in the 427th year of the province, on the 7th day before the Ides of September.
EDCS-26800779.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and date of acceptance of Iulius Sandalius in the 427th year of the province.

EDCS-26800780.translation: Memorial of Marcellus. He departed on Tuesday, the 21st day of the moon, in the Ides of August, in the 441st year of the province.
EDCS-26800780.summary: This funerary inscription records the departure date of Marcellus in the 441st year of the province.

EDCS-26800781.translation: By the gods and Christ, Umbrius Felix, the leader, made this, fulfilled his vow to the Lord, and prays for his sins to be saved in the 369th year of the province.
EDCS-26800781.summary: This is a votive inscription by Umbrius Felix, a leader, dedicated to God and Christ in the 369th year of the province.

EDCS-26800782.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Saturus, of the Livonian tribe [91?], his grieving parents, his brother Caius Minius, and his mother Rocna [PEFVRI?], in the 355th year of the province.
EDCS-26800782.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Saturus, mentioning his parents, brother, and mother, dated to the 355th year of the province.

EDCS-26800783.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Victor, together with his daughters Peregrina and Messora, made this tomb for Peregrinus, his father, and for Aulurula, his pious wife, who lived 40 years; he departed in law and sorrow in the 395th year of the province.
EDCS-26800783.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Victor, dedicated to his deceased father and wife, with details of his wife's lifespan, dated to the 395th year of the province.

EDCS-26800784.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. By the will of God and Christ, pious Demetrius, together with Domitia Sures and Lucciosa Martius, made this sarcophagus for his father Donatus in the year [---].
EDCS-26800784.summary: This funerary inscription records the construction of a sarcophagus for Donatus by his son Demetrius and others, under divine inspiration.

EDCS-26800785.translation: To Caius Vettius Aunus and his sisters who died before, and to his mother Iulia Maxima, Valens [ACI D?] in the 369th year of the province.
EDCS-26800785.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Caius Vettius Aunus, his sisters, and his mother Iulia Maxima, dated to the 369th year of the province.

EDCS-26800788.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caelius Italicus, a duplicarius (soldier receiving double pay) from Saldita, served 26 years and lived 46 years. He lies here.
EDCS-26800788.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caelius Italicus, a soldier who served for 26 years and lived 46 years.

EDCS-26800791.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilius Suavis, standard-bearer, a soldier of the second cohort of Breuci, lived 43 years and 5 months. For Pomponia Secunda, who deserved well.
EDCS-26800791.summary: This funerary inscription is for Aemilius Suavis, a standard-bearer in the second cohort of Breuci, and is dedicated to Pomponia Secunda.

EDCS-26800792.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Here lies Ulpius Optatus, once a youth of noble name, a soldier who bore the standard, the glory and honor of virtue, of a praiseworthy lineage and long posterity. He subdued many and laid low enemies on mountains and everywhere, and endured terrible wars. What many young men could not do, he always did alone, as he expended all his fury. Death by the spear met the Roman youth through enemy wounds, though he himself, the victor, encircled by darts and the foes he subdued, did not permit them to resist; he himself [fell] in his own destruction and life.
EDCS-26800792.summary: This funerary epitaph praises the deceased soldier Ulpius Optatus for his bravery and military achievements, lamenting his death in battle.

EDCS-26800793.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Geminianus Crescentius, who lived 53 years, 8 days, and 7 hours.
EDCS-26800793.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Geminianus Crescentius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800794.translation: To Licinia Secunda, of good memory, a wife whose fate was such that she did not even leave a pledge of her shared memory as a solace to us, and who, having begun her life's journey at the very threshold of youth, lived 25 years, 7 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-26800794.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinia Secunda, a wife who died young, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800795.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Marius Fortunatus, who lived 45 years; made by Avita Victorina for her most sweet husband.
EDCS-26800795.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Marius Fortunatus by his wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800796.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, [in memory of] Pudentis, who lived 63 years, 5 months, 2 days. Vibia Flora with her son Iucundus made this.
EDCS-26800796.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pudentis, set up by his wife Vibia Flora and son Iucundus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800798.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, [in memory of] Furnia Prima. I lived 70 years. To Furnius Primus, son of Furnius, knight of the ala miliaria, with 27 years of service. Sacred to the departed spirits. Salustius Martialis, knight of the ala miliaria, of Valentis's turma, master of the bargemen, served 20 years. I lived 44 years. Furnia Prima.
EDCS-26800798.summary: A complex funerary epitaph commemorating Furnia Prima and Furnius Primus, both with military connections, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800799.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Antonia, who lived 27 years. Julius Acilus placed this for his deserving wife. Cornelia Maura, who lived 11 years, her father Aelius Avitannus took care to have this made for his daughter. Aelius Avitannus lived 3 years.
EDCS-26800799.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Antonia and her younger brother Aelius Avitannus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800800.translation: In memory of our saintly and ever-glorious father Nemessanus, bishop, who lived 60 years, among which he administered the priesthood for the Lord for 18 years, and rested in peace on the 11th day before the Kalends of January in the 383rd year of the province. Iulia Geliola, sister of the sacred priest, lived 50 years and rested in peace on the Nones of October in the 383rd year of the province.
EDCS-26800800.summary: A funerary inscription for Nemessanus, a bishop who lived 60 years and served as priest for 18, and his sister Iulia Geliola, dating to the 383rd provincial year (likely late 4th century CE).

EDCS-26800801.translation: In memory of our saintly father, the bishop, who lived 80 years, among which he administered the priesthood for the Lord; he rested [on the day] before the day of the Sun. The deacon made this token for his brother.
EDCS-26800801.summary: A funerary inscription for a bishop who lived 80 years and served as priest, set up by his deacon brother, likely dating to the Late Roman or Byzantine period.

EDCS-26800803.translation: In memory of Crescentius, presbyter, who lived 55 years, departed on the 3rd day before the Kalends of March in the 395th year of the province.
EDCS-26800803.summary: A funerary inscription for Crescentius, a presbyter, who died in the 395th provincial year (likely late 4th century CE).

EDCS-26800804.translation: In memory of Victor, presbyter, who lived 52 years. He departed on the 11th day before the Kalends of October. Lucianus, his brother, made this in the 394th year of the province.
EDCS-26800804.summary: A funerary inscription for Victor, a presbyter, set up by his brother Lucianus in the 394th provincial year (likely late 4th century CE).

EDCS-26800805.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Gaius Calvius lived more or less 80 years.
EDCS-26800805.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Gaius Calvius, who lived approximately 80 years.

EDCS-26800806.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Iulius Severus lived more or less 41 years.
EDCS-26800806.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulius Severus, who lived approximately 41 years.

EDCS-26800807.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Iulia Postuma lived more or less 50 years.
EDCS-26800807.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iulia Postuma, who lived approximately 50 years.

EDCS-26800808.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Malucius lived more or less [---] years.
EDCS-26800808.summary: This funerary epitaph records the approximate lifespan of Malucius, with the exact number of years missing.

EDCS-26800809.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Nonius Satala lived 100 years.
EDCS-26800809.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Nonius Satala, who lived 100 years.

EDCS-26800810.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Numisius, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-26800811.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Iuved Svedia lived 85 years.
EDCS-26800811.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Iuved Svedia, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-26800813.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Arrius Iulius Rogatus lived more or less 60 years; erected in the 400th year of the province.
EDCS-26800813.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Arrius Iulius Rogatus and the year of erection of the monument, the 400th year of the province.

EDCS-26800814.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Publia Aurelia IIAII.
EDCS-26800814.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name Publia Aurelia, with illegible details regarding her life or death.

EDCS-26800815.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Blattius Macximus lived 65 years and died in the 410th year of the province.
EDCS-26800815.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Blattius Macximus and the year of his death, the 410th year of the province.

EDCS-26800816.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Calpurnia Amacrona, untimely died, lived 5 years and 8 years and died in the 436th year of the province.
EDCS-26800816.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Calpurnia Amacrona and her age.

EDCS-26800817.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Securus, Ianuaria A, lived in the province.
EDCS-26800817.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names of Sextus Securus and Ianuaria A and their life in the province.

EDCS-26800818.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Of the Iulii.
EDCS-26800818.summary: This funerary epitaph is a dedication to the spirits of the departed for members of the Iulii family.

EDCS-26800819.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Lucianus, subdeacon, lived 60 years and died in the 418th year of the province.
EDCS-26800819.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Lucianus, a subdeacon, and his age and death year.

EDCS-26800820.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Lucius lived 70 years in peace in the 400th year of the province.
EDCS-26800820.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Lucius, his age, and death year.

EDCS-26800821.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Secundus, untimely died, lived 33 years in the 412th year of the province.
EDCS-26800821.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Secundus, his age, and death year.

EDCS-26800823.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Veturia Fortunata lived 80 years in the 429th year of the province.
EDCS-26800823.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Veturia Fortunata, her age, and death year.

EDCS-26800824.translation: Ulpius Cilanus lived 30 years and died in the 424th year of the province.
EDCS-26800824.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Ulpius Cilanus, his age, and death year.

EDCS-26800825.translation: Ulpius Donatus lived 10 years and died on the 4th day before the Kalends of November in the 400th year of the province.
EDCS-26800825.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Ulpius Donatus, his age, and death date.

EDCS-26800826.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpius Marcus lived 56 years in the 449th year of the province and died on the 3rd Ides of March.
EDCS-26800826.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Ulpius Marcus, his age, and death date.

EDCS-26800827.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ulpius Sarunne lived 23 years and 485 days.
EDCS-26800827.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpius Sarunne, recording his age at death.

EDCS-26800838.translation: For the genius of the house, for the well-being of our Gemellus, Marcus Vivatius Gallus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-26800838.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the genius of a house for the health of Gemellus, paid by Marcus Vivatius Gallus.

EDCS-26800840.translation: Lucius Servatoris willingly paid his vow to the god.
EDCS-26800840.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Servatoris, dedicating a vow to an unnamed god.

EDCS-26800842.translation: Caecilius F willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-26800842.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caecilius F, who willingly paid a vow.

EDCS-26800843.summary: This is a dedication by Ianuarius Felix to an unnamed god.

EDCS-26800844.summary: This is a brief dedication by Iulius Victor, possibly for a vow fulfilled.

EDCS-26800845.summary: This is a votive inscription by Umbrocius, who willingly paid a vow.

EDCS-26800850.translation: To the deified Great Antoninus, Lucius Aquilius Secundus, son of Lucius, of the voting tribe Quirina, dedicated this at his own expense, by decree of the decurions, for the honor of his quinquennality.
EDCS-26800850.summary: This is a public dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, funded by Lucius Aquilius Secundus in honor of his quinquennial office, likely in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26800852.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lurius Rogatus, an equestrian of the ala miliaria, who was permitted to serve only 13 months and was discharged in the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, was killed on the 8th day before the Ides of January. He lived 34 years. Luria Valeria made this for her son, whose death she could not bear.
EDCS-26800852.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lurius Rogatus, an equestrian soldier who served briefly and died in Mauretania Caesariensis, set up by his mother, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800853.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Nonius Iulianus, an equestrian of the ala miliaria, lived 25 years. His most devoted brothers made this.
EDCS-26800853.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nonius Iulianus, an equestrian soldier who died at age 25, erected by his brothers, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800854.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Silicius Catus, a soldier of the ala of the Parthians, served for 2 years and lived for 24 years. Marcus and Valeria [made this] for their most devoted son.
EDCS-26800854.summary: A tombstone set up by parents for their son, Marcus Silicius Catus, a soldier who died at age 24 after 2 years of service.

EDCS-26800855.translation: A cavalryman of the first Ulpian ala of cataphracts, in the troop of Martinus, with 19 years of service.
EDCS-26800855.summary: An inscription recording the service of a cavalryman in a specific Roman auxiliary unit, dating likely to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26800856.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Slacnest, freedman of Trofimus, paid tribute.
EDCS-26800856.summary: A funerary inscription for Slacnest, a freedman, indicating his status and likely his death.

EDCS-26800857.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Victor lived for 79 years.
EDCS-26800857.summary: A tombstone commemorating Tiberius Claudius Victor, who died at the age of 79.

EDCS-26800858.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Dirlia lived for 45 years.
EDCS-26800858.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Dirlia, recording her lifespan of 45 years.

EDCS-26800859.translation: By order of the Holy Eternal Gods, Pomaris, prophetess, who died at about 40 years of age, and her daughter Appertia Valentina, who lived for 5 years. Appertius Sorcius [dedicated this] to his wife and dearest mother.
EDCS-26800859.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Appertius Sorcius to his wife Pomaris and daughter Appertia Valentina, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE given the reference to 'profetae' and 'Dei Sancti Aeterni'.

EDCS-26800860.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aquilius Felix, priest, created by decree of the town council according to his vow, built and enclosed [this] at his own expense from the ground up, for Gaius Julius Demetrius R COSMNOMO.
EDCS-26800860.summary: An inscription recording the construction of a building or structure by the priest Aquilius Felix at his own expense, dedicated to Gaius Julius Demetrius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26800862.translation: Publius Valerius Longus, president, son of the president Publius Valerius Longus, erected this statue for Aurelius Antoninus, father of Augustus Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax, as he had vowed according to public record.
EDCS-26800862.summary: A statue erected by Publius Valerius Longus for Aurelius Antoninus, the father of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26800863.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iunius Crescens, a cavalryman of the ala of the Parthians, lived for 24 years. Aurelia Catola, mother, made [this] for [her son]. Province CCCXVI.
EDCS-26800863.summary: A tombstone set up by Aurelia Catola for her son, Iunius Crescens, a cavalryman who died at age 24, possibly indicating a provincial context (CCCXVI).

EDCS-26800865.translation: Marcus Aurelius Saturninus lived for 40 years. His son erected [this] for him, deserving well. He is buried here.
EDCS-26800865.summary: A tombstone erected by a son for his father, Marcus Aurelius Saturninus, who lived for 40 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-26800866.translation: To the memory of Aelia Romula, who preceded us in the Lord's peace, lived more or less 9 years, and departed on the fourth day before the Kalends of January in the provincial year 455.
EDCS-26800866.summary: This is a memorial inscription for Aelia Romula, likely dating to the provincial year 455.

EDCS-26800867.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Antonius Victor, most loving husband, who lived 80 years, completed the table in the provincial year 343. Sacred to the departed spirits. For Antonius Felix, most loving son, who lived more or less 12 years. Victor, his father and mother, made the table in the provincial year 333.
EDCS-26800867.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Antonius Victor and his son Antonius Felix, with a table constructed by the father and mother in provincial years 343 and 333 respectively.

EDCS-26800868.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Aufidius Pequarius and Secundus, who lived more or less [X] years. Aufidius Bassurius and Aurelia his wife made [this] in memory in the provincial year 333.
EDCS-26800868.summary: This inscription is a memorial to Aufidius Pequarius and Secundus, erected by Aufidius Bassurius and Aurelia in the provincial year 333.

EDCS-26800869.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Bassiolus lived 10 years. Cassia Ancilla made [this] for her well-deserving grandson.
EDCS-26800869.summary: This funerary inscription records Aurelius Bassiolus, who lived 10 years, and was commemorated by his grandmother Cassia Ancilla.

EDCS-26800870.translation: To the memory of Aurelius Lucius, who preceded us in the Lord's peace, lived more or less 20 years, in the provincial year 420.
EDCS-26800870.summary: This memorial inscription is for Aurelius Lucius, who died in the provincial year 420.

EDCS-26800872.translation: To the memory of Aurelia, sister, who preceded us in the Lord's peace, and lived more or less 55 years, and departed on the tenth day before the Kalends of July in the provincial year 371. Aemilius Victorinus together with his sons made the table.
EDCS-26800872.summary: This memorial inscription is for Aurelia, a sister, who died on the 10th of the Kalends of July in the provincial year 371, and was commemorated by Aemilius Victorinus and his sons.

EDCS-26800873.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Saturina, well-remembered, who lived more or less 60 years, in the provincial year 356. Mallius Fidensus together with his sons made [this].
EDCS-26800873.summary: This funerary inscription records Aurelia Saturina, who lived approximately 60 years and died in the provincial year 356, with the monument made by Mallius Fidensus and his sons.

EDCS-26800874.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Eppidius Cassus, who preceded us in peace, and lived more or less 73 years. His wife together with his son made [this] in the provincial year 380.
EDCS-26800874.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Eppidius Cassus, who lived approximately 73 years and died in the provincial year 380, with the monument made by his wife and son.

EDCS-26800875.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gallonius Antonia had the stone made for herself and her sister Attula. RATIRIO lived 44 years and his son Defensor, who placed the altar.
EDCS-26800875.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Gallonius Antonia and her sister Attula, with RATIRIO and his son Defensor also mentioned.

EDCS-26800876.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To the memory of Iulius Felix, who lived 82 years, and Caecilia Maria, who lived 80 years. Their son Iulius Venustus made [this] in the provincial year 352.
EDCS-26800876.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Iulius Felix and Caecilia Maria, commemorated by their son Iulius Venustus in the provincial year 352.

EDCS-26800877.translation: To the memory of Iulia Celsa, who preceded us in the Lord's peace, lived about 31 years, and departed on the third day before the Kalends of [?] in provincial year 431.
EDCS-26800877.summary: This epitaph commemorates Iulia Celsa, noting her lifespan and death date in provincial year 431.

EDCS-26800878.translation: To the memory of Iulia Valenfa, who preceded us in the Lord's peace, lived 15 years. Iulius Caius made the tombstone in provincial year 352.
EDCS-26800878.summary: This inscription records the death of Iulia Valenfa, who died at 15 years old, with her tombstone made by Iulius Caius in provincial year 352.

EDCS-26800879.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Maiorica made this monument for her most loving husband, Marcus Lollio Sabino, who died by the violence of the Bavari, lived 31 years. In provincial year 327. Manlius Lollio F. S. lived 21 years.
EDCS-26800879.summary: This funerary monument commemorates Marcus Lollio Sabino, killed by the Bavari, and his son Manlius Lollio F. S., with the monument made by Aurelia Maiorica in provincial year 327.

EDCS-26800880.translation: To Lollia Vittuna, who preceded us in the Lord's peace, lived about 28 years. Her husband Caius Iulius Acutus made the memorial in provincial year 313. Died on the eleventh day before the Kalends of September.
EDCS-26800880.summary: This inscription is a memorial made by Caius Iulius Acutus for his wife Lollia Vittuna, who died in provincial year 313.

EDCS-26800881.translation: RAL, who lived about 65 years. His wife Munassia Iulia and sons Marcus and Petronius made the tombstone in provincial year 308.
EDCS-26800881.summary: This tombstone was made by Munassia Iulia and her sons Marcus and Petronius for RAL, who lived about 65 years, in provincial year 308.

EDCS-26800882.translation: Primosus ordered this altar to be made for himself. He lived 21 years.
EDCS-26800882.summary: This altar was commissioned by Primosus for himself before his death at the age of 21.

EDCS-26800884.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Rogat.
EDCS-26800884.summary: A brief funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-26800885.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Sempronius Uxs erma. He lived 35 years.
EDCS-26800885.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Marcus Sempronius Uxs erma, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-26800886.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Bassus SILI. Valerius Calvus lived 23 years. Valeria Longina lived 30 years.
EDCS-26800886.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Valerius Bassus SILI and records the lifespans of Valerius Calvus (23 years) and Valeria Longina (30 years).

EDCS-26800887.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Tuscilius Victor, the dutiful one, lived.
EDCS-26800887.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Caius Tuscilius Victor, mentioning his piety and that he lived.

EDCS-26800888.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Vilia, daughter of Ulpius Damareus, lived 14 years and is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26800888.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Vilia, daughter of Ulpius Damareus.

EDCS-26800889.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. M. Sarura H. E. CL Zarure.
EDCS-26800889.summary: A tombstone of M. Sarura, possibly recording her age or years of service.

EDCS-26800890.translation: In peace. Cavi AI LXV and learn. The 6th day before the Ides of February in the 444th year of the province.
EDCS-26800890.summary: A funerary inscription with a date of death in the 444th year of the province.

EDCS-26800892.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Culumas, son of Marcus, lived 40 years.
EDCS-26800892.summary: A tombstone recording the life of Publius Culumas, son of Marcus.

EDCS-26800893.translation: Nidvc Gnass F. Com N. Flav Vni Nril Cia QV E S.
EDCS-26800893.summary: A fragmented inscription, possibly a dedication or memorial, with personal names.

EDCS-26800895.translation: To the memory of Ruge ni, who lived 60 years and departed in the 314th year of the province.
EDCS-26800895.summary: A memorial inscription for Ruge ni, with a date of death in the 314th year of the province.

EDCS-26800896.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Valerius Saturnus lived 5 months and 21 days.
EDCS-26800896.summary: A tombstone recording the short life of Lucius Valerius Saturnus.

EDCS-26800898.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Vettia Iunonia, most beloved wife, who lived 38 years, 5 months, and 17 days. To Titus Vettius Severus, most innocent son, who lived 6 years, 4 months, and 19 days. Vettius P. made this for his wife and child.
EDCS-26800898.summary: A funerary monument erected by Vettius P. for his wife Vettia Iunonia and son Titus Vettius Severus.

EDCS-26800899.translation: ius L. Victori F. made this. 6.
EDCS-26800899.summary: A fragment possibly related to a dedication or funerary monument by ius L. Victori F.

EDCS-26800900.translation: bi son lived 10 years. ius Victor tis.
EDCS-26800900.summary: A fragment of a tombstone recording the life of a son and possibly his father.

EDCS-26800901.translation: Vitalis Ba, here lies, may the earth lie lightly upon you. Diadumenus, fellow slave, made this from his own resources.
EDCS-26800901.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vitalis Ba, set up by his fellow slave Diadumenus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-26800903.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Ulpius Hammonius the younger lived for 5 years and 2 months. Baniura was his statue. Marcus Ulpius Hammonius and Gargilia Archelais made this for their dearest common son.
EDCS-26800903.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy, Marcus Ulpius Hammonius the younger, indicating his parents erected a statue and the tombstone for him, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800904.translation: Marcus Ulpius Iaerax lived for 35 years. Ulpia Astorope made this monument for her most devoted, well-deserving partner. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26800904.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Iaerax, erected by his partner Ulpia Astorope, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800905.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aper, son of Ulpius Quadra, lived for 85 years. Ulpius Claudius Copicius had this made at his own expense for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-26800905.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpius Quadra Aper, funded by his son Ulpius Claudius Copicius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800906.translation: The body of Ulpius Valerianus, my most rare son, is buried here, who was in this world for 2 years and 27 days. Ulpius Victor, his father, placed this inscription for Ulpius Valerianus, my most rare son, whose body is buried here, who was in this world for 2 years and 27 days. Ulpius Victor, his father, placed this tomb.
EDCS-26800906.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpius Valerianus, erected by his father Ulpius Victor, lamenting the child's short life of 2 years and 27 days, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800907.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Ulpia Avia lived for 17 years. Ulpius Emeritus placed her here, his most dutiful daughter. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26800907.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpia Avia, erected by her father Ulpius Emeritus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800908.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Ulpia Felicissima lived for 27 years and 28 days. Lucius Octavius Philippus made this for his most dutiful mother. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26800908.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpia Felicissima, erected by her son Lucius Octavius Philippus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800909.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Ulpia Julia lived for 35 years. Julius Saturninus made this monument for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26800909.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpia Iulia, erected by her husband Julius Saturninus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800910.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sperata lived for 50 years. Ulpius Antiochus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26800910.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sperata, erected by her husband Ulpius Antiochus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800911.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For our most dutiful mother. The sons made this. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For our most dutiful father. The sons made this.
EDCS-26800911.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by sons for both their mother, Primula, and their father, Urbanus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26800913.translation: To Pano, my most innocent son.
EDCS-26800913.summary: A funerary dedication to a son, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26800914.translation: Ovius lived 57 years; he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26800914.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death and a wish for the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800915.translation: Dulcis is buried here, aged two years and two months.
EDCS-26800915.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death of a young child, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800922.translation: Red Cato [erected this] monument for the people of the municipality.
EDCS-26800922.summary: An inscription recording the erection of a public monument by Red Cato for his municipality, date uncertain.

EDCS-26800955.translation: Toosa, to her most devoted grandmother.
EDCS-26800955.summary: A funerary dedication to a grandmother, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26800957.translation: His sister lovingly and deservedly made this monument.
EDCS-26800957.summary: A funerary inscription recording a sister's dedication of a monument to her deceased sibling, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800960.summary: A funerary dedication to a spouse, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800986.translation: In memory of Aelius Placens, who lived 40 days.
EDCS-26800986.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Aelius Placens, notable for the very short lifespan recorded, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800987.summary: A commemorative inscription for Amanda, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800988.translation: In good memory of Aufidius, who lived...
EDCS-26800988.summary: A commemorative inscription for Aufidius, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26800991.translation: Fridila rests here in peace, for a good memory.
EDCS-26800991.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Fridila, dated to the early Christian period.

EDCS-26800993.translation: For a good memory, Pulli lived 11 months and nine days.
EDCS-26800993.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pulli, recording the short lifespan of a child, dated to the early Christian period.

EDCS-26800994.translation: For a good memory, Saturas lived more or less 70 years, rests here in peace; his sons and grandsons made this.
EDCS-26800994.summary: This funerary inscription for Saturas, who lived to approximately 70 years old, was erected by his descendants and is dated to the early Christian period.

EDCS-26800995.translation: For a good memory, Saturninus rests here in peace; he made the memorials together with his sons; he lived sixty years.
EDCS-26800995.summary: This funerary inscription for Saturninus, who lived to be sixty years old, was erected by him and his sons and is dated to the early Christian period.

EDCS-26800999.translation: Securae and Victor made [this] for their mother.
EDCS-26800999.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Securae and Victor dedicated a monument to their mother, likely from the early Christian period.

EDCS-26801000.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription stating that Tilauge rests here, likely from the early Christian period.

EDCS-26801001.summary: This is a concise funerary inscription indicating that Venantius rests in peace, likely from the early Christian period.

EDCS-26801003.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription stating that Cosmician rested, likely from the early Christian period.

EDCS-26801015.translation: The Republic of the Gunugitani [erected this] to Gaius Fulcinius Fabius Maximus Optatus, a most distinguished man, quaestor, tribune of the plebs, praetor, legate of the province of Baetica, and incomparable patron.
EDCS-26801015.summary: This is a public dedication by the community of Gunugitani to Gaius Fulcinius Fabius Maximus Optatus, honoring his distinguished career and patronage, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-26801016.translation: Lucius Sempronius Venustus [erected this] to his excellent friend Quintus Julius Clemens, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, aedile, twice chief magistrate, five-year chief magistrate, flamen of Augustus, tribune elected by the council, of the Salutaris Silonensis district, for his services.
EDCS-26801016.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Sempronius Venustus to his friend Quintus Julius Clemens, listing numerous local magistracies and priesthoods, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.
