EDCS-26801017.translation: To the Divine Shades. Initus served as a soldier in the elite Pannonian infantry of Lower Pannonia for 39 years and 19 campaigns; his standard-bearer Calimenus and his optio Viator, his heirs, placed this monument to him here.
EDCS-26801017.summary: A tombstone for Initus, a soldier in the Pannonian infantry, erected by his standard-bearer and optio, Calimenus and Viator, who were also his heirs.

EDCS-26801018.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelia Sampurica lived 17 years and 5 months. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801018.summary: A tombstone for Aelia Sampurica, who died at the age of 17 years and 5 months.

EDCS-26801019.translation: To the Divine Shades. Afidius Ennasius lived 23 years and 4 months.
EDCS-26801019.summary: A tombstone for Afidius Ennasius, who died at the age of 23 years and 4 months.

EDCS-26801020.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Aurelius Rufinus lived for...
EDCS-26801020.summary: An incomplete tombstone for Lucius Aurelius Rufinus, recording his life span.

EDCS-26801022.translation: To the Divine Shades. Claudia Spes lived 16 years, 7 months, and 8 days. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801022.summary: A tombstone for Claudia Spes, who died at the age of 16 years, 7 months, and 8 days.

EDCS-26801023.translation: To the Divine Shades. M. Silh lived 80 years.
EDCS-26801023.summary: A tombstone for M. Silh, who died at the age of 80 years.

EDCS-26801024.translation: To the Divine Shades. An excellent woman remains, enjoying an eternal age, perpetual security. She lived 59 years, 2 months, and 22 days. Her husband COPRSCANAS placed [this monument] for eternity. Farewell.
EDCS-26801024.summary: A tombstone for an unnamed woman, erected by her husband COPRSCANAS, who died at the age of 59 years, 2 months, and 22 days.

EDCS-26801026.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iiinus lived 87 years.
EDCS-26801026.summary: A tombstone for Iiinus, who died at the age of 87 years.

EDCS-26801028.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelius Apollonius the physician lived 60 years.
EDCS-26801028.summary: A tombstone for Aelius Apollonius, a physician, who died at the age of 60 years.

EDCS-26801029.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Flavius Sossianus the elder made this [monument] for his father, Titus Flavius Virilis, weeping, who lived 72 years.
EDCS-26801029.summary: A tombstone for Titus Flavius Virilis, erected by his son Titus Flavius Sossianus the elder, who died at the age of 72 years.

EDCS-26801030.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Fortunata lived 6 years. Aelius Teres lived years
EDCS-26801030.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortunata, mentioning her age and father, Aelius Teres.

EDCS-26801031.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Julius Fa
EDCS-26801031.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the underworld, beginning with the name Marcus Julius Fa.

EDCS-26801032.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Livius Maximus made this monument for Antonia Immidis, his wife, who well deserved it. She lived 30 years.
EDCS-26801032.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Marcus Livius Maximus for his deserving wife Antonia Immidis, recording her age at death.

EDCS-26801033.translation: To the god Mars. ... 95 years, 3 months. Voconius Celsus, who is also called Innocent
EDCS-26801033.summary: This inscription, partly dedicated to Mars, records the lifespan of Voconius Celsus (also known as Innocent).

EDCS-26801034.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Matrona lived 45 years. Her husband, Aelius Ianuarius, made this.
EDCS-26801034.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Matrona, who lived 45 years, erected by her husband Aelius Ianuarius.

EDCS-26801035.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Ofellia Marthvs, his wife, who lived 30 years, well deserving. Lucius Antonius [honored her] for her merits.
EDCS-26801035.summary: This funerary inscription honors the deserving wife Ofellia Marthvs, who lived 30 years, and was set up by Lucius Antonius.

EDCS-26801037.translation: Mensa Cuccatis, which his wife Iulia made.
EDCS-26801037.summary: This is a funerary table (mensa) dedicated to Cuccatis, made by his wife Iulia.

EDCS-26801038.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing the name Magnia Euassa.

EDCS-26801039.translation: To the memory of Philroga, who well deserved it. Hermogenes made this.
EDCS-26801039.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Hermogenes in memory of the deserving Philroga.

EDCS-26801040.translation: Donatus made this table for Victor.
EDCS-26801040.summary: This inscription records that Donatus made a funerary table (mensa) for Victor.

EDCS-26801043.translation: The younger worshippers of Abaddir Sanctus erected this altar at their own expense for...
EDCS-26801043.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar by junior worshippers of a deity named Abaddir Sanctus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801045.translation: To Gaius Valerius Lucius, from his father C. CEST POTITV.
EDCS-26801045.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Valerius Lucius by his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801048.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Honoratus lived 27 years, 7 months, 13 days. Ulpia Saturnina for her dearest brother.
EDCS-26801048.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Honoratus, recording his lifespan and erected by his sister Ulpia Saturnina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801050.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Saturnina lived more or less 55 years. Iulius S made [this].
EDCS-26801050.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Saturnina and was made by Iulius S, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801051.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sittia Vera and Flavius Primus, both married, shall be in this burial place.
EDCS-26801051.summary: This funerary inscription records that Sittia Vera and Flavius Primus are to be buried together, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801153.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus PEcius, recording his lifespan, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801157.translation: Rogata Sacrina happily fulfilled her vow to Saturnus.
EDCS-26801157.summary: This inscription records Rogata Sacrina fulfilling a vow to the god Saturnus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801158.translation: Publius Cornelius Dammaeus lived 90 years, he is buried here. Publius Cornelius P made [this] for his father.
EDCS-26801158.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Cornelius Dammaeus, recording his lifespan and burial, and it was made by his son Publius Cornelius P, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801159.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publius Cornelius Iustus, son of Dammaeus, is buried here.
EDCS-26801159.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Publius Cornelius Iustus, son of Dammaeus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801161.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, possibly recording the very short life of an infant and mentioning a father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801162.translation: To Lucius Suminus, his freedman, and his brother.
EDCS-26801162.summary: This is a dedication to a freedman and his brother, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26801163.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Here lies Kalpurnia Flavia, known by the nickname Dicta, daughter of Kalpurnius Tancinus, who was formerly a decemvir. She lived for 56 years, 2 months, and 23 days. Her husband, Aemilius Vitellianus, known by the nickname Dictus, sets up this monument in return for her services, a devoted husband.
EDCS-26801163.summary: This is a tombstone for Kalpurnia Flavia, set up by her husband, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26801183.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Comunalis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26801202.translation: To the memory of Aurelius Zeno, a most distinguished man, Iulius Antoninus, holder of military commands, for his merits.
EDCS-26801202.summary: This is a dedication honoring Aurelius Zeno by Iulius Antoninus for his distinguished service, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26801203.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Sigerus lived for 60 years. Fortunata made this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-26801203.summary: This is a tombstone for Flavius Sigerus, set up by his wife Fortunata, dating to the 4th century CE.

EDCS-26801204.translation: Tiberius Claudius Thalamus of Alexandria is buried here.
EDCS-26801204.summary: This is a tombstone for Tiberius Claudius Thalamus of Alexandria, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26801209.translation: Sextus Caecilius Rogatus lived for 1 year, 3 months, and 3 days. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Quintus Caecilius.
EDCS-26801209.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Caecilius Rogatus, set up by Quintus Caecilius, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26801210.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextus Caecilius Paulinus lived for 15 years, 5 months, and 24 days.
EDCS-26801210.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Caecilius Paulinus, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26801211.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilius Saturninus lived for 16 years.
EDCS-26801211.summary: This is a tombstone for Caecilius Saturninus, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26801213.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Colonica lived for 18 years. Gallonius Saturninus made this for his most devoted wife, and also for her sister Stercorosa, who lived for 6 years.
EDCS-26801213.summary: This is a tombstone for Caecilia Colonica and her sister Stercorosa, set up by Gallonius Saturninus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26801214.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Maura lived 85 years; she is buried here. The heir took care to have this made.
EDCS-26801214.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Maura, dating to the Roman Imperial period, recording her lifespan and specifying that her heir commissioned the monument.

EDCS-26801215.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [---] lived 35 years and 2 months. Cae Asambarili, his wife, made this.
EDCS-26801215.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, for an unnamed individual, stating his lifespan and that his wife commissioned the monument.

EDCS-26801216.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cal Domitianus lived 15 years and [---] months.
EDCS-26801216.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, recording the short lifespan of Cal Domitianus.

EDCS-26801217.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Calpurnius Modestus, son of Calpurnius, lived 5 years, 10 months, and 6 days.
EDCS-26801217.summary: A funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, detailing the lifespan of a child named Calpurnius Modestus.

EDCS-26801219.translation: [---] lived 20 years and [---] months. To Chresimus, his most dutiful son.
EDCS-26801219.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, possibly commemorating a son who died young.

EDCS-26801220.translation: Cinnamus made this for his wife Viola, for her merits.
EDCS-26801220.summary: A funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, commissioned by Cinnamus for his wife Viola in recognition of her virtues.

EDCS-26801221.summary: A brief funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, recording the lifespan of Claudius Cellus.

EDCS-26801222.translation: Claudius Felix, freedman of Petronius Capito, son of Philumenes, lived 11 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26801222.summary: A funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period for Claudius Felix, a freedman, detailing his age and burial location.

EDCS-26801223.translation: Tiberius Claudius Hospes lived 40 years; he is buried here. May the earth be light for you. Tribia, the matron, took care to have this made for her son.
EDCS-26801223.summary: A funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period for Tiberius Claudius Hospes, commissioned by his mother Tribia, including a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-26801224.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tiberius Claudius Sossianus lived 11 years. Tiberius Claudius Adauctus, his father, [dedicated this] for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-26801224.summary: A funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, commissioned by Tiberius Claudius Adauctus for his son Tiberius Claudius Sossianus, emphasizing filial piety.

EDCS-26801225.translation: Claudia Atia lived for 20 years. She is buried here. S
EDCS-26801225.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the life and death of Claudia Atia.

EDCS-26801226.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the age of Claudia Attia.

EDCS-26801227.translation: Antonia Rufina, maternal aunt of Claudia Dammina, daughter of Damianus.
EDCS-26801227.summary: This inscription commemorates Antonia Rufina, the maternal aunt of Claudia Dammina, daughter of Damianus.

EDCS-26801228.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld: Claudia Elpis, mother most good and fortunate, and Quirina FVRIIE [set this up].
EDCS-26801228.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Claudia Elpis, set up by Quirina FVRIIE, likely in the Imperial period given the name and context.

EDCS-26801229.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Claudia Extricata, set up by her sister.

EDCS-26801230.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld: Claudius Honora, Claudius Honoratia [set this up] for [their] brother Rogatinus.
EDCS-26801230.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Rogatinus, set up by his brothers Claudius Honora and Claudius Honoratia.

EDCS-26801231.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld: Claudia Monula instituted this monument for her most devoted husband Lollio Masclus. He lived for 40 years. [She] instituted [this].
EDCS-26801231.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Claudia Monula for her husband Lollio Masclus, who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-26801232.translation: [Flavius Clemens] set this up for his well-deserving sister. She is buried here.
EDCS-26801232.summary: This is a funerary dedication for an unnamed sister, set up by Flavius Clemens.

EDCS-26801233.translation: Clodius Luciolus lived for 35 years, 11 months, and 10 days. Clodius Antistius, father, [set this up]. O.
EDCS-26801233.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan of Clodius Luciolus and was set up by his father, Clodius Antistius.

EDCS-26801234.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld: For Comicus of Tarentum, who lived 88 years. Menander [set this up]. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801234.summary: This funerary inscription honors Comicus of Tarentum, who lived to be 88, and was set up by Menander.

EDCS-26801235.translation: Lucius Cornelius Fuscus lived 27 years and 9 months; he is buried here. Aemilia Veneria made this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-26801235.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius Fuscus, set up by his mother, Aemilia Veneria.

EDCS-26801236.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription possibly naming Cornelia Munia.

EDCS-26801237.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Inulina as the daughter of Lucius Cornelius.

EDCS-26801238.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Cornelia Tryphosa as a freedwoman of a man named Domitius.

EDCS-26801239.translation: To Cosmo, my most devoted husband, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801239.summary: This is a funerary epitaph expressing affection for a husband named Cosmo, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26801240.translation: He lived 11 years and 10 days. [Made for] Crescens, who deserved well. [---]
EDCS-26801240.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Crescens, who died at a young age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801241.translation: Whoever you are, stranger, I beg you, read this: 'May you live well!' Where I was and in what state death, the envious one, took me. I lived for twenty-two years. It was pleasing to always appease my husband through my wifely devotion. Hastening the course of fate, he deprived me. However, in this way, I gave a body for a body as a pledge. We have a son, born, whom lofty Jupiter may favor and may bid him beget future children. You who have read this, if it is not too much, please say of Crispina: 'May no weight burden the earth.'
EDCS-26801241.summary: This is a poetic epitaph for Crispina, who died young, possibly in childbirth, and mourns her early death while expressing hope for her surviving son and husband.

EDCS-26801242.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Cutilius Solutor lived 70 years and 5 months.
EDCS-26801242.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Cutilius Solutor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801243.translation: Marcus Damatius Felix lived 25 years, 9 months, and 18 days. Marcus Damatius Faustinus, his father, [made this] for his dearest son. Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. He is buried here and placed.
EDCS-26801243.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Damatius Felix, set up by his father, Marcus Damatius Faustinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801245.translation: Donatus, freedman of the freedman of Kalami, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. He lived 50 years.
EDCS-26801245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Donatus, who was the freedman of another freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801246.translation: Echi, son of O, lived 21 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26801246.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young man named Echi, recording his age at death.

EDCS-26801247.translation: Oclatius Chereas to his wife, Evatia Venusta.
EDCS-26801247.summary: This is a dedication from a husband to his wife, Evatia Venusta.

EDCS-26801248.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Fabius Asiellus lived 37 years, is buried here. Charinus made this for his uncle out of his own funds.
EDCS-26801248.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Quintus Fabius Asiellus at age 37 and was funded by his uncle, Charinus.

EDCS-26801249.translation: Marcus Fabius Crescens, Marcus M.E., lived 23 years.
EDCS-26801249.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and age at death of Marcus Fabius Crescens.

EDCS-26801250.translation: Fabia Marcia is buried here. She lived 65 years. Martha made this for her daughter. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26801250.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Fabia Marcia at age 65 and was dedicated by her daughter, Martha.

EDCS-26801252.translation: Felix, a slave, aged 25, is buried here. His wife Venusta took care to have this made at her own expense. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26801252.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Felix, a slave aged 25, and was commissioned by his wife Venusta.

EDCS-26801254.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly recording a date or a dedication.

EDCS-26801255.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavius Vindex is buried here.
EDCS-26801255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the burial place to the spirits of the underworld for Flavius Vindex.

EDCS-26801256.translation: Florus, son of Epagathus of Maros, lived 2 years and 7 months, is buried here.
EDCS-26801256.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a young child, Florus, at the age of 2 years and 7 months.

EDCS-26801257.translation: Florus, of the Vibii, son of Crispus and Secundus.
EDCS-26801257.summary: This inscription records the name Florus and his parentage, possibly indicating a familial dedication or ownership.

EDCS-26801258.translation: Anus made [this]. Florus lived. Buried here.
EDCS-26801258.summary: This inscription records that Anus made a monument for Florus, who is buried here.

EDCS-26801260.translation: To Andanius Icianus, dearest son. Crementius lived 17 years and 6 months. His father and mother [dedicated this].
EDCS-26801260.summary: This epitaph commemorates Crementius, son of Andanius Icianus, and records his age at death, dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-26801261.translation: Gabinius Crescens is buried here. He died at the age of 4, having paid his debt too soon.
EDCS-26801261.summary: This inscription records the burial of Gabinius Crescens, who died at the young age of 4.

EDCS-26801263.summary: This is an incomplete inscription recording the lifespan of Gelius.

EDCS-26801264.translation: Lucius Gellius Latro, son of Aulus Gellius Latro, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801264.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Lucius Gellius Latro, son of Aulus Gellius Latro, and includes a common funerary wish.

EDCS-26801265.translation: Gelia Attica made this monument for her well-deserving husband Flavius Abascantus. He lived 55 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801265.summary: This epitaph records the death and burial of Flavius Abascantus, dedicated by his wife Gelia Attica.

EDCS-26801266.translation: Gelia, daughter of Ingenuus, lived for [?]. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Mustela MERE.
EDCS-26801266.summary: This inscription records the burial of Gelia, daughter of Ingenuus, with an unclear final phrase.

EDCS-26801267.translation: Gellia Severa the dutiful lived 70 years and is buried here. Caecilia Severa the dutiful lived 40 years and is buried here. Marcus Caprarius, son, is buried here with his mother and grandmother.
EDCS-26801267.summary: This inscription records the burials of Gellia Severa, Caecilia Severa, and Marcus Caprarius with his mother and grandmother.

EDCS-26801268.translation: Gemela, daughter of Florus, lived 4 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801268.summary: This epitaph commemorates Gemela, daughter of Florus, who died at the age of 4.

EDCS-26801269.translation: Philocalus the father made [this] for Gemella, his daughter.
EDCS-26801269.summary: This inscription records that Philocalus dedicated a monument to his daughter Gemella.

EDCS-26801270.translation: Helpis lies here, [dedicated by] Helpidius, OATNIIA MS CIRCII CELIE CIC.
EDCS-26801270.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Helpis, likely set up by Helpidius, with some unclear names or titles.

EDCS-26801271.translation: Herenia Mustela lived for 13 years, 11 months, and 25 days; she lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26801271.summary: A funerary epitaph for Herenia Mustela, recording her age at death and expressing a wish for her peaceful repose.

EDCS-26801272.translation: To the Divine Manes; Hermia Ingenua.
EDCS-26801272.summary: A funerary dedication to the spirits of the dead for Hermia Ingenua.

EDCS-26801273.translation: [Erected by] Hetaerus for his freedwoman Hetaera, his wife. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26801273.summary: A funerary inscription by Hetaerus for his freedwoman and wife, Hetaera, with a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-26801274.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hilar, with some unclear accompanying words or names.

EDCS-26801275.translation: Acceptus Barbarion, freedman and brother, aged 25, made this tombstone for Hilara.
EDCS-26801275.summary: This inscription records the tombstone made by Acceptus Barbarion for his sister Hilara.

EDCS-26801276.translation: [She/He] made [this] for [his/her] daughter Honerata, who lived.
EDCS-26801276.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely indicating that someone made a monument for their daughter Honerata.

EDCS-26801278.translation: Ingen lies here, on account of m...
EDCS-26801278.summary: A partial funerary inscription for Ingen, with the reason for placement being incomplete.

EDCS-26801280.summary: A very fragmentary inscription that seems to describe someone named Iulian.

EDCS-26801281.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Africanus for his most devoted mother, Iulia Statia. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26801281.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Iulius Africanus to his devoted mother, Iulia Statia.

EDCS-26801282.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Cerialis made this for his son, one year and seven months old.
EDCS-26801282.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a young child and erected by his father.

EDCS-26801283.translation: Iulius Barus made this for his most beloved son.
EDCS-26801283.summary: A funerary epitaph recording a father's dedication to his deceased son.

EDCS-26801284.translation: Gaius Julius Crispinus is buried here.
EDCS-26801284.summary: A simple tombstone marking the burial place of Gaius Julius Crispinus.

EDCS-26801285.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decembir lived for eighteen years. Iulia Eutycha made this for her father.
EDCS-26801285.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Decembir and dedicated by his daughter.

EDCS-26801286.translation: To his beloved wife, Fau. Iulius made this out of devotion.
EDCS-26801286.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating a husband's devotion to his deceased wife.

EDCS-26801287.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Felix, dutiful, lived for twenty-four years and fifteen days; he is buried here.
EDCS-26801287.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Gaius Julius Felix.

EDCS-26801288.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Honoratus lived for twenty-four years, seven months, and eight days. Iulia Nemesa made this for her father.
EDCS-26801288.summary: A funerary epitaph detailing the lifespan of Gaius Julius Honoratus and dedicated by his daughter.

EDCS-26801289.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Niger made this monument for his well-deserving father, Gaius Julius Niger. He lived for seventy years.
EDCS-26801289.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by a son for his father, recording his age at death.

EDCS-26801290.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Relatus lived for one year, nine months. Iulius Euzelus made this for his son.
EDCS-26801290.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short life of a child and dedicated by his father.

EDCS-26801291.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Romulus lived for forty-five years. Gaius Julius Segontius made this for his most dutiful brother. He is buried here.
EDCS-26801291.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by a brother for his deceased sibling, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-26801292.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Julius S.

EDCS-26801293.summary: This is a funerary inscription that likely records the name of the deceased, Gaius Julius Solutor.

EDCS-26801295.translation: Julius M lies here. He lived for [XX] years. Iulia Sc [erected this].
EDCS-26801295.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the death and age of Julius M, likely erected by Iulia Sc.

EDCS-26801296.translation: [This] man, by fate's will, by fate snatched away suddenly, while giving [thanks?], if the Fates wished, he accepted these things. Julius TAV [erected this].
EDCS-26801296.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription that seems to lament the sudden death of an individual named Julius TAV, possibly due to fate.

EDCS-26801297.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Julius Victoritas lived for 4 years and 2 months.
EDCS-26801297.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Marcus Julius Victoritas, who died at a young age.

EDCS-26801299.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Fortun lived for 40 years. Her devoted daughter made this for her mother.
EDCS-26801299.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a daughter for her mother, Iulia Fortun, who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-26801300.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Iulia Eraclia, who lived for 27 years. Her husband Julius Martialis made this for his sweetest wife, who lived with him for 10 years from their wedding day.
EDCS-26801300.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a husband, Julius Martialis, for his wife Iulia Eraclia, detailing her age and their years of marriage.

EDCS-26801301.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Iulia Hopsita, [erected by] Nonius Firminus Aquila Extricatus for his mother.
EDCS-26801301.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Nonius Firminus Aquila Extricatus for his mother, Iulia Hopsita.

EDCS-26801302.translation: Iulia Laeta, daughter of Campanius, the good wife of Caius Caecilius Rogatus of Nassae, lies here.
EDCS-26801302.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Iulia Laeta, wife of Caius Caecilius Rogatus of Nassae.

EDCS-26801303.translation: Iulia, daughter of M. Ili [---], [erected this].
EDCS-26801303.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name of Iulia and her father, M. Ili.

EDCS-26801304.translation: Iulia Maxuma Perpetua, wife of Caius Iulius Lydus, lived 28 years.
EDCS-26801304.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Maxuma Perpetua.

EDCS-26801305.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia P. lived 7 years and 7 days. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801305.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia P., recording her age at death and offering a common farewell.

EDCS-26801306.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Sapida lived 2 years, 1 month, and 25 days; they set up this monument as a solace for her piety.
EDCS-26801306.summary: This funerary epitaph records the short life of Iulia Sapida and is dedicated by those who recognized her piety.

EDCS-26801307.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph recording the name of Iulia Selene.

EDCS-26801308.translation: Iulia Sever lived 5 years and 5 months.
EDCS-26801308.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Iulia Sever at the time of her death.

EDCS-26801311.translation: Marcus Iulius Ianumus, pious, 4 years old, son of Medianus.
EDCS-26801311.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, age, and parentage of Marcus Iulius Ianumus.

EDCS-26801312.translation: Laetorius Secundus and Laetoria Iulia, his heirs, dedicated this from their own funds.
EDCS-26801312.summary: This is a dedication by heirs, Laetorius Secundus and Laetoria Iulia, who funded the monument.

EDCS-26801313.translation: To RVaium Iasis and Leucoia, whoever... good...
EDCS-26801313.summary: This inscription is fragmentary but appears to be a dedication to two individuals, RVaium Iasis and Leucoia.

EDCS-26801315.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Argyris lived 60 years. Staphilus to his well-deserving mother-in-law.
EDCS-26801315.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Licinia Argyris and is dedicated by her son-in-law, Staphilus.

EDCS-26801316.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Crispina lived 8 years. Licinius Sig, her father, made this for his daughter.
EDCS-26801316.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Licinia Crispina and was made by her father, Licinius Sig.

EDCS-26801317.translation: Longinia Ammonus, his most excellent and pious wife, [erected this for her] husband, who lived 70 years.
EDCS-26801317.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of a husband and identifies his wife as the dedicator.

EDCS-26801318.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Quintus Lovius Proculus lived 21 years, 7 months, and 5 days. [Erected by] his brothers Lovi Honoratus and Caecilianus.
EDCS-26801318.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Quintus Lovius Proculus and names his brothers as the dedicator.

EDCS-26801321.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Lucretius Helenus is buried here. He lived 25 years.
EDCS-26801321.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Lucretius Helenus.

EDCS-26801322.translation: Lucrio Gal is buried here. He lived [some number of] years. Primus, his freedman, [erected this].
EDCS-26801322.summary: This inscription records the burial of Lucrio Gal and identifies his freedman, Primus, as the dedicator.

EDCS-26801324.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. [This is for] Caius Mammius Alburnus.
EDCS-26801324.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Caius Mammius Alburnus.

EDCS-26801325.translation: Marcella is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801325.summary: This is a simple epitaph for Marcella with a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-26801326.translation: Marcius Vitalio erected this for his most pious and deserving wife, Iulia Pompeia.
EDCS-26801326.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Marcius Vitalio for his wife, Iulia Pompeia.

EDCS-26801327.summary: This brief inscription likely records Trichlia dedicating something to Marcia, possibly a tomb or memorial.

EDCS-26801328.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Maura Marcia lived 17 years, 9 months, and 28 days. She is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Titus Flavius Martialis arranged for this to be made for his wife.
EDCS-26801328.summary: This funerary inscription details the lifespan of Marcia Maura and identifies her husband, Titus Flavius Martialis, as the one who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-26801329.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Marius Donatus lived 14 years, 4 months, and 6 days. He is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Caius Marius Felix erected this monument for his most pious son.
EDCS-26801329.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Marius Donatus and identifies his father, Caius Marius Felix, as the dedicator.

EDCS-26801330.translation: Matuta, daughter of Claudius Iason, lived for 4 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26801330.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young girl named Matuta and includes a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-26801331.translation: To Maximo, from the foremost INI, Happy, soon.
EDCS-26801331.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a dedication or a personal note, with unclear place or group names.

EDCS-26801332.translation: Meipo, freedman of Eutactus, set up this monument for his daughter Ciripsa, 6 years old; she is buried here. Helice set up this monument for her son Samanae, 10 months old; he is buried here.
EDCS-26801332.summary: This funerary monument records the deaths of two children, a daughter and a son, with their respective parents providing the inscriptions.

EDCS-26801334.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a familial identification, recording the parentage of Nibalis.

EDCS-26801335.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Callistus erected this for his sweetest mother, Messia Aphrodisia.
EDCS-26801335.summary: This funerary dedication records a son's memorial to his beloved mother.

EDCS-26801336.translation: To the departed spirits. Messor lived for 47 years. Ulpia Monnina [set this up] for [him] well-deserving. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-26801336.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Messor, who lived 47 years, and was commemorated by Ulpia Monnina.

EDCS-26801337.translation: To the departed spirits. Servius Mevianus lived for 21 years, 4 months, and 18 days. His mother Honorata made [this monument].
EDCS-26801337.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Servius Mevianus at the age of 21 years, 4 months, and 18 days, commemorated by his mother.

EDCS-26801338.translation: For Modia Victorina, his sweetest daughter, who [died] at an immature age
EDCS-26801338.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicating a monument to a daughter who died young.

EDCS-26801339.translation: To the departed spirits. Mussidia Euporia made [this monument] for her sweetest daughter Mussidia Calliste, who lived for 1 year.
EDCS-26801339.summary: This funerary inscription records a mother's dedication to her very young daughter.

EDCS-26801341.translation: Gaius [---] lived for [---] years. Valeria Iucunda, his devoted wife, lived for 50 years.
EDCS-26801341.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of a wife, Valeria Iucunda, and potentially her husband, Gaius.

EDCS-26801342.translation: Hail, Olumphia, my sweet, dear wife; life of the earth, may it be light for you. She lived 20 years. Secundio made this for his wife. Buried here.
EDCS-26801342.summary: A funerary epitaph for Olumphia, set up by her husband Secundio, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801343.translation: His remains are here, aged 48 years. Olymphion.
EDCS-26801343.summary: A funerary epitaph for Olymphion, indicating his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801344.translation: To Quinto Rogato, son of Quinto Pompeio.
EDCS-26801344.summary: A dedication to Quinto Rogato, son of Quinto Pompeio, likely on a tomb or monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801345.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Pompeia Honerata, most beloved wife, by her husband Iulius Victorinus, for her merits. She lived 37 years, 5 months, 13 days.
EDCS-26801345.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pompeia Honerata, erected by her husband Iulius Victorinus, recording her age at death and her merits, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801346.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Pomponius I Emeritus lived 50 years.
EDCS-26801346.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Pomponius I Emeritus, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801349.translation: Pudens and Recepta [set this up] for Fausto, a dear boy, who is buried here.
EDCS-26801349.summary: A funerary epitaph for a boy named Fausto, set up by Pudens and Recepta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801350.summary: A dedication to Pullaenius Martialis, possibly an epitaph or a dedication of a monument, by ETIOSIVS, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801351.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Pullus Peregrinus lived 20 years, and Publia Ulpia Iulia his sister lived 11 years. Publius Ulpius Saturninus, their most devoted father, [wishes] for you, his children, to be light.
EDCS-26801351.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pullus Peregrinus and his sister Publia Ulpia Iulia, set up by their father Publius Ulpius Saturninus, recording their ages at death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801352.translation: To Quintio and Iucundus, most devoted companions, for Faustus, most loving.
EDCS-26801352.summary: A dedication likely on a tomb or monument, honoring Quintio and Iucundus for their devotion to Faustus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801353.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. T. Quintula lived 45 years. From P. Faustus, for his mother.
EDCS-26801353.summary: A funerary epitaph for T. Quintula, erected by her son P. Faustus, recording her age at death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26801355.translation: To the eternal spirit of Repentinus.
EDCS-26801355.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Repentinus.

EDCS-26801356.translation: May the earth lie lightly upon you, devoted Respectus.
EDCS-26801356.summary: This is a funerary inscription wishing peace to Respectus.

EDCS-26801358.translation: To the Divine Manes of Rufina and Quinta, sisters; they lived for two years.
EDCS-26801358.summary: This funerary inscription records the short lives of two sisters, Rufina and Quinta.

EDCS-26801359.translation: Lucius Safinus Hilarus is buried here.
EDCS-26801359.summary: This is a simple burial marker for Lucius Safinus Hilarus.

EDCS-26801360.translation: Salvius, faithful and innocent, who had completed twenty years of life, was here among the gods above. The land of Libya gave birth to him and the same land received him; now dust and bones lie in this tomb. And he who previously benefited his brother, is now pressed by stone, both of them ashes, who spoke with tears of their last fate as a fellow slave.
EDCS-26801360.summary: This complex funerary poem records the death of Salvius at age 20, lamenting his fate and the shared burial with another individual.

EDCS-26801361.translation: [Son] of Saturn, lived [..] years, is buried here.
EDCS-26801361.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription indicates the burial of an individual identified as the son of Saturn.

EDCS-26801362.translation: Saturnina made this monument for her mother; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801362.summary: This monument was erected by Saturnina for her mother, with a wish for her peace.

EDCS-26801363.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Satur lived 50 years.
EDCS-26801363.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes and records the lifespan of Satur as 50 years.

EDCS-26801366.translation: Sequnda, daughter of Sitti and Sceva, is buried here.
EDCS-26801366.summary: This inscription records the burial of Sequnda, daughter of Sitti and Sceva.

EDCS-26801367.translation: Secundus made this for his daughter Hedia, who lived for 2 years, 2 months, and 5 days; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801367.summary: This monument was created by Secundus for his daughter Hedia, who died in infancy, with a wish for her peace.

EDCS-26801368.translation: Secundus lived [some number of] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-26801368.summary: A funerary inscription recording the life and burial of Secundus.

EDCS-26801370.translation: Semnia lived 65 years; Unus made this for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-26801370.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Semnia, who lived 65 years, and dedicated by Unus.

EDCS-26801371.summary: A funerary dedication to Septimius Agathus.

EDCS-26801372.translation: [Cin]ia Severa, [whose age was] 23 years, made this for her most dutiful MA R.
EDCS-26801372.summary: A funerary dedication by Cinia Severa for her MA R, likely her mother, aged 23.

EDCS-26801373.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulius Victor made this for his most beloved wife, Sicinia Rustica, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-26801373.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulius Victor for his wife Sicinia Rustica.

EDCS-26801374.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. [Li]stechor lived 7 months; he is buried here, [a monument] for the most dutiful Seutycus.
EDCS-26801374.summary: A funerary inscription for Listechor, who lived 7 months, with a dedication to Seutycus.

EDCS-26801375.translation: Stephanus, slave of Sei Anterus, is buried here; he lived 35 years.
EDCS-26801375.summary: A funerary inscription for Stephanus, a slave of Sei Anterus.

EDCS-26801377.translation: Fecit CORIF to Lucius Sulpicius Callistus. To the Spirits of the Underworld.
EDCS-26801377.summary: A funerary dedication by CORIF to Lucius Sulpicius Callistus.

EDCS-26801378.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Valerius Processus [made this] for his wife Tannonia Antonia, who lived 26 years, and for his son Valerius Antonius, who lived 2 years, and for his mother-in-law Tannonia Felicia, who lived 48 years.
EDCS-26801378.summary: A funerary inscription by Valerius Processus commemorating his wife, son, and mother-in-law.

EDCS-26801380.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Claudius Eleuther made this for Threptena, a girl of 18 years and 1 month, for whom may the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-26801380.summary: A funerary epitaph for Threptena, a young girl, by Claudius Eleuther.

EDCS-26801382.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld: Tyche.
EDCS-26801382.summary: This is a dedication to the underworld spirits by Tyche.

EDCS-26801383.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Valerius Muriclio lived 17 years, 20 months, 5 days. Valeria Demetria, his mother, placed [this] here for her most devoted son.
EDCS-26801383.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Valerius Muriclio, dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-26801384.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Valerius Saturninus lived 30 years, 7 days. Ponteia Mar lived 30 years, 20 days. For the deserving one.
EDCS-26801384.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives of Valerius Saturninus and Ponteia Mar, likely a couple.

EDCS-26900126.translation: Italicus made [this] for his spouse, sacred to the spirits of the underworld.
EDCS-26900126.summary: This funerary inscription records a spouse's dedication to the deceased.

EDCS-26900190.summary: This is a personal religious statement or epitaph.

EDCS-27000185.summary: This is a commercial inscription advertising lamps for sale from Assene.

EDCS-27000186.summary: This is a commercial inscription advertising lamps for sale from Assene.

EDCS-27000187.translation: Filtered lamps from the workshop of Donatus.
EDCS-27000187.summary: This is a commercial inscription indicating the origin and quality of lamps from Donatus' workshop.

EDCS-27000188.translation: Filtered lamps from the workshop of Assene.
EDCS-27000188.summary: This is a commercial inscription indicating the origin and quality of lamps from Assene's workshop.

EDCS-27000189.summary: This is a commercial inscription encouraging the purchase of lamps from Assene.

EDCS-27000190.summary: This is a purchase order for lamps from Assene, likely from a merchant.

EDCS-27000191.summary: This is a purchase order for lamps from Assene, likely from a merchant.

EDCS-27000192.summary: This is a purchase order for lamps from Assene, likely from a merchant.

EDCS-27000193.summary: This is a purchase order for lamps from Assene, likely from a merchant.

EDCS-27000194.summary: This is a purchase order for lamps from Assene, likely from a merchant.

EDCS-27000195.summary: This is a purchase order for lamps from Assene, likely from a merchant.

EDCS-27000200.summary: This is a dedication or commission from Vita to Donatus, who held the position of guild master.

EDCS-27000223.summary: This is a commercial notice advertising lamps for sale originating from Assene.

EDCS-27000227.summary: This is a commercial notice advertising lamps for sale originating from Assene.

EDCS-27000228.summary: This is a commercial notice advertising lamps for sale originating from Assene.

EDCS-27000231.summary: This inscription is a product label for filtered lamps originating from the workshop of Assenius.

EDCS-27000232.summary: This inscription seems to be a label or marker indicating filtered lamps from Assene.

EDCS-27000236.summary: This inscription appears to be a label or marker indicating filtered lamps from Assene, similar to EDCS-27000232.

EDCS-27100081.translation: Vitalis, a pious father, placed [this] for his well-deserving daughter Prima, who has passed away.
EDCS-27100081.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Vitalis for his deceased daughter Prima, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27100084.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Saturinus lived 11 years, for [himself] well-deserving.
EDCS-27100084.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Saturinus, recording his age at death and dedication to the Dis Manes.

EDCS-27100087.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudius Lucianus lived 3 years and 10 months. Claudius Rogatus, father, standard-bearer of the cohort of the Sardinians, made [this] for the most dutiful [son]. Mother Marina [also contributed].
EDCS-27100087.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Lucianus, who died at a young age, set up by his father, a standard-bearer from the cohort of the Sardinians, and his mother Marina.

EDCS-27100088.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Adventus, an explorer of the Batavi, lived 40 years; here he is.
EDCS-27100088.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Adventus, an explorer from the Batavi who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-27100089.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Junius Capito of Lindo, a soldier of the 10th Gemina legion, with 10 years of service. Julius Primus, standard-bearer, saw to its construction as heir.
EDCS-27100089.summary: This funerary epitaph records Marcus Junius Capito, a soldier of the 10th Gemina legion with 10 years of service, and notes that Julius Primus, a standard-bearer, arranged for the monument as heir.

EDCS-27100090.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Successus, formerly a veteran, son of Numi, and Dextra, daughter of Marcianus, lived [so many] years and [so many] months.
EDCS-27100090.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Successus, a former veteran, and Dextra, daughter of Marcianus, recording their lifespans.

EDCS-27100091.translation: Terentius Cutteus and his wife Ma Monnula, together with their sons Terentius Cutteus Ianuarius, Considius Augustinus, and Felicissimus, established and dedicated a new aqueduct built from the ground up on their own properties.
EDCS-27100091.summary: This inscription records the establishment and dedication of a new aqueduct built by Terentius Cutteus, his wife Ma Monnula, and their sons on their own lands.

EDCS-27100092.translation: Aelius Tiaugm, to you, Cinvce, from the mountain.
EDCS-27100092.summary: This inscription is a dedication, likely of a military nature, with the name Aelius Tiaugm dedicated to Cinvce.

EDCS-27100093.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilius Peregrinus lived 20 years. Aemilius Florus, a veteran father, had this made.
EDCS-27100093.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Aemilius Peregrinus at age 20, commissioned by his father, Aemilius Florus, a veteran.

EDCS-27100094.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aennia Iulia lived more or less 75 years and preceded us in the peace of the Lord on the 4th day before the Kalends of March in the 480th year of the province.
EDCS-27100094.summary: This epitaph commemorates Aennia Iulia, who lived approximately 75 years and died in the Christian era during the 480th year of the province.

EDCS-27100095.translation: Here lies Antonia Emerita, who lived more or less 60 years and departed in the peace of the Lord in the 452nd year of the province.
EDCS-27100095.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Antonia Emerita, who died at approximately 60 years of age in the 452nd year of the province.

EDCS-27100096.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caia Antonia Silvana lived 25 years. Caia Antonia Novella, her sister, made this.
EDCS-27100096.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caia Antonia Silvana at age 25, commissioned by her sister, Caia Antonia Novella.

EDCS-27100097.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aufidia Victorina Saqsa lived more or less 61 years. Aufidia Mama had this made for her mother.
EDCS-27100097.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Aufidia Victorina Saqsa, who lived approximately 61 years, and was commissioned by her daughter, Aufidia Mama.

EDCS-27100098.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. A Crassus, son of Aurelius Festus, made this for his well-deserving father, Aurelius Severus, who lived 60 years, 25 months, and days.
EDCS-27100098.summary: This funerary monument was erected by A. Crassus for his father, Aurelius Severus, who lived 60 years, and provides specific durations of life.

EDCS-27100099.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Aurelius Macersumis, who lived more or less 65 years and departed in the peace of the Lord in the 430th year.
EDCS-27100099.summary: This Christian funerary inscription commemorates Aurelius Macersumis, who lived approximately 65 years and died in the 430th year of the province.

EDCS-27100100.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Aurelius Secundus, who lived 3 years and 35 months and departed in the peace on the Kalends of September in the 430th year of the province.
EDCS-27100100.summary: This epitaph records the death of Aurelius Secundus at a young age (3 years, 35 months) on the Kalends of September in the 430th year of the province.

EDCS-27100101.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, in memory of Aurelia Lorida, who lived 35 years and departed in the peace on the 5th day before the Ides of August in the 436th year of the province.
EDCS-27100101.summary: This Christian funerary inscription commemorates Aurelia Lorida, who lived 35 years and died on the 5th day before the Ides of August in the 436th year of the province.

EDCS-27100102.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Celestinus lived 23 years. Marcus Lucretianus Vitalis Clemens [made this] for his brother.
EDCS-27100102.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Celestinus at age 23, with Marcus Lucretianus Vitalis Clemens dedicating it to him.

EDCS-27100103.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fabius Victorinus, the heir, made [this] for Valerius Nestor, deserving of good.
EDCS-27100103.summary: This funerary inscription records that Fabius Victorinus, as heir, dedicated a monument to Valerius Nestor.

EDCS-27100104.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Geminius Rogatus lived 35 years. Aemilia Maxima [made this] for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-27100104.summary: This funerary inscription records that Aemilia Maxima dedicated a monument to her husband, Geminius Rogatus, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-27100105.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Iulius Aristorius lived 71 years. Herennius Felix [made this] at a cost of HS. He is buried here.
EDCS-27100105.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Caius Iulius Aristorius who lived 71 years, dedicated by Herennius Felix, and possibly indicates a funerary cost.

EDCS-27100106.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. In memory of Iulius Emeritus, who lived more or less 80 years, and departed in the peace of the Lord in the provincial year 431.
EDCS-27100106.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Iulius Emeritus, who lived approximately 80 years and died in the provincial year 431.

EDCS-27100107.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. In memory of Iulius Ianuarius, who lived more or less 30 years, and departed in the peace of the Lord on the ninth day before the Kalends of January in the provincial year 453.
EDCS-27100107.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Iulius Ianuarius, who lived approximately 30 years and died on December 24th in the provincial year 453.

EDCS-27100108.translation: Iulius Masso of the Poblilia tribe.
EDCS-27100108.summary: This is a very brief inscription identifying Iulius Masso and his tribe.

EDCS-27100109.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Rogatus lived 21 years. Iulius Ninus made [this] for his deserving son.
EDCS-27100109.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius Rogatus at age 21, dedicated by his father Iulius Ninus.

EDCS-27100110.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Secundus lived 20 years. Iulius Maximus [made this] for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-27100110.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius Secundus at age 20, dedicated by his brother Iulius Maximus.

EDCS-27100111.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Donatus lived 20 years. Quinta took care to have [this] made.
EDCS-27100111.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius Donatus at age 20, with Quinta arranging for its creation.

EDCS-27100112.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. In memory of Iunius Secundus, who lived about 80 years and departed in the Lord's peace in the 386th year of the province.
EDCS-27100112.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Iunius Secundus, likely from the late Roman Empire, noting his lifespan and peaceful departure.

EDCS-27100113.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iunia Sinti, who lived 55 years, made this for Aurelia Luciosa, her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-27100113.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Iunia Sinti for her mother Aurelia Luciosa, recording their names and lifespan.

EDCS-27100114.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Luciosa Properti, who lived 60 years and 5 days. Rufina, her granddaughter, lived 4 years. Messor and Caninius made this at their instigation.
EDCS-27100114.summary: This inscription records the deaths of Luciosa Properti and her young granddaughter Rufina, and names Messor and Caninius as those who erected the monument.

EDCS-27100115.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publia Urbana, who lived 24 years. Her wife arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-27100115.summary: A funerary inscription for Publia Urbana, who died at age 24, with the monument erected by her wife.

EDCS-27100116.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. In memory of Ponponius Feva, who lived about 30 years and departed in the Lord's peace on the 10th day before the Kalends of March in the 400th year of the province.
EDCS-27100116.summary: This funerary text commemorates Ponponius Feva, noting his age, peaceful Christian death, and the provincial year of his passing.

EDCS-27100117.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Servilius Saturninus, who lived 91 years.
EDCS-27100117.summary: A simple funerary epitaph recording the name and long lifespan of Servilius Saturninus.

EDCS-27100118.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Silanius Aurelianus, her son, who lived 15 years. Petronia Maria, his wife, for his merits.
EDCS-27100118.summary: This epitaph is dedicated by Petronia Maria to her son Silanius Aurelianus, noting his age and her marital relationship.

EDCS-27100119.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. In memory of Marcus Valerius Quintosus, who lived about 100 years and succeeded in the Lord's peace.
EDCS-27100119.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Quintosus, a centenarian, noting his Christian faith and peaceful death.

EDCS-27100120.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Valerius Valerianus, who lived 13 years. Genia and Valent for their son.
EDCS-27100120.summary: This inscription is a memorial set up by Genia and Valent for their son Valerius Valerianus, who died at age 13.

EDCS-27100121.translation: His wife Ianuaria made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27100121.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a wife named Ianuaria for her deserving deceased husband.

EDCS-27100123.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudius Ianuarius lived 4 years. Claudius Caecilius, his father, deeply mourning.
EDCS-27100123.summary: A tombstone recording the short life of Claudius Ianuarius, dedicated by his grieving father.

EDCS-27100124.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Hortessus, son of Lucius, lived 92 years and departed on the Kalends of July. Seven [people] made it for a deserving son.
EDCS-27100124.summary: A tombstone for Hortessus, who died on July 1st, made by seven individuals for their deserving son.

EDCS-27100125.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Saturninus lived [an unspecified number of] years.
EDCS-27100125.summary: A tombstone recording the life of Aurelius Saturninus, with an incomplete duration.

EDCS-27100126.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudius Rufinus lived 6 years and 5 months. Claudius Maianesis lived 2 years. Claudius R [made it] for his sons.
EDCS-27100126.summary: A tombstone for two young children, Claudius Rufinus and Claudius Maianesis, with a dedication by Claudius R.

EDCS-27100127.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Colaibutsenis lived 75 years. Messor, Caninius, and MONAN P VIS made it for the sepulchre.
EDCS-27100127.summary: A tombstone for Colaibutsenis, who lived 75 years, with the monument erected by Messor, Caninius, and MONAN P VIS.

EDCS-27100128.translation: Of Fortunatus. Lived 30 years. Arator made it.
EDCS-27100128.summary: A tombstone recording the life of Fortunatus, who lived 30 years, made by Arator.

EDCS-27100129.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Satura lived 20 years. Vinnius Felix made it for his deserving wife.
EDCS-27100129.summary: A tombstone for Flavia Satura, who lived 20 years, dedicated by her husband Vinnius Felix.

EDCS-27100130.translation: MA DDA G MVCT lived 73 years, son of Elio.
EDCS-27100130.summary: A tombstone fragment indicating a person named MA DDA G MVCT lived 73 years and was the son of Elio.

EDCS-27100131.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. RAN Nabtafis lived 81 years. Donata, his wife, made it for her deserving husband.
EDCS-27100131.summary: A tombstone for RAN Nabtafis, who lived 81 years, dedicated by his wife Donata.

EDCS-27100132.translation: M D SV ICI VM SVMARI BVIEME R I made it for his sister, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-27100132.summary: A tombstone fragment recording the death of a sister who lived 20 years, with an incomplete dedication.

EDCS-27100134.translation: Julius Marcellus, son of Marcus, prefect of financial affairs, dedicated this in his generosity to the municipality of Mit with eight stone columns and pavement, on the fourth day before the Nones [of a month].
EDCS-27100134.summary: This inscription records the dedication of eight stone columns and pavement to the municipality of Mit by Julius Marcellus, a prefect of financial affairs, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100135.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Quiontus lived 20 years. His father Fortu [placed this].
EDCS-27100135.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Quiontus, who lived 20 years, set up by his father Fortu, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100136.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Iulia Matrona, deserving of her husband Bebius Caecilius, [this] monument. She lived more or less 50 years.
EDCS-27100136.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Bebius Caecilius for his wife Iulia Matrona, who lived approximately 50 years, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100141.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Domitius Berenicianus lived 55 years, served 23 years. Made by Valeria Luciosa.
EDCS-27100141.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and military service of Domitius Berenicianus, who lived 55 years and served 23 years, created by Valeria Luciosa, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100143.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Martis lived 36 years, departed on the second day before the Kalends of April in the 313th year of the province.
EDCS-27100143.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Aurelius Martis at age 36, on the second day before the Kalends of April in the 313th provincial year, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100144.translation: In memory of Aurelia Tifzalis, who lived 8 years, departed on the Ides of November in the 411th year of the province.
EDCS-27100144.summary: This memorial inscription records the death of Aurelia Tifzalis at age 8, on the Ides of November in the 411th provincial year, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100145.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Matronica lived 35 years.
EDCS-27100145.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Matronica, who lived 35 years, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100146.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Calventius Peregrinus lived 35 years. His most beloved, pious wife erected and established [this monument].
EDCS-27100146.summary: This funerary inscription records that Calventius Peregrinus lived 35 years and was honored by his devoted wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100147.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caninius Felix, [who lived] 1 year and 27 days, [his] most beloved son [set this up].
EDCS-27100147.summary: This funerary inscription is for Caninius Felix, who lived for 1 year and 27 days, dedicated by his beloved son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100148.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Crepereius Honorius lived 30 years, departed on the seventeenth day before the Kalends of November in the 386th year of the province. [His] most beloved father made [this].
EDCS-27100148.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Crepereius Honorius at age 30, on the seventeenth day before the Kalends of November in the 386th provincial year, erected by his loving father, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100151.translation: In memory of Flavius Ianuarius, who lived 12 years and departed on the 7th day before the Ides of January in provincial year 355.
EDCS-27100151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Ianuarius, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-27100152.translation: In memory of Flavius Mazica, wife of SDIOPR, who lived 45 years, 5 months, and 5 days, departing on the Kalends of January in provincial year 441.
EDCS-27100152.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Mazica, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-27100153.translation: In memory of Flavia Saturina, a devotee, who lived approximately 20 years and departed on the 18th day before the Kalends of October in provincial year 491.
EDCS-27100153.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Saturina, who died around the age of 20.

EDCS-27100154.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Venusta lived 20 years. Sittius Maximus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-27100154.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Flavia Venusta by her husband, Sittius Maximus.

EDCS-27100155.translation: In memory of Florentia Fabia, who lived 85 years and departed on the 3rd day before the Ides of July in provincial year 384.
EDCS-27100155.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Florentia Fabia, who died at the age of 85.

EDCS-27100156.translation: In memory of Julius Capsarius, the presbyter, who lived 92 years and departed on the 13th day before the Kalends of May in provincial year 456.
EDCS-27100156.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Capsarius, a presbyter, who died at the age of 92.

EDCS-27100157.translation: In memory of Julius Domissus, who lived...
EDCS-27100157.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Julius Domissus.

EDCS-27100158.translation: Julius Donatus lived 1 year. Lollius made this for his son. He departed on the 3rd day before the Kalends of February in provincial year 373.
EDCS-27100158.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Donatus, who died at the age of 1, made by his father Lollius.

EDCS-27100159.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Faosa lived 17 years and departed on the 16th day before the Kalends of December in provincial year 393.
EDCS-27100159.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Faosa, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-27100160.translation: In memory of Iulia Prima, who lived 70 years and departed on the 4th day before the Kalends of September in provincial year...
EDCS-27100160.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Iulia Prima, recording her age at death.

EDCS-27100161.translation: In memory of Iulia Sapida, who lived 90 years and died 16 days before the Kalends of November in the 391st year of the province.
EDCS-27100161.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Iulia Sapida, with her lifespan and date of death recorded in relation to the provincial era.

EDCS-27100163.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Victorina lived for two years, 10 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-27100163.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Victorina, detailing her age at death.

EDCS-27100165.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Erinia Inocensa lived for 70 years and died the day before the Ides of November in the 315th year of the province.
EDCS-27100165.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Erinia Inocensa, including her age and date of death in the provincial era.

EDCS-27100166.translation: In memory of Letorius Donatus, who lived 3 years and died on the 2nd day before the Ides of October in the 359th year of the province.
EDCS-27100166.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Letorius Donatus, stating his age and date of death according to the provincial calendar.

EDCS-27100168.translation: Macrobius lived for 25 years and died 15 days before the Kalends of December in the 360th year of the province.
EDCS-27100168.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and death date of Macrobius, referencing the provincial era.

EDCS-27100169.translation: In memory of Tissia Silvana, who lived 55 years and died on the Nones of September in the 381st year of the province.
EDCS-27100169.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription for Tissia Silvana, detailing her age and death date within the provincial dating system.

EDCS-27100171.translation: EMV [?] Navar, the innocent, lived for 4 years and died 4 days before the Kalends of [?] in the 356th year of the province. His father had it made for his dearest son.
EDCS-27100171.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young boy, Navar, including his age, date of death in the provincial era, and notes that his father commissioned the monument.

EDCS-27100172.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Numitoria Satura lived for 27 years. Ulpius Donatus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-27100172.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Ulpius Donatus to his wife Numitoria Satura, stating her age at death.

EDCS-27100173.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Petronius Pamphilus lived for 10 years and died 15 days before the Kalends of November in the 442nd year of the province. He returned his debt in Christ.
EDCS-27100173.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Petronius Pamphilus at age 10 and mentions a Christian context for his passing.

EDCS-27100174.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sedata Ianuaria lived for 60 years and died 17 days before the Kalends of June in the 312th year of the province.
EDCS-27100174.summary: This funerary inscription details the lifespan and death date of Sedata Ianuaria, using the provincial calendar for dating.

EDCS-27100175.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sempronius Ianuarius lived for about 35 years.
EDCS-27100175.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Sempronius Ianuarius, dated approximately to the late Roman period.

EDCS-27100176.translation: In memory of Sittia Prima, who lived 16 years and 11 days, on the day before the Kalends of March, in the 446th year of the province.
EDCS-27100176.summary: This epitaph commemorates Sittia Prima, detailing her age at death and dating the inscription to the 446th year of the province.

EDCS-27100177.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Slacia R ia lived and departed on the 3rd day before the Ides of [Month], in the 340th year of the province.
EDCS-27100177.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Slacia R ia, providing a partial date and mentioning the 340th year of the province.

EDCS-27100178.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For the most loving daughter, Titia Saturnina, who lived [age]. Her most loving father, Iunius Statuleni, erected this memorial to his deserving wife.
EDCS-27100178.summary: This funerary epitaph is a memorial erected by a father, Iunius Statuleni, for his beloved wife and daughter, Titia Saturnina.

EDCS-27100179.translation: In memory of Sulpicia Faustina, who lived 26 years and departed on the 3rd day before the Ides of August. Her husband erected this for his dearest wife, in the 313th year of the province.
EDCS-27100179.summary: This epitaph records the death of Sulpicia Faustina at the age of 26, commissioned by her husband and dated to the 313th year of the province.

EDCS-27100180.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titius Salvianus lived 25 years and departed on the 4th day before the Ides of January, in the 362nd year of the province. His dearest brothers erected this.
EDCS-27100180.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Titius Salvianus, who died at age 25, with the monument erected by his brothers and dated to the 362nd year of the province.

EDCS-27100181.translation: In memory of Valeria Germana, who lived 28 years and departed on the 5th day before the Ides of December, in the 354th year of the province. Her most loving father erected this.
EDCS-27100181.summary: This memorial honors Valeria Germana, who died at age 28, with the monument erected by her loving father and dated to the 354th year of the province.

EDCS-27100183.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ulpius Germanus lived 45 years and departed on the 7th day before the Kalends of March, in the 385th year of the province. His father erected this memorial for his son.
EDCS-27100183.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ulpius Germanus at age 45, commissioned by his father and dated to the 385th year of the province.

EDCS-27100184.translation: Ulpius erected this for his son, who lived [age] and departed in [Month] of October, in the [number]th year of the province.
EDCS-27100184.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph indicating that Ulpius erected a monument for his son, with the date and lifespan partially preserved.

EDCS-27100186.translation: In memory of Ulpia Micara, who lived [age] and departed on the day of the Ides of June, in the 536th year of the province.
EDCS-27100186.summary: This epitaph commemorates Ulpia Micara, noting her death on the Ides of June and dating the inscription to the 536th year of the province.

EDCS-27100187.translation: In memory of Emitta, who lived 61 years and departed the day before the Nones of July in the 407th year of the province.
EDCS-27100187.summary: A memorial inscription for Emitta, dating to the 407th year of the province.

EDCS-27100191.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Calpurnianus, an equestrian officer of the Parthian ala, chief clerk of military pay for two years, lived 16 years and 11 months. His devoted [mother/wife, LINER is unclear] made this.
EDCS-27100191.summary: A tomb inscription for Calpurnianus, an equestrian officer, made by a relative.

EDCS-27100192.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. M. FC lived [some duration] Pomarensian years.
EDCS-27100192.summary: A tomb inscription for M. FC, with an unclear duration of life given in 'Pomarensian years'.

EDCS-27100193.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anulia Ianuaria, the cruel one, lived 25 years; her parents and husband made this for her.
EDCS-27100193.summary: A tomb inscription for Anulia Ianuaria, made by her parents and husband.

EDCS-27100194.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Iulia lived 12 years; her mother made this eternal home for her in the 420th year of the province.
EDCS-27100194.summary: A tomb inscription for Aurelia Iulia, made by her mother and dating to the 420th year of the province.

EDCS-27100195.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilius Zabullus, who lived 26 years. Caecilius Rogatianus [made this] for his deserving father.
EDCS-27100195.summary: A tomb inscription for Caecilius Zabullus, made by his son Caecilius Rogatianus.

EDCS-27100196.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Coddea, dearest mother, who lived 70 years. Iulius Victor and Cresre, sons, made this Roman home for her in the 333rd year of the province.
EDCS-27100196.summary: A tomb inscription for Caecilia Coddea, made by her sons and dating to the 333rd year of the province.

EDCS-27100197.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius. Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius.
EDCS-27100197.summary: A fragmentary tomb inscription for Iulius.

EDCS-27100198.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nonnia Monula lived approximately 55 years; her sons made this eternal home for her.
EDCS-27100198.summary: A tomb inscription for Nonnia Monula, made by her sons.

EDCS-27100199.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Onesimus, the nurse and well-deserving freedman. Valerius Hirrutus, patron, made this. He lived 76 years.
EDCS-27100199.summary: A tomb inscription for the freedman Onesimus, made by his patron Valerius Hirrutus.

EDCS-27100200.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Celsus and Palmunis made and dedicated an eternal home for their most excellent father Pomponius Palmuni, who lived 55 years, four months after his death.
EDCS-27100200.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponius Palmuni, erected by his sons Celsus and Palmunis.

EDCS-27100201.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Zabullus lived about 40 years; his sons made him an eternal home in the 419th year of the province.
EDCS-27100201.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius Zabullus, erected by his sons, dating to the provincial year 419.

EDCS-27100202.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Flavia lived 25 years. Secundus, her husband, made her an eternal home together with himself.
EDCS-27100202.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Flavia, erected by her husband Secundus.

EDCS-27100203.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Matrona lived about 30 years; her brothers made her an eternal home in the 5th year of the province.
EDCS-27100203.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Matrona, erected by her brothers, dating to the provincial year 5.

EDCS-27100204.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, in peace. Naila lived about 80 years; her sons made her an eternal home in the 550th year of the province.
EDCS-27100204.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Naila, erected by her sons, dating to the provincial year 550.

EDCS-27100205.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Adiutor made an eternal home for his good wife Aretusa, who lived 42 years.
EDCS-27100205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aretusa, erected by her husband Iulius Adiutor.

EDCS-27100207.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Satura, who lived 40 years, departed on the 10th day before the Kalends of November, [and was given] a Roman home in the 386th year of the province.
EDCS-27100207.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Satura, noting her age, date of death, and burial date, dating to the provincial year 386.

EDCS-27100208.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cecilia Rogata, most beloved mother, who lived about 70 years, [was given] a Roman home sorrowfully by her son, Cecilius Restutus, in the 320th year of the province.
EDCS-27100208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cecilia Rogata, erected by her son Cecilius Restutus, dating to the provincial year 320.

EDCS-27100209.translation: Claudia Urbana, who lived about 60 years, departed on the 4th day before the Ides of November. Sepius Donatianus and Mateus [her son(s)] established a Roman home in the 380th year of the province.
EDCS-27100209.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Urbana, noting her age and date of death, erected by her son(s), dating to the provincial year 380.

EDCS-27100210.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Rufinus, most beloved brother, who lived 45 years. His brothers Iuli Florius and Conantius and his wife Aurelia Iulia made him a Roman home in the 421st year of the province.
