EDCS-46400167.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Gaius Atilius Crescens, a veteran and former decurion, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400168.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Victorinus of the V Cohort.
EDCS-46400168.summary: This funerary inscription records Iulius Victorinus, who served in the V Cohort, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400169.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Volusius Secundus, son of Caius, of the Arnensis tribe, a decurion appointed by selection, lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46400169.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Volusius Secundus, a decurion appointed by selection, who lived 70 years, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400170.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Arnuleius Victor, son of Aemilia, of the Arnensis tribe, priest of the colony of Tubusuctita, lived 90 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46400170.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Arnuleius Victor, a priest of the colony of Tubusuctita, who lived 90 years, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400172.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anicetus lived 70 years.
EDCS-46400172.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Anicetus, who lived 70 years, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400173.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Arapsanius Felix lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46400173.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Arapsanius Felix, who lived 35 years, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400174.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Arius Marcus lived 50 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46400174.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Arius Marcus, who lived 50 years, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400175.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextus Aveius Bissus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-46400175.summary: This funerary inscription records Sextus Aveius Bissus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400176.translation: Caelius Rusticus lived 63 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-46400176.summary: This funerary inscription records Caelius Rusticus, who lived 63 years, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400177.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cluvia Maior lived 60 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-46400177.summary: This funerary inscription records Cluvia Maior, who lived 60 years, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400178.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Cornelius Faustus, son of Publius, of the Arnensian tribe, most devoted to his father Bellicus, lived 61 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400178.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Cornelius Faustus, recording his devotion to his father and his age at death.

EDCS-46400179.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Dellius Martialis, son of Quintus, of the Arnensian tribe, lived 65 years; he is buried here, happily.
EDCS-46400179.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Dellius Martialis, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46400181.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Iulius Dativus lived 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400181.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Dativus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46400182.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Octavia lived 51 years.
EDCS-46400182.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Octavia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-46400183.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Licinia Tertulla, daughter of Quintus, lived 85 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-46400183.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinia Tertulla, recording her age at death.

EDCS-46400184.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Licinia Urbana, daughter of Decimus, lived 41 years; she is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-46400184.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinia Urbana, recording her age at death and including a common funerary wish.

EDCS-46400185.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Octavia, daughter of Publius, from Fundi.
EDCS-46400185.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Octavia, identifying her father and origin.

EDCS-46400186.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Petronius Rogatus, son of Caius, of the Cornelia tribe, lived 7 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400186.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Petronius Rogatus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46400187.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Rogata, daughter of Marcus, lived [age missing]. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Sallius Victor, son of Marcus, of the Arnensian tribe, lived 75 years.
EDCS-46400187.summary: This inscription appears to be a conflation of two separate funerary epitaphs, one for Iulia Rogata and one for Marcus Sallius Victor.

EDCS-46400188.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. SVACO lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400188.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for SVACO, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46400189.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Vitalio lived 23 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400189.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vitalio, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46400190.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Gessius Optatus, a boy of outstanding character, lived 9 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400190.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Gessius Optatus, a young boy, noting his character and age at death.

EDCS-46400191.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Aemilius Priscus lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400191.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Priscus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46400192.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Claudia Victoria.
EDCS-46400192.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a burial place to Claudia Victoria.

EDCS-46400193.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Sextius Fundanus, son of Quintus, of the Arnensis tribe, lived 26 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-46400193.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Sextius Fundanus, noting his tribal affiliation and age at death.

EDCS-46400213.translation: To Perpetuus, our Emperor, Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, Invictus, Pius, Felix Augustus, 172.
EDCS-46400213.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Aurelian, possibly related to a military unit or distance marker, dating from his reign.

EDCS-46400214.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caelia Maior.
EDCS-46400214.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a burial place to Caelia Maior.

EDCS-46400216.summary: This inscription likely refers to a table or altar dedicated by or associated with a person named Peregrinus.

EDCS-46400218.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Cocceia Secunda lived 37 years.
EDCS-46400218.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cocceia Secunda, recording her age at death.

EDCS-46400219.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Sentius Saturninus, a veteran, lived 80 years.
EDCS-46400219.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sentius Saturninus, a veteran, recording his age at death.

EDCS-46400220.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aelia Merita made this for her daughter, Marcus Aurelius Rogatus, a veteran, according to his will; he lived 2 years.
EDCS-46400220.summary: This epitaph records a funerary monument set up by Aelia Merita for her daughter, who lived only 2 years.

EDCS-46400221.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Volussius Honoratus lived 65 years; his mother-in-law, Volussia Honorata, made this.
EDCS-46400221.summary: This epitaph commemorates Volussius Honoratus, who lived 65 years, and was erected by his mother-in-law.

EDCS-46400224.translation: He lived. Mar TTL Maximi ordered this to be placed and made it at his own expense.
EDCS-46400224.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument erected at his own expense by Mar TTL Maximi for someone who lived and has now died.

EDCS-46800216.translation: Iulia Monis with her son, measurers, for their memory.
EDCS-46800216.summary: This inscription, likely funerary, commemorates Iulia Monis and her son, who were measurers and possibly builders.

EDCS-46800217.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sentius lived 43 years; his wife Postumia Therine made this.
EDCS-46800217.summary: This epitaph records the death of Sentius at age 43 and was erected by his wife, Postumia Therine.

EDCS-46900275.translation: To our Lords Emperors, the Flavians Valerius Constantinus Maximus and Licinianus Licinius, Augusti.
EDCS-46900275.summary: This is a dedication to the Roman Emperors Constantine I and Licinius II, dated to their joint reign (c. 317-324 AD).

EDCS-46900276.translation: To our Lord Iulianus, Invictus, Perpetual Augustus.
EDCS-46900276.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a Roman emperor named Iulianus, likely Julian the Apostate (r. 361-363 AD).

EDCS-46900277.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, Invictus, Pius, Felix, Augustus, from Aras, one mile.
EDCS-46900277.summary: This inscription marks a distance of one mile from Aras, dedicated to Emperor Aurelian (r. 270-275 AD).

EDCS-47100044.translation: Lucius Furius Maximus lived 85 years; the heir made and dedicated him here.
EDCS-47100044.summary: This epitaph records the death of Lucius Furius Maximus at age 85 and was set up by his heir.

EDCS-47100045.summary: This epitaph records the death of Arruntia Secundiana at the young age of 12.

EDCS-47100046.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Gallus, dutiful, lived 85 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-47100046.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Marcus Antonius Gallus, who lived 85 years, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-47100048.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fania Gemina lived 66 years. Her heir set up this monument at his own expense.
EDCS-47100048.summary: A funerary inscription for Fania Gemina, who lived 66 years, with the monument erected by her heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-47200069.translation: In memory of Atininus. He lived 53 years and departed on the 7th of February in the 336th year of the province. Made by Flavius Dativus, his son-in-law, and Saturnina, his daughter, in Christ.
EDCS-47200069.summary: A funerary inscription in Latin and Greek for Atininus, who died on February 7th in provincial year 336 (AD 310/311), set up by his son-in-law and daughter, reflecting a Christian context.

EDCS-47200070.translation: In memory of Iulianus Cesconus. He lived 40 years and died the day before the Kalends of January in the 352nd year of the province.
EDCS-47200070.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Iulianus Cesconus, who died on December 31st in provincial year 352 (AD 326/327).

EDCS-47200071.translation: Table of Iulianus. He lived three years. Buried on the 4th of the Nones of September in the 353rd year of the province.
EDCS-47200071.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulianus, who died at the age of three on September 2nd in provincial year 353 (AD 327/328).

EDCS-47200072.translation: Table in memory of Marius Samardacus, also called Munatianus. He lived 36 years and died on the 16th of the Kalends of November in the 361st and first year of the province.
EDCS-47200072.summary: A funerary inscription for Marius Samardacus (Munatianus), who died on October 17th in provincial year 361 (AD 335/336).

EDCS-47200073.translation: In memory of Pegassia. She lived 22 years and departed on the 5th of the Nones of October in the 366th year of the province.
EDCS-47200073.summary: A funerary inscription for Pegassia, who died on October 3rd in provincial year 366 (AD 340/341).

EDCS-47200074.translation: In memory of Aelius Secundus. He lived 17 years and 4 months and died on the 4th of the Kalends of November in the 366th year of the province.
EDCS-47200074.summary: A funerary inscription for Aelius Secundus, who died on October 29th in provincial year 366 (AD 340/341).

EDCS-47200075.translation: After the consulship of Bassus and Philippus, most excellent men. Burial of Aelius Equitus, also called Calvaronis. He lived 70 years and died on the 15th of the Kalends of February, the day of the Sun, in the 370th year of the province.
EDCS-47200075.summary: A funerary inscription marking the burial of Aelius Equitus (Calvaronis), who died on January 18th in provincial year 370 (AD 344/345), with a reference to consuls and the day of the Sun.

EDCS-47200076.translation: Table and memorial of Iulia Feli. She lived [years?] and died on the 11th of the Kalends of December in the 362nd year of the province. From God, Vesenius [?], husband, [erected it]. Died at 85 years old on the 8th of the Kalends of April in the 381st year of the province.
EDCS-47200076.summary: A complex funerary inscription likely commemorating Iulia Feli, who died in provincial year 362 (AD 336/337), and her husband, CN. Passus, who died at 85 in provincial year 381 (AD 355/356), possibly with Christian elements.

EDCS-47200077.translation: By the public money of Antoninus Pius.
EDCS-47200077.summary: This inscription, likely from a public building project, records funding provided by Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-47200078.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Namgiddo Casca lived 75 years; he rests here. His father had this made.
EDCS-47200078.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Namgiddo Casca at age 75, with the monument erected by his father.

EDCS-47200079.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Ianuarius lived 60 years; he rests here.
EDCS-47200079.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Julius Ianuarius at age 60.

EDCS-47200080.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Gemnus lived 6 years; he rests here.
EDCS-47200080.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Julius Gemnus at the young age of 6.

EDCS-47200081.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Cittin lived 7 years.
EDCS-47200081.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Julius Cittin at the young age of 7.

EDCS-47200082.translation: Pompeia Rustica lived 80 years. To the Divine Shades. Quintus Gargilius Secundus lived 90 years. To the Divine Shades. Sacred. Quintus Gargilius Victor lived 29 years and 13 days.
EDCS-47200082.summary: This inscription appears to record the deaths of three individuals, possibly family members, with Quintus Gargilius Victor being the last and youngest to die.

EDCS-47200083.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Otacilia Maiorica lived 30 years; she rests here.
EDCS-47200083.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Otacilia Maiorica at age 30.

EDCS-47200085.translation: Mecius Datus lived 35 years. Aelia Extrica made this for her husband.
EDCS-47200085.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Mecius Datus at age 35, with the monument erected by his wife Aelia Extrica.

EDCS-47200086.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Maior lived 62 years for her most dutiful mother. Iulius Optatus, son, made this. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Verecundus lived 3 years for his sweetest son.
EDCS-47200086.summary: This inscription records two funerary monuments, one for Iulia Maior dedicated by her son Iulius Optatus, and another for Iulius Verecundus dedicated by a parent.

EDCS-47200087.translation: Iunia Vitalica lived 28 years. Largius Fortunatus made this for his dearest. Largia Herennia lived four years and eight months. Largius Fortunatus made this for his sweetest.
EDCS-47200087.summary: This inscription records two funerary monuments erected by Largius Fortunatus for his wife Iunia Vitalica and daughter Largia Herennia.

EDCS-47200088.translation: Gato lies here, is placed here; he lived 32 years.
EDCS-47200088.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Gato, who lived 32 years.

EDCS-47200089.translation: Saturninus and Antonia Maxsima gladly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-47200089.summary: This is a dedication recording Saturninus and Antonia Maxsima fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-47200090.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Getulicus.

EDCS-47200091.translation: Pius, son of Julius Vic, lived 15 years.
EDCS-47200091.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Pius, son of Julius Vic, who lived 15 years.

EDCS-47200143.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Donatus, his son. Sacred to the departed spirits. Gargilia Prisca, his wife. Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Datus, priest, lived 80 years. His sons made [this]. Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Datius, his son.
EDCS-47200143.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, likely erected by the sons of Julius Datus, a priest, and mentioning his wife and son.

EDCS-47200144.translation: lived 71 years; he was placed here.
EDCS-47200144.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of an unnamed individual who lived 71 years.

EDCS-47200145.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Pupnius Rogatus, to his son Caio Pupnio Rato, lived 8 years. [---] made [this].
EDCS-47200145.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, erected by Gaius Pupnius Rogatus to his son Caio Pupnio Rato, who died at age 8.

EDCS-47200146.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucisca Satura, son, lived [---] years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Paticius Rogatus, lived [---] years; he completed [this] in his lifetime. Sacred to the departed spirits. Rogatus, son, to his dearest mother Aria Amanda, lived 92 years, made [this].
EDCS-47200146.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, mentioning Lucisca Satura, Paticius Rogatus, and Aria Amanda, who was honored by her son Rogatus.

EDCS-47200147.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Servilius Mitteus lived 45 years. Sentius Victorinus made [this] for his dearest [relative/friend] from his own [property].
EDCS-47200147.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, erected by Sentius Victorinus for Servilius Mitteus, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-47200148.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunata lived 8 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-47200148.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Fortunata, who lived 8 years.

EDCS-47200149.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marius Maxutius lived 35 years.
EDCS-47200149.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marius Maxutius.

EDCS-47200337.translation: Dativa and her sons set up a table in memory of Iulius Martialis.
EDCS-47200337.summary: A funerary monument where Dativa and her sons dedicated a table in memory of Iulius Martialis.

EDCS-47200338.translation: Iulia Donata deposited a table for her sons Victor, Malpolax, and Lanr.
EDCS-47200338.summary: A funerary record of Iulia Donata depositing a table for her sons.

EDCS-47200340.translation: In memory of Caelia Musa Iacina, who is buried here. Her husband and sons set up a table. She lived 40 years, in the provincial year 323.
EDCS-47200340.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caelia Musa Iacina, including her lifespan and the dedication of a table by her family, dated to the provincial year 323.

EDCS-47400306.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Soricinus lived 45 years for his deserving father, in the provincial year 370.
EDCS-47400306.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Soricinus, dedicated to his father and dated to the provincial year 370.

EDCS-47400307.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antissius Victor lived 75 years and departed on the 8th day before the Kalends of May. His sons Florinsus, Donatus, and Boinus, brothers, performed the funeral rites for their deserving father, in the provincial year 350.
EDCS-47400307.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antissius Victor, detailing his lifespan and death date, with funeral rites performed by his sons, dated to the provincial year 350.

EDCS-47400308.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Donatus lived 6 years. Ianuarius, his father, made this for his dearest son, in the provincial year 340.
EDCS-47400308.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Donatus, who died at age 6, dedicated by his father and dated to the provincial year 340.

EDCS-47400318.translation: Aiax, son of Caius Iulius Pelazus, lived 6 years and 10 days. He is buried here.
EDCS-47400318.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aiax, son of Caius Iulius Pelazus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-47400319.translation: Ianuaria, daughter of Marcius, lived 18 days. She is buried here.
EDCS-47400319.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, daughter of Marcius, recording her lifespan of 18 days.

EDCS-47400320.translation: Himerus, freedman of Quintus, aged 33 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-47400320.summary: A funerary epitaph for Himerus, a freedman of Quintus, recording his age at death and burial.

EDCS-47400322.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Glicira lived 22 years and 11 days. She made this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-47400322.summary: A funerary dedication to Iulia Glicira, likely from her family, with a mention of her age.

EDCS-47400323.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Livia Maxima, abbosa, lived 90 years.
EDCS-47400323.summary: A funerary dedication to Livia Maxima, mentioning her age.

EDCS-47400324.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Diria lived 21 years, 4 months, and 7 days. Iulius Rufus made this for his sweetest wife.
EDCS-47400324.summary: A funerary dedication from Iulius Rufus to his wife Iulia Diria, specifying her age at death.

EDCS-47400325.translation: To Marcus Messius Masculus, by his will. Publius Cornelius Honoratus, perpetual high priest of the Augusti, patron of the colony, grandson and successor through inheritance, for his share.
EDCS-47400325.summary: A dedication or legal notice concerning the inheritance and patronage rights related to Marcus Messius Masculus.

EDCS-47400339.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilius Rogatianus lived 36 years. Aemilia Pacatilla Metilia Flavia made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-47400339.summary: A funerary dedication from Aemilia Pacatilla Metilia Flavia to her husband Aemilius Rogatianus, mentioning his age.

EDCS-47400340.translation: Publius Iulius Sofeius lived 30 years. He lies here.
EDCS-47400340.summary: A simple tombstone inscription for Publius Iulius Sofeius, stating his age and burial location.

EDCS-47400341.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Faustina made this. She lived 45 years. She lies here.
EDCS-47400341.summary: A funerary dedication to Aemilia Faustina, stating her age and burial location.

EDCS-47400361.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and to the Emperor Caesar Carinus, his noblest son, and to Caesar Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, noblest Caesar.
EDCS-47400361.summary: A dedication or official titulature for the emperors Carus, Carinus, and Numerian, listing their honors and titles.

EDCS-47400371.translation: Agide placed this vessel for his mother Futusai, for her merits. She lived 97 years.
EDCS-47400371.summary: A funerary dedication by Agide for his mother Futusai, mentioning her age and perhaps a dedication of a drinking vessel (cipus).

EDCS-47400372.translation: To the memory of Po osus the presbyter. Here in peace. Hallelujah. Amen.
EDCS-47400372.summary: A Christian funerary inscription for Po osus the presbyter, using liturgical phrases.

EDCS-47500651.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Septimius Severus Pius Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the divine Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland; set up by decree of the local council at public expense.
EDCS-47500651.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by public decree, dedicated to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, likely during his reign.

EDCS-47600072.translation: Baebia Quintula set this up to her dearest daughter Victoria, who lived 18 years.
EDCS-47600072.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Baebia Quintula for her daughter Victoria, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-47600073.translation: She lived 60 years. D(is) C(onsecratus) V(ixit) L(ongissimus) O(b:) C(onsecratus) V(ita) L(o...) [---] has placed this.
EDCS-47600073.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of an individual, likely a woman, with a partial funerary dedication.

EDCS-47600557.translation: To the divine shades. Iulia Venusta set up this monument to [---] and Iustinus, who lived...
EDCS-47600557.summary: This is a funerary inscription initiated by Iulia Venusta for herself and Iustinus, with the date of death missing.

EDCS-47600558.translation: To the divine shades. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Nus V IIO, who lived seventy-seventh year.
EDCS-47600558.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the shades, mentioning an individual named Nus who died in their seventy-seventh year.

EDCS-47700111.translation: He lived 30 years. His sons made this.
EDCS-47700111.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of the deceased and stating that their sons erected the monument.

EDCS-48900619.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of a sale or award of filtered lamps to an individual named Assenus.

EDCS-51500129.translation: Titus Octavius Longus, trierarch of Augustus, restored this altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, which had fallen down due to storms.
EDCS-51500129.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Octavius Longus, a naval officer, likely during the early Imperial period.

EDCS-52100138.summary: This is a simple tomb inscription identifying Caius Vilonius Medicus as buried at this location.

EDCS-54800037.summary: This inscription identifies Caius Iulius Fortunatus by his name and his priestly office.

EDCS-54800038.translation: Gaius Terentius Flavianus, priest, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-54800038.summary: A priest named Gaius Terentius Flavianus gladly fulfilled a vow, likely on a religious dedication or monument, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800046.translation: To the Lord God, holy Saturn Augustus.
EDCS-54800046.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Saturn Augustus, likely part of a religious monument, possibly from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800047.translation: Gaius Fulvinius Mutthun gladly fulfilled his vow to Saturn Augustus.
EDCS-54800047.summary: Gaius Fulvinius Mutthun fulfilled a vow to Saturn Augustus, probably on a religious dedication, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800048.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus; Gaius Julius Ae, priest, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-54800048.summary: A priest named Gaius Julius Ae fulfilled a vow to Saturn Augustus, likely on a religious dedication, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800052.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Tucia Marciana lived 95 years. Made by Tucia Quartilla, placed.
EDCS-54800052.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and age of Tucia Marciana, set up by Tucia Quartilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800053.translation: Marcus Ulpius Romulus, son of Marcus, and Petronia Honorata made this willingly.
EDCS-54800053.summary: Marcus Ulpius Romulus and Petronia Honorata created this monument, likely a tomb or dedication, as indicated by their voluntary action, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800056.translation: Dudit Mutumbalis, daughter, gladly and successfully fulfilled her vow to the god Saturn.
EDCS-54800056.summary: Dudit Mutumbalis fulfilled a vow to the god Saturn, likely on a religious dedication, with a note of good fortune, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800057.translation: Quintus Aemilius Victor fulfilled the sacred rite.
EDCS-54800057.summary: Quintus Aemilius Victor performed a sacred rite, likely a dedication or offering, possibly from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800058.translation: Sulpicia Monna, mother, fulfilled the sacred rite for Julius Victor.
EDCS-54800058.summary: Sulpicia Monna, as a mother, performed a sacred rite or dedication in honor of Julius Victor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800059.translation: Marcus Accius Crescens, son, gladly fulfilled [his vow] to Saturn.
EDCS-54800059.summary: Marcus Accius Crescens fulfilled a vow to Saturn, likely on a religious dedication, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54800060.translation: To the Holy Eternal God, Julius Botrius gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-54800060.summary: A votive inscription dedicated by Julius Botrius to a deity, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-58000059.summary: This inscription records a table belonging to Honorius Vitoria, possibly a funerary marker or a functional object.

EDCS-58000062.translation: In memory of Iulius Iunior, who lived for eight years and died on the day before the Ides of September, his son Gavosus with his brothers made this in the 333rd year of the province.
EDCS-58000062.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Iulius Iunior, erected by his sons and dated to 333 AD (assuming the era of the province of Africa).

EDCS-58000069.translation: Table of Adei Timilaias, made by Adei Saulonus Usandis and Germana.
EDCS-58000069.summary: This inscription indicates a table belonging to Adei Timilaias, created by Adei Saulonus Usandis and Germana, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-58000070.translation: Table of Iuli Sev, placed by Iulis Spenfis Rogatus.
EDCS-58000070.summary: This inscription records a table belonging to Iuli Sev, set up by Iulis Spenfis Rogatus, possibly a funerary marker.

EDCS-58200051.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Quirila lived for XXVI years.
EDCS-58200051.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Iulia Quirila, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-58200052.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Valerius Rusticus, pious, lived for V years; he is buried here.
EDCS-58200052.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Lucius Valerius Rusticus, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-58400005.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Valerius Diocletian, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, and to Caesar Flavius Valerius Maximian, Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Maximian, the Unconquered, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, and to Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius.
EDCS-58400005.summary: A dedication to Emperor Diocletian, co-emperor Maximian, and Caesar Constantius, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-58400017.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Victorina lived for [XX] years and [X] months.
EDCS-58400017.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Iulia Victorina, with her age partially restored.

EDCS-58400018.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Victorina lived for LXXXV years; Geminius made this. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Geminius Verus lived for XCV years; Geminius made this.
EDCS-58400018.summary: A double funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Iulia Victorina and Geminius Verus, both erected by Geminius.

EDCS-58400023.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cesia Maria lived 77 years.
EDCS-58400023.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Cesia Maria, recording her lifespan of 77 years.

EDCS-58400024.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Rogata lived 17 years.
EDCS-58400024.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Rogata, recording her lifespan of 17 years.

EDCS-58400025.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. C. Metunus lived 75 years and 700 days.
EDCS-58400025.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to C. Metunus, recording his lifespan of 75 years and 700 days.

EDCS-58400026.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Claudia Barbara lived 40 years.
EDCS-58400026.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Claudia Barbara, recording her lifespan of 40 years.

EDCS-58400029.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, Invictus, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-58400029.summary: An inscription honoring the emperor Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, likely a dedication on a public monument.

EDCS-58400030.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Saturnina, wife of Cominius the veteran, lived 65 years.
EDCS-58400030.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Saturnina, wife of a veteran, recording her lifespan of 65 years.

EDCS-58400033.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavia Bari lived 65 years. Aurelia, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-58400033.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavia Bari, recording her lifespan of 65 years and mentioning Aurelia, possibly her daughter.

EDCS-58500283.summary: A short funerary inscription stating that Obulcius is buried here, with a possible indication of age or an ordinal number.

EDCS-59600090.translation: Annia Quinta fulfilled her vow to the Good Goddess.
EDCS-59600090.summary: A votive inscription by Annia Quinta to the Bona Dea (Good Goddess).

EDCS-60200059.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Caecilius Caecilianus, son of Caius, grandson of Victor, lived 37 years, my people.
EDCS-60200059.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Caecilius Caecilianus, detailing his ancestry and lifespan of 37 years, with a familial closing.

EDCS-60200061.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Sorices lived 33 days. Caius Iulius Ianuarius lived 3 years, 3 months, and 1 day.
EDCS-60200061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of two individuals, Marcus Aurelius Sorices and Caius Iulius Ianuarius.

EDCS-60200063.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Iulia Bona.

EDCS-60200064.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Iulius Marius Marinus lived approximately twenty years.
EDCS-60200064.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the approximate lifespan of Publius Iulius Marius Marinus.

EDCS-60200066.translation: A memorial table for Iulia Valeria, who lived thirty years and departed on the ninth day before the Kalends of November in the 350th year of the province.
EDCS-60200066.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Iulia Valeria, noting her age at death and the date of her passing within the provincial era.

EDCS-60200067.translation: A memorial for Iulia Urbana. Iulius Hospes set [this up]. Buried on the 7th day before the Ides of September in the 325th year of the province. She lived 70 years.
EDCS-60200067.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial date and lifespan of Iulia Urbana, set up by her relative Iulius Hospes.

EDCS-60200068.translation: Marcus Annius Hilarus lived 55 years. Anni Paetinus and Annianus, his sons and heirs, made [this] for their most devoted father.
EDCS-60200068.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Marcus Annius Hilarus, dedicated by his sons.

EDCS-60200069.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Faonius Venustus lived 85 years. Faonius the younger [set this up for his] father.
EDCS-60200069.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Faonius Venustus, dedicated by his son.

EDCS-60200070.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Victoria lived 60 years. Caius Volussius Donatus lived 85 years. The sons DII made [this].
EDCS-60200070.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespans of Caecilia Victoria and Caius Volussius Donatus, dedicated by their sons.

EDCS-60200071.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Fortunata, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-60200071.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Iulia Fortunata, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-60200072.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aufidius Victor, the father, dedicated this tablet to his most longed-for son, Aufidius Messorus.
EDCS-60200072.summary: This funerary inscription records a father's dedication of a memorial tablet to his deceased son.

EDCS-60200073.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Crispinus made this for his father, who lived 75 years and 6 hours.
EDCS-60200073.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Crispinus to his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60500060.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph recording the name and age of the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60500085.translation: To Catia, daughter of Publius Cornelius.
EDCS-60500085.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Catia, daughter of Publius Cornelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60500099.translation: To the Emperors Caesars Gaius Valerius Diocletianus and Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus, Invicti, Pii, Felices, Augusti, and to the Caesars Gaius Flavius Constantius and Galerius Valerius Maximianus, the younger, most noble.
EDCS-60500099.summary: This inscription lists the titles of four Roman Emperors, likely Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Galerius, and dates to the Tetrarchy (late 3rd to early 4th century AD).

EDCS-60500100.summary: This is a milestone inscription indicating a distance of one mile from the location 'Aras', likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60500101.summary: This is a milestone inscription indicating a distance of two miles from the location 'Aras', likely dating to the reign of Emperor Gordian III (238-244 AD).

EDCS-60500103.translation: The pontifex maximus, consul, proconsul, holder of tribunician power, ordered milestones to be made through Titus Aelius Decrianus, procurator.
EDCS-60500103.summary: This inscription records an official order by a high-ranking magistrate to erect milestones, managed by a procurator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60500104.translation: Ordered milestones to be made through his procurator Publius Flavius Clemens, five miles from Aras.
EDCS-60500104.summary: This inscription records an official order by a procurator to erect milestones, indicating a distance of five miles from 'Aras', likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-61800290.translation: [After completing the seventh year of [my] parents, and after [---] children, for so many years will you always please me here, you who order beautiful inscriptions to be hidden, [proven] in exile.]
EDCS-61800290.summary: This is a fragmented and difficult funerary or memorial inscription, possibly reflecting on family, children, and the lasting nature of inscriptions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63300066.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying 'Donatus' as a priest, likely from the Late Roman or Early Christian period.

EDCS-65900121.translation: Monument of Crescens. He lived 89 years.
EDCS-65900121.summary: This is a funerary monument recording the lifespan of Crescens.

EDCS-67500067.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Clodius Rufus lived 67 years. Clodia and Sitia made this for their father.
EDCS-67500067.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Clodius Rufus, set up by his daughters Clodia and Sitia.

EDCS-70000042.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Licinia Marcelina lived 36 years. Felix Nati made this for her, well deserving.
EDCS-70000042.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Licinia Marcelina, set up by Felix Nati.

EDCS-70000043.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Publius Catellius Optatus lived 18 years. His devoted father, Publius Catellius Maximus, made this.
EDCS-70000043.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Publius Catellius Optatus, set up by his father.

EDCS-70000044.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Octavia Victoria, pious, lived 80 years. Her devoted sons made this, together with Titus, her grandson.
EDCS-70000044.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Octavia Victoria, set up by her sons and grandson.

EDCS-70000045.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Didin lived 3 years.
EDCS-70000045.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Didin.

EDCS-70000046.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Manlius Rogatus lived... years.
EDCS-70000046.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription records the name of Manlius Rogatus.

EDCS-70000048.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Clarus Faustus lived 71 years. His most devoted sons made this for their most devoted father, well deserving.
EDCS-70000048.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Clarus Faustus, set up by his sons.

EDCS-70000049.translation: Marcia Possidonia, his wife, made this for him, well deserving, [after he lived] 45 years.
EDCS-70000049.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a man who lived 45 years, set up by his wife Marcia Possidonia.

EDCS-70000050.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caecilia Victorina lived 18 years. Considius Emeritus made this.
EDCS-70000050.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caecilia Victorina, set up by Considius Emeritus.

EDCS-70000051.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caterius Aurelianus lived for 65 years. His heirs placed and dedicated [this].
EDCS-70000051.summary: A funerary dedication for Caterius Aurelianus, set up by his heirs.

EDCS-70000052.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Laucius Vitalis during life.
EDCS-70000052.summary: A funerary dedication for Laucius Vitalis, made during his lifetime.

EDCS-70000054.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Antonius Valens, standard-bearer of the II Cohort of Sardinians, served for 10 years, lived for 40 years.
EDCS-70000054.summary: A funerary inscription for Antonius Valens, a soldier who served in the II Cohort of Sardinians.

EDCS-70000055.translation: Iulius Longinus lived for 70 years. His pious sons made this for him, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-70000055.summary: A funerary monument for Iulius Longinus, erected by his sons.

EDCS-70000057.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Iulia Tertulina, his dearest wife. Iulius Victor, her husband, lived for 70 years.
EDCS-70000057.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulius Victor to his wife, Iulia Tertulina.

EDCS-70100003.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Quinto Volusio Emerito, who lived for 45 years. Munia Amanda placed this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-70100003.summary: A funerary dedication by Munia Amanda to her husband, Quinto Volusio Emerito.

EDCS-70100036.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius Vitalis lived for 27 years. He made [this] from the province in the year 317.
EDCS-70100036.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavius Vitalis, likely set up by a family member, dating to the provincial year 317.

EDCS-70100037.translation: In memory of Lucius Iunior, who lived for 51 years and died the day before the Ides of September. His son Gaudosus made this with his brothers in the 333rd year of the province.
EDCS-70100037.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Iunior, erected by his son Gaudosus and brothers, dating to the provincial year 333.

EDCS-70100040.translation: The infant named Honorata departed this life in her fifth month, thirteen days before the Kalends of July, in the 365th year of the province.
EDCS-70100040.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for an infant named Honorata, dating to the 365th year of the province.

EDCS-70200001.translation: A holy memorial. Victorinus, on the seventh day before the Ides of September, of the holy wood of the Cross, from the land of promise where Christ was born, with the names of the apostles Peter and Paul and of the martyrs Datianus, Donatianus, Cyprianus, Nemesianus, Cirtinus, and Victoricus. In the 320th year of the province, Benenatus and Paquarla placed this.
EDCS-70200001.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Benenatus and Paquarla in the 320th year of the province, commemorating martyrs and bearing relics of the True Cross.

EDCS-70400021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Umbricius Hospes.

EDCS-70400025.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gabinius Mercator.

EDCS-70400029.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Umbricius Hospes.

EDCS-70400030.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gabinius Mercator.

EDCS-71300327.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Favouring Gods, a vow fulfilled by Gaius Octavius Pudens, procurator of Severus Augustus, for the Bavari who were slain and captured.
EDCS-71300327.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter and other gods by Gaius Octavius Pudens, a procurator, after a military victory over the Bavari.

EDCS-73700047.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Clodius Successus.

EDCS-73700048.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Clodius Successus.

EDCS-73700049.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Madiecus.

EDCS-73700052.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Clodius Successus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-73700057.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Clodius Successus, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-73700059.summary: This inscription records the name Gaius Oppius Restitutus, possibly as a dedication or ownership marker.

EDCS-73700060.summary: This inscription records the name Clodius Successus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-73700061.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Clodius Successus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-73700065.summary: This inscription records the name Manius Aufidius, possibly as a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-73700067.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Clodius Successus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-73700069.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Munatius Adiectus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-73700070.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Clodius Successus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-73700071.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Clodius Successus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-73700075.summary: This is a personal name, likely a tomb inscription or a name on a monument.

EDCS-73700076.summary: This is a personal name, likely a tomb inscription or a name on a monument.

EDCS-73700078.summary: This inscription records the name of Caius Oppius Restitutus, likely for a tomb or monument.

EDCS-73700081.summary: This inscription records the name of Caius Oppius Restitutus, likely for a tomb or monument.

EDCS-73900432.translation: In the name of Christ, Flavius Mastien, a most distinguished man and comes, happily completed this property boundary of the province, 317 years.
EDCS-73900432.summary: This is a Christian inscription from the 4th century CE recording the completion of a property boundary by Flavius Mastien.

EDCS-74100037.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Antiochus lived 3 years, 10 months, and 23 days. An altar for his son.
EDCS-74100037.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Antiochus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100038.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Rutilius Victor lived 13 years, 10 months. Publius Rutilius Victor made this for a deserving son.
EDCS-74100038.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young boy, son of Publius Rutilius Victor, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100042.summary: This inscription records the name Diomedes and his father Caius Tellius, likely part of a tomb or monument.

EDCS-74100050.summary: This is a personal name, likely a tomb inscription or a name on a monument.

EDCS-75300387.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Umbrius Faustus, a soldier of the 4th Cohort of the Sugambri, lived 23 years, killed by barbarians.
EDCS-75300387.summary: This is a military funerary inscription for Umbrius Faustus, a soldier of the 4th Cohort of the Sugambri, who died in battle around the 1st century CE.

EDCS-75300388.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caelius Fortunatus, clerk of the Sygambrian cohort, lived 31 years and 10 months; killed in war. His father, loving and willing, dedicated and made this for his dutiful son.
EDCS-75300388.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a father to his son, Lucius Caelio Fortunato, a clerk in the Sygambrian cohort, who died in battle.

EDCS-75300389.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Sentius Primianus.
EDCS-75300389.summary: A simple funerary dedication to Gaius Sentius Primianus.

EDCS-75300390.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Idun (VI A). Lived 20 years. His father Caius established this.
EDCS-75300390.summary: A funerary monument recording the life of Idun, who lived 20 years, established by his father Caius.

EDCS-75600250.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Agathopotius lived 2 months and 5 days. His father Eutychianus made this for one well-deserving.
EDCS-75600250.summary: A funerary dedication by a father, Eutychianus, for his child Agathopotius, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-75600255.translation: To Marcus Junius Asclepiades, son of Marcus, of the voting tribe Quirina, honored with the public equestrian rank and having performed the rites of the Lupercalia.
EDCS-75600255.summary: An inscription honoring Marcus Junius Asclepiades for his public service, including equestrian rank and participation in the Lupercalia festival.

EDCS-75600257.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Cassiodorus Cito Priscus, son of Tiberius Claudius Diodorus, decurion with aedile duties, for his merits as his father's aedile, by the Forenses.
EDCS-75600257.summary: A dedication by the people of Forenses to Tiberius Claudius Cassiodorus Cito Priscus, son of a decurion, honoring his father's service as aedile.

EDCS-75600258.translation: To the prefect of our Augustus' province of Mauretania Caesariensis, Osrhoenorum Phuti[---], to his sons, and to their candidates.
EDCS-75600258.summary: An inscription dedicating something to a prefect of Mauretania Caesariensis and his associates.

EDCS-75700104.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Hermadion lived 16 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-75700104.summary: A funerary monument for Hermadion, who lived 16 years.

EDCS-75700105.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Gargilius Nobilianus, also called Pelagius, lived 16 years. To Arria Marciana Mileitana, who lived 97 years. Lucius Gargilius Rogatianus cared for (this).
EDCS-75700105.summary: A funerary inscription for two individuals, Arria Marciana Mileitana and Publius Gargilius Nobilianus (also known as Pelagius), likely cared for by Lucius Gargilius Rogatianus.

EDCS-75700106.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Publilius Secundus, son of Lucius, of the voting tribe Arnensi, lived 63 years. He is buried here. Caelia Colonica made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-75700106.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a wife, Caelia Colonica, to her husband, Lucius Publilius Secundus, who lived 63 years.

EDCS-75700107.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Minucius lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-75700107.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Minucius, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-75700108.translation: Sedia Urbana made this, her family servant.
EDCS-75700108.summary: This inscription records that Sedia Urbana dedicated something, likely a monument, made by her household slave.

EDCS-75700109.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Claudia Nice lived 45 years; she is buried here, to her dearest wife, by Claudius Anullinus.
EDCS-75700109.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Claudia Nice, who lived 45 years, and was dedicated by her husband Claudius Anullinus.

EDCS-75700110.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Claudia Valen...
EDCS-75700110.summary: This funerary epitaph is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld for Claudia Valen, but is incomplete.

EDCS-75700111.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucio Milo Socratus lived 15 years.
EDCS-75700111.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Lucio Milo Socratus, who lived 15 years.

EDCS-75700113.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Pompeius Sisulus lived 8 years.
EDCS-75700113.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caius Pompeius Sisulus, who lived 8 years.

EDCS-75700114.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Publius Aemilius Secundus lived about 45 years.
EDCS-75700114.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Publius Aemilius Secundus, who lived approximately 45 years.

EDCS-75700115.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Euthyches lived 4 years and 7 months; he is buried here. Rogatianus made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-75700115.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Euthyches, who lived 4 years and 7 months, and was dedicated by his father Rogatianus.

EDCS-75700116.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Successa lived 30 years. Propertius made this for his dearest, most obedient mother. Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Turritus lived 52 years. Propertius made this for his dearest father.
EDCS-75700116.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Successa and Turritus, dedicated by Propertius to his mother and father respectively.

EDCS-75700117.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Vestina lived 75 years.
EDCS-75700117.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulia Vestina, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-75800223.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Otacilia Maxima lived 21 years.
EDCS-75800223.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Otacilia Maxima.

EDCS-75800224.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Tertulla lived 36 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-75800224.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial place of Iulia Tertulla.

EDCS-75800225.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Valerius.
EDCS-75800225.summary: A funerary dedication to the spirits of the departed by Caius Valerius.

EDCS-75900086.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Tutina lived 95 years.
EDCS-75900086.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Aemilia Tutina.

EDCS-78300398.translation: Monument of Crescens, who lived 89 years.
EDCS-78300398.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Crescens and his lifespan.

EDCS-79000127.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Bomana Sissoi lived [illegible] years.
EDCS-79000127.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Bomana Sissoi, with an illegible age.

EDCS-79000129.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Africanus, decurion, lived 40 years. Valerius Felix made this for his dearest son; he is buried here.
EDCS-79000129.summary: A funerary epitaph by Valerius Felix for his son Valerius Africanus, a decurion, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-79500055.translation: From the workshop of Donatus: polished lamps.
EDCS-79500055.summary: An inscription indicating the origin and type of goods produced by Donatus's workshop.

EDCS-80100017.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning Rogatus, identified as a freedman.

EDCS-80100018.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording that Domitius was the maker of something.

EDCS-80100023.translation: Julia M, mother of a 25-year-old son, wishes the earth may lie lightly on you.
EDCS-80100023.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a mother who has died, with a wish for the deceased to rest in peace.

EDCS-80100026.translation: To the departed spirits. For Primi, mother of Atrinus.
EDCS-80100026.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits, likely for a mother named Primi.

EDCS-80100027.translation: To the departed spirits. Quinta, to her son, rests here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-80100027.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a son, with a dedication to the departed spirits and a wish for his repose.

EDCS-80100028.translation: To Anna, to her sister Lus.
EDCS-80100028.summary: This is a dedication to a sister named Lus, likely a funerary inscription.

EDCS-80100114.translation: Cartir Caesi Prisci's brewers rest here.
EDCS-80100114.summary: This inscription records the burial place of brewers associated with Cartir Caesi Prisci.

EDCS-80100147.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iugurtha lived 9 years.
EDCS-80100147.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Iugurtha, who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-80100148.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Donatula, pious, lived 20 years; she rests here. Aprilis, her wife, made this.
EDCS-80100148.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Donatula, who died at 20, dedicated by her wife Aprilis.

EDCS-80100149.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucio Malio Socrato lived 15 years.
EDCS-80100149.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Lucio Malio Socrato, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-80100150.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Matutina lived 95 years; she rests here.
EDCS-80100150.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Matutina, who died at the age of 95.

EDCS-80100151.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius, a man, is buried here.
EDCS-80100151.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits, recording the burial of a man named Gaius Julius.

EDCS-80100152.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vettia Masinissa dedicated this to her pupil, who lived for 5 years.
EDCS-80100152.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a deceased child by Vettia Masinissa.

EDCS-80100153.translation: Tertius Fortunatus Mussicius, pious, lived for 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-80100153.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tertius Fortunatus Mussicius, noting his age and burial.

EDCS-80100154.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Antonius Quinti Mutulus lived for... Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonia Rogata lived for 60 years; Antonius Luceus dedicated this to his pious parents.
EDCS-80100154.summary: This inscription, likely a tombstone, records the lives of Quintus Antonius Quinti Mutulus, Antonia Rogata, and Antonius Luceus, with the latter dedicating it to his parents.

EDCS-80100155.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Cornelius Victoricus lived for 40 years. Samia Emerita made this for her most beloved husband; he is buried here.
EDCS-80100155.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Publius Cornelius Victoricus and was made by his wife, Samia Emerita.

EDCS-80100156.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Manilius Rusticus lived for 55 years. Iulia Spes made this for her dearest spouse.
EDCS-80100156.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Quintus Manilius Rusticus and was made by his wife, Iulia Spes.

EDCS-80100157.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Miltacus Certunis, son, lived for 40 years. Iulius Felix made this for his deserving brother. Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Certus lived for 75 years. Iulius Felix made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-80100157.summary: This inscription commemorates two individuals, Iulius Miltacus and Gaius Iulius Certus, with Iulius Felix acting as dedicator for both, likely his brother and father.

EDCS-80100158.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caecilius Tanginus, also known as Oleaster, lived for 5 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Gargilia Romana, his wife, lived for 61 years. Lollia Columba, their grandchild, lived for 5 years and 1 month and 10 days. Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caecilius Tanginus, also known as Gregius, lived for 18 years and 7 months.
EDCS-80100158.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives of Lucius Caecilius Tanginus (and his agnomen Oleaster), Gargilia Romana, their grandchild Lollia Columba, and another Lucius Caecilius Tanginus (with agnomen Gregius).

EDCS-80100159.translation: Quintus Publilius Felix, freedman, lived for 42 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-80100159.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Publilius Felix, a freedman, noting his age and burial.

EDCS-80100161.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Donata lived for 65 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Caesius Primus lived for 115 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Calpurnius lived for 70 years.
EDCS-80100161.summary: This inscription records the ages of three individuals: Iulia Donata, Caius Caesius Primus, and Calpurnius.

EDCS-80100162.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Geminius Martialis lived for 70 years. Iulia Saturnina lived for 105 years.
EDCS-80100162.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of Publius Geminius Martialis and Iulia Saturnina.

EDCS-80200002.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Julius S. lived for 41 years.
EDCS-80200002.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Julius S.

EDCS-80200004.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gabini Clo.
EDCS-80200004.summary: A funerary inscription, likely for someone named Gabini Clo, dedicated to the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-80200012.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Donata Timpanaria, who lived 110 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-80200012.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Donata Timpanaria, noting her exceptional longevity and merit.

EDCS-80200013.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. [Son of] Baric, pious, lived for 2 years. Aeli.
EDCS-80200013.summary: A funerary inscription for a young child, son of Baric, identified as Aeli.

EDCS-80200019.summary: A dedication to the deity Saturn Augustus.

EDCS-80200020.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus. Lucius C I, priest.
EDCS-80200020.summary: A dedication to Saturn Augustus by Lucius C I, who identifies himself as a priest.

EDCS-80200022.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Octavius V.
EDCS-80200022.summary: A funerary inscription, likely for Octavius V, dedicated to the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-80200023.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Amibar.
EDCS-80200023.summary: A funerary inscription, likely for Caius Amibar, dedicated to the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-80200026.summary: An inscription that appears to be the name of a person, possibly a dedication or a tomb marker.

EDCS-80200039.summary: An inscription that names Hilarus and his father Caius Volusenus, likely a funerary or dedicatory context.

EDCS-80200041.summary: This inscription is a name, likely the owner or subject of a monument.

EDCS-80200051.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Modia Prima lived 80 years. Roga made this.
EDCS-80200051.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Modia Prima, with Roga as the dedicator.

EDCS-80600031.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Ulpius Annius lived 55 years.
EDCS-80600031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Marcus Ulpius Annius.

EDCS-80600032.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Rutilia Certa lies here.
EDCS-80600032.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating that Rutilia Certa is buried here.

EDCS-80600033.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Gargilius, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, Sterceius, lived 54 years and 12 days. Gargilii Secundus and Sterceius Felix made this for their father.
EDCS-80600033.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Gargilius, erected by his sons.

EDCS-80600034.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Otacilius lived 71 years. He lies here.
EDCS-80600034.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Caius Otacilius and his burial.

EDCS-80600035.translation: Marcus Avilius Ianuarius, an outstanding man, made this for Avilia Quartula, his dearest sister, and for Mamilius Rogatus, the incomparable.
EDCS-80600035.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating Avilia Quartula and Mamilius Rogatus, erected by Marcus Avilius Ianuarius.

EDCS-80600064.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gavia Tra lived 55 years. I. Longeus lived 96 years.
EDCS-80600064.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Gavia Tra and I. Longeus.

EDCS-80600065.translation: [he] dedicated and consecrated [this] for Silvana, his wife, [and] for his parents.
EDCS-80600065.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the dedication of a monument to a wife and parents.

EDCS-80600066.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Q. Memur lived 80 years. Ma lived...
EDCS-80600066.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the lifespans of two individuals, Q. Memur and Ma.

EDCS-80600067.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Miggin, aged 65.
EDCS-80600067.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Miggin at the age of 65.

EDCS-80600071.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Caus lived [X years].
EDCS-80600071.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Marcus Caus, with the duration of his life missing.

EDCS-80600073.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Cestius Nervianus lived 8 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-80600073.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cestius Nervianus, who died at the age of 8 and is buried here.

EDCS-80600074.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Titus Pompeius Saturninus the younger lived 25 years. His father for his dearest son.
EDCS-80600074.summary: This funerary epitaph records Titus Pompeius Saturninus the younger, who died at 25, and was honored by his father.

EDCS-80600075.translation: Lucius Gargilius Castus, aged 80, for his parent for his merits.
EDCS-80600075.summary: This funerary epitaph records Lucius Gargilius Castus, aged 80, and likely honors his parent.

EDCS-80600076.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Licinius Urbanus made and dedicated [this] for himself while alive.
EDCS-80600076.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Licinius Urbanus for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-80600078.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iulia Sorica lived 56 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-80600078.summary: This funerary epitaph records Iulia Sorica, who died at the age of 56 and is buried here.

EDCS-80600079.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavius Iucundus lived 54 years.
EDCS-80600079.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Flavius Iucundus, who died at the age of 54.

EDCS-80600080.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Fortunata the dutiful lived 55 years. Aemilius Geoia, son, lived 2 years.
EDCS-80600080.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Fortunata (aged 55) and her son Aemilius Geoia (aged 2).

EDCS-80600081.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Caius Anicius Victor lived 25 years.
EDCS-80600081.summary: This funerary epitaph records Caius Anicius Victor, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-80600083.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aelia Tursen lived 26 years. Thialis the dutiful made this.
EDCS-80600083.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Aelia Tursen, made by Thialis, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80600085.summary: This inscription is a simple name, likely a tombstone or ownership mark from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81200114.translation: To the holy god Dracon, and to the Genius of the place, Caecilius Felicianus, decurion of the cohort of Pannonians, gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-81200114.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caecilius Felicianus, a decurion, to a deity named Dracon and the local Genius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82400024.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, son of the divine Great Antoninus Pius Augustus, conqueror of Britain and Germany, greatest conqueror of Parthia, grandson of the divine Pius Severus Arabicus Adiabenicus, greatest conqueror of Parthia and Britain, great-grandson of the divine Commodus and the divine Marcus Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, father of the fatherland, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan and the divine Nerva, holding tribunician power, consul, emperor for the second time, for the colonists of the colony Julia Augusta Tubusuctitanus, from the immunis legion.
EDCS-82400024.summary: This is a lengthy titulature inscription for Emperor Severus Alexander, likely commemorating his relationship with the colony of Tubuspecti or a military unit stationed there, dating to his reign (222-235 CE).
